# Pebble Text-Entry Regression Plan

## Design Mock — shared canvas text entry

### Audit and evidence

Pebble has one canvas-drawn `TextField` implementation in
[`UIManagerM.swift`](Sources/Pebble/UIManagerM.swift), used by every shipping `TextField` found in
the source tree:

- [`CreateWorldScreen`](Sources/Pebble/MenusM.swift): World Name and Seed;
- [`LANLobbyScreen`](Sources/Pebble/LANLobbyScreen.swift): Player, host Code/Port, manual Host,
  join Port/Code;
- [`SettingsScreen`](Sources/Pebble/MenusM.swift): Ollama Model;
- [`AnvilScreen`](Sources/Pebble/ScreensM.swift): item rename;
- [`CreativeScreen`](Sources/Pebble/ScreensM.swift): item Search;
- [`TemplateNameScreen`](Sources/Pebble/ScreensM.swift): copied-template Name.

The complete typed-text inventory is broader than `TextField`. A second source audit of every
`onChar` owner found three custom editors in [`ScreensM.swift`](Sources/Pebble/ScreensM.swift):

- `CraftingRecipePopup`/`CraftingRecipeTypeahead`, shared by the survival Inventory 2x2 recipe menu
  and Crafting Table 3x3 recipe menu;
- `SignScreen`, a four-line append-at-end sign editor;
- `ChatScreen`, used for both ordinary chat and command entry (the command binding opens the same
  screen prefilled with `/`; there is no separate shipping `CommandScreen`).

`RPGCharacterScreen.onChar` deliberately returns false and is not a text editor. All remaining
`onChar` overrides delegate only to one of the shared `TextField` instances already listed.

The established visual language is a black field, one-pixel gray idle outline, white focused
outline, white entered text, dim-gray placeholder, and a one-pixel white caret blinking on a 400 ms
cadence. Clicking a field gives it exclusive focus; clicking elsewhere clears field focus. Shared
Backspace and Left/Right behavior is Character-indexed, and relayout preserves text, caret, and
focus. AppKit printable characters and Edit -> Paste feed the same screen path; Command/Control
printable chords do not become text.

Historical installed-app evidence confirms the default and Single Biome Create New World layouts
fit with both fields and bottom actions visible, and prior ordinary-app sessions successfully showed
a typed world name and seed, LAN fields, an Ollama model value, Creative search queries, and typed
template names. It also shows populated Inventory/Crafting recipe queries and an ordinary `/ai`
chat/command entry. No retained installed evidence exercises sign editing. That evidence establishes
the intended interaction and visual conventions, but it
predates the current input-router regression and is not current-build proof. No current real-app
capture demonstrating successful text entry was available during this Design Mock; final sign-off
must therefore inspect the rebuilt installed app rather than infer success from source or old frames.

### Surface contract

| Surface | Initial/empty behavior | Frozen limit and domain | Existing completion behavior |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Create World Name | Empty buffer displays `New World`; empty Create uses that default | 64 Characters; world display name | Create World/Create & Host button only |
| Create World Seed | Empty buffer displays `Leave blank for random` and means random seed | 64 Characters; numeric or deterministic text seed | Create button only |
| LAN Player | Prefilled sanitized local name when empty | 32 Characters and existing 96-byte sanitizer | Return joins only where the current LAN screen already does so |
| LAN host Code / Port | Empty optional code; default port | 8 / 5 Characters; existing code and UInt16 validation | Host World button |
| LAN manual Host / Port / Code | Host empty; default port; code empty | 253 / 5 / 8 Characters; existing host, UInt16, and code validation | Return joins from the existing player/manual-join focus set; Join World button remains primary |
| Settings Ollama Model | Existing saved value or `ollama model` placeholder | 128 Characters; existing local-model ASCII grammar | Return saves; Save/Refresh/Done keep their existing save boundary |
| Anvil rename | Empty until the item/result flow supplies or accepts a name | Existing shared 64-Character limit and anvil rules | Live result/cost refresh; no new Return action |
| Creative Search | Empty `Search` placeholder; category tab clears it | Existing shared 64-Character limit | Live filtering; no new Return action |
| Template Name | Empty `template name` placeholder and initially focused | 48 Characters; existing ASCII name grammar | Return/Numpad Return or Save attempts save and retains visible validation on failure |

There is no established generic Tab traversal for Pebble canvas fields, so this repair does not
invent one. Tab must neither insert a tab nor leak to world/hotbar/RPG actions while a text-entry
screen owns input. Any future focus traversal is a separate, screen-wide keyboard-navigation design.

### Custom editor contract

| Owner | Activation and existing editing | Frozen limit/completion | Paste expectation |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Inventory/Crafting Table recipe query | Opening `Recipes` gives the popup implicit text ownership; input appends at end; Backspace and Delete remove the last Character; clicking outside the recipe button/list closes and clears the query | 48 Characters; control scalars and DEL are ignored; excess input keeps the newest 48 Characters; Up/Down and wheel move the highlighted recipe; Return/Numpad Return selects it; close/reopen clears query | While the popup is open, single-line Paste follows the same control filter and newest-48 suffix rule; when closed it receives nothing |
| Sign editor | Opening the screen owns all printable input; append and Backspace operate at the end of the active line; Up/Down changes among four lines | No Character cap; each proposed append is accepted only while rendered line width remains strictly below 90 pixels; Return advances and closes only after wrapping from line 4 to line 1; Escape closes; close commits all four lines | Single-line Paste appends Character-by-Character to only the active line until the existing width boundary; newlines/control characters do not distribute across lines |
| Chat and `/` command entry | Opening Chat owns all printable input at the end; command mode is identical with `/` prefilled; Backspace removes the final Character; Up/Down traverse shared history | No explicit input-length cap; input wraps and displays at most the last four input lines; Tab completes/cycles the final command-line item token; Return submits nonblank input or closes blank input; Escape closes without submission | Single-line Paste appends exact Characters at the end, resets completion, and preserves the `/` prefix; newlines/control characters are rejected |

The repair must not add mid-string caret navigation, selection, multiline paste distribution,
history editing, or a new query/chat/sign limit. Those are separate product/security decisions. It
must restore the existing append-only owners faithfully and route input only while their explicit or
implicit active state owns the screen.

### Checkable acceptance criteria

1. **Focus and caret**
   - A primary click inside one field focuses exactly that field, uses the white outline, and places
     the caret at the nearest valid Character boundary for the clicked text position. Switching
     fields removes the old focus; clicking a non-field area removes all field focus without
     changing text.
   - The caret is drawn only for the focused field, never outside its inner bounds, remains visible
     as text exceeds the visible width, and resumes at the same Character boundary after resize.
     Horizontal scrolling/clipping keeps entered text, caret, label, neighboring controls, and panel
     bounds disjoint.
   - Left and Right move exactly one extended grapheme cluster and clamp at 0/text.count. Backspace
     removes exactly the preceding cluster, is a no-op at zero, and never traps after click,
     relayout, paste, validation, or programmatic field replacement.

2. **Printable text and event ownership**
   - For letters, digits, space, and punctuation, one physical key event inserts exactly the AppKit
     `characters` result once. The `performKeyEquivalent`/`keyDown` pair cannot duplicate or drop the
     character; allowed key repeat remains repeatable.
   - Shifted and Option-produced printable characters insert their resolved character once.
     Command- or Control-modified printable chords do not enter the buffer. Existing global/system
     shortcuts keep their current behavior, and no focused-field event reaches movement, hotbar,
     object-template, RPG, or other world commands behind the screen.
   - Edit -> Paste / Command-V inserts the same bounded, single-line character stream as typing;
     newlines and control characters are rejected. Paste cannot exceed the field limit, split a
     grapheme, bypass domain validation, or invoke world-mode Command-V placement while a field is
     focused. No unreviewed Select All, Copy, Cut, word-delete, or word-navigation behavior is
     implied by this repair.
   - The same physical-event de-duplication and world-command exclusion applies to every custom
     owner. Recipe input is accepted only while its popup is open; Sign and Chat/command input only
     while those screens are topmost. A custom owner consumes accepted characters once and must not
     require a synthetic `TextField` focus flag.

3. **Limits, placeholders, and validation**
   - Every field enforces the table limit after the whole proposed insertion, measured in Swift
     `Character` values. At the exact limit the final complete Character is accepted; the next typed
     or pasted Character is rejected with text and caret unchanged.
   - Placeholder text is never stored, submitted, measured as entered content, or exposed as the
     accessibility value. Empty World Name retains its existing `New World` default; empty Seed
     retains random-seed meaning; other empty fields retain their existing validation/defaults.
   - Domain-specific normalization or rejection occurs at the existing commit boundary and remains
     visibly understandable. Failed LAN, cloud-model, or template-name validation keeps the field
     editable and preserves the user's source text unless the existing explicit sanitizer is the
     committed result.

4. **Unicode and graphemes**
   - Direct AppKit character delivery and paste preserve composed Characters atomically. Test at
     least ASCII, an accented composed grapheme, a non-Latin grapheme, and a multi-scalar emoji;
     caret movement, Backspace, limits, and relayout must never split a grapheme or misindex.
   - Fields whose existing domain is ASCII-only still reject unsupported values at their existing
     validation boundary. Domain-permitted Unicode retains exact model/accessibility text. A glyph
     absent from Pebble's bitmap font consumes one fallback cell, not one cell per scalar, and never
     corrupts the stored value; adding normalization or expanding a domain grammar requires separate
     review.
   - Recipe suffix truncation, Sign width rejection, Chat append/history, custom Backspace/Delete,
     and command completion remain Character-safe. A multi-scalar grapheme is appended, measured,
     truncated, recalled, completed around, or removed as one unit; no custom owner may iterate its
     UTF-8 bytes as separate input events.

5. **Screen-specific completion and loss of focus**
   - Preserve only the Return behavior listed in the table. Create World, Anvil, and Creative do not
     gain implicit submission; LAN host fields do not silently become join submission; Settings and
     Template retain their current explicit Return behavior. Numpad Return is supported only where
     the existing screen already names it.
   - Escape, Back/Cancel, screen replacement, application resignation, and window close stop text
     delivery immediately and never mutate a covered screen or world behind the dismissed surface.
     Returning from temporary application inactivity shows one truthful focus state and preserves
     the uncommitted buffer; reopening a dismissed screen follows that screen's existing default or
     persisted-value behavior.
   - Resize across 360x224, 520x330, and 700x420 preserves buffer, caret, exclusive focus, field
     order, and action placement. The already approved Create World compact/default/Single Biome
     layouts remain fully contained.
   - Recipe popup open/query/highlight state, active Sign line and four line buffers, and Chat input,
     history position, completion cycle, and output scroll remain coherent across resize. Clicking
     outside a recipe popup clears only that query; Escape/close follows each custom owner's frozen
     close/commit behavior and never submits a chat command accidentally.

6. **Accessibility and real-target sign-off**
   - Every visible canvas field is exposed as one macOS text-field accessibility element with the
     nearby caption (or unambiguous placeholder) as label, exact entered text as value, enabled and
     focused state, and a valid insertion-point position. Placeholder is descriptive help, not
     value. Focus/value changes notify accessibility clients; dismissed or hidden fields are absent.
   - The open recipe query is exposed as one search field with its exact query and highlighted-result
     relationship; Sign exposes one four-line editor with the active line; Chat/command exposes one
     append-only text field with its exact value and command prefix. Their accessibility nodes exist
     only while the owning popup/screen is active and do not falsely advertise unsupported selection
     or caret movement.
   - Keyboard-only and VoiceOver focus must not activate a neighboring button or world action.
     Validation/status copy is announced without exposing hidden paths, secrets, or unrelated field
     values.
   - Final Design Sign-off uses a freshly built and installed `/Applications/Pebble.app`. On every
     `TextField` surface above, exercise click focus, middle insertion, Left/Right, Backspace,
     exact-limit and one-over input, Shift/Option characters, Command-V, Escape/loss of focus, and
     resize. Capture focused/unfocused, empty/placeholder, filled, horizontally scrolled,
     validation-failure, and High Contrast states. New World must then create a world with the exact
     entered name and seed.
   - Separately exercise both recipe popup owners with a 48-Character/one-over query, Backspace,
     Delete, Up/Down, wheel, Return, close/reopen, Paste, and resize; Sign with all four lines, exact
     width/one-over characters, Up/Down/Return/Escape, Paste, resize, close, and world reopen; and
     Chat plus `/` command mode with typing, grapheme Backspace, history, Tab completion cycling,
     Paste, wrapped four-line presentation, Return, and Escape. Verify the selected recipe, persisted
     sign text, chat output, and command effect. No sign-off is valid if based only on unit tests, the
     AppKit RPG harness, or historical frames.

**Amended Design Mock verdict: PASS.** The intended behavior now covers every actual typed-text
owner without changing copy, adding a synthetic `CommandScreen`, or inventing generic field
Tab/Return or custom mid-string editing. Build remains blocked until Architecture maps shared
`TextField` focus plus recipe/sign/chat implicit ownership and Security reviews paste routing,
Unicode indexing, shortcut precedence, the recipe cap, the currently uncapped Sign/Chat buffers,
and dismissal/commit behavior. Current installed-app proof is explicitly outstanding.

## Architecture — one text-ingress boundary, explicit owners

### Confirmed causes and retained behavior

The current New World failure is an input-boundary regression, not a world-creation defect.
`GameView` now sends both `performKeyEquivalent` and `keyDown` to `AppInputRouter`, but
`AppInputRouter.route(event:source:)` discards `source`. It may therefore attempt ordinary text
delivery during the key-equivalent preflight, whose character payload/delivery is not the canonical
canvas-editor callback, then fingerprint-consume the event before `keyDown`; or decline it without
establishing exclusive screen ownership. Canvas fields are not `NSTextView`s, so falling through to
`super.keyDown` cannot recover their text. `keyDown` must be the sole ordinary key/text delivery;
`performKeyEquivalent` remains only the menu/protected-shortcut preflight.

Three additional causes are independent of that drop:

- `AppDelegate.pasteText` requires a focused `TextField` and loops the paste itself. Recipe queries,
  Sign, and Chat/command are real `onChar` owners with no synthetic field, so Edit -> Paste can never
  reach them.
- `TextField.type` checks only the old `text.count < maxLength`; a multi-Character AppKit insertion
  can cross the limit. Clicking only toggles `focused`, never places the caret. Rendering draws the
  full buffer without a visible-range/clipping policy, so long text and the caret escape the field.
- `GameView.accessibilityChildren` currently publishes only the RPG bridge. None of the twelve
  canvas fields or the recipe, Sign, and Chat/command editors is exposed as a text accessibility
  element.

The existing router does correctly put `screenPresent` ahead of world bindings, and the twelve field
callers retain their existing commit-time sanitizers. Preserve those facts. Recipe's newest-48
suffix, Sign's strict 90-pixel per-line boundary, and uncapped append-only Chat are frozen by the
amended Design Mock; this repair may not invent a new custom-editor cap, selection model, generic Tab
order, Return behavior, or separate command screen. Security must explicitly accept or return the
uncapped local Chat/paste risk to Design; Builder may not resolve that trade-off silently.

### 1. Shared text value and geometry (`UIManagerM.swift`)

Keep `TextField` App-target-local. Add stable `id` and `accessibilityLabel` values, an integer
Character caret, and an integer `visibleStart` Character offset. Add these closed operations:

- `replaceText(_:caret:)` clamps one coherent programmatic replacement.
- `insert(_:) -> Bool` accepts one already-filtered single-line String only when
  `text.count + insertion.count <= maxLength`; rejection leaves text, caret, and viewport unchanged.
- `deleteBackward`, `moveCaret(by:)`, and `focus(atX:)` operate only on Swift `Character`
  boundaries. Click placement compares the pointer with measured prefix boundary midpoints in the
  currently visible slice.
- `ensureCaretVisible(measure:)` and `visiblePresentation(measure:)` choose the largest contiguous
  Character slice that fits the inner width (`x + 4 ... x + w - 4`) and includes the caret. The
  renderer draws only that slice and computes the caret from the same prefix measurement; it never
  mutates the stored string. Placeholder uses the same bounded presentation but is never a value.

`Screen.onMouseDown` gives focus only for a primary click, focuses at the clicked Character boundary,
clears every sibling field, and consumes the field hit before buttons/slots. A non-field primary
click clears all field focus. `UIManager.resize` preserves field state by stable `id`, not array
position, then recomputes the visible slice; duplicate/missing IDs fail closed to an unfocused field
rather than transferring text to another control.

All twelve construction sites set exact stable IDs/labels and existing limits:

- `MenusM.swift`: `create.worldName` / `World Name`, `create.seed` / `Seed`, and
  `settings.ollamaModel` / `Ollama Model`.
- `LANLobbyScreen.swift`: `lan.player` / `Player`, `lan.hostCode` / `Host Join Code`,
  `lan.hostPort` / `Host Port`, `lan.joinHost` / `Manual Host`, `lan.joinPort` / `Join Port`, and
  `lan.joinCode` / `Join Code`.
- `ScreensM.swift`: `anvil.name` / `Item Name`, `creative.search` / `Search`, and
  `template.name` / `Template Name`.

Callers that set text/caret together use `replaceText`; they do not reach into a half-updated state.
Keep the existing 64/64, 32/8/5/253/5/8, 128, 64, 64, and 48 Character limits and every existing
commit/default rule.

### 2. One Character-safe ingress contract

In `UIManagerM.swift`, add one App-local pure filter that processes the input as `Character`s. A
Character is retained only if all its scalars are single-line printable content: reject newline,
C0/C1 controls, DEL, invalid/control scalars, and AppKit function-key private-use scalars. Preserve
accepted Characters byte-for-byte; do not normalize, lowercase, transliterate, or iterate UTF-8.

Add explicit `Screen` hooks:

- `ownsTextInput(_:_:) -> Bool` (base: exactly one focused field),
- `insertText(_:_::_:) -> Bool` (base: one whole-proposal `TextField.insert`), and
- `pasteText(_:_::_:) -> Bool` (base delegates the whole filtered proposal once).

`onChar` remains a compatibility delegator to `insertText`; it is not a second ingress policy.
Backspace/Left/Right remain in `onKeyEvent` and consume repeats where currently meaningful. Tab is
consumed without insertion or traversal when a field owns input. Command/Control printable events
never call an insertion hook.

In `AppInputRouterM.swift`, use `AppKeyEventSource` instead of discarding it:

1. `performKeyEquivalent` handles only F11 and the existing protected/shipping menu path. Ordinary
   screen keys and text return to AppKit for the canonical `keyDown`; they are not fingerprinted as
   consumed.
2. `keyDown` is the sole screen-key/text callback. Route structured `onKeyEvent` first, then one
   filtered `event.characters` proposal for Shift/Option/unmodified text, then consume the otherwise
   unhandled screen event so it cannot reach AppKit or any world path. Accepted key repeat produces
   one insertion per physical repeat callback. The existing fingerprint ledger still de-duplicates
   a genuinely duplicated consumed shortcut.
3. An unmapped non-text key fails closed at an open screen. A text-producing key may not be dropped
   merely because no gameplay terminal exists: retain an adapter-only text path for valid
   `keyDown.characters` after shortcut classification, without manufacturing a bindable
   `PebbleTerminalKey` or widening Core's key grammar.

In `main.swift`, `pasteText` asks only the current top screen whether it owns text, filters the
pasteboard once, and calls that screen's `pasteText` once. `pasteOrPlaceTemplate` keeps current
precedence: world mode may place a template; any open active text owner receives Paste; an inactive
screen does neither. Do not add Select All, Copy, Cut, marked-text/IME composition, or raw
`NSPasteboard` access outside this boundary.

### 3. Custom editor adapters

- `CraftingRecipePopup` exposes `isOpen`. Inventory and Crafting override the three ownership hooks
  only while that popup is open (or delegate to a genuinely focused base field). In
  `CraftingRecipeTypeahead.append` (`PebbleCore/Systems/Crafting.swift`), filter by whole Character,
  append the proposal, and keep the newest 48 Characters exactly as today. Backspace/Delete,
  highlight, wheel, Return, selection, and close-clear behavior are unchanged.
- `SignScreen` always owns text while topmost. Typed input uses one proposed Character; paste walks
  the filtered proposal Character-by-Character and accepts the longest prefix for which the active
  line remains strictly under 90 rendered pixels. It never distributes newline content. Its four
  buffers, active line, Enter wrap/close, Escape commit, and no mid-line caret remain unchanged.
- `ChatScreen` always owns text while topmost. Type and paste append the complete filtered proposal
  at the end and reset completion once. Preserve the initial `/`, uncapped buffer, four-line visible
  tail, Character-safe Backspace, shared history, Tab completion, Return submit, and Escape cancel.
  History recall/completion replacement clamps its append-only insertion position to the end.
- `RPGCharacterScreen.onChar == false` remains explicit and publishes no text owner.

### 4. Accessibility integration

Add `Sources/Pebble/TextEntryAccessibilityM.swift`, separate from the RPG activation bridge. Define
an immutable App-local descriptor containing stable ID, role, label, exact value, placeholder/help,
frame, enabled/focused state, and insertion Character offset. Base `Screen` derives descriptors from
visible fields. Inventory/Crafting publish one search-field descriptor only while Recipe is open;
Sign publishes one four-line text editor with newline-joined value and active-line insertion offset;
Chat publishes one text field with exact input and end insertion offset.

`GameView.accessibilityChildren` merges the current text-entry bridge children with existing RPG
children; neither bridge may erase the other. Elements expose only supported macOS text attributes:
role/value/label/help/focused/enabled/frame and insertion range. They do not claim selection,
replacement, or arbitrary cursor movement. Accessibility focus invokes the same screen focus/owner
boundary; value mutation still arrives through the guarded text ingress. Refresh after open/close,
field click, accepted/rejected mutation, custom state change, resize, and application activation;
post `.focusedUIElementChanged`, `.valueChanged`, or `.layoutChanged` only for the corresponding
change. Hidden/dismissed owners are invalidated and cannot mutate a replacement screen.

Update the README accessibility/control text to describe restored field/search/sign/chat keyboard
and Paste support without promising IME, selection, or generic Tab traversal.

### Tests and evidence

- Add `TextEntrySourceTests.swift` for exact single ownership: source-aware callback split, one
  `keyDown.characters` ingress, screen exclusivity, menu-paste dispatch, all twelve stable IDs/
  limits, custom owner overrides, no raw per-scalar mutation, and accessibility bridge merging.
- Extend `InputChordTests` and `AppInputRouterSourceTests` for perform/keyDown precedence,
  fingerprints, repeat, Command/Control exclusion, unmapped printable delivery, Tab/function-key
  rejection, open-screen world exclusion, and Command-V menu precedence.
- Add focused pure `CraftingRecipeTypeahead` tests for ASCII, composed accent, non-Latin, emoji,
  controls, exact 48/newest suffix, one-over, Backspace/Delete, open/close, and deterministic
  highlight.
- Add source/model tests for TextField whole-proposal limits, Character caret/backspace, click
  midpoint selection, visible-slice/caret bounds, stable-ID resize restoration, Sign prefix paste,
  Chat/history/completion, placeholders/defaults, and every screen-specific Return rule. Source tests
  do not replace installed proof.
- Add accessibility source tests for labels, exact values, insertion offsets, focus/value/layout
  notifications, hidden invalidation, custom roles, and coexistence with the RPG bridge.
- Run affected input, controls, crafting, screen, LAN sanitizer, settings, template, RPG routing, and
  accessibility suites; then security scan, warning-free release, release verifier, full XCTest,
  457-check `pebsmoke`, and `scripts/pipeline.sh`. App/Core source changes require reviewed
  `PebbleCore.o`, Pebble, pebsmoke, and verifier pins; storage API/manifests and PebbleStorage must not
  change.

Final Design Sign-off and Deploy use the exact signed `/Applications/Pebble.app`, not a release-only
probe seam or the RPG AppKit harness. Execute the complete amended Design Mock matrix at 360x224,
520x330, and 700x420 in Standard and High Contrast. At minimum retain screenshots/logs proving:
exact New World name/seed survives focus, middle edit, horizontal scrolling, resize, and creates the
named seeded world; all ten other field contexts type/paste/validate; both recipe owners select the
queried recipe; four sign lines persist after world reopen; Chat and `/` command type/paste/history/
completion/submit correctly; VoiceOver sees truthful focused values; and no text event triggers a
world, template, hotbar, or RPG action. Record installed executable hash/signature and absence of new
crash reports. Any post-proof code change invalidates this evidence.

### Risk-to-test map

| Risk | Closing evidence |
| --- | --- |
| Preflight consumes/drops ordinary text | Source-aware router tests plus installed New World typing |
| Same event inserts twice | Fingerprint/repeat tests and physical installed typing |
| Paste reaches wrong owner or world template | TextField/Recipe/Sign/Chat Command-V matrix |
| Limit/paste splits grapheme or crosses cap | Composed/emoji exact-limit and whole-proposal tests |
| Long text escapes field/caret | Visible-slice geometry tests and three-viewport screenshots |
| Resize transfers text to wrong field | Stable-ID duplicate/missing/reorder tests |
| Custom editor behavior is accidentally normalized | Recipe suffix, Sign width, Chat/history/completion tests |
| Hidden accessibility node mutates replacement screen | Invalidation/identity/focus tests and VoiceOver proof |
| Existing RPG/world routing regresses | App router, RPG routing, controls, full suite, pebsmoke |
| Uncapped Chat paste is unacceptable | Explicit independent Security-plan verdict; no Builder policy invention |

### Dependency order and Conditions for Builder

1. Amended Design Mock — **PASS**.
2. This Architecture — **PASS**.
3. Design Review reconciles platform behavior (keyDown, menu Paste, focused canvas accessibility)
   without expanding to NSTextInputClient/IME or selection. Security (plan) must PASS the filter,
   paste ownership, uncapped Chat constraint, stale-screen invalidation, and accessibility mutation
   boundary before Build.
4. Builder implements in order: TextField value/geometry; Screen ownership/filter; source-aware
   AppInputRouter; menu Paste; Recipe/Sign/Chat adapters; accessibility bridge; callers/docs; tests.
5. Security (code), Design Sign-off, independent Test, and Deploy follow in mandatory order.

Builder must preserve all current domain sanitizers, completion actions, placeholders, strings,
field limits, custom limits/no-limit decisions, recipe/sign/chat state, screen exclusivity, RPG
receipt routing, resource-pack measurement, and unrelated dirty work. It must add no synthetic
field for custom owners, generic Tab traversal, Return submission, selection/clipboard suite, IME,
normalization, new chat/sign cap, public Core text API, release-only input hook, or accessibility
bypass around screen ownership. Stage only this logical change; update artifact hashes only from the
exact reviewed build.

**Architecture verdict: PASS.** The dropped New World input, missing custom Paste, unsafe shared
field insertion/geometry, and absent text accessibility surface have exact owners and a bounded
dependency path. Build remains blocked until Design Review and independent Security-plan PASS; a
Security rejection of uncapped Chat or a request for IME/selection returns to Design and
Architecture rather than being smuggled into this repair.

## Design Review/Revision — 2026-07-11

The Architecture realizes the amended Design Mock's complete owner inventory, preserves the exact
field and custom-editor completion semantics, keeps one screen-exclusive ingress boundary, and does
not invent selection, IME, generic Tab traversal, a separate command screen, or new Sign/Chat
limits. The source split is appropriate: AppKit routing, field geometry, and accessibility stay in
the app; only the already-public recipe typeahead's Character-safe behavior changes in Core.

The following corrections are binding revisions to the Architecture:

1. **Stable identity and relayout are one-to-one.** Before restoring any field state, validate that
   old and rebuilt ID sets are individually unique. Restore only an exact one-old/one-new match;
   unmatched new fields keep their initializer/programmatic value, unmatched old state is discarded,
   and duplicate IDs restore nothing for that ID. At most one restored field may remain focused.
   State is scoped to the same `Screen` instance and may never cross a replacement or stacked screen.
2. **Field clipping includes every drawn pixel.** Prefix measurement, click midpoint selection,
   visible slicing, and caret placement use the same active resource-pack measurement. The inner
   width must reserve the bitmap shadow pixel (or draw field text without shadow), and neither glyph,
   shadow, placeholder, nor caret may escape the field. Standard and High Contrast keep identical
   advances, slices, caret positions, and control geometry.
3. **App-local behavior needs executable tests, not source assertions alone.** Add a test-only target
   dependency that can `@testable import Pebble` and directly exercise the pure field/filter/
   presentation and accessibility-descriptor kernels. Prove that this imports and runs without
   launching the app before relying on it. If SwiftPM cannot test the executable module, return to
   Architecture for a test-only module split; do not publish a new Core text API or accept regex
   source tests as behavioral proof.
4. **Shortcut and Paste ownership are exact.** `performKeyEquivalent` must return unconsumed to
   AppKit for menu-owned Command-V, with no fingerprint entry; only world-mode eligible template
   placement consumes Command-V ahead of the menu. The menu action filters once and dispatches one
   proposal to the top owner's `pasteText`. Base fields reject an over-limit proposal atomically;
   Recipe keeps the newest 48; Sign accepts the longest width-fitting Character prefix; Chat appends
   the whole filtered proposal. Sign applies that prefix rule to any multi-Character typed proposal
   too, since `keyDown.characters` is not guaranteed to contain only one Character. Existing
   `onChar` overrides must be removed or reduced to one compatibility delegation so no second path
   can insert.
5. **The callback cause must be demonstrated, not inferred.** Preserve a regression trace/test that
   distinguishes `performKeyEquivalent` from `keyDown`, proves ordinary text is never consumed or
   fingerprinted during preflight, and proves mapped and unmapped printable keyDown events reach the
   active owner exactly once. A non-owner open screen still fails closed to world actions.
6. **Accessibility coordinates and ranges use platform units.** Cache elements by screen identity
   plus stable descriptor ID. Convert Character insertion boundaries to UTF-16 `NSRange` values;
   Sign's joined-line offset includes preceding UTF-16 line lengths and separators. Convert Pebble's
   top-left logical frames through GUI scale, view/window coordinates, backing scale, flipped Y, and
   screen origin before publishing macOS screen-space frames. Recipe's empty implicit search owner
   receives a truthful nonzero popup-region frame but does not claim a visual caret that is not
   drawn. Hidden/replaced identities invalidate before any focus or mutation callback.
7. **Installed proof must use feasible ordinary setup.** Use a disposable creative world and only
   ordinary installed UI/chat commands to obtain and place an anvil, crafting table, and sign; do not
   add a release hook. Physical/Accessibility automation may type long boundary strings only through
   the installed `GameView`. For each required viewport, record window content size, backing scale,
   GUI-scale setting, and the derived logical UI size rather than naming a window size as proof.
   Reopen the world to prove sign persistence. Capture the previously unproven Sign state and both
   Chat and slash-prefilled command modes, alongside every `TextField` and both recipe owners.

The Architecture's phrase “uncapped Sign/Chat” is interpreted precisely: Sign has no Character cap
but is bounded by the strict rendered-width rule; only Chat lacks an input-length bound. Security
must review those distinct postures.

**Design Review/Revision verdict: PASS.** With the seven corrections above incorporated as binding
architecture, the plan realizes the visual, focus, caret, clipping, adaptive, completion,
accessibility, and installed-app contracts without widening the product. Security-plan review may
proceed; Build remains blocked until Security PASS.

## Security (plan) — 2026-07-11

Reviewed the exact Design Review input at SHA-256
`851a52e172f2dbcc31e32228b5d7d03ca031fdb9e74a7c03d13dedafe813a225`. The threat model treats
`NSEvent.characters`, every explicit Paste payload, completion/history/prefill replacements, and
Accessibility callbacks as untrusted even when they originate on the local Mac. The protected
assets are bounded main-thread CPU and memory, truthful single-owner screen state, absence of hidden
world/menu actions, clipboard confidentiality, stable accessibility identity, and release-product
isolation from test seams.

### Findings

- **[HIGH] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:308-311,395-400` ->** Chat is deliberately unbounded,
  accepts the complete filtered Paste, retains submitted strings in an unbounded shared history,
  and the Conditions for Builder prohibit the only direct mitigation. One large paste can therefore
  force unbounded filtering, concatenation, wrapping, drawing, history retention, completion, and
  command parsing on the main thread. Return to Design and Architecture and bind all Chat creation
  and replacement paths (`init` prefill, typing, Paste, history, completion, Accessibility, and
  submission) to **2,048 Characters and 16,384 UTF-8 bytes**, reject an insertion/replacement
  atomically when either result cap would be exceeded, and retain at most **100** bounded history
  entries. No path may concatenate or wrap a proposal before its raw/proposed-result bounds have
  passed. Add exact-limit, one-over, repeated-history, and oversized-Paste time/memory regression
  tests. These values are a security ceiling; Design may choose stricter product limits.
- **[HIGH] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:254-263,299-310` ->** Character-count and rendered-width
  limits do not bound storage or work: a single extended grapheme can contain an attacker-controlled
  number of combining/format scalars and bytes, Recipe takes its suffix only after append, and Sign
  measures the resulting grapheme as one visible unit. Revise the shared ingress contract to reject
  a Character over **64 Unicode scalars or 256 UTF-8 bytes**, reject all private-use scalars (not only
  AppKit function keys), Unicode noncharacters/unassigned scalars, bidi embeddings/overrides/isolates
  and invisible directional marks, and newline/line/paragraph separators. Permit U+200D only inside
  an otherwise visible bounded Character so ordinary emoji sequences remain usable. Give every
  buffer a UTF-8 byte ceiling in addition to its existing Character/width ceiling (at most **4,096
  bytes** for any TextField or Sign line and **1,024 bytes** for Recipe); stream Recipe suffix and Sign
  prefix decisions without first constructing an unbounded intermediate. Test decomposed accents,
  emoji ZWJ sequences, maximum-scalar graphemes, one-over graphemes, bidi controls, arbitrary
  private-use values, and repeated combining marks.
- **[HIGH] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:291-295,435-443` ->** The plan does not bound the raw
  pasteboard representation or require ownership to be revalidated before the clipboard is read.
  Resolve the top-screen owner and eligible action first; read only the public string type, only from
  an explicit user Paste action, at most once, and reject raw data over **65,536 bytes before String
  decoding/filtering**. Do not poll, preflight, log, cache, or expose rejected clipboard data through
  Accessibility. Immediately before dispatch, revalidate that the same screen instance/generation is
  still topmost and owns text. A text-owning top screen takes precedence over template placement;
  template placement may consume Command-V only when `!ui.hasScreen()` and the world action is still
  eligible. Add a changing-owner test and prove an oversized/rejected Paste causes no world, menu,
  fingerprint, notification, or buffer mutation.
- **[MEDIUM] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:316-330,419-423,448-454` ->** Stable descriptor ID plus
  screen object identity does not fully specify ABA/stale-callback rejection, and “value mutation”
  conflicts with the promise not to claim replacement. Attach a monotonically increasing
  presentation generation to every open/rebuild/resize and cache entry; every focus callback holds a
  weak origin screen plus generation and, on the main actor immediately before acting, verifies the
  exact top screen, generation, descriptor ID, visibility, enabled state, and current ownership.
  Publish a read-only Accessibility value (`isAccessibilityValueSettable == false`) and permit no
  direct Accessibility replacement/mutation API; assistive typing and Paste use the same bounded
  keyboard/menu ingress. Validate all UTF-16 ranges against the current exact value, reject overflow,
  and publish only finite, nonnegative, screen-clamped frames after the documented coordinate
  conversion. Test close/reopen, replacement with the same stable ID, resize generations, delayed
  callbacks, non-BMP ranges, separator offsets, invalid ranges, and non-finite coordinates.
- **[MEDIUM] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:429-434` ->** A test target importing the executable can
  accidentally force production visibility, launch side effects, or test-only hooks into the release
  graph. Keep the dependency exclusively under `.testTarget`; no product or non-test target may
  depend on the harness, and no source symbol may become `public`, acquire a release environment
  trigger, or weaken access control solely for tests. The gate must prove `@testable import Pebble`
  runs without application launch/global side effects, inspect the release package graph, and scan
  the signed release executable (`nm`/`strings` plus source scan) for named test hooks and test-only
  dependencies. If direct executable import cannot meet those constraints, return to Architecture
  for an internal App-local support module linked normally by Pebble and testable without any release
  probe API.

### Review boundary and verdict

Reviewed: the Design Mock, Architecture, seven binding Design Review corrections, documented
TextField/Recipe/Sign/Chat ownership and limits, shortcut/menu/world precedence, pasteboard boundary,
resize/restoration rules, Accessibility identity/focus/range/coordinate contract, and proposed
SwiftPM executable-test exposure. Current source was consulted read-only only to validate the attack
paths; it was not approved as implementation.

Not reviewed: any Builder diff, compiled package graph or symbols, runtime Accessibility behavior,
tests or verifier quality, build/pipeline output, signing, installed-app behavior, deployment, or git
publication. Those remain downstream gates after an amended plan passes Security.

**Security (plan) verdict: FAIL.** The uncapped Chat/paste design and grapheme-only resource limits
are exploitable local denial-of-service boundaries, and the current clipboard/Accessibility/test
contracts do not yet close ownership, privacy, stale-callback, and release-exposure risks. Build is
blocked. Design and Architecture own the five exact revisions above; Design Review must reapprove
the changed product limits and behavior, after which an independent Security-plan rerun must PASS
before Builder work begins.

## Design Security Revision — bounded truthful text entry

This revision supersedes every earlier statement that Chat is uncapped, that Chat history is
unbounded, that custom editors may not gain security ceilings, or that Character/width limits alone
are sufficient. The limits below are now product behavior, not hidden implementation details.

### 1. Product-visible buffer limits

- Chat and slash-prefilled command input accept at most **2,048 Swift Characters and 16,384 UTF-8
  bytes**. The cap applies identically to initial prefill, typing, Paste, history recall, Tab
  completion, Accessibility-focused typing, and every replacement before submission. Submitted
  history retains at most **100** already-bounded entries; entry 101 evicts the oldest entry. No
  rejected value enters history or the command parser. An invalid/over-limit prefill prevents the
  screen from becoming a text owner; the two shipping prefills remain exactly empty and `/`.
- Every `TextField` retains its existing Character cap and additionally accepts at most **4,096
  UTF-8 bytes**. A proposed typed or pasted insertion that would exceed either resulting cap is
  rejected atomically: text, caret, visible slice, focus, persisted value, and neighboring controls
  remain unchanged.
- Each Sign line remains strictly below 90 rendered pixels and is additionally capped at **4,096
  UTF-8 bytes**. One typed Character is atomic. Paste accepts the longest leading sequence of whole
  valid Characters that satisfies both limits, then stops; it never skips a rejected Character and
  resumes later in the payload.
- Recipe query retains its established rolling suffix behavior, with at most **48 Characters and
  1,024 UTF-8 bytes**. Characters are streamed one at a time; after each valid Character, whole
  leading Characters are removed until both suffix limits hold. Neither the full proposal nor an
  over-limit intermediate query is constructed.

Before allocating or concatenating a result, compute proposed Character and UTF-8 byte counts
incrementally with overflow-safe arithmetic. Chat and `TextField` reject an over-limit result before
construction; Sign and Recipe consume only their bounded stream. Measurement, wrapping, completion,
history, and submission may inspect only the resulting bounded value.

At an exact limit, the final complete Character is accepted. A one-over TextField or Chat proposal
is rejected as a whole; a one-over Sign Paste retains only its valid prefix; Recipe visibly rolls its
oldest complete Character away. Rejection never truncates a grapheme, substitutes text, moves focus,
submits, closes a screen, selects a recipe, commits a sign, invokes a world/template action, or
creates an Accessibility mutation.

Chat shows the exact nonmodal amber status `Input limit reached` in the existing chat background
above the input, without changing the input wrap or caret geometry, until the next accepted edit or
dismissal. Sign shows `Line is full` in place of its instruction until the next
accepted edit or line change. Fixed-cap `TextField`s and the rolling Recipe query add no persistent
copy or layout: their unchanged text/caret is the familiar limit indication. When assistive
technology is active, each rejected user action produces at most one matching announcement
(`Input limit reached`, `Line is full`, or `Text limit reached`) without reading the rejected text.

### 2. Bounded grapheme and scalar policy

Before any buffer concatenation, measurement, wrapping, completion, or history work, each proposed
Character is independently validated. Reject the entire typed Character; for Paste, stop before the
first rejected Character. One Character may contain at most **64 Unicode scalars and 256 UTF-8
bytes**. Ordinary composed accents and emoji ZWJ sequences remain accepted within those ceilings.

Reject a Character containing newline, carriage return, next-line, Unicode line/paragraph
separators, C0/C1 or DEL controls, any private-use scalar, Unicode noncharacter or unassigned scalar,
bidi embedding/override/isolate controls, or invisible directional marks including ALM/LRM/RLM.
Reject standalone format controls. U+200D ZERO WIDTH JOINER is permitted only inside an otherwise
visible Character containing at least one accepted non-format base/symbol; it may not form an
invisible-only Character. Preserve accepted Characters byte-for-byte with no normalization,
transliteration, case folding, or scalar splitting.

The filter is shared by typing and Paste, while each owner applies its distinct result rule above.
Tests and installed proof include decomposed accents, non-Latin text, bounded emoji ZWJ, 64-scalar
and 65-scalar graphemes, 256-byte and one-over Characters, repeated combining marks, bidi controls,
private-use values, and separators.

### 3. Clipboard ownership and privacy

Paste exists only as an explicit user menu/Command-V action. Resolve the exact top screen,
presentation generation, and eligible text owner before reading the clipboard. A text-owning top
screen always wins over template placement; template placement may consume Command-V only with no
screen open and while the world action remains eligible.

Read the public string pasteboard type once and no other type. Reject its raw representation above
**65,536 bytes before String decoding or filtering**. Do not poll, preflight, cache, log, preview,
measure, announce, or expose raw/rejected clipboard contents. Immediately before dispatch, recheck
on the main actor that the same weak screen instance and generation are still topmost, visible,
enabled, and the current owner. Any ownership mismatch or raw oversize payload is a silent no-op
with no announcement or notification; it changes no buffer, fingerprint, menu, Accessibility node,
or world state. A decoded, valid-owner proposal rejected only by that owner's product buffer limit
uses the generic status/announcement contract above without disclosing content.

Installed acceptance sets a unique nonsensitive sentinel clipboard at 65,536 raw bytes and one byte
over, changes owner during a controlled Paste attempt, and proves only the exact eligible owner can
change. Evidence records hashes/counts, never clipboard content.

### 4. Read-only, generation-bound Accessibility

Every open, rebuild, and resize advances a monotonically increasing presentation generation.
Accessibility elements bind weakly to the exact origin screen plus generation and descriptor ID.
Immediately before focus, they revalidate top-screen identity, generation, visibility, enabled
state, finite screen-clamped frame, and current text ownership. Close/reopen or replacement with the
same stable ID cannot revive an old callback.

Accessibility values are read-only: `isAccessibilityValueSettable == false`, with no direct set,
replace, selection, or arbitrary cursor API. Assistive typing and Paste use only the same bounded
keyboard/menu ingress. UTF-16 insertion ranges are derived from and validated against the current
exact value; overflow, stale/non-BMP/separator-invalid ranges, and nonfinite coordinates fail closed
without notification or mutation. Announcements disclose only the generic limit/status copy.

### 5. Test-only exposure and installed acceptance

Any executable-import harness is referenced exclusively by a `.testTarget`. No product or non-test
target may depend on it; no symbol becomes public, gains a release environment trigger, performs
global/app launch side effects, or weakens access solely for tests. Verification must inspect the
package graph and signed release `nm`/`strings` plus source surface for named hooks/dependencies. If
`@testable import Pebble` cannot satisfy this, Architecture must use an internal App-local support
module linked normally by Pebble and tests, with no release probe API.

The installed matrix now adds, through ordinary `GameView` input only:

- exact and one-over Character/byte results for every `TextField`, Recipe, each Sign line, and
  Chat/command;
- 100-history retention plus deterministic oldest eviction on entry 101;
- oversized raw Paste, rejected grapheme/control cases, stale owner/generation, and same-ID reopen;
- visible Chat/Sign limit status, one Accessibility announcement per rejected action, read-only
  values, valid non-BMP UTF-16 insertion range, and zero hidden world/menu/template effects;
- bounded timing and memory evidence for oversized Paste and repeated history.

All prior viewport, High Contrast, focus/caret/clipping, completion, persisted Sign, recipe
selection, chat/command, hash/signature, and crash-report requirements remain binding.

**Design Security Revision verdict: PASS.** The revised user experience has explicit finite limits,
predictable exact/over-limit feedback, grapheme-safe behavior, private single-owner Paste, read-only
Accessibility, and test isolation. Architecture must now incorporate these changed product
contracts, Design Review must reapprove that revision, and independent Security-plan must PASS
before Build.

## Architecture Security Revision — bounded streaming and generation ownership

This section supersedes Architecture statements that preserve uncapped Chat/history, prohibit new
Sign/Chat byte ceilings, permit a decoded clipboard read before owner capture, allow Accessibility
value mutation, or rely on source assertions/direct executable import for behavior tests.

### Empirical test-target decision

An isolated SwiftPM 6.0 probe was run under the current toolchain. A `.testTarget` depending on a
simple `@main` executable successfully used `@testable import` without calling `main` (1 XCTest,
0 failures). Repeating the probe with top-level `main.swift` code, matching Pebble's bootstrap
shape, produced XCTest signal 11; its side-effect marker remained absent. Direct
`@testable import Pebble` is therefore rejected for this repository.

Add a normal, non-product `PebbleTextInput` target at `Sources/PebbleTextInput`, with no dependency
on PebbleCore, AppKit, or storage. `PebbleCore` and `Pebble` depend on it normally;
`PebbleTextInputTests` depends only on it, and existing `PebbleCoreTests` may depend on it for recipe
integration. Cross-module declarations use Swift `package` access, never `public` solely for tests.
The release links the same pure kernels tested in debug; there is no environment trigger, probe API,
test callback, or alternate implementation.

### Closed pure API

`PebbleTextInput` owns these package-scoped values:

```swift
package struct PebbleTextLimit: Equatable, Sendable {
    let maximumCharacters: Int
    let maximumUTF8Bytes: Int
}
package struct PebbleValidatedCharacter: Equatable, Sendable {
    let value: Character
    let scalarCount: Int
    let utf8ByteCount: Int
}
package enum PebbleTextValidationResult: Equatable, Sendable {
    case accepted(PebbleValidatedCharacter)
    case rejected
}
package func pebbleValidateTextCharacter(_ value: Character) -> PebbleTextValidationResult
package struct PebbleBoundedTextBuffer: Equatable, Sendable {
    private(set) var text: String
    private(set) var characterCount: Int
    private(set) var utf8ByteCount: Int
    mutating func replaceAtomically(_ proposal: String, limit: PebbleTextLimit) -> Bool
    mutating func insertAtomically(_ proposal: String, atCharacterOffset: Int,
                                   limit: PebbleTextLimit) -> Bool
    mutating func deleteBackward(atCharacterOffset: inout Int) -> Bool
}
package struct PebbleTextPresentationClock: Equatable, Sendable {
    mutating func next() -> UInt64?
}
package struct PebbleTextOwnerToken: Equatable, Sendable {
    let screenIdentity: UInt64
    let presentationGeneration: UInt64
    let descriptorID: String
}
```

The validator walks `Character.unicodeScalars` once and stops at scalar 65 or UTF-8 byte 257; it
allocates no scalar/byte array. It rejects C0/C1/DEL, CR/LF/NEL/U+2028/U+2029, every private-use,
noncharacter, unassigned scalar, U+061C/U+200E/U+200F, U+202A...U+202E,
U+2066...U+2069, and every standalone format control. U+200D is accepted only when the same
bounded Character also contains at least one accepted non-format base/symbol. Accepted Characters
are returned unchanged; no normalization or scalar reconstruction occurs.

`replaceAtomically` and `insertAtomically` validate/count the proposal incrementally with
overflow-reporting arithmetic into a temporary bounded builder no larger than the destination's
remaining caps. They do not concatenate with the stored value until every Character and proposed
result count passes. Cached counts are checked after every mutation in DEBUG tests but are not
caller-settable. Limits are: each TextField `(existing Character limit, 4_096 bytes)`, Chat
`(2_048, 16_384)`, Recipe `(48, 1_024)`, and each Sign line `(rendered width < 90, 4_096 bytes)`.

### Owner-specific bounded flow

- `TextField` wraps `PebbleBoundedTextBuffer`; typing and Paste use atomic insert, so a one-over
  proposal changes nothing. Programmatic `replaceText` uses the same limit before changing caret,
  focus, viewport, persistence, or Accessibility.
- `ChatScreen` validates empty or `/` prefill before becoming an owner. Type, Paste, history recall,
  Tab completion, and every replacement call the same atomic buffer API. Completion may calculate
  only from already-bounded input and its candidate is bounded before assignment. Return validates
  the current bounded value before command parsing/submission, then appends it to a maximum-100
  history ring and evicts exactly the oldest on entry 101. Rejection sets only
  `Input limit reached`; the next accepted edit/dismissal clears it.
- `CraftingRecipeTypeahead.append` receives the shared validated Character stream. Before appending
  each Character, it removes whole leading Characters from the current bounded query until adding
  that Character fits both 48/1,024; only then appends. Thus the query is never transiently over cap
  and no full accepted Paste is materialized. Invalid Paste stops at its first rejected Character.
- `SignScreen` validates one Character at a time. It checks byte addition first; only then forms the
  at-most-4,096-byte candidate needed for `textWidth`. Type/Paste stops at the first invalid,
  byte-over, or width-over Character and keeps only the longest accepted prefix. Rejection sets
  `Line is full`; accepted edit/line change/dismissal clears it.
- The menu/key ingress walks decoded input by Character and dispatches to the owner incrementally or
  through its bounded atomic builder. It never constructs an unbounded filtered String. A typed
  multi-Character proposal is atomic for TextField/Chat and streaming-prefix for Sign/Recipe, as
  required by the revised Design contract.

### Paste privacy and generation

`UIManager` assigns each `Screen` a checked monotonic `screenIdentity` on open and advances one
global `PebbleTextPresentationClock` on open, every `initScreen` rebuild, and resize. All text-screen
rebuilds go through one `UIManager.rebuild(_:game:)` API; source tests reject direct text-owner
`initScreen` calls elsewhere. Overflow latches async Paste/Accessibility unavailable and invalidates
their nodes; synchronous current-screen keyboard handling remains screen-exclusive.

`UIManager.captureTextOwner()` returns a main-actor capture holding a weak screen plus
`PebbleTextOwnerToken`; it succeeds only for the visible, enabled top owner. Explicit Paste follows
this exact order:

1. Capture/revalidate the text owner before touching `NSPasteboard`. If present, it takes precedence.
2. Read `NSPasteboard.PasteboardType.string` via `data(forType:)` exactly once; read no other type.
3. Reject `data.count > 65_536` before `String(data:encoding:.utf8)`. Do not log/cache/announce it.
4. Decode UTF-8 once, then immediately revalidate the same weak screen, identity, generation,
   descriptor, visibility, enabled state, top position, and ownership on the main actor.
5. Dispatch to that owner. Owner-cap rejection may publish one generic limit status/announcement;
   raw-size, decode, validation, and stale-owner rejection are silent no-ops.

If no text owner was captured, template placement is considered only when `!ui.hasScreen()`, a
world exists, and the existing placement action remains eligible. Menu-owned Command-V remains
unconsumed/unfingerprinted in `performKeyEquivalent`; `keyDown` remains the sole ordinary text
callback. Tests inject a production protocol-shaped pasteboard reader into the coordinator (not a
global hook) to replace the top screen during its one read and prove the second validation closes
the race.

### Read-only Accessibility ABA contract

`TextEntryAccessibilityM.swift` caches by `(screenIdentity, presentationGeneration, descriptorID)`.
Each element holds only a weak screen and immutable token. Immediately before focus it verifies the
exact top screen/token, current generation, descriptor presence, visibility, enabled state, current
ownership, and a finite screen-clamped frame. Close/reopen, same-ID replacement, rebuild, or resize
invalidates the old token before callbacks can act.

Values are read-only: `isAccessibilityValueSettable == false`; implement no value setter,
replacement, selection, or arbitrary caret action. Keyboard and menu Paste remain the only mutation
paths. Convert a Character insertion offset to UTF-16 by taking the current bounded prefix and using
its `utf16.count` with overflow checks. Sign offsets add each preceding line's UTF-16 count plus one
newline separator. Reject ranges not exactly valid for the current value. Frame conversion uses the
same audited RPG stages (logical top-left -> GUI scale -> view/window -> backing scale/flipped Y ->
screen origin), then rejects nonfinite, negative, overflowed, or off-screen results. Limit
announcements disclose only the three approved generic strings and occur at most once per rejected
user action.

### Tests, graph proof, and order

`PebbleTextInputTests` directly imports `@testable PebbleTextInput` and covers: exact/one-over
character and byte caps; 64/65 scalars; 256/257 bytes; decomposed accents; non-Latin; bounded emoji
ZWJ; standalone ZWJ; repeated marks; every bidi/private-use/noncharacter/unassigned/separator class;
overflow arithmetic; atomic insert/replace; Recipe rolling suffix; Sign streaming prefix helper;
Chat replacement/history ring; generation exhaustion; UTF-16 non-BMP/separator offsets; and finite
frame math. Seeded property tests stream random valid/invalid Characters and compare against a
bounded independent oracle, printing seed/minimized input.

App integration/source tests prove the exact callback trace, mapped/unmapped printable keyDown,
preflight non-consumption, repeat/de-duplication, all owner policies, one pasteboard read, predecode
65,536/65,537 boundary, owner swap/generation ABA, zero rejected side effects, read-only AX, and no
second `onChar` insertion path. Installed proof adds 2,048/16,384 exact/one-over Chat, 100/101
history, all TextField/Recipe/Sign byte boundaries, statuses/announcements, raw Paste boundary, and
same-ID close/reopen through ordinary `GameView` only.

Before Build, update `Package.swift` in this dependency order:
`PebbleTextInput -> PebbleCore -> Pebble`, with test targets depending downward only. After Build:

- `swift package describe --type json` must show no product for `PebbleTextInput`, no non-test
  dependency on a test target, and no test dependency on executable `Pebble`;
- source scan rejects `XCTest`, `@testable`, test fixture names, or test environment switches under
  `Sources/`;
- `otool -L`, `nm`, and `strings` over the fresh release and signed installed executable reject
  XCTest/Testing linkage and exact new test/probe names (`PebbleTextInputTests`,
  `probeLaunchMarker`, `TextInputTestHook`, `InjectedPasteboard`);
- release verifier freshness/hashes cover the new normal object dependency plus changed Core,
  Pebble, and pebsmoke; PebbleStorage/API manifests remain exact;
- focused suites precede security scan, warning-free release, full XCTest, 457-check `pebsmoke`,
  pipeline, install, installed matrix, and final pipeline renewal after any change.

Implementation order is: pure target/limits/validator -> direct pure tests -> TextField/Recipe/Sign/
Chat buffers -> generation-bound Screen/UIManager ownership -> source-aware router -> paste
coordinator -> read-only AX bridge -> caller statuses/docs -> integration/property tests -> Security
code review -> Design Sign-off -> independent Test -> Deploy.

### Amended Conditions for Builder

The Design Security Revision's finite caps and feedback supersede every older contradictory
condition. Builder must use the shared validator before concatenation/measurement/wrapping/
completion/history/submission; preserve accepted bytes; stop Paste at the first invalid Character;
perform predecode clipboard and postread generation checks; keep Accessibility read-only; and use
the normal `PebbleTextInput` module with package access. It may not import the executable in tests,
expose a public/Core text API, add a release probe, poll/log/cache clipboard data, add selection/IME,
or bypass existing domain validation, screen exclusivity, RPG routing, or installed proof.

**Architecture Security Revision verdict: PASS.** All five Security-plan findings now have exact
bounded algorithms, ownership generations, read-only Accessibility, a safe empirically selected
test topology, artifact scans, and ordered closing evidence. Build remains blocked until renewed
Design Review and independent Security-plan PASS of this revision.

## Renewed Design Review — Security Revisions (2026-07-11)

Reviewed the complete Design and Architecture Security Revisions through SHA-256
`f77a44d6da6dff0f4c8b38b0eeb39cf1c180a0b0805b8d4bcc4b61f2f994225d`. The finite limits,
single-owner model, bounded streaming, read-only Accessibility, and installed-app requirement
preserve Pebble's existing interaction language. The following clarifications are binding so the
security behavior remains truthful and usable rather than merely bounded:

1. **Atomic and prefix behavior are owner- and cause-specific.** TextField and Chat typing,
   completion, history, prefill, replacement, and submission are whole-proposal atomic. For Paste,
   first stream the longest valid Character prefix up to the first invalid Character, then apply
   that prefix atomically to TextField or Chat; if its resulting cap fails, insert none of it. Sign
   retains the streamed valid prefix until the first invalid, byte-over, or width-over Character.
   Recipe retains its rolling suffix and drops whole oldest Characters. No owner may skip an invalid
   Character and resume later, split a grapheme, or construct an unbounded filtered proposal.
2. **Feedback names only the condition that occurred.** Chat shows `Input limit reached` only for a
   2,048-Character/16,384-byte result rejection. Sign shows `Line is full` only for its byte or
   rendered-width stop. TextField has no persistent visual status and, when assistive technology is
   active, may announce `Text limit reached` once for a cap rejection. Recipe rolls without a limit
   error. Invalid Unicode, failed decode, raw oversize, missing/stale owner, or generation mismatch
   is a silent no-op with no status, announcement, beep, notification, or world/menu mutation.
   Accepted valid Paste prefixes remain accepted silently. This distinction supersedes the
   Architecture's broader phrases “rejection sets” and “validation ... silent.”
3. **Limit status must not damage the editor it explains.** Chat's one-line status stays inside the
   existing chat backdrop immediately above the input and never changes or overlaps its four-line
   input wrap, caret, or command prefix. Sign replaces only the existing instruction row. Both use
   the approved exact copy, remain clipped within the safe panel at every required logical size,
   and preserve state across resize. Color is supplemental: Standard may use amber, while High
   Contrast uses its existing high-contrast foreground/background treatment and the words carry the
   meaning. The next accepted edit clears Chat; the next accepted edit or line change clears Sign;
   dismissal clears either.
4. **Accepted Unicode remains internationally usable.** Reject every Unicode format scalar except
   U+200D in the approved visible, bounded ZWJ Character; this includes ZWNJ and hidden directional
   formatting. Continue to accept bounded composed/decomposed accents, combining marks, variation
   selectors, emoji modifiers, non-Latin scripts, and visible emoji/ZWJ sequences. Preserve their
   exact bytes. Direct tests must prove these accepted classes as well as every rejected class so a
   security filter cannot silently collapse the usable language set.
5. **Read-only Accessibility still communicates editability.** Each active owner retains the
   approved text/search-field role, exact value, truthful focused state, insertion range, label, and
   concise help describing its keyboard editing model; it must not be announced as supporting
   selection or direct value replacement. Accessibility focus may establish the normal screen/field
   focus but never mutate text. A resize/rebuild invalidates the old element while preserving the
   model's logical field, active Sign line, and focus on the freshly generated descriptor; layout
   notification must not create duplicate limit announcements or move focus to a neighbor.
6. **Clipboard rejection is visibly distinguishable only when safe.** A valid-owner, decoded Paste
   whose bounded proposal exceeds Chat/Sign/TextField product limits follows correction 2. The
   65,537-byte raw case, decode failure, invalid first Character, and owner/generation race remain
   completely silent. The 65,536-byte case may produce only the destination's generic cap feedback,
   never payload content or byte-count copy. A text owner continues to beat template placement;
   any open non-owner screen blocks both text Paste and template placement.
7. **Installed evidence must test reachable UX, not manufacture an impossible Sign state.** Exercise
   exact/one-over Character and byte limits wherever each is independently reachable, including
   Chat 2,048/16,384, history 100/101, Recipe rolling, TextFields, and raw Paste 65,536/65,537. On
   Sign, the strict width cap will normally bind before 4,096 bytes; installed proof must use a
   byte-heavy accepted stream and show the first real width-or-byte boundary, prefix retention,
   `Line is full`, and persistence. Pure/property tests independently prove the 4,096-byte guard and
   that no measurement candidate exceeds it. At 360x224, 520x330, and 700x420 logical sizes in
   Standard and High Contrast, retain the full earlier owner matrix plus status placement,
   VoiceOver help/read-only values/focus across resize, exactly one eligible announcement, and zero
   clipboard-to-world leakage. Evidence records hashes and counts, never clipboard contents.

No infeasible new interaction is introduced: selection, IME, generic Tab traversal, multiline
Paste, and direct Accessibility replacement remain out of scope. The normal `PebbleTextInput`
module is an implementation/test topology and adds no product-visible surface.

**Renewed Design Review verdict: PASS.** With the seven corrections above binding, the revised
Architecture realizes finite, predictable progression from accepted input to truthful feedback
without corrupting text, obscuring controls, weakening international input, or leaking Paste into
the world. Build remains blocked until the independent Security-plan rerun passes this exact
revision.

## Security (plan) rerun — 2026-07-11

Reviewed the complete revised plan at SHA-256
`af2f8d24dba9462f1a58ca0de6c5df8fc34facd8fb7b8bd16af7afc37bc5eed5` against all five prior
findings. Chat/history caps and atomicity are closed; Unicode scalar/byte validation and streaming
are closed for new ingress but not loaded Sign state; clipboard capture/predecode/postread ownership
is closed; read-only Accessibility mutation and ABA tokens are closed but focus authorization is
contradictory; and the non-product normal module plus package/binary scans closes executable-test
exposure. Product-limit feedback is bounded, generic, cause-specific, and does not disclose payloads.

### Findings

- **[HIGH] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:578-590,761-779,932-938` ->** The Sign cap is specified only
  for newly typed/pasted Characters. Existing `BlockEntityData.lines` loaded from a save, template,
  or LAN state is a programmatic replacement that can contain fewer or more than four lines or an
  arbitrarily large/invalid line; the plan never routes it through the bounded model before indexing,
  measurement, drawing, Accessibility publication, or commit. Require Sign initialization to produce
  exactly four editor buffers before the screen becomes visible or owns input: take at most four
  source lines, pad missing lines with empty strings, and stream each line only to the first invalid
  Character, 4,096-byte boundary, or strict width boundary using the same pack measurement, never
  constructing/measuring an over-cap candidate. Keep repaired data local until ordinary close commits
  it. Design must approve one exact non-content-bearing `Sign text repaired` instruction-row status
  and matching single Accessibility announcement when cardinality/content repair occurred; invalid
  content itself is never disclosed. Add adversarial save/template/LAN tests for nil and 0/1/3/4/5
  lines, huge lines, invalid Unicode, width/byte boundaries, safe open/draw/AX/close, and bounded
  persisted output.
- **[MEDIUM] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:814-818,919-925` ->** Accessibility focus both must verify
  “current ownership” before acting and may establish normal focus on an unfocused field. Base
  ownership requires one already-focused field, so those rules cannot both be implemented and invite
  either unusable focus or a bypass around the owner check. Before focus, validate the exact weak
  top-screen/token/generation, unique current descriptor, visibility, enabled state, finite frame, and
  that the descriptor names a focusable field on that screen; do **not** require prior ownership.
  Invoke one screen-owned `focusTextDescriptor(id:)` operation that clears sibling focus, then
  immediately verify that the same token remains current and the target is the sole owner before
  notifying. Custom implicit owners only focus the `GameView`; failure/staleness is a silent no-op.
  Keep values read-only. Test an initially unfocused field, sibling clearing, duplicate IDs,
  close/reopen and resize during callback, custom owners, and zero neighboring button/world mutation.

### Review boundary and verdict

Reviewed: revised limits/streaming algorithms, all owner creation/replacement paths named by the
plan, Unicode policy, Paste privacy and action precedence, generation overflow/ABA handling,
read-only Accessibility, package topology and release scans, generic feedback, and test/deploy order.

Not reviewed: Builder code, runtime behavior, compiled graph/symbols, tests, build/pipeline output,
installed application, signing, deployment, or publication. Current source was read only to verify
the persisted Sign boundary; no implementation file was edited or approved.

**Security (plan) rerun verdict: FAIL.** Three prior findings are fully closed, but persisted Sign
state can still bypass every new resource limit and the Accessibility focus precondition remains
internally contradictory. Build stays blocked. Design and Architecture must incorporate the two
exact fixes above, renew Design Review, and rerun Security (plan) before Builder work.

## Design Security Revision 2 — repaired Signs and authorized focus

This revision closes the two product-level gaps from the Security rerun. It changes neither the
four-line Sign editing model nor read-only Accessibility values.

### Truthful bounded Sign opening and commit

Before `SignScreen` is visible, measured, drawn, exposed to Accessibility, or allowed to own input,
it converts save, placed-template, or LAN Sign state into exactly four local editor lines. It takes
only the first four source entries, pads missing entries with empty lines, and streams each entry by
whole validated Character. A line stops before its first invalid Character, a Character that would
exceed 4,096 bytes, or a candidate whose active-resource-pack width would not remain strictly below
90 pixels; no rejected Character or over-cap candidate is concatenated, rendered, announced, or
published as an AX value. Width is measured only for an already byte-bounded candidate. Accepted
prefixes retain their exact bytes.

Nil state, any line count other than four, or any truncated line is a repair. A repaired screen
shows the exact non-content-bearing instruction-row status `Sign text repaired`. When assistive
technology is active, it posts the same phrase once after the repaired screen's current-generation
element is available, never once per repaired line and never again on resize/rebuild. The message
contains no source text, count, reason, peer identity, or discarded content. It remains until the
first accepted edit or line change; a later genuine width/byte stop replaces it with `Line is full`.
Dismissal clears presentation status.

Repair is a local editing snapshot, not an eager migration. Merely opening, drawing, focusing,
resizing, or reading the Sign through Accessibility changes no block entity, template source,
save, LAN packet, fingerprint, or peer state. Ordinary close uses the existing authoritative commit
path to write exactly the four bounded local lines. If the block/session/authority is no longer the
same at commit, the stale edit writes and broadcasts nothing. A successful commit is what makes the
repair durable: world reopen and reconnect then show the same four safe lines without another
repair status. A template-derived Sign repairs only the placed Sign; it never rewrites the source
template. On LAN, host authority remains unchanged: a guest may view/edit the safe local snapshot,
but no peer observes it until the existing authorized close commit succeeds, after which host and
guests converge on the bounded four-line value.

Acceptance covers nil and 0/1/3/4/5-line save, template, and LAN inputs; huge and invalid lines;
byte/width boundaries; safe draw and exact repaired AX value; one status/announcement; no mutation
before close; authorized close, rejected stale/disconnected close, world reopen, and host/guest
convergence. Fixtures use nonsensitive sentinel text, and evidence never records discarded content.

### Authorized Accessibility focus

An Accessibility focus request does not require the target field to own text already. Before any
focus change, validate the exact weak top-screen instance and immutable token/generation, one unique
current descriptor with the requested ID, visibility, enabled state, a finite screen-clamped frame,
and that the descriptor names a focusable field on that screen. Only then may one screen-owned
`focusTextDescriptor(id:)` operation clear every sibling field and focus the target. Immediately
afterward, revalidate the same token and require that target to be the sole text owner before text
ingress is enabled or a notification is posted.

If prevalidation, focusing, or postvalidation fails, the request is silent: no Accessibility
notification, button activation, world action, text mutation, or ownership transfer survives. If
the original screen is still current after a postcheck failure, its field focus is cleared; if it
was replaced, the stale instance is simply ignored. Recipe, Sign, and Chat are implicit custom
owners, so their Accessibility focus action only makes the already-validated `GameView` first
responder and preserves query, active Sign line, Chat state, and every neighboring control.

After a successful postcheck, publish exactly one `.focusedUIElementChanged`; refreshed descriptors
report the target focused and all siblings unfocused. Resize/rebuild may publish the already-approved
layout notification for the new generation but must preserve logical focus and must not duplicate
the focus or repair/limit announcement. Values remain read-only and all subsequent typing/Paste
still crosses the bounded owner ingress. Tests and installed VoiceOver proof cover an initially
