unfocused field, switching from a focused sibling, duplicate IDs, close/reopen and resize during the
callback, all three implicit owners, notification count/order, immediate typing after focus, and
zero neighboring button/world mutation.

**Design Security Revision 2 verdict: PASS.** Malformed external Sign state now has a bounded,
content-private, user-visible repair lifecycle through authoritative commit and reopen/LAN
convergence, while authorized Accessibility focus can establish exactly one usable owner without
weakening generation checks or read-only values. Architecture must incorporate these contracts,
renewed Design Review must approve them, and Security (plan) must PASS before Build.

## Architecture Security Revision 2 — bounded Sign repair and two-phase AX focus

This revision supersedes the earlier Architecture wherever it described Sign lines as an ordinary
four-element array, permitted a persisted line to reach presentation before validation, required an
Accessibility target to own text before it could be focused, or referred to an already-existing LAN
Sign close protocol. There is no such LAN Sign-edit protocol in current source; Builder must add the
typed host-authoritative path below rather than route Sign text through inventory/container edits.

### Four-line Sign repair: API, ownership, and data flow

Add these package-visible value types and pure functions to the normal `PebbleTextInput` target:

- `PebbleSignLine` owns one bounded `String` and exposes Character iteration, UTF-8 byte count, and
  replacement only through the approved streaming validator.
- `PebbleFourSignLines` stores four named slots (`first` through `fourth`), exposes access by a
  `SignLineIndex: CaseIterable` enum, and emits an exact four-element array only for serialization.
  It has no integer subscript and cannot represent nil, fewer, or extra lines.
- `PebbleSignRepairResult { lines: PebbleFourSignLines, repaired: Bool }` is the only result accepted
  by `SignScreen` initialization.
- `pebbleRepairSignLines(_:widthStep:)` accepts optional `[String]`, obtains an iterator without
  reading `count`, consumes no more than five entries (four inputs plus one excess probe), and pads
  absent entries. For each of the first four entries it iterates whole Characters, applies the
  approved scalar policy, checks overflow-safe prospective UTF-8 bytes `<= 4096`, then asks the
  production streaming glyph-width cursor whether the prospective total remains `< 90`. Only an
  accepted Character is appended. The width cursor consumes one Character and formatting state; it
  never receives or constructs a candidate String. Thus invalid, byte-over, and width-over values
  are neither concatenated nor measured as a String. Nil, missing/excess entries, or any stopped
  line sets `repaired`; accepted bytes are unchanged.

The App layer supplies `UICanvas.makeTextWidthCursor()` from the active resource pack. Its streaming
advance rules must be the same implementation used by `textWidth`/`drawText`, including formatting
state; a parallel approximation is forbidden. `SignScreen` captures the raw optional source but,
in its first `initScreen`, performs repair before registration, layout, draw, AX descriptor creation,
or input ownership. It immediately discards the raw reference and thereafter stores only
`PebbleFourSignLines`. Drawing and editing iterate `SignLineIndex.allCases`; no `lines[i]`, unchecked
cardinality assertion, force unwrap, or pre-repair measurement/publication remains.

The repair sources and ownership rules are exact:

- Save load supplies the current world `BlockEntityData.lines`.
- A placed template supplies the placed world block entity; the source `ObjectTemplate` is never an
  owner or repair destination.
- LAN supplies the current mirrored Sign snapshot and its host revision. A guest owns only a local
  editor snapshot until an accepted close.
- A new local Sign with nil data repairs to four empty lines. Opening, drawing, focus, resize, AX,
  and cancel/dismiss do not write any source.

`SignScreen` also captures an immutable `SignCommitToken`: world/session generation, dimension and
position, expected Sign block identity, weak expected block-entity identity (or explicit absence for
a new entity), LAN connection/authority generation when applicable, and the host Sign revision.
World replacement/load, dimension change, target block replacement, block-entity replacement,
disconnect/reconnect, authority change, or generation overflow invalidates it fail-closed.

Close calls one `GameCore.commitSignEdit(token:lines:) -> Bool`. On a local authoritative world it
revalidates every token field on the serial world/save path, creates a Sign block entity only for a
still-absent expected entity, assigns the exact four bounded lines atomically, marks the chunk dirty,
and lets normal save persistence serialize that single placed entity. Failure changes nothing. On a
LAN guest it changes no mirrored entity and sends a new `LANSignEditIntent` containing dimension,
position, expected host revision, and the exact four bounded lines. Append `signEditIntent` at the
next unused `LANMessageKind` value (currently 27); never renumber an existing kind. The host applies
the existing message/frame cap and rate limit, checks authenticated player, build permission, reach,
dimension, Sign block/entity, revision, exact four-line scalar/byte bounds, and reruns the same strict
streaming width validator with the host's active pack. It accepts only when revalidation returns the
submitted value unchanged, mutates atomically, advances the Sign revision, and broadcasts the
ordinary authoritative block-entity snapshot including optional `lines` and revision. Any failed
check commits and broadcasts nothing; the next authoritative snapshot restores the guest mirror.
Container inventory intent, conservation checks, and container permission are never reused.

Repair presentation is screen-local state. If `repaired`, the existing Sign instruction row reads
exactly `Sign text repaired`; with assistive technology active, that phrase is announced exactly once
after a current-generation Sign descriptor exists. Resize/rebuild does not repeat it. The first
accepted edit or line change clears it; a later real capacity stop replaces it with `Line is full`;
dismissal clears it. No source text, cardinality, rejection reason, position, or peer data enters
status, logs, announcements, snapshots, or test artifacts.

### Accessibility focus: authorize, focus, postvalidate

`UIManager` owns immutable `TextDescriptorToken(screenID,generation,descriptorID)` values and exposes
three synchronous main-thread phases with no suspension point:

1. `authorizeTextDescriptorFocus(token:) -> TextFocusAuthorization?` weakly resolves the exact
   current top-screen instance and generation; finds exactly one current descriptor ID; and verifies
   visible, enabled, finite screen-clamped frame, plus a focusable field mapping on that screen. It
   deliberately does not require an existing text owner. Authorization is an ephemeral capability
   containing weak screen identity and the captured generation; it is neither cached nor accepted by
   text/Paste ingress.
2. `screen.focusTextDescriptor(id:) -> Bool` consumes that capability on the same screen. A normal
   field screen resolves exactly one field, clears every sibling, and focuses only the target. An
   implicit Recipe/Sign/Chat owner may only make the already-authorized `GameView` first responder;
   it must preserve its query/active line/chat model and cannot activate a button or world action.
3. `completeTextDescriptorFocus(_:) -> Bool` immediately rechecks the same top-screen identity,
   generation, unique descriptor, and that the target is now the sole normal text owner (or the same
   implicit owner with `GameView` first responder). Only success enables subsequent owner ingress and
   posts one `.focusedUIElementChanged`. On failure, it posts nothing; if the captured screen and
   generation are still current it clears that screen's field focus, otherwise it never touches the
   replacement screen.

Every descriptor rebuild increments the screen generation with checked overflow; overflow retires
the screen and all tokens. Resize preserves a logical focused-field ID in model state, rebuilds the
descriptor under a new generation, and may post only the approved layout notification—not another
focus, repair, or limit announcement. Close/reopen, screen replacement, duplicate IDs, non-finite
frames, and callbacks that synchronously rebuild descriptors therefore fail closed. AX values remain
read-only; AX focus never edits text.

### Revised dependency order and verification map

1. Implement/test the pure scalar validator, `PebbleFourSignLines`, iterator-bounded repair, and
   streaming production width cursor equivalence. No UI or network code proceeds while these fail.
2. Replace `SignScreen` raw-array state and add local tokenized atomic commit; prove no source
   mutation before successful close and persistence after reopen.
3. Add optional bounded Sign lines/revision to LAN snapshots and append typed message kind 27; then
   implement host authorization, rejection, revision advance, and convergence. Complete protocol
   codec fuzz/property tests before enabling the send path.
4. Add AX authorization/focus/postvalidation and generation retirement, then wire notifications.
5. Rerun Security (code), inspect the installed Sign/AX surfaces, run focused and full gates, and
   deploy only after all renewed reviews pass.

Focused tests must cover save/template/LAN nil and 0/1/3/4/5 lines; huge, invalid, byte-edge, and
width-edge lines; an iterator and width spy proving at most five source pulls and no rejected
Character/String measurement; exact four safe draw/AX values; one repair status/announcement; no
pre-close or stale-close mutation; local reopen; placed-template/source separation; LAN frame caps,
codec fuzzing, permission/reach/revision/rate/pack-width rejection, disconnect races, accepted host
broadcast, and two-peer convergence. AX tests cover initially unfocused focus, sibling clearing,
duplicate IDs, disabled/hidden/non-finite descriptors, close/reopen and resize during the callback,
generation overflow, implicit owners, notification order/count, immediate bounded typing, and zero
button/world mutation. Source scans reject integer Sign-line indexing, raw `BlockEntityData.lines`
publication, Sign use of container intents, cached focus authorizations, and notification before
postvalidation. The full warning-free release build, XCTest, 457-check `pebsmoke`, security scan,
pipeline, installed-app Sign/VoiceOver proof, and installed LAN two-machine proof remain mandatory.

**Architecture Security Revision 2 verdict: PASS.** The two Security findings now have exact bounded
representations, ownership and commit boundaries, generation semantics, dependency order, and
adversarial evidence requirements. Build remains blocked until renewed Design Review and Security
(plan) independently PASS this revision.

## Renewed Design Review 2 — repaired Sign lifecycle and AX focus

Reviewed Architecture Security Revision 2 through SHA-256
`00c63b2d7368806185d2c25dc36285de9192ca76efa28ce88eac809a92b9357a`. Its four-line value,
streaming repair, content-private `Sign text repaired` presentation, template isolation,
host-authoritative LAN validation, and authorize/focus/postvalidate Accessibility sequence realize
Design Security Revision 2. These corrections are binding:

1. **Name every close state truthfully.** Escape and Return wrapping from line four are the existing
   ordinary commit gestures; they are not the Architecture's undefined “cancel/dismiss.” Opening,
   focus, resize, screen replacement, target invalidation, and application/window teardown never
   commit. A successful local/host commit closes normally. A synchronous failed commit leaves the
   bounded editor open with exact instruction-row copy `Sign update failed`; an accepted edit clears
   that copy and permits retry, while a second unchanged Escape explicitly discards and closes.
2. **LAN close needs an acknowledged pending state.** A guest ordinary-close gesture sends one
   request and keeps the Sign screen visible but temporarily non-editable with exact copy
   `Saving sign…`; it must not imply persistence merely because an intent was sent. A typed,
   request-correlated host result is required: acceptance closes only after the matching
   authoritative revision is applied; rejection, timeout, or disconnect returns to a bounded
   editable snapshot with `Sign update failed` and no peer mutation. Repeated Return/Escape while
   pending sends nothing. No status includes text, position, player, permission, or rejection reason.
   Architecture must add this result path and test late/duplicate/out-of-order replies and
   disconnect-after-accept ambiguity before Build.
3. **Repair feedback has stable precedence.** `Sign text repaired` survives focus, resize, and AX
   reads; it clears only on the approved accepted edit/line-change/dismissal. `Line is full` may
   replace it only after a genuine capacity stop. `Saving sign…` and `Sign update failed` supersede
   either only during their commit states. All four messages occupy the same instruction row,
   remain legible in Standard and High Contrast, and generate only their approved generic AX
   announcement when applicable. Reopening after an accepted local or LAN commit shows the bounded
   four lines with no repair message; rejected/stale close never changes the world or template.
4. **AX focus notifications describe changes, not requests.** Authorization remains ephemeral and
   unusable by text/Paste ingress. Initially unfocused or sibling-to-target success posts exactly one
   `.focusedUIElementChanged` only after postvalidation. A request for the already sole-focused
   descriptor is an idempotent success with no duplicate focus notification. Suppress reentrant
   AppKit focus notifications until postvalidation; failure and stale generations remain silent.
   Custom implicit owners follow the same idempotence rule and never change active Sign line, query,
   Chat state, buttons, or world state.
5. **Real-target evidence covers the state transitions.** In addition to the adversarial pure and
   integration matrix, installed proof must show local repaired-open -> status -> edit/clear ->
   commit -> world reopen; placed-template repair without template mutation; and two-machine LAN
   repaired-open -> pending -> accepted convergence plus rejected/disconnected recovery. VoiceOver
   proof covers initially unfocused and sibling switching, repeated focus, resize during focus,
   exact notification counts/order, immediate bounded typing after success, and read-only values.

**Renewed Design Review 2 verdict: PASS.** With these five corrections binding, repaired Signs are
safe and understandable from local/template/LAN open through acknowledged commit or discard, and
two-phase Accessibility focus is both usable and notification-correct. Security (plan) must review
the added LAN result and pending-state boundary and PASS before Build.

## Security (plan) rerun 2 — 2026-07-11

Reviewed the complete plan at SHA-256
`66f0214f7d17efb1db48969a4b3a93aff8a99e2bd5b981428550119068c39e98`. The four-line Sign repair now
closes cardinality, invalid-scalar, byte, width, presentation, template-isolation, and pre-commit
mutation risks. The original Chat/history, grapheme, clipboard, read-only AX value, and test/release
surface findings also remain closed. The new asynchronous LAN commit and reentrant AX focus paths are
not yet specified tightly enough to authorize Build.

### Findings

- **[HIGH] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:1131-1144,1227-1235` ->** The Architecture defines only
  `LANSignEditIntent`; the binding Design Review requires a typed correlated result but supplies no
  codec fields, request-ID allocation, host replay rule, or client transition authority. Add closed
  payloads `LANSignEditIntent(requestID: UInt64, dimension, position, expectedRevision, lines)` and
  `LANSignEditResult(requestID: UInt64, outcome: accepted(committedRevision: UInt64) | rejected)`;
  rejection carries no reason or submitted metadata. Each connection generation starts a checked
  request counter at one, permits one pending request, and fails closed on overflow. The host requires
  the next request ID for that authenticated peer/generation, caches the last bounded result, resends
  that result for an exact duplicate without reapplying, and rejects lower/gapped IDs without world
  mutation. The guest pending record binds request ID, connection generation, exact screen identity,
  Sign commit token, submitted bounded lines, and expected revision. Acceptance closes only after
  both the matching accepted result and the authoritative snapshot for the same Sign contain the
  returned revision and exact submitted lines, in either arrival order. Matching rejection restores
  editability; duplicate/out-of-order/old-connection results are silent. A fixed **10-second
  monotonic timeout**, disconnect, or screen replacement retires the pending record with no automatic
  replay; late results never close or mutate a replacement screen, while ordinary authoritative
  snapshots may still update the world mirror. Retry allocates a new ID against the latest matching
  authoritative revision. Add state-machine/property tests for result-before-snapshot,
  snapshot-before-result, duplicate intent/result, gaps, replay, timeout, disconnect-before/after
  host commit, reconnect, replaced screen, request/revision overflow, and exactly-once host mutation.
- **[HIGH] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:1135-1143,1188-1200` ->** “next unused ... currently 27” is
  not a safe protocol assignment: 27 is free only in the live V5 `LANMultiplayerMessageKind`, while
  V6 already freezes 27/28/29 as ownerManifest/ownerChunk/clientReady, and no result kind or
  compatibility negotiation is allocated. Append intent/result as **27/28 only in V5** and reserve
  **30/31 in `LANV6MessageKind`**; update every exhaustive flow/admission/payload-budget switch and
  golden without renumbering 1...29. Negotiate a `signEditRequestResult` capability in hello/accept;
  a peer lacking it remains read-only and neither side sends an unknown kind. Admit intent only from
  an authenticated client to its host and result only from that host to its connected client; both
  use the non-replication frame cap, while decoded lines still undergo exact-four and host scalar/
  byte/pack-width validation. Malformed/unknown/disallowed frames follow the existing bounded
  protocol violation path. Tests must cover mixed-capability peers, V5/V6 raw-value stability,
  direction/phase denial, payload limits, exhaustive mutation-clock classification, and codec fuzzing
  for both payloads before enabling send.
- **[MEDIUM] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:1155-1179,1243-1249` ->** Three synchronous calls do not
  by themselves prevent reentrant AppKit/AX focus callbacks, and `focusTextDescriptor(id:)` can be
  called without consuming the authorization it supposedly requires. Make
  `TextFocusAuthorization` nonconstructible outside `UIManager`, one-shot, and required by
  `focusTextDescriptor(authorization:)`. `UIManager` owns an `idle | focusing(token)` main-actor
  transaction with `defer` cleanup: an already sole-focused target returns success without calling
  AppKit or notifying; an identical reentrant request while focusing coalesces without a second focus
  or notification; a different reentrant target fails silently. Suppress/defer AppKit focus
  notifications during the transaction and emit exactly one only after postvalidation succeeds.
  Capability reuse, generation change, close/reopen, descriptor rebuild, or postcheck failure retires
  it; rollback never touches a replacement screen. Test same/different-target reentrancy from
  `makeFirstResponder` and notification callbacks, capability reuse/forgery, already-focused
  idempotence, failure cleanup, and immediate bounded typing only after successful completion.

Host/client status and evidence remain content-private: wire rejection has no reason string; UI/AX
uses only `Saving sign…` or `Sign update failed`; aggregate diagnostics may count outcomes but may not
record Sign text, position, request ID, player/peer identity, permission reason, or discarded repair
data. Repeated/late/stale events produce no extra status or announcement.

### Review boundary and verdict

Reviewed: all prior findings, repaired Sign open/commit/LAN authority, pending-state UX, request/result
trust boundaries, replay/stale/disconnect cases, status privacy, AX focus authorization/reentrancy/
idempotency, package topology, and the complete downstream evidence map.

Not reviewed: Builder code, actual codecs/admission tables, runtime concurrency, compiled graph,
tests, build/pipeline output, installed two-machine behavior, signing, deployment, or publication. No
implementation file was edited or approved.

**Security (plan) rerun 2 verdict: FAIL.** Sign repair and all original findings are closed, but the
new LAN request/result path lacks a collision-free negotiated wire contract and exactly-once
correlation/replay state machine, while AX focus lacks an enforceable one-shot reentrancy boundary.
Build remains blocked. Architecture and Design Review must incorporate the three exact fixes above,
then Security (plan) must rerun and PASS before Builder work.

## Design Security Revision 3 — authoritative Sign editing and one-shot AX focus

This revision deliberately removes the LAN guest Sign-write expansion. It supersedes every prior
Design/Architecture requirement for `LANSignEditIntent`, a result message, request IDs, negotiated
capabilities, revisions, pending/retry state, `Saving sign…`, or guest-side Sign commit. None belongs
in this scoped text-entry repair.

### Sign authority is simple and visible

- Single-player and the authoritative LAN host retain the full repaired four-line Sign editor.
  Typing, Paste, width/byte limits, `Sign text repaired`, `Line is full`, synchronous tokenized
  commit, template-source isolation, and local/host reopen behavior remain exactly as approved. A
  successful host edit reaches guests only through Pebble's existing authoritative world/block-
  entity replication; this work adds no Sign-edit wire kind or client write capability.
- A LAN guest may open a Sign only as a bounded read-only viewer. The instruction row always reads
  exactly `Only the host can edit signs.` It draws the safely repaired four-line snapshot, with no
  caret, active editing line, text focus, Paste target, completion, or commit gesture. Typing,
  Backspace/Delete, arrows, Return, and Paste cause no text/world/template/network mutation; the open
  screen still consumes them so they cannot leak to gameplay. Escape closes without sending or
  writing anything.
- The guest viewer is never a text owner. Accessibility exposes one readable, enabled static Sign
  value plus the exact authority status/help, not an editable text/search field, insertion range,
  focused text value, or focus-to-edit action. Its bounded repaired display discloses no rejected
  content. Because the guest cannot repair or commit authority state, the authority message
  supersedes guest `Sign text repaired` and is announced at most once per open when assistive
  technology is active; local/host repair feedback is unchanged.
- Authority changes are fail-closed. If a host editor loses authority/session/target identity before
  close, commit writes nothing and follows the approved synchronous `Sign update failed` recovery.
  If a guest becomes host, it must close and reopen before gaining the editor; an already-open guest
  viewer never upgrades in place. A host becoming guest immediately retires text ownership and
  rebuilds as the read-only viewer without committing the local buffer.

Installed acceptance now proves: repaired local edit -> commit -> world reopen; placed-template
repair without template mutation; authoritative host edit/commit -> ordinary two-machine guest
replication; and guest open -> exact read-only status/value -> attempted typing/Paste/Return with no
buffer, world, template, packet, or fingerprint mutation -> Escape close. Protocol/source scans must
show no new Sign intent/result kind, capability, request counter, pending state, or guest commit path.

### Accessibility focus capability is enforceable but invisible

Accessibility focus retains the approved authorize/focus/postvalidate experience, with these
non-negotiable mechanics:

- Only `UIManager` can mint a nonconstructible `TextFocusAuthorization` after validating the exact
  weak top screen, generation, unique visible/enabled/focusable descriptor, and finite frame. The
  capability is one-shot, bound to that screen/generation/descriptor, never cached, and is the only
  argument accepted by `focusTextDescriptor(authorization:)`; raw descriptor IDs cannot invoke the
  focus mutation.
- On the main actor, `UIManager` owns an `idle | focusing(token)` transaction with guaranteed cleanup.
  Reuse, forgery, generation change, close/reopen, rebuild, or postcheck failure retires the
  capability. An identical reentrant request coalesces without a second AppKit call or notification;
  a different target fails silently. AppKit focus notifications are suppressed/deferred until the
  same-token sole-owner postcheck succeeds.
- An already sole-focused target is an idempotent success with no AppKit refocus and no notification.
  A genuine initially-unfocused or sibling-to-target success posts exactly one
  `.focusedUIElementChanged`, exposes the refreshed focused state, and permits immediate bounded
  typing/Paste. Failure leaves no surviving ownership, text, button, or world change and never touches
  a replacement screen. Mouse/keyboard focus, logical focus across resize, and local/host implicit
  Recipe/Sign/Chat ownership remain visually unchanged. The LAN guest Sign viewer offers no text
  focus authorization at all.

Tests and installed VoiceOver proof cover unforgeability and one-shot reuse, same/different-target
reentrancy, notification-callback reentrancy, initially unfocused and sibling switching, already-
focused idempotence, resize/reopen/generation failure, immediate bounded typing after success, custom
implicit owners, and the readable but non-editable guest Sign.

**Design Security Revision 3 verdict: PASS.** The scoped product now has full Sign editing only where
authority already exists, an unmistakable and safe guest read-only state with no protocol expansion,
and a one-shot AX focus boundary that preserves the approved visible and VoiceOver experience.
Architecture must remove the superseded LAN machinery, incorporate the capability contract, renew
Design Review, and obtain Security (plan) PASS before Build.

## Architecture Security Revision 3 — existing Sign authority and one-shot AX focus

Reviewed Design Security Revision 3 at SHA-256
`028fd864ce2dcc4fcae7cfa6be9e24a141fa3bc3a64ae48008d22c0e3ba2f928`. This revision supersedes and
removes every Revision 2 requirement for `LANSignEditIntent`, `LANSignEditResult`, message kinds
27/28/30/31, Sign-write negotiation/capabilities, request or revision counters, replay caches,
pending/retry/timeout states, and `Saving sign…`. Builder must add none of those symbols, packets, or
state machines. Sign writes never travel guest-to-host.

### One authority gate, two Sign surfaces

`GameCore.isLANClientWorld` is the existing source of truth: `false` means this process owns the
world (single-player or LAN host), and `true` means it is a replicated guest mirror. On the main
thread, `SignScreen.initScreen` samples that property into a non-upgradeable
`SignAccessMode.authoritative(SignCommitToken) | .guestViewer` only after performing the approved
four-line streaming repair and before creating controls, descriptors, or input ownership.

In `.authoritative`, the local/host editor retains the typed `PebbleFourSignLines`, caret, bounded
typing/Paste, local repair/capacity status, and synchronous `GameCore.commitSignEdit`. Its commit
token now contains only authoritative world/session generation, dimension/position, target Sign
block identity, and expected block-entity identity/absence; all proposed LAN request/connection/
host-revision fields are deleted. Commit rechecks `!game.isLANClientWorld` plus every token field on
the serial world/save path, atomically writes exactly four bounded lines, marks the chunk dirty, and
returns success or leaves the editor open with `Sign update failed`. No check failure writes, saves,
replicates, or closes.

In `.guestViewer`, the same repaired value is presentation-only. It creates no text field/owner,
caret, insertion range, active editing line, Paste handler, completion, or commit token. Its sole
instruction status is `Only the host can edit signs.` and its AX descriptor is a static readable
Sign value/help, never an editable/search field or focus-to-edit action. Every character/key entry
point rechecks the mode and consumes typing, Backspace/Delete, arrows, and Return without changing
the snapshot; the global Paste resolver returns no owner while the open non-owner screen blocks
template/world fallback. Escape alone closes. No guest path calls `commitSignEdit`, container edit,
LAN send, or any transport closure. The authority status supersedes repair/capacity status and is
announced at most once per open after the current static descriptor exists.

Authority is rechecked before owner resolution, every edit, Paste capture and apply, update/draw,
and close. An authoritative screen observing `isLANClientWorld == true` first invalidates its UI/AX
generation, retires owner/Paste focus, discards its uncommitted buffer, rebuilds from the current
replicated block entity as `.guestViewer`, and writes/sends nothing. A guest viewer observing
`isLANClientWorld == false` invalidates and closes without mutation; it never upgrades in place, so
the user must reopen to mint a fresh authoritative commit token. Session/target invalidation while
remaining authoritative follows `Sign update failed`. Generation overflow closes fail-closed.

Host convergence uses only the existing host-to-client `.replicationBatch` path. Extend the existing
optional `LANBlockEntitySnapshot` payload and normalization/application functions to carry a
bounded `signLines: [String]?` for a compatible Sign block entity; permit a Sign snapshot without
inventory slots. Snapshot construction emits exactly the already-validated four host lines, and
guest application replaces only the mirrored Sign entity after the existing dimension/chunk/block-
compatibility checks. Unknown optional fields remain decoder-compatible. This is ordinary
authoritative block-entity replication, not a new kind, capability, request, acknowledgement, or
guest state machine. A guest viewer rebuild/next open reads that mirror and reruns repair before
publication. Protocol scans must prove no Sign-specific client-to-host frame exists.

Tests therefore cover both values of `isLANClientWorld` at open and at every transition boundary;
host/local edit, synchronous stale/authority-loss rejection, reopen persistence, and template-source
isolation; guest static AX semantics and exhaustive key/Paste consumption; editor-to-viewer discard,
viewer authority-gain close/reopen, disconnect/reconnect and screen replacement; and byte-identical
guest buffer/world/template/outbound-frame fingerprints after attempted edits. Existing replication
tests must prove host commit produces one bounded ordinary block-entity snapshot and two-machine
guest convergence, while malformed/absent Sign payloads repair safely. Raw-kind/golden/source scans
must prove no new Sign request/result kind, capability, counter, pending status, retry, timeout, or
container-intent branch.

### UIManager-private one-shot focus transaction

Define `UIManager.TextFocusAuthorization` as a nested final reference type with a `private`
initializer. It exposes only read-only screen/generation/descriptor identity; no screen, test helper,
decoder, protocol conformance, copy initializer, or raw descriptor API can construct it. `UIManager`
keeps the sole strong reference and matches `ObjectIdentifier` against the active transaction.
Screens expose only `focusTextDescriptor(authorization:)`; the old ID-taking mutation is removed.
Its first operation asks `UIManager` to claim that exact capability for that exact screen. Claim
atomically changes `minted -> consumed` and returns the descriptor ID once; reuse or a mismatched
caller returns nil before focus mutation.

The main-actor state is exactly `idle | focusing(FocusTransaction)`. A transaction holds the token,
weak screen, capability identity/state, pre-focus logical owner and first responder, whether focus
actually changed, a coalesced-identical bit, a deferred-AppKit-notification bit, and an
`ingressCommitted` bit. The request algorithm is:

1. While `idle`, prevalidate the exact current weak top screen/generation, one unique visible,
   enabled, focusable descriptor, finite clamped frame, and field mapping. If it is already the sole
   owner, return idempotent success without minting, calling AppKit, or notifying. Otherwise mint one
   capability, install `.focusing` before external code, and use `defer` to retire the capability,
   discard deferred events, and restore `.idle` on every exit.
2. While `.focusing`, an exact same screen/generation/descriptor request only sets the coalesced bit
   and returns without a second capability, screen/AppKit call, or notification. Any other target,
   generation, or screen is rejected silently. Thus reentry from `makeFirstResponder`, descriptor
   callbacks, or notification observers cannot nest a focus transaction.
3. Invoke `focusTextDescriptor(authorization:)` once. Keep text/Paste ingress disabled while it
   claims the capability, clears sibling fields, and focuses the target. Immediately postvalidate
   the unchanged top screen/generation/unique descriptor and target sole ownership. Capability
   reuse, rebuild, close/reopen, replacement, or failed postcheck fails the transaction.
4. On failure, if and only if the original screen/generation is still current, restore its captured
   logical focus/first responder when that responder is still valid, otherwise clear the attempted
   focus. Never touch a replacement screen and never emit a focus notification. On success set
   `ingressCommitted`, allowing immediate bounded ingress. If focus changed, emit exactly one
   `.focusedUIElementChanged` while the transaction remains `.focusing`; if not, emit none.

AppKit/AX focus observers consult this state before forwarding. During `.focusing` they set only the
single deferred bit and return—no callback is queued and no notification is reposted. A successful
changed transaction emits the one canonical notification after postvalidation and drops the bit;
failure and idempotent/coalesced requests drop it silently. Notification-callback reentry therefore
coalesces the same target or rejects a different target before `defer` retires the capability.
Resize preserves logical focus in the new generation through the existing rebuild path, not by
reusing a capability. The guest Sign static descriptor is never eligible for authorization.

Direct tests use UIManager test seams that can request focus but cannot construct a capability. They
cover attempted forgery/copy/serialization, double claim/reuse, wrong screen, initially unfocused and
sibling switching, already-focused idempotence, same/different-target reentry from both AppKit and
notification callbacks, deferred-event collapse, close/reopen/resize/generation-overflow during each
phase, failure rollback and guaranteed idle cleanup, exact AppKit-call/notification counts, and
bounded typing/Paste only after `ingressCommitted`. Installed VoiceOver proof repeats the visible
focus/idempotence/reentry cases for normal fields and local/host implicit Recipe/Sign/Chat owners,
and confirms the guest Sign remains readable but cannot receive text focus.

### Dependency and gate amendment

Build order is now: pure four-line repair/streaming width tests; authoritative/editor versus guest-
viewer gate and local tokenized commit; optional Sign data on existing one-way replication; private
one-shot AX transaction; Security (code); Design sign-off; focused/property/fuzz and full tests; then
installed local, VoiceOver, and two-machine replication proof. Delete/supersede the Revision 2 LAN
request/result dependency and all of its correlation, replay, negotiation, pending-state, and codec
tests. The warning-free release build, XCTest, 457-check `pebsmoke`, security scan, pipeline, and
installed deploy proof remain mandatory after the final code change.

**Architecture Security Revision 3 verdict: PASS.** Sign mutation is now confined to Pebble's
existing local/host authority, guests have a fail-closed non-owning viewer with zero outbound write
surface, ordinary one-way replication proves convergence, and UIManager alone can execute one
non-reentrant, one-shot, postvalidated AX focus transaction. Renewed Design Review and Security
(plan) must independently PASS this revision before Build.

## Renewed Design Review 3 — authoritative editor, guest viewer, one-shot focus

Reviewed Architecture Security Revision 3 through SHA-256
`02a2d43e1c6b7e6aa91694818df33c99723e2cccc52aa9cdb0ef3d3a9f7b97e7`. The existing-authority
gate, full local/host editor, bounded guest viewer, ordinary one-way replication, and UIManager-only
focus transaction realize Design Security Revision 3. These corrections are binding:

1. **Preserve the complete authoritative close recovery.** Escape and line-four Return attempt the
   synchronous local/host commit. Success closes. Failure writes/replicates nothing, keeps the editor
   open with `Sign update failed`, and preserves its four bounded lines; an accepted edit clears the
   status and permits retry, while a second unchanged Escape discards and closes. No generic
   “cancel/dismiss” path may accidentally commit.
2. **A visible guest viewer must not become stale.** When ordinary replication changes the viewed
   Sign, rebuild its safely repaired static value under a new presentation generation while retaining
   `Only the host can edit signs.` and no text owner. Post one truthful AX value/layout change, not a
   focus or authority-status repeat. If the target ceases to be a compatible Sign or the session is
   replaced, close silently. Guest authority gain still requires close/reopen; host authority loss
   still discards the uncommitted editor before rebuilding the viewer.
3. **Static read-only remains accessible.** The guest Sign value/status stays reachable by ordinary
   VoiceOver navigation and is announced as read-only help; it is merely ineligible for the
   text-focus authorization and never makes `GameView` first responder. Typing/Paste/Return remain
   consumed without clipboard read, packet, fingerprint, template, or world mutation; Escape alone
   closes.
4. **Idempotent AX focus includes keyboard readiness.** “Already focused” may bypass capability
   minting only when the same descriptor is the sole logical owner **and** `GameView` is the valid
   first responder. Otherwise the one-shot transaction must establish first responder, postvalidate,
   and emit the single canonical focus notification before bounded ingress. This applies to normal
   fields and implicit Recipe/local-or-host Sign/Chat owners. Capability identity, reentrancy,
   rollback, and replacement-screen isolation otherwise match the Architecture exactly.
5. **Keep installed proof empirical and feasible.** Direct tests, not a release hook, force
   same/different-target reentrancy, forgery/reuse, and callbacks during transaction phases. The
   installed app proves observable initially-unfocused/sibling/already-focused behavior, immediate
   typing, notification counts, resize, and guest static navigation. Two current installed apps prove
   host edit/commit, existing replication with an already-open and reopened guest viewer, exact guest
   authority copy, attempted guest edits with zero outbound mutation, and Escape close. Binary/source
   scans prove there is no Sign request/result kind, negotiation, counter, pending/retry state,
   `Saving sign…`, or guest commit symbol; the optional existing replication field is not a new
   bidirectional capability.

**Renewed Design Review 3 verdict: PASS.** With these five corrections binding, local/host editing
and guest viewing are clear and current, no superseded LAN-write interaction remains, installed proof
uses real observable states, and one-shot Accessibility focus remains keyboard-usable without a
visible regression. Security (plan) must PASS this exact revision before Build.

## Security (plan) rerun 3 — 2026-07-11

Reviewed the complete plan at SHA-256
`e4582ba55bb3cb3ff2345aadd6dd00664c7c44c65648189daccbfce23843fc8c`. All Sign-specific guest-write
protocol, capability, correlation, pending, retry, and status surfaces are explicitly removed. The
guest viewer has no text owner, Paste read, edit/commit token, transport closure, or in-place authority
upgrade; role changes invalidate before mutation. The four-line repair and every original Chat,
Unicode, clipboard, Accessibility-value, and test-graph limit remain closed. Two boundedness/readiness
gaps remain in the replacement design.

### Findings

- **[HIGH] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:1457-1475,1531-1537` ->** Optional one-way `signLines` are
  individually bounded, but the plan gives the existing replication batch no aggregate byte budget
  or block-entity-only split rule. Up to 64 snapshots each carrying four 4,096-byte lines can exceed
  the 1,048,576-byte frame after JSON overhead; the existing oversized fallback only splits chunk
  sections, so an oversized batch with no sections can be requeued forever and starve authoritative
  Sign convergence. Add exact-four/scalar/byte normalization to `LANBlockEntitySnapshot` decode and
  `isWithinReplicationCaps`, then extend the existing frame builder to greedily encode bounded
  block-entity groups and recursively halve a group on `oversizedFrame` down to one snapshot. Send
  non-BE batch content once and BE-only follow-up batches thereafter; never drop a dirty Sign. A
  single still-oversized snapshot is a protocol/host invariant failure that is not sent, is counted
  without content/position/peer data, and cannot spin the same dirty entry indefinitely. Preserve
  deterministic order and requeue only unsent groups on backpressure/error. Test 0/1/64 maximal
  Signs, mixed containers/Signs/chunk sections, exact frame boundary, single-snapshot failure,
  partial-send retry without duplicates/loss, malformed optional arrays, older decoder compatibility,
  and two-machine eventual convergence under backpressure.
- **[MEDIUM] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:1493-1516,1568-1573` ->** The Architecture sets
  `ingressCommitted` before posting the canonical focus notification and its postcheck verifies only
  logical ownership, while the binding Design Review requires a valid `GameView` first responder and
  the notification before bounded ingress. Make idempotent success require the exact descriptor as
  sole logical owner, `GameView` as valid first responder, and an already-ready owner generation;
  otherwise run the one-shot transaction. Postvalidation must include first-responder identity. Keep
  keyboard and Paste ingress disabled—including no clipboard read—through `makeFirstResponder`, all
  reentrant callbacks, postvalidation, and the single canonical notification. Only after that
  notification returns successfully may UIManager mark the exact screen/generation/descriptor ready,
  then retire the capability and return to `idle`; callers can type immediately after the focus action
  returns. Failure, rebuild, role change, or replacement clears readiness. Test reentrant typing and
  Command-V from `makeFirstResponder` and notification observers, wrong first responder, ordinary
  resize readiness renewal, already-focused idempotence, and zero pre-ready text/clipboard/world
  mutation.

### Review boundary and verdict

Reviewed: absence of a guest Sign-write protocol and symbols; existing authority gate; guest
open/input/Paste/close and role transitions; optional host-to-guest replication; Sign repair and
status privacy; UIManager-private capability construction/consumption, reentrancy, idempotency, and
keyboard readiness; plus all original resource, clipboard, AX-value, and release-test boundaries.

Not reviewed: Builder code, actual replication splitting/normalization, runtime focus callbacks,
compiled graph, tests, build/pipeline output, installed local/two-machine/VoiceOver behavior, signing,
deployment, or publication. No implementation file was edited or approved.

**Security (plan) rerun 3 verdict: FAIL.** Removing guest Sign writes closes the prior LAN protocol
findings, and the authority/viewer split is fail-closed. Build remains blocked because optional Sign
replication lacks a lossless aggregate frame-budget strategy and AX focus can expose ingress before
the approved readiness boundary. Architecture and Design Review must incorporate the two exact fixes
above, then Security (plan) must rerun and PASS before Builder work.

## Design Security Revision 4 — zero LAN surface and notification-ordered focus

This revision removes the cause of the remaining LAN finding instead of expanding the replication
scheduler. It supersedes **all** prior requirements for `signLines`, Sign fields in
`LANBlockEntitySnapshot`, Sign normalization or compatibility changes, Sign-specific
`.replicationBatch` construction/splitting/backpressure, Sign dirty-entry handling, live guest Sign
refresh, or new packet/convergence proof. This text-entry repair makes zero LAN wire, schema,
capability, codec, admission, frame-budget, scheduler, or retry changes.

### Guest Sign is a truthful snapshot viewer

- Local single-player and the authoritative host retain the complete bounded Sign editor and local
  commit proof. A guest viewer reads only the `BlockEntityData` already present in its existing
  replicated world when the screen opens, repairs that value safely for presentation, and remains a
  non-owning static viewer with `Only the host can edit signs.` No text-entry work attempts to make
  that state newer or more complete.
- The displayed guest value is explicitly a snapshot for that screen presentation. If existing
  world replication happens to update the mirror while it is open, this repair need not live-refresh
  it; Escape and reopen reads the then-current existing mirror. The authority copy and read-only AX
  help make that interaction truthful. No “live,” “saved,” “synced,” or convergence claim is shown.
- Guest typing, Paste, editing keys, and Return remain consumed with no clipboard read, text owner,
  local mutation, template/world fallback, or outbound packet. Escape closes. Authority changes
  retain the approved close/reopen boundary. Guest repair never writes back. There is no Sign-
  specific snapshot field, encoder/decoder branch, packet kind, host-send branch, batch split,
  backpressure rule, dirty retry, or two-machine Sign convergence requirement.

Installed acceptance is correspondingly finite: prove repaired local edit/commit/world reopen,
placed-template source isolation, and the same full editor on an authoritative host; on a guest,
open the current existing mirrored state, verify the exact authority status and static AX value,
attempt typing/Paste/Return with no owner/write/new outbound packet, close, and reopen safely. Source,
binary, protocol-golden, and captured-frame comparisons must prove this change adds no LAN field,
kind, capability, scheduler path, or Sign-specific traffic. They do not promise that current Pebble
replication transports host Sign edits.

### Focus becomes ready only after its notification completes

The one-shot capability and `idle | focusing(token)` transaction remain, but visible logical focus
is not permission to ingest text. Readiness is an exact
`(screen identity, generation, descriptor ID)` state owned only by `UIManager`:

1. Mint/consume the unforgeable capability, keep readiness false, establish the target as sole
   logical owner, and make `GameView` first responder. Pre-notification postvalidation must confirm
   the unchanged current screen/generation/descriptor, sole owner, and first-responder identity.
2. While still `focusing` and still not ready, post the one canonical
   `.focusedUIElementChanged`. Reentrant typing and key events are consumed without mutation or world
   fallback; reentrant Paste is consumed **before any clipboard read**. Nothing is buffered, cached,
   replayed, or delivered to another owner.
3. Only after the notification call returns, revalidate the same screen/generation/descriptor,
   logical sole owner, and `GameView` first responder. Success then marks that exact generation ready,
   retires the capability, and returns to `idle`. Failure rolls back/clears focus as previously
   approved and leaves ingress disabled.

Idempotent success requires an already-ready exact owner generation plus `GameView` first responder;
logical focus alone reruns the transaction. Resize/rebuild invalidates old readiness. During the same
synchronous rebuild, `UIManager` may renew readiness for the new generation without a duplicate focus
notification only after validating the preserved sole owner, new unique descriptor/frame, and
unchanged valid first responder; otherwise the next AX focus request performs the full transaction.
Close, replacement, role change, overflow, or focus loss clears readiness immediately.

The focus outline may become visible before readiness, but the main-actor transaction does not yield
to the ordinary event loop. Real user events remain ordered after the focus action returns and are
handled once ready; only hostile/reentrant callbacks can arrive in the guarded interval, and those
are consumed without being dropped into another subsystem. Installed behavior therefore remains
responsive with no visible delay, duplicate announcement, lost ordinary keystroke, clipboard read,
or gameplay leakage. Direct tests force typing and Command-V from `makeFirstResponder`, the canonical
notification, and resize/rebuild callbacks; installed VoiceOver proof verifies immediate ordinary
typing/Paste after focus returns and correct readiness renewal after resize.

**Design Security Revision 4 verdict: PASS.** The scoped repair now has no LAN protocol or scheduler
surface at all, a truthful guest snapshot viewer with no freshness promise, and an AX focus sequence
whose canonical notification completes before exact-generation ingress becomes ready. Architecture
must remove every superseded Sign replication promise, incorporate this ordering, renew Design
Review, and obtain Security (plan) PASS before Build.

## Architecture Security Revision 4 — zero Sign LAN changes and notification-ordered readiness

Reviewed Design Security Revision 4 at SHA-256
`c3cb9dff02b77786efdd837be37d9cbeeb10de4c617afeb99706d8ad44319dd3`. This revision supersedes
Architecture Revisions 2 and 3 wherever they require any Sign-specific LAN request/result, payload,
field, schema, snapshot normalization, compatibility branch, message kind, capability, admission,
frame split, backpressure/requeue, dirty tracking, live refresh, acknowledgement, or convergence
work. There are **zero Sign edits** to `LANTransport.swift`, `LANMultiplayer.swift`,
`LANReplication.swift`, their V6 equivalents, or their protocol goldens. Current wire bytes and
message-kind tables remain unchanged.

### Sign authority and snapshot lifetime

`GameCore.isLANClientWorld` remains the only authority gate. At `SignScreen.initScreen`, before any
descriptor or owner is created, the screen reads the current existing `BlockEntityData.lines` once,
runs the approved iterator-bounded four-line repair, drops the raw source, and chooses:

- `false`: the existing local/host editor with typed bounded lines and synchronous tokenized local
  commit; or
- `true`: a `.guestViewer` value containing only the repaired four-line presentation snapshot and
  presentation generation.

The guest snapshot is not tied to, observed against, or refreshed from later world/block-entity
changes. Draw and static AX read only that owned bounded value. Escape closes; a later explicit open
performs a new one-time read of whatever block entity the existing mirror then contains. No text,
status, or help claims live, saved, synchronized, or converged state. `Only the host can edit signs.`
remains the sole guest instruction and at-most-once authority announcement.

The viewer has no caret, active line, text descriptor/owner, insertion range, Paste target, commit
token, or transport reference. Its character/key handler consumes typing, editing/navigation keys,
and Return. UIManager classifies the open viewer as a blocking non-owner before action fallback; a
Paste gesture is consumed before `NSPasteboard` access. Escape closes without reading the clipboard
or touching its snapshot, block entity, template, fingerprint, save, or network. Guest repair never
writes back. A guest-to-host authority transition closes the viewer and requires reopen; a host-to-
guest transition retires readiness/ownership, discards the uncommitted editor, and creates one new
viewer snapshot from the then-current existing block entity without committing. Session/screen
replacement closes fail-closed. Ordinary mirror changes while the viewer remains open do nothing.

The authoritative editor retains Revision 3's local `SignCommitToken`, exact close/retry/discard
semantics, save/reopen proof, and placed-template isolation. Its successful LAN-host mutation may
have whatever behavior Pebble's current unmodified replication already provides; this scope neither
depends on nor asserts Sign transport. Tests and product copy make no guest freshness/convergence
promise.

### One-shot focus transaction and ingress ordering

Keep the UIManager-private, nonconstructible, identity-matched, one-shot
`TextFocusAuthorization` and `idle | focusing(transaction)` state from Revision 3. Add a separate
UIManager-owned optional `TextIngressReadiness(screenID,generation,descriptorID)`. Only UIManager can
create it; owner resolution treats logical focus without an exact ready tuple as non-ingress. It is
cleared synchronously before focus loss, descriptor rebuild, close/reopen, screen or role change,
generation retirement/overflow, or entry into a non-idempotent focus transaction.

The exact main-actor order is:

1. Prevalidate current weak top-screen identity, generation, one unique visible/enabled/focusable
   descriptor with finite clamped frame, and field mapping. Idempotent success is permitted only if
   that descriptor is already the sole logical owner, the window's current first responder is the
   exact `GameView`, and readiness exactly matches the same screen/generation/descriptor. It performs
   no AppKit call or notification.
2. Otherwise clear readiness, mint/install one capability and `.focusing` transaction, then invoke
   `focusTextDescriptor(authorization:)` once. Its one-time claim clears siblings/focuses the target
   and makes the exact `GameView` first responder. The pre-notification postcheck requires unchanged
   top screen, generation and unique descriptor, target as sole owner, and identity equality
   `window.firstResponder === gameView`.
3. With state still `.focusing` and readiness nil, synchronously post exactly one canonical
   `.focusedUIElementChanged` for a genuine change. Observed AppKit focus events are reduced to the
   transaction's single deferred bit. Same-target focus reentry coalesces; other-target reentry fails.
   Every reentrant text/key event is consumed at the UIManager ingress gate. Command-V is recognized
   and consumed there **before** any pasteboard capture/read/decode; nothing is buffered or replayed
   and no template/gameplay fallback runs.
4. Only after the notification call returns, postvalidate again: exact current screen/generation/
   descriptor, sole owner, and exact `GameView` first responder. On success install readiness for
   that tuple, retire the capability, drop deferred events, return `.idle`, then return from the focus
   action; ordinary subsequent typing/Paste can immediately use the bounded owner. On failure keep
   readiness nil, restore/clear focus only on the still-current same-generation screen as previously
   approved, retire capability/events, return `.idle`, and notify nothing further.

`defer` guarantees capability retirement, deferred-event deletion, nil readiness on failure, and
`.idle` restoration even when focus or notification callbacks rebuild/replace state. A notification
is never interpreted as successful readiness merely because it was invoked; the second complete
postcheck is authoritative. Different-screen rollback remains forbidden.

For an ordinary synchronous resize while `idle`, capture the old ready tuple and logical focused ID,
clear readiness **before** incrementing generation, rebuild, then optionally renew readiness without
a focus notification only if the same screen instance now has one new-generation descriptor for the
same logical sole owner, its visibility/enabled/finite-frame checks pass, and `GameView` remains the
exact first responder. Any mismatch leaves readiness nil and the next AX focus request runs the full
transaction. Resize during `.focusing` never renews: its generation change invalidates the active
capability and both postchecks fail closed. Guest Sign's static descriptor is never eligible.

### Files, dependency order, and evidence amendment

Implementation scope is limited to the normal `PebbleTextInput` support target and its tests,
`Sources/Pebble/ScreensM.swift` for Sign editor/viewer state, UIManager/GameView text-owner,
Accessibility, key, and Paste routing files for capability/readiness ordering, plus focused UI test
seams and durable text-entry docs. No Sign-related Core networking, transport, codec, replication,
scheduler, or protocol test file may change.

Build order is: pure validator/four-line/streaming-width tests; local/host Sign editor and one-read
guest viewer/authority transitions; UIManager one-shot focus plus readiness ingress gate; resize
renewal; Security (code); installed Design sign-off; focused/property tests; full gates; deploy proof.
Delete every prior dependency/test for Sign snapshot fields, normalization, live viewer refresh,
batch sizing/splitting/backpressure, dirty retry, packet capability, or two-machine Sign convergence.

Required Sign evidence covers nil and 0/1/3/4/5 one-time sources, huge/invalid/boundary repair,
local/host commit/retry/discard and reopen, template isolation, guest open/static AX/status, attempted
typing/editing/Return/Paste with zero clipboard/world/template/outbound-frame change, ignored mirror
mutation while open, and close/reopen reading a newly available existing mirror. Source/binary/raw-
frame comparisons must prove zero Sign field/kind/codec/transport/scheduler additions and no guest
commit path.

Required focus tests cover unforgeable/reused capabilities; wrong and changing first responder;
initial/sibling/idempotent focus; same/different-target reentry; typing and Command-V from
`makeFirstResponder` and canonical-notification callbacks with zero clipboard read/mutation/fallback;
first and second postcheck failures; cleanup; ordinary resize renewal and resize-during-focus denial;
close/reopen, role change, replacement and overflow; exact notification/AppKit counts; and immediate
bounded ingress only after the action returns. Installed VoiceOver proof covers observable focus,
idempotence, resize renewal, immediate ordinary typing/Paste, and guest static navigation. The
warning-free release build, XCTest, 457-check `pebsmoke`, security scan, pipeline, and installed app
proof remain mandatory after the final code change.

**Architecture Security Revision 4 verdict: PASS.** The scoped implementation now has no Sign LAN
schema, transport, replication, scheduler, or freshness obligation, the guest reads one safe static
snapshot per open with zero mutation surface, and exact-generation text ingress becomes ready only
after notification delivery and a same-generation first-responder postcheck. Renewed Design Review
and Security (plan) must independently PASS before Build.

## Renewed Design Review 4 — final scope and readiness check

Reviewed Architecture Security Revision 4 through SHA-256
`8c342ece6d7292c1fe68f47ffabd911e205c5f66dec0a0c0d2e18e484dca6f0d`. It matches Design
Security Revision 4 without further correction: no LAN wire/schema/codec/replication/scheduler file
or behavior changes; the local/authoritative-host editor retains bounded repair, commit recovery,
template isolation, and reopen proof; and the guest is an unmistakable non-owning static viewer of
only the existing mirror value captured at open. Escape/reopen is a feasible, truthful freshness
boundary and no copy promises live/saved/synchronized state.

The one-shot AX transaction also preserves usable ordering: logical focus and first responder are
postchecked, the canonical notification completes while keyboard/Paste/clipboard ingress remains
disabled, the same generation is postchecked again, and only then does readiness permit ordinary
input. Reentrant input cannot leak or read the clipboard, while real user events arrive after the
synchronous action returns; validated resize renewal avoids a needless refocus or duplicate
notification. The installed local/host, guest no-owner/no-write/no-new-packet, and observable
VoiceOver matrix is feasible without a release hook or protocol fixture.

**Renewed Design Review 4 verdict: PASS.** The narrowed product remains polished and usable, has zero
Sign-specific LAN scope, and makes Accessibility focus visible and operable without exposing input
before notification-ordered readiness. Security (plan) may rerun; Build remains blocked until PASS.

## Security (plan) rerun 4 — 2026-07-11

Reviewed the complete plan at SHA-256
`f09aa31019f30b202fffa517b7f9a122c76c250a48753bd940b19d6f65ad08f5`. The prior LAN blocker is
closed by removing all Sign-specific LAN schema, payload, codec, replication, scheduler, capability,
and convergence work. A guest has no text owner, edit/commit token, Paste read, fallback, outbound
packet, or upgrade-in-place path; its existing bounded block-entity source is repaired before static
draw/AX publication and never written back. AX notification ordering, one-shot capability,
reentrancy, first-responder checks, failure rollback, and resize renewal now close the prior AX
transaction finding. One readiness-coverage gap remains for ordinary non-AX focus.

### Findings

- **[HIGH] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:1684-1705,1768-1811,1815-1840` ->** Global owner resolution
  now requires an exact `TextIngressReadiness` tuple, but the plan mints/renews that tuple only after
  an AX focus transaction or from an already-ready resize. It does not authorize ordinary mouse field
  focus, keyboard-opened or button-opened implicit Recipe/authoritative-Sign/Chat owners, initial
  programmatic field focus, or application focus restoration. Those established paths would either
  become unable to type/Paste or tempt Builder to bypass the readiness gate, recreating inconsistent
  pre-notification ingress. Add one UIManager-owned `establishOrdinaryTextReadiness` path used by
  every non-AX focus/open/reactivation boundary. On the main actor it must clear old readiness,
  validate the exact current screen/generation/unique descriptor, sole intended owner and exact
  `GameView` first responder, post the required focus/value/layout notification for a genuine change
  while ingress and clipboard remain disabled, revalidate after notification, then install the exact
  tuple. Mouse handlers and screen-open callbacks may not set field focus or implicit ownership
  outside this operation; app resignation clears readiness and activation must re-establish it before
  input. Failure clears attempted focus/ownership without world fallback. Guest Sign's static
  descriptor remains ineligible. Add direct and installed tests for click-to-type/Paste, switching
  fields, initially focused Template Name, Recipe open, local/host Sign open, Chat and slash-command
  open, app resign/reactivate, ordinary focus notification reentrancy, close/replacement, resize both
  before and after readiness, and zero text/clipboard/world mutation before each operation returns.

### Review boundary and verdict

Reviewed: zero Sign-specific LAN diffs and guest authority surface; bounded one-read Sign repair;
role transitions; key/Paste/fallback exclusion; UIManager-private capability identity and reuse;
same/different-target reentrancy; first-responder and double-postchecks; notification-before-ingress;
resize renewal; all original Chat/Unicode/pasteboard/Accessibility-value/test-module limits; and the
downstream empirical gate.

Not reviewed: Builder code, runtime focus/open/reactivation integration, compiled graph, tests,
build/pipeline output, installed local/guest/VoiceOver behavior, signing, deployment, or publication.
No implementation file was edited or approved.

**Security (plan) rerun 4 verdict: FAIL.** Zero LAN scope and the AX transaction itself are now safe,
and every original finding remains closed. Build is still blocked because ordinary shipping focus and
screen-open paths have no authorized way to obtain the readiness now required for text ingress.
Architecture and Design Review must bind the single ordinary-readiness operation above, then Security
(plan) must rerun and PASS before Builder work.

## Design Security Revision 5 — ordinary focus and open readiness

Every non-Accessibility route uses one UIManager-owned
`establishOrdinaryTextReadiness(screen:generation:descriptor:cause:)` operation. Primary field
click, initially focused Template Name, Recipe popup open, authoritative Sign open, Chat or slash-
command open, reveal/reopen of a previously covered screen, and application reactivation may not set
lasting focus/implicit ownership or enable text outside this operation. The existing unforgeable AX
focus path remains separate at authorization entry but shares the same notification-before-readiness
state machine and exact readiness tuple.

### One polished activation sequence

On the main actor, the ordinary operation clears old readiness, validates the exact visible top
screen and generation, one unique enabled descriptor with finite frame, and the intended sole owner,
then makes `GameView` first responder. Before notifications, the on-screen and AX model must already
be truthful: a clicked field has its nearest Character-boundary caret and all siblings unfocused;
Template has its validated initial value and caret; Recipe has its exact empty/current query; Sign
has its repaired four lines, active line, and applicable status; Chat has its exact empty or single
`/` prefill. No value is exposed in a half-initialized state.

While ingress, Paste, and clipboard reads remain disabled, post the canonical notification sequence
for actual changes only: `.layoutChanged` when an owner descriptor newly appears/reappears, then one
`.focusedUIElementChanged` for the final owner; do not post `.valueChanged` merely because a new
descriptor has an initial value. An approved open-time Sign status announcement follows the current-
generation descriptor/focus notifications and is not repeated on reactivation. After the final
notification returns, revalidate the same top screen/generation/unique descriptor, sole owner, exact
value/focus state, and `GameView` first responder. Only success installs readiness for that exact
tuple. Idempotent click/focus requires the tuple already ready and posts nothing.

The transaction is synchronous and does not yield to the ordinary event loop. Reentrant key events
are consumed without mutation or fallback; Command-V is consumed before any pasteboard read; nothing
is buffered or replayed. Ordinary user input queued after the initiating action is handled once the
operation returns ready. Failure leaves no readiness or world fallback: a clicked/programmatic field
is visibly unfocused, a newly opened implicit Recipe/Sign/Chat owner closes without selection,
submission, commit, or status side effects, and reactivation failure preserves buffers but leaves the
screen unfocused until a new authorized attempt.

### Triggering events are neither inserted twice nor misrouted

- A primary field click is consumed once by focus/caret placement and carries no text. The next
  physical key or Paste action enters the ready field once. Clicking a different field switches once;
  clicking a non-field clears focus/readiness and may perform only that screen's intended control
  action.
- A button/world action that opens Recipe or authoritative Sign is only the open action; it is never
  replayed as editor text. The Chat-open binding is consumed as activation and does not insert its
  binding character. The slash-command binding creates the approved `/` prefill exactly once and its
  triggering slash is not delivered again. Template Name's opening action exposes its programmatic
  initial value once and requires no second click before typing.
- Reveal/reopen establishes readiness for only the still-valid preserved owner after its new
  descriptor exists. Application resignation clears readiness immediately without clearing the
  approved uncommitted buffer; reactivation re-establishes the same logical owner through this
  operation before input. If AppKit delivers an activation click inside a field, that click's final
  target/caret wins within the single transaction, so no double focus notification or second click is
  required.

Non-owner screens never receive readiness: guest Sign remains a static viewer; Recipe with popup
closed, RPG Character, and any screen with no focused field block text/Paste/world fallback exactly
as already approved. Existing `Sign text repaired`, `Line is full`, `Sign update failed`,
`Input limit reached`, and guest-authority status visibility/announcement rules are unchanged.

Direct and installed evidence covers click-to-type/Paste, field switching and non-field click,
initial Template typing, both Recipe popup owners, repaired local/host Sign, Chat and slash-command
first-character behavior, cover/reveal and close/reopen, resignation/reactivation with and without an
activation click, failure rollback, resize before/after readiness, exact notification counts/order,
and reentrant typing/Command-V with zero text, clipboard, template, or world leakage.

**Design Security Revision 5 verdict: PASS.** Every shipping ordinary focus/open/reactivation path
now reaches the same truthful, notification-ordered exact-generation readiness as AX without losing
or duplicating its initiating event, while non-owners remain incapable of text ingress. Architecture
must bind this operation, renew Design Review, and obtain Security (plan) PASS before Build.

## Architecture Security Revision 5 — ordinary text readiness

Reviewed Design Security Revision 5 at SHA-256
`250b6cc9f0ab9aa8e5409b5f374c45b7cfdfe07fbbaa485afed196cc0c164795`. This revision adds no LAN
scope and does not alter Architecture Revision 4's zero-Sign-LAN contract. It supersedes every rule
that lets a mouse handler, `initScreen`, popup toggle, Chat binding, reveal, resize, or application
callback set lasting field/implicit focus or `TextIngressReadiness` directly.

### Single ordinary authorization operation

UIManager owns the only entry point:

`establishOrdinaryTextReadiness(screen:generation:descriptor:cause:) -> Bool`

`cause` is a closed enum: `.primaryClick(point)`, `.initialOpen`, `.implicitOwnerOpen`,
`.screenReveal`, `.applicationReactivation`, `.reactivationClick(point)`, or `.resizeRenewal`.
The call captures a UIManager-private one-shot activation token containing weak exact screen
identity, screen generation, descriptor ID, cause, intended caret/active-line identity, and the
bounded pre-notification descriptor value/focus snapshot. It is non-Codable, noncopyable by API,
never logged/cached, and can be consumed only by that screen inside the current UIManager focus
transaction. The existing AX authorization remains a separate unforgeable entry capability; after
its prevalidation, AX and ordinary paths use the same focusing/notification/postcheck machinery.

Before beginning, UIManager clears old readiness and validates: exact visible top screen and
generation; exactly one matching enabled/focusable descriptor with finite clamped frame; eligible
owner kind; and the cause-specific intended owner. It enters `.focusing` before calling screen code,
so key/Paste routing is already closed. The screen consumes the token once to establish the complete
truthful model: clear siblings and focus the target; place a click caret at the nearest valid
Character boundary; preserve/initialize the approved bounded value; select the intended Recipe
query, authoritative Sign active line, or Chat input; and make the exact `GameView` first responder.
No raw descriptor-ID focus mutator remains.

Pre-notification postvalidation requires the same top screen/generation/unique descriptor, exact
captured bounded value and intended caret/active-line state, target as sole logical owner, and
`window.firstResponder === gameView`. UIManager then posts actual changes in this order while
readiness remains nil: one `.layoutChanged` if the owner descriptor newly appeared/reappeared, then
one `.focusedUIElementChanged` if logical/AppKit focus genuinely changed. Initial values do not cause
`.valueChanged`. A pending approved Sign status announcement follows those notifications and is
guarded by the screen's once-per-open announcement bit.

After the final synchronous notification returns, UIManager repeats the complete identity,
generation, descriptor, bounded value/focus, sole-owner, and exact-first-responder postcheck. Only
success installs `TextIngressReadiness(screenID,generation,descriptorID)`, retires the token/events,
returns `.idle`, and returns `true`. Idempotence requires that exact readiness tuple, exact sole
owner, descriptor, and first responder already be current; it posts nothing. Failure leaves
readiness nil and, only on the same current screen/generation, clears the attempted field/implicit
focus. A newly opened implicit owner closes without submit/commit/status side effects; reactivation
failure preserves bounded buffers but leaves them unfocused. Replacement screens are never rolled
back. `defer` guarantees token retirement and idle cleanup.

During `.focusing`, same-target requests coalesce and other targets fail silently. Every reentrant
character/key event is consumed before screen or gameplay dispatch. Command-V is consumed before
any `NSPasteboard` capture/read/decode; nothing is buffered or replayed. Owner capture succeeds only
when the current top screen/generation/unique descriptor, sole logical owner, exact `GameView` first
responder, and readiness tuple all agree. An open non-owner still blocks Paste/template/world
fallback. Focus loss, resignation, close/replacement, role change, rebuild, or overflow clears
readiness synchronously before other callbacks.

### Exact call sites and initiating-event consumption

- `Screen.onMouseDown` no longer assigns `TextField.focused`. UIManager resolves one primary hit. A
  field hit calls the operation with `.primaryClick`, consumes that click once, and uses its position
  only for caret placement. A different field switches once. A non-field click clears readiness and
  field focus before invoking only the existing button/slider/slot action; it never becomes text.
- `UIManager.open`, after `initScreen` has produced a stable descriptor generation, calls
  `.initialOpen` for Template Name and `.implicitOwnerOpen` for a newly opened Recipe popup,
  authoritative Sign, or Chat. `TemplateNameScreen.initScreen` creates its bounded initial value but
  no longer sets `focused` itself. Recipe toggle creates/refreshes query state before the call.
- `HostBridge.openChat("")` consumes the Chat binding solely as activation. `openChat("/")` creates
  the bounded `/` prefill before readiness exactly once; the physical slash is marked consumed and
  never reaches `onChar`. Failure closes Chat without running/submitting a command. The same rule
  prevents Recipe/Sign opener buttons or world clicks from being replayed into their owner.
- `UIManager.closeTop` clears covered-screen readiness before pop. After the revealed screen's new
  descriptor generation exists, it calls `.screenReveal` only for its still-valid preserved logical
  owner; otherwise it leaves no readiness. A reopened screen follows its normal open cause.
- Application resignation clears readiness immediately and records only weak screen identity,
  logical descriptor ID, and generation-independent preserved-owner intent—not an authorization.
  Reactivation without a GameView activation click calls `.applicationReactivation` after the window
  is key and descriptors are visible. If the activating event is a primary GameView field click,
  UIManager suppresses the preserved-owner attempt and routes one `.reactivationClick`; that click's
  field/caret is final, with one transaction and no duplicate notification.
- Resize clears readiness before generation increment/rebuild. While idle it invokes the same
  operation with `.resizeRenewal` for the preserved sole logical owner after the new descriptor
  exists. The operation posts `.layoutChanged`, but no focus notification when logical focus and
  first responder remained unchanged. Resize during `.focusing` invalidates the transaction and
  does not renew; a later authorized attempt is required.

Guest Sign, closed Recipe popup, RPG Character, and every screen without an eligible focused owner
never call the operation and cannot receive readiness. Existing status/value/authority rules remain
unchanged.

### Files, dependencies, and evidence

Builder changes are confined to UIManager/Screen focus and owner routing, GameView/AppDelegate mouse,
Paste and activation routing, the ordinary owner screens/popups in `ScreensM.swift`, HostBridge Chat
open routing in `main.swift`, the normal text-input support/tests, and this durable plan. No LAN,
protocol, replication, or scheduler file changes are authorized.

Dependency order is: shared transaction/token/readiness core and owner-capture denial; primary click
and non-field clearing; initial Template/Recipe/Sign/Chat open and slash consumption; reveal and app
resign/reactivate; resize renewal; Security (code), installed Design sign-off, focused/full tests,
then deploy proof. Each stage must keep ingress closed until the operation returns.

Direct tests cover click-to-type/Paste, nearest Character caret, sibling switching, non-field action,
Template initial value, both Recipe popup owners, repaired local/host Sign, Chat and `/` exactly-once
prefill/first character, opener-event non-replay, cover/reveal and close/reopen, resignation/
reactivation with and without an activation click, resize before/after readiness and during focus,
wrong first responder, notification order/count and idempotence, same/different-target reentrancy,
and failure at each validation phase. Forced key and Command-V callbacks from owner mutation and
every notification must prove zero text, clipboard-read, template, command, commit, or world leakage
