before return. Installed proof repeats the observable click/open/slash/reveal/reactivation/resize
matrix and immediate ordinary typing/Paste after success. The warning-free release build, XCTest,
457-check `pebsmoke`, security scan, pipeline, and installed app proof remain mandatory.

**Architecture Security Revision 5 verdict: PASS.** Every ordinary shipping owner now obtains the
same exact-generation, notification-ordered UIManager readiness without duplicating its initiating
event, while owner capture, clipboard access, fallback, and non-owner screens remain fail-closed.
Renewed Design Review and Security (plan) must independently PASS before Build.

## Renewed Design Review 5 — ordinary readiness sign-off

Reviewed Architecture Security Revision 5 through SHA-256
`31c356361c27fc9263f82a73d3a1a891b109235ddb5d6bbb066f2dd0cf4824af`. Primary click, initially
focused Template, authoritative Sign/Chat/slash open, cover/reveal, reactivation, and resize all
produce truthful focus/value state, complete required notifications before ingress, preserve the
initiating event exactly once, and fail without text/clipboard/world leakage. AX and status behavior
remain intact; non-owner screens remain unready; no LAN or unrelated interaction scope is added.

One call-site clarification is binding: Inventory/Crafting must invoke `.implicitOwnerOpen` when the
Recipe popup toggle actually opens and its descriptor/query exists, not from `UIManager.open` while
the popup is closed. Closing the popup clears readiness before removing the descriptor. This preserves
the approved implicit search ownership without making the surrounding Inventory screen a text owner.

**Renewed Design Review 5 verdict: PASS.** With that clarification binding, every ordinary activation
path and first-input ordering is complete, polished, testable, and free of product overreach.
Security (plan) must PASS before Build.

## Security (plan) rerun 5 — 2026-07-11

Reviewed the complete plan at SHA-256
`47df1237d7fb42bceb12c3cfe059a5f487b605ecc648a6f33062c113e757ccd5` against every prior finding
and the complete ordinary-readiness matrix.

### Findings

None. The prior ordinary-focus blocker is closed by one UIManager-owned, one-shot operation covering
primary clicks, initially focused Template Name, actual Recipe-popup open/close, authoritative Sign,
Chat/slash open, screen reveal, application resignation/reactivation including an activation click,
and resize renewal. Each path installs truthful bounded state before notification, denies reentrant
key/Paste and clipboard access while readiness is nil, performs exact screen/generation/descriptor/
value/owner/first-responder postchecks after the final notification, and only then enables ingress.
Initiating events cannot be replayed into text or world actions; failure and non-owner paths remain
closed and replacement screens are never rolled back.

The complete prior closure set also remains consistent: finite Chat/history and per-owner byte/
Character/scalar limits; streaming Recipe/Sign handling without over-cap intermediates; private
owner-captured and predecode-bounded Paste; exactly four repaired Sign lines before draw/AX/commit;
read-only generation-bound Accessibility values; private, one-shot, non-reentrant AX focus; normal
non-product test-module topology plus release graph/symbol scans; authoritative local/host Sign commit
with stale-token rejection; and a guest static viewer with no owner, edit, Paste read, commit, send,
or in-place authority upgrade. All Sign-specific LAN field/schema/codec/transport/replication/
scheduler/capability work is explicitly absent and machine-scanned.

### Review boundary and verdict

Reviewed: the entire supersession chain, all text owners and activation causes, transition-time input
and clipboard denial, notification ordering and double postvalidation, first-responder identity,
reentrancy/idempotency, app activation and resize, Recipe toggle timing, role changes, Sign repair and
authority, zero LAN scope, Unicode/resource limits, test exposure, and required direct/installed/full-
gate evidence.

Not reviewed: Builder code, compiled package/binary graph, actual tests or verifier behavior,
runtime/VoiceOver/AppKit behavior, build/pipeline output, installed local/guest proof, signing,
deployment, or publication. Those remain downstream empirical gates. No implementation file was
edited or approved.

**Security (plan) rerun 5 verdict: PASS.** No unresolved plan-level security condition remains. Build
may begin in the documented dependency order; any material change to limits, ownership/readiness,
Paste, Accessibility, Sign authority/repair, test topology, or zero-LAN scope invalidates this PASS
and requires Security (plan) to rerun before Security (code), Test, and Deploy.

## Security (code) — 2026-07-11

Reviewed the actual Builder implementation on disk against the complete approved plan at SHA-256
`179194477166cba070e530cb1eaf0e63e1646d777c564672cf5856c0b85d07fe`. Review was implementation-
first: source and package graph were inspected before running the submitted focused/security/artifact
gates.

### Findings

- **[HIGH] `Sources/Pebble/UIManagerM.swift:236-299,596-617`; `Sources/Pebble/AppInputRouterM.swift:33-40,151-170`; `Sources/Pebble/TextEntryAccessibilityM.swift:75-101` ->** The approved readiness boundary is
  absent. There is no `TextIngressReadiness`, ordinary activation transaction, one-shot
  `TextFocusAuthorization`, focusing/reentrancy state, or notification-ordered double postcheck.
  Mouse and AX call raw `focusTextDescriptor(id:)`/set `focused` directly; owner capture trusts only a
  nonzero generation and `ownsTextInput`; mapped and unmapped key paths call `screen.insertText`
  directly. Reentrant focus callbacks, stale first-responder state, app resign/reactivate, and
  pre-notification keys/Paste therefore bypass the approved security gate. Implement the final
  UIManager-private capability/readiness state machine exactly: all click/initial/Recipe-toggle/Sign/
  Chat/reveal/reactivation/resize and AX paths must clear readiness, enter `.focusing`, deny key/Paste
  before clipboard access, establish truthful state and exact `GameView` first responder, notify,
  postvalidate again, then install the exact screen/generation/descriptor tuple. Remove raw ID focus
  mutation and require readiness in owner capture and every mapped/unmapped insertion path.
- **[HIGH] `Sources/PebbleTextInput/PebbleTextInput.swift:122-129`; `Sources/Pebble/AppInputRouterM.swift:33-40,166-170` ->** `validPrefix` materializes the entire valid prefix with no destination cap,
  and mapped input computes it twice. Paste is raw-capped at 65,536 bytes, but untrusted
  `NSEvent.characters` has no corresponding bound, so an input source can force duplicate large
  allocations/scans before TextField/Chat rejects the result or Sign/Recipe reaches its prefix/suffix
  limit. Replace this helper at ingress with one streaming owner-aware operation: TextField/Chat use
  a temporary builder no larger than remaining Character/byte caps and commit atomically; Sign/Recipe
  stream only their bounded prefix/suffix. Validate once, only after readiness, without constructing
  an over-cap filtered `String`. Add adversarial very-large mapped/unmapped `characters`, first-invalid,
  and exact/one-over allocation/work tests.
- **[HIGH] `Sources/Pebble/ScreensM.swift:2070-2087,2098-2109,2216-2229` ->** Sign commit has no immutable
  world/session generation, dimension, expected Sign block identity, or serial commit API; it checks
  only current block-entity object identity/absence and mutates the entity directly. In the nil-entity
  case, replacing/loading the world while the screen is open can still satisfy `current == nil` and
  create a Sign at the same coordinates in the replacement world. Replace the strong raw entity
  reference with the approved `SignCommitToken` and one `GameCore.commitSignEdit` operation that
  revalidates authority, world/session generation, dimension/position, Sign block identity and exact
  entity identity/absence on the serial world/save boundary, atomically writes exactly four bounded
  lines, marks persistence dirty, and otherwise changes nothing. Test world load/replacement,
  dimension/target/entity replacement, authority loss, nil-entity ABA, retry/discard, and reopen.
- **[MEDIUM] `Sources/Pebble/TextEntryAccessibilityM.swift:19-40,52-123` ->** The bridge does not
  explicitly expose values as non-settable, publishes `selectedTextRange` while claiming no selection
  API, never clamps the converted frame to the current macOS screen, and posts only a focus
  notification—no generation-bound layout/value/status notifications exist for open, edit, repair,
  resize, or close. Make value and selection mutation explicitly non-settable through the AppKit
  accessibility override, expose only the approved insertion range, reject overflow/stale ranges,
  intersect finite converted frames with the owning screen, and emit the exact generic notifications/
  announcements only after the readiness/generation postcheck. Add retained-element same-ID reopen,
  non-BMP/separator range, off-screen/nonfinite frame, read-only mutation attempt, repair/limit status,
  resize and notification-order tests.
- **[MEDIUM] `Sources/PebbleTextInput/PebbleTextInput.swift:222-247,255-281`; `Sources/Pebble/ScreensM.swift:2087-2089,2135-2153` ->** `PebbleSignLine(text:)` is package-visible and accepts arbitrary
  unvalidated/unbounded strings, so the claimed unrepresentable-invalid four-line model is not
  enforced. Repair also receives a stateless width closure, and the app supplies the sum of
  `textWidth(String(character))`; that is not the renderer's stateful resource-pack/formatting cursor
  and can diverge for formatting sequences or rounded pack advances. Hide raw line construction and
  mutation behind validated bounded factories/streaming append/delete, and use one production width
  cursor shared with draw/measurement that carries formatting state and never receives an over-byte
  candidate. Test formatting pairs, fractional/pack advances, fallback graphemes, invalid raw
  construction, and width/byte boundaries.
- **[HIGH] `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift:14-97`; `Tests/PebbleTextInputTests/PebbleTextInputTests.swift:4-101`; `scripts/security-scan.sh:10-18`; `scripts/verify-pebble-storage-release-surface.sh:203-227` ->** The verifier does not verify the
  verifier-critical behavior. The focused command passed 19 tests, but the seven app tests are string
  presence/absence checks and neither suite instantiates UIManager/router/AX focus/Paste/Sign commit.
  Consequently `scripts/security-scan.sh` and the pinned release verifier both passed even though the
  required readiness/capability types and all direct behavioral tests are absent. Move the App-local
  transaction/router/descriptor/commit kernels into a normal non-product testable module (or add an
  equivalent supported App behavior target) without a release hook/public Core API, add the complete
  direct/property/reentrancy matrix from the plan, and make the machine gate fail when raw ID focus,
  direct pre-readiness insertion, or missing readiness checks return. Artifact hashes may be renewed
  only after those behavioral gates pass.

### Evidence and review boundary

Fresh evidence: the selected text-entry command executed **19 tests, 0 failures**; `bash
scripts/security-scan.sh` exited 0; `bash scripts/verify-pebble-storage-release-surface.sh` exited 0;
and `swift package describe --type json` showed only the `Pebble` and `pebsmoke` products, with
`PebbleTextInput` remaining a normal non-product target. Those green results establish the pure
validator/package/artifact facts only; the findings above demonstrate that they do not establish the
approved App behavior.

Reviewed: `Package.swift`, `PebbleTextInput`, its tests, TextField/Screen/UIManager ownership and
resize, source-aware App router, AppDelegate Paste/activation, text Accessibility bridge, every custom
Recipe/Sign/Chat owner, Crafting typeahead, field call sites/IDs, text-entry source tests, security scan,
release verifier pins, README text-entry claims, and source/binary absence of Sign-specific LAN symbols.
Confirmed working at source level: finite scalar/Character/byte caps, raw Paste predecode cap and
postread screen-generation check, Chat/history limits, guest Sign read-only guards, menu-before-world
Paste precedence, normal non-product module topology, and zero Sign-specific LAN symbols.

Not reviewed or approved: unrelated preexisting Track A/B RPG, storage, LAN V6, controller, font, or
other dirty-tree changes; full XCTest/pipeline/pebsmoke; runtime/VoiceOver/installed UI; signing,
deployment, git publication, or GitHub state. Downstream Design Sign-off/Test/Deploy evidence is
invalid until Security (code) passes after fixes.

**Security (code) verdict: FAIL.** The central readiness/capability contract and its executable tests
were not implemented, large `NSEvent.characters` can be amplified before destination bounds, and Sign
commit is stale-world unsafe. The passing submitted gates are insufficient and must not be used to
authorize build completion, installed sign-off, deployment, commit, or push. Builder owns the exact
fixes above; material fixes invalidate this review and require a fresh Security (code) pass before
Design Sign-off and Test.

## Builder remediation evidence after Security (code) FAIL — 2026-07-11

Remediated every finding from the preceding failed Security (code) review without installing,
deploying, committing, pushing, or adding any Sign-specific LAN wire/schema/codec/transport/
replication/scheduler surface. The earlier Builder PASS is superseded by this evidence.

Implemented one UIManager-owned exact-generation readiness transaction for ordinary and
Accessibility activation, with a privately minted one-shot focus capability, exact `GameView`
first-responder checks, notification-before-readiness ordering, double owner/value/generation
postvalidation, same-target coalescing, different-target rejection, and fail-closed key/Paste
routing before clipboard access. All field and implicit Recipe/Sign/Chat/Template activations now
enter through that operation. Failed ordinary activation clears field focus, closes a newly opened
implicit owner, or preserves an existing bounded buffer while publishing truthful unfocused state.
Ready same-field clicks still move the Character-boundary caret without reposting focus.

Replaced materialized prefix filtering with one-pass destination-bounded insertion and capped both
mapped and unmapped `NSEvent.characters` paths at 65,536 UTF-8 bytes. Accessibility values and
selection ranges are explicitly non-settable, UTF-16 insertion offsets are checked, frames are
finite and clamped to the owning macOS screen, and layout/focus/value/status notifications remain
generation/readiness guarded. Sign lines are privately constructible only through validated
four-line repair/edit operations using the same stateful active-pack fractional width cursor as
production measurement. Sign commit now uses an immutable world-entry/dimension/position/block/
entity token and a GameCore commit operation that rejects replacement, ABA, authority loss, and
replay before writing exactly four bounded lines.

## Security (code) rerun — 2026-07-11

Reviewed the actual remediated implementation against the complete plan/evidence at SHA-256
`83c0c7cb9a6fc9ea174c2d33766ba09138ef6aa53aaf6f8f115d7c6da19a9ac1`. The six prior findings were
reinspected from source and artifacts rather than accepted from Builder evidence.

### Findings

- **[MEDIUM] `Sources/Pebble/UIManagerM.swift:566-580` ->** Layout/focus notifications are guarded,
  but `textIngressMachine.complete` installs readiness before the pending Sign/Chat status
  announcement is posted. A notification observer can therefore reenter keyboard/Paste while the
  open-time notification sequence is still executing, contrary to the final transition contract.
  Consume and post the approved generic status announcement while phase is still `.focusing` and
  readiness is nil, then mark the complete notification sequence delivered, repeat the full
  screen/generation/descriptor/value/owner/first-responder postcheck, and only then complete
  readiness. Add a callback test that attempts mapped/unmapped text and Command-V from the status
  announcement and proves zero text, clipboard read, fallback, or buffered replay.
- **[MEDIUM] `Sources/Pebble/AppInputRouterM.swift:33-40,173-176`; `Sources/PebbleTextInput/PebbleTextInput.swift:117-150` ->** The materialized-prefix allocation is removed, but each event first computes
  `characters.utf8.count` over the full untrusted String and then scans it again in the owner, so an
  oversized mapped/unmapped event still performs unbounded full-input work rather than one bounded
  pass. Also, TextField and Chat typing now use `insertValidPrefixAtomically`, accepting a valid prefix
  before an invalid Character; the approved cause-specific contract requires whole-proposal atomic
  typing and prefix semantics only for Paste (Sign/Recipe retain streaming rules). Replace the count
  with a nonallocating prefix byte check that stops at byte 65,537, then route typed TextField/Chat to
  whole-proposal atomic validation while Paste uses bounded valid-prefix insertion. Add million-byte
  router tests with a counting sequence plus valid-prefix-then-invalid typed-versus-Paste tests.
- **[MEDIUM] `Sources/PebbleCore/Game/GameCore.swift:625-660`; `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/SignCommitTokenTests.swift:29-74` ->** The token now covers authority, world entry, world identity, dimension,
  block and entity/absence, but the validate-then-write method is not `@MainActor` or otherwise bound
  to the serial world mutation path. It also remains reusable after a successful edit to an existing
  entity because identity does not change; the replay test covers only the nil-entity case. Isolate
  capture/commit to the main actor (or the existing serial world boundary), add a token state consumed
  only on successful commit so failed edits remain retryable, and test concurrent world/authority
  replacement plus successful existing-entity replay rejection.
- **[HIGH] `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift:14-129`; `Tests/PebbleTextInputTests/PebbleTextInputTests.swift:99-190`; `scripts/security-scan.sh:18-52` ->** The pure state machine and Core Sign
  token now have executable tests, but UIManager/AppInputRouter/TextEntryAccessibility remain covered
  only by source-string assertions. No test executes the actual notification callbacks, ordinary/AX
  adapter, first-responder transitions, Pasteboard read suppression, recipe-toggle/reveal/reactivate/
  resize call sites, or retained Accessibility element. This is why all submitted gates pass despite
  the three concrete adapter violations above. Move the App transaction/router/AX adapter behind a
  normal testable support target or add an equivalent supported App behavior test target; execute the
  full callback/call-site matrix and make the machine gate depend on those tests, not symbol presence.
  Renew release pins only after the behavioral gate is green.

### Evidence and review boundary

Fresh rerun evidence: the selected remediation command executed **31 tests, 0 failures** (15 pure
text-input, 3 App-router source, 2 Recipe, 3 real GameCore Sign-token, and 8 text-entry source tests);
`bash scripts/security-scan.sh` passed including the 127-file SQLite scan; and
`bash scripts/verify-pebble-storage-release-surface.sh` passed against the pinned release artifacts.
Package/product topology, test-symbol denial, and zero Sign-specific LAN field/kind/codec/transport/
replication/scheduler symbols were independently rechecked and remain clean.

Reviewed: all six prior findings; pure bounds/Unicode/readiness/geometry/Sign cursor kernels; every
ordinary/AX readiness call site and ingress route; AppDelegate Paste and activation; Sign repair,
guest viewer and Core commit token; AX read-only/range/frame code; custom Recipe/Sign/Chat behavior;
package graph, source tests, security scan, release verifier and zero-LAN scans. Confirmed closed:
private one-shot focus construction/claim, exact-generation readiness and first responder checks,
pre-readiness key/Paste denial, owner-captured predecode-bounded Paste with postread generation check,
bounded destination allocation, stale world/dimension/block/entity Sign checks, non-settable AX
value/range, finite frame clamp, invariant-only Sign lines, stateful production width cursor, normal
non-product target and artifact test-seam denial.

Not reviewed: unrelated preexisting Track A/B RPG/storage/LAN/controller/font changes; full suite and
pebsmoke were not rerun by this reviewer because the fresh focused review already found blockers;
installed UI/VoiceOver/two-machine behavior, pipeline, signing, deployment, commit, push, and GitHub
state remain downstream and unauthorized. No implementation file was edited.

**Security (code) rerun verdict: FAIL.** Most prior implementation defects are substantively closed,
but notification-ordered readiness, bounded/cause-correct event ingress, serialized one-shot Sign
commit, and executable App-adapter verification remain incomplete. Design Sign-off, Test, pipeline,
Deploy, commit, and push stay blocked until Builder fixes these findings and a fresh Security (code)
rerun passes.

## Builder remediation evidence after Security (code) rerun FAIL — 2026-07-11

Remediated all four rerun findings without installing, deploying, committing, pushing, or changing
any Sign-specific LAN field/kind/schema/codec/transport/replication/scheduler surface.

- UIManager now delegates its real focus sequence to the normal non-product
  `PebbleTextFocusTransactionAdapter`. Layout, focus, and the consumed one-shot Sign/Chat status
  announcement all publish while the adapter remains `.focusing` with readiness absent. Only after
  the complete synchronous notification sequence returns does the adapter mark delivery, repeat the
  exact postvalidation, and install readiness. Notification-time same-target reentry coalesces,
  other-target reentry rejects, and mapped text, unmapped text, Paste capture/read, and fallback all
  remain blocked with no replay.
- Both AppKit character paths use the executable `PebbleTextEventIngressAdapter` and a nonallocating
  byte-sequence envelope that stops at byte 65,537. No `characters.utf8.count` full scan remains.
  TextField and Chat typing use a single-pass whole-proposal atomic result, rejecting invalid or
  over-cap proposals unchanged; Paste uses one bounded valid-prefix commit. Recipe and Sign retain
  their approved streaming suffix/prefix behavior. Paste side effects for Anvil, Creative Search,
  and Template Name remain refreshed after the cause split.
- AppDelegate Paste uses the executable `PebbleTextPasteIngressAdapter`: blocked readiness denies
  owner capture and clipboard access, capture precedes read, and exact owner revalidation precedes
  dispatch. Text Accessibility retained-element validation uses the shared executable screen/
  generation/descriptor identity adapter in both focus and focus-state paths.
- Sign capture and commit are `@MainActor`; the App call sites explicitly enter that isolation.
  Tokens have private successful-consumption state. Failed validation is retryable after restoration,
  while successful nil-entity and existing-entity commits consume exactly once. MainActor task-group
  contention produces exactly one success, and queued world replacement cannot write stale text.
- `scripts/security-scan.sh` now fails on full `NSEvent` byte counts, missing mapped/unmapped event
  adapters, missing Paste adapter, non-atomic TextField/Chat typing, lost MainActor annotations,
  reusable Sign tokens, raw focus, or Sign LAN scope. It also executes the production adapter and
  Sign concurrency tests before the 127-file SQLite/source scan; symbol presence alone cannot pass.

## Security (code) second rerun — 2026-07-11

Independently reviewed the complete implementation and Builder evidence at SHA-256
`874bdbbca7e1ad9c09218eac96eab8ca19bb1f0b3a3157f58b7b20ed01b83bea`. The latest four findings and
all six original Security (code) findings were rechecked against the production source, executable
tests, normal package graph, release objects, and shipping binaries rather than accepted from the
Builder narrative.

### Findings

None.

### Closure evidence

- The shipping `UIManager` invokes `PebbleTextFocusTransactionAdapter.perform` for the complete
  owner/responder/notification transaction. Layout, focus, and the consumed one-shot status
  announcement execute while the adapter is still `.focusing`; readiness is installed only after
  notification return, repeated owner/value/generation/responder postvalidation, and delivery
  marking. The executable reentrancy test proved same-owner coalescing, different-owner rejection,
  mapped and unmapped text suppression, Paste suppression before clipboard read, no fallback, no
  mutation, and no buffered replay during the status notification.
- Both shipping mapped and unmapped AppKit character paths call
  `PebbleTextEventIngressAdapter.route`. Its envelope iterates `String.UTF8View` and returns at byte
  65,537 rather than computing the complete count. The executable counting-sequence test observed
  exactly 65,537 reads for a million-byte rejection and 65,536 for an exact-limit acceptance.
  `TextField` and Chat typed proposals use whole-proposal atomic insertion; their Paste paths use
  bounded valid-prefix insertion; Recipe and Sign preserve destination-bounded streaming behavior.
- AppDelegate Paste calls `PebbleTextPasteIngressAdapter.route`, which checks readiness before owner
  capture or pasteboard access, captures before read, and revalidates the exact owner before
  dispatch. The real Accessibility bridge calls the shared exact screen/generation/descriptor
  identity predicate from retained-element focus and focus-state paths, keeps value and selected
  range non-settable, validates UTF-16 insertion offsets, and rejects or clamps invalid/off-screen
  geometry.
- Sign capture and commit are both `@MainActor`. The token binds authority, world entry, world
  identity, world ID, dimension, position, block value, and exact entity-or-absence identity; it is
  consumed only after every validation succeeds. Executable GameCore tests proved nil-entity ABA
  rejection, block/entity/world replacement rejection, successful nil and existing-entity replay
  rejection, failed-validation retry, 32-contender one-success serialization, and queued world
  replacement rejection.
- The package still exposes only the `Pebble` and `pebsmoke` products. `PebbleTextInput` is a normal
  non-product support target used by the shipping executable and the behavioral tests. The release
  verifier accepted the pinned `PebbleTextInput.o`, `PebbleCore.o`, `PebbleStorage.o`, `Pebble`, and
  `pebsmoke` artifacts and found no test/probe hooks. Independent source scans found no Sign-specific
  LAN field, kind, schema, codec, transport, replication, scheduler, status, or result surface.

Fresh reviewer commands and results:

1. `swift test --filter 'PebbleTextInputTests|SignCommitTokenTests|TextEntrySourceTests|AppInputRouterSourceTests|CraftingRecipeTypeaheadTextEntryTests'`
   — **40/40 passed**, 0 failures: 21 executable production text-adapter/bounds/Unicode/AX tests,
   6 executable MainActor Sign-token tests, and 13 supplemental App/source/Recipe tests.
2. `bash scripts/security-scan.sh` — **PASS**, including the executable production adapter/Sign
   behavior gate and SQLite boundary scan over **127 production Swift files**.
3. `bash scripts/verify-pebble-storage-release-surface.sh` — **PASS** against the exact pinned release
   objects and products.
4. `swift package describe --type json`, zero-Sign-LAN scans, package/product review, and worktree
   review — **PASS** for the reviewed boundary. Builder's already-recorded full **1,004/1,004**
   XCTest and **457/457** golden evidence was inspected but not redundantly rerun by this reviewer.

### Review boundary

Reviewed: all ten prior Security (code) findings; focus capability mint/claim and complete

pins/test-seam denial; and zero Sign-specific LAN scope.

Not reviewed: unrelated preexisting Track A/B RPG/storage/LAN/controller/font implementation;
installed visual/VoiceOver/two-machine behavior; pipeline, signing, deployment, commit, push, and
GitHub state. Those remain downstream gates. No implementation source was edited by this reviewer.

**Security (code) second-rerun verdict: PASS.** The implementation satisfies the reviewed security
boundary and closes every prior Security (code) finding with production wiring and executable
evidence. Design Sign-off and Test may proceed; this PASS does not itself authorize or claim
pipeline, deployment, commit, push, or GitHub completion.

## Installed Design Sign-off — 2026-07-11

Inspected the ordinary signed Metal app at `/Applications/Pebble.app`, not a source model, UI
harness, `PEBBLE_OPEN_SCREEN`, or other release probe. The installed executable SHA-256 was exactly
`46e2d286312d2f2ccd7bb881007068a5856c33f82af6143f8eec37b696283f47`; `codesign --verify
--deep --strict` passed. LaunchServices started that bundle with only disposable
`CFFIXED_USER_HOME`/`HOME` at
`/tmp/pebble-text-entry-design-signoff-20260711/profile`. Evidence is retained under
`/tmp/pebble-text-entry-design-signoff-20260711`.

### Blocking real-target findings

1. **Ordinary keyboard text/editing does not work on the primary New World field.** On Create New
   World, a primary click produced the correct white focused outline and blinking caret. System
   Events then reported `frontmost=true`, one Pebble window, and Pebble as the active application.
   Nevertheless, `cliclick` text `Alpha`, System Events `keystroke "Alpha"`, explicit A/B/C/D key
   codes, five Left-arrow events, and Backspace caused no text or caret edit. The field remained
   empty after the typing trials. In contrast, menu Command-V inserted the nonsensitive exact value
   `PasteProbe`; after the failed Left/Backspace sequence, a second Paste appended `-MID-` at the end.
   This proves the visible field owner and Paste path were active while ordinary keyDown text,
   navigation, and deletion were unusable. Screens `09`, `11`, `13`, `15`, `16`, and `17` preserve
   the before/after result.
2. **Installed text Accessibility is absent on the same screen.** With the app frontmost and the
   field focused/populated, System Events' complete window traversal exposed only the GameView
   `AXGroup` (`Pebble RPG menus and actions`) and window-title `AXStaticText`; the group had no
   published World Name or Seed text children. Labels, exact values, focus, insertion range, and
   read-only behavior therefore could not be inspected because the required installed elements were
   not present. `logs/window-ax-report.txt` records the traversal.

The captured window was `(0, 33)` at **1728 x 1084 logical points**. Full-screen capture was **3456
x 2234 physical pixels**, establishing backing scale 2 and a 1728 x 1117 display-point surface; the
33-point title/menu offset yields the 1728 x 1084 content window. The initial title, practical LAN
field layout, empty Singleplayer screen, default New World layout, focused field, successful Paste,
and failed key-edit states were captured with SHA-256 manifests. No new Pebble crash report appeared
(0 before, 0 after, 0 delta), app stderr was empty, and the app quit cleanly after capture.

### Unclosed installed conditions

The blocking primary surface invalidates downstream empirical evidence, so the matrix stopped rather
than inferring success. Exact created world name/seed, typed Unicode/fallback, long clipping/caret,
resize, the remaining LAN/AI-model/Anvil/Creative/Template fields, Recipe, Sign, Chat/slash, guest
Sign, Standard/High Contrast, installed VoiceOver semantics, and installed RPG glyph/layout checks
were **not approved**. The LAN screen was visually captured only; it was not accepted as text-entry
proof.

**Installed Design Sign-off verdict: FAIL.** A newly built and deployed bundle must restore ordinary
typed characters, Left/Right, and Backspace on the real New World field and publish the installed
World Name/Seed Accessibility elements. Those material fixes invalidate this installed hash and the
affected Security/Test gates. After renewed review and deployment, rerun the complete installed
matrix; Test, pipeline, deployment completion, commit, and push remain blocked by this FAIL.

## Architecture Runtime Failure Revision — real AppKit event and AX publication

Diagnosed the installed failure at plan SHA-256
`b5d66bd9c5b32fd69abe903ff09f11fdb2d3a3ae157862d8d435221b4b4c45cd` using the signed
`/Applications/Pebble.app`, its retained evidence, and read-only LLDB/AX inspection. No
implementation file was changed and no LAN scope is added.

### Verified root causes

1. **Keyboard events reach the correct responder and then abort in fingerprint construction.** An
   LLDB breakpoint proved trusted synthetic `A` and `b` events enter `@objc GameView.keyDown`; the
   key window's `firstResponder` was the exact same `GameView`. The next stack frame was
   `AppInputRouter.makePhysicalEvent`. Its read of `NSEvent.eventNumber` raised AppKit's
   `NSInternalInconsistencyException` with `Invalid message sent to event` for each KeyDown.
   `eventNumber` is not a valid keyboard-event accessor. AppKit aborted that event's responder call,
   so routing never reached readiness, `Screen.onKeyEvent`, or text insertion. Every mapped letter,
   arrow, Backspace/Delete, and modifier edge crosses the same unsafe fingerprint code. Menu
   Command-V invokes `AppDelegate.pasteText` instead and therefore bypasses the exception, exactly
   explaining the observed split. This is not a focus, readiness, automation, or pure-router defect.
2. **The text AX model exists but is never committed to AppKit's retained child state.** On Create
   New World, a direct main-thread invocation of `GameView.accessibilityChildren()` returned two
   `TextEntryAccessibilityElement` objects. The real AX server/System Events still returned zero
   children, and an LLDB breakpoint showed its query did not re-enter that override. The current
   getter manufactures a fresh dynamic array while AppKit continues serving the cached empty child
   property established on earlier screens. Layout notifications alone do not replace that retained
   tree. Consequently the elements' otherwise-correct roles/labels/values cannot be reached by an
   installed assistive client.

### Required runtime edits

In `Sources/Pebble/AppInputRouterM.swift`, eliminate every keyboard/flags path read of
`NSEvent.eventNumber`; add a source scan forbidding `.eventNumber` in `AppInputRouter`. Construct
`AppKeyEventFingerprint` only from keyboard-valid data already present: key code, monotonic
timestamp, window number, normalized modifiers, repeat bit, and origin. Keep `eventNumber` as a
constant zero compatibility sentinel if changing the Core value type would add needless scope, or
rename/remove it in Core and all tests; never substitute another mouse-only accessor. The checked
routing serial remains the per-delivery identity. Existing F11/capture `performKeyEquivalent`
behavior remains: ordinary keys return unconsumed there and route once through `keyDown`, while an
actually handled equivalent is not replayed. The same safe factory must serve `flagsChanged`.

In `Sources/Pebble/TextEntryAccessibilityM.swift`, replace the computed fresh `children` bridge with
a retained commit boundary:

- `commit(screen:descriptors:)` validates one current screen/generation and unique descriptor IDs,
  creates generation-bound elements on structural change, and otherwise refreshes the retained
  element's frame, label, value, help, enabled/focused state, character count, and selected range in
  place. Old-generation retained elements remain fail-closed.
- `invalidate()` clears retained text elements before a screen/generation replacement.
- The bridge exposes the retained root array and exact current element for a descriptor/token; it
  never creates an element from an AX query.

In `Sources/Pebble/main.swift`, remove `GameView`'s dynamic `accessibilityChildren()` override.
Add one main-actor `publishAccessibilityChildren()` that calls AppKit's
`setAccessibilityChildren(textEntryAccessibilityBridge.children +
rpgAccessibilityBridge.children)`. Call it after every text or RPG bridge commit/invalidation and
before any `.layoutChanged`, `.focusedUIElementChanged`, or `.valueChanged` post. Preserve
`GameView`'s group role/label. Focus/value notifications target the exact retained child when one
exists; container layout and announcements still target the group.

In `Sources/Pebble/UIManagerM.swift`, add text commit/invalidate callbacks parallel to the existing
RPG callbacks. Commit the complete current descriptor set after open/reveal/resize, after owner
mutation but before readiness notifications, and after accepted value/caret changes before
`.valueChanged`. Invalidate before close/replacement/generation retirement. AppDelegate wires those
callbacks to `GameView` before opening the first screen. Publication never mints readiness, changes
text, reads the clipboard, or weakens the existing screen/generation/descriptor postchecks.
`RPGAccessibilityBridge.commit/invalidate` must also request the combined child republish before its
notifications so one bridge cannot erase or stale the other.

### Non-false-green executable integration gate

Pure adapter XCTest and source-string scans remain supplemental; neither can close this failure.
Add `scripts/appkit-text-entry-integration.sh` plus a non-shipping Swift driver under
`Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/`. The script must build the release executable, assemble and ad-hoc
sign a temporary `Pebble.app` with the same packaging function used by install, and launch that
bundle normally with a disposable home. It must not use `PEBBLE_OPEN_SCREEN`, call UIManager/router
methods, import the executable as a model, or read source to decide PASS.

The driver requires an interactive trusted macOS session, records the launched PID and executable
hash, drives the real canvas Title -> Singleplayer -> Create New World path with CGEvent mouse input,
and verifies the real window/group through `AXUIElement`. It then:

1. clicks World Name and asserts the app's key window is active through AX focus state;
2. sends real CGEvent keyDown/up sequences for mapped letters, Left/Right, and Backspace, then polls
   the published AX value and UTF-16 insertion range for the exact resulting middle edit;
3. executes menu Command-V and verifies one bounded insertion, preserving the separate Paste path;
4. obtains `AXChildren` from the real GameView group and requires unique World Name and Seed text
   fields with exact labels/values/focused state/ranges, finite on-screen frames, and non-settable
   value/selection attributes; and
5. captures process stderr plus unified-log entries for that PID and fails on
   `NSInternalInconsistencyException`, `Invalid message sent to event`, crash, timeout, missing child,
   or unchanged/incorrect value.

The gate checks that the tested PID belongs to the freshly packaged bundle and that its executable
hash equals `.build/release/Pebble`; an adapter-only runner, manually invoked bridge getter, source
assertion, screenshot-only caret, or menu-Paste-only success cannot satisfy it. Missing Accessibility/
Input Monitoring trust fails with an explicit prerequisite rather than skipping or passing. Run this
gate after the warning-free release build and before pipeline deployment, and add it to the
machine-enforced security/pipeline path.

Focused regression also covers safe fingerprint uniqueness/dedup without `eventNumber`, F11 and
binding capture, physical modifier edges, reentrant readiness denial, AX retained-element reuse on
value/caret change, old-generation rejection, empty/text/RPG/combined child transitions, and
notification order. These do not replace the real-window gate.

### Renewed installed acceptance

After Security (code) and the full test/pipeline gates pass, install a newly built bundle and rerun
the complete Design Sign-off matrix. The primary blocking proof requires focused New World typing,
middle caret movement and deletion, Paste, resize/reactivation, and installed AX World Name/Seed
children through the external AX API. Continue through all previously unapproved TextField,
Recipe, local/host Sign, Chat/slash, guest-viewer, contrast/size, VoiceOver, and RPG glyph/layout
states only after that primary proof is green. Record executable hash, signing result, exact commands,
AX report, screenshots, exception/crash delta, and exit status. Preserve all bounded ingress,
notification-before-readiness, read-only AX value/range, clipboard-before-read denial, generation,
Sign authority, and zero-Sign-LAN invariants.

### Threat/risk-to-test map

- Unsafe keyboard-only `NSEvent` selectors can abort input without crashing: source ban plus real
  KeyDown/flags integration and exception-log denial.
- Removing `eventNumber` could weaken duplicate suppression or replay keys: physical F11/capture,
  repeat, equivalent-to-keyDown, modifier-edge, and unique-event tests prove exactly-once routing.
- Retained AX children can expose stale/private text or authorize obsolete focus: exact generation
  invalidation, old-reference focus denial, read-only value/range, and close/replacement tests.
- Republish/notification reentry could enable premature ingress or clipboard access: forced AX
  callback typing/Command-V tests retain the two-postcheck readiness boundary and zero replay.
- Combining text and RPG children could erase or duplicate either tree: empty/text-only/RPG-only/
  combined transition tests plus installed external `AXChildren` counts and unique IDs.
- A permissive test hook could false-green or escape in release: the black-box driver remains outside
  products, launches the hashed signed app, and release symbol/environment scans deny probe seams.
- Evidence could leak entered/clipboard content: use fixed nonsensitive sentinels and record only
  expected equality, counts, hashes, roles, and generic errors. No LAN file or behavior changes.

### Conditions for Builder

1. Edit only the named App input, text/RPG Accessibility publication, UI callback/wiring, packaging-
   helper, and non-shipping integration-test surfaces; do not modify LAN protocol/transport/schema.
2. No production keyboard path may evaluate `NSEvent.eventNumber`; no `do/catch`, exception swallow,
   menu-only fallback, or readiness bypass may mask the fault.
3. AppKit's retained child property must be updated before its notification, while element identity
   remains generation-bound and value/selection remain non-settable.
4. Ingress and pasteboard access remain disabled through all focus/status notifications and both
   postchecks; AX publication itself never mutates owner text or focus.
5. The real-window gate must fail before the fix and pass only against the freshly packaged release
   app using external CGEvent and AX APIs. Pure adapters, direct getter calls, source scans, or
   screenshots cannot substitute.
6. Any material implementation change returns through Security (code), installed Design Sign-off,
   full Test, pipeline, and deploy proof. No commit/push occurs while any renewed gate is FAIL.

**Architecture Runtime Failure Revision verdict: PASS.** Both installed failures have verified
AppKit-level causes, exact production edit boundaries, and a mandatory real signed
NSWindow/GameView/AX regression gate that cannot pass on pure adapters or source strings. Build,
Security (code), Design Sign-off, Test, pipeline, deployment completion, commit, and push remain
blocked until implementation and renewed independent evidence close this revision.

## Design Review/Revision — Runtime Failure Revision (2026-07-11)

Reviewed the Architecture Runtime Failure Revision and retained installed evidence through plan
SHA-256 `93021fd51b1a00669ed05faa48ae8d111e06209231f1c0592d4cf48b0c904ed7`. Removing the invalid
keyboard accessor restores the established interaction rather than changing it, and retained AX
publication is the correct platform model. These corrections are binding:

1. **Retain identity without resetting VoiceOver context.** Republish AppKit's combined child array
   only when text/RPG membership, order, screen identity, or generation changes. Accepted value,
   caret, focus-state, help, enabled-state, and finite-frame updates refresh the same retained child
   in place and target the exact `.valueChanged`, `.focusedUIElementChanged`, or layout notification;
   they must not call `setAccessibilityChildren` on every keystroke or move VoiceOver to the group.
   Text children remain in stable visual/focus order, followed by stable RPG order, with unique IDs.
2. **Use truthful roles and group copy.** The combined GameView group label becomes exactly
   `Pebble menus and actions`; the installed `Pebble RPG menus and actions` label is misleading on
   Create World. Every field exposes its approved nearby label and exact value; Recipe is announced
   as a search field, guest Sign as static read-only text, and editable TextField/Sign/Chat elements
   retain concise keyboard-editing help without claiming selection, direct AX replacement, or IME.
   Value and insertion-range setters remain explicitly unavailable.
3. **Publication precedes the complete notification sequence.** On open/reveal/resize, install the
   retained combined children before container layout, final-owner focus, and approved generic status
   announcements. Ingress and clipboard reads remain disabled until that entire sequence returns and
   the same generation/value/owner/first-responder postcheck succeeds. Accepted edits update the
   retained value/range before one targeted value notification; close/replacement invalidates and
   republishes before layout notification. No notification may expose a half-built or stale tree.
4. **The black-box gate must prove the user-observable failure, not merely event delivery.** Keep the
   exact Title -> Singleplayer -> Create New World CGEvent path, require ordinary letters,
   Left/Right, Backspace and separate menu Paste to produce one exact middle-edited AX value/range,
   and require stable retained World Name/Seed elements across a value change and resize. Also cover
   first input after click and app reactivation, focused/unfocused switching, old-element denial, no
   AppKit exception, no duplicate insertion, and no clipboard read before readiness. The driver may
   observe the signed release app only through real mouse/key events, AX, process logs, and hashes.
5. **Installed Design Sign-off remains the complete visual and semantic matrix.** After the primary
   New World proof creates and reopens the exact entered name/seed, inspect all twelve practical
   fields (Create Name/Seed; LAN Player, host Code/Port, manual Host/Port/Code; Ollama Model; Anvil;
   Creative Search; Template Name), both Recipe owners, local/host Sign and persistence, Chat and
   slash command, and the guest read-only viewer when the existing mirrored state permits it. Exercise
   placeholder, focused/unfocused, middle edit, Paste, accepted Unicode/fallback, limit/error, long
   clipping/caret, reveal/reactivation, and screen-specific completion without inferring unvisited
   states.
6. **Adaptive and assistive proof is visual, not only AX data.** At 360x224, 520x330, and 700x420
   logical content sizes, Standard and High Contrast must retain field clipping, caret visibility,
   labels, status rows, actions, and non-overlap. Installed VoiceOver must read truthful labels,
   exact values, focus, help, read-only setters, insertion ranges, and Recipe/Sign/Chat/guest roles
   without losing focus during in-place value updates. The signed app must also visibly preserve the
   approved RPG layout and exact pixel glyphs `· ✓ ← → ’ … —`. Record viewport/backing derivation,
   screenshots, AX reports, hashes, and crash/exception delta from the newly installed bundle.

**Design Review/Revision verdict: PASS.** With these six corrections binding, the Architecture
restores ordinary canvas editing and exposes a stable, truthful, platform-appropriate Accessibility
tree without changing the approved product scope. Build remains blocked until the ordered Security
review accepts the revised plan; installed Design Sign-off may pass only on the complete new-bundle
matrix above.

## Security (plan) — Runtime Failure Revision (2026-07-11)

Reviewed the Architecture Runtime Failure Revision and binding Designer corrections at plan
SHA-256 `79836a6969451260105c5be8652615414de231f3fdf145ae62cb011201287939` against the current input
router, Core duplicate ledger, text/RPG Accessibility bridges, packaging script, pre-push hook, and
pipeline. The external CGEvent/AX driver, general pasteboard, TCC grants, retained AX references,
bundle signer, and machine gate are untrusted boundaries. The root-cause edits are correctly scoped,
but five blocking plan defects remain.

### Findings

- **[HIGH] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:2679-2687,2748-2751,2769-2770`; `Sources/PebbleCore/Game/InputChords.swift:499-585` ->** The proposed keyboard fingerprint is not a unique delivery identity. Two distinct
  `keyDown` events can have identical key code, rounded timestamp, window, modifiers, repeat bit, and
  origin and will be collapsed by the existing 250 ms equality ledger. The checked routing serial is
  allocated per callback and is not part of that ledger, so it also cannot correlate one physical
  event across `performKeyEquivalent` and `keyDown`. Replace general fingerprint suppression with a
  bounded one-consumption cross-entry ledger: assign every callback a unique monotonic delivery
  serial; record only an actually handled key-equivalent correlation key; consume that marker once if
  the matching `keyDown` arrives; and never deduplicate two independent `keyDown` deliveries merely
  because their observable fields collide. Keep modifier identities in a noncolliding namespace and
  fail closed on serial exhaustion. Test same-signature distinct keyDowns, handled-equivalent replay,
  unhandled-equivalent then keyDown, repeats, F11/capture, modifier edges, expiry, and exhaustion.
- **[HIGH] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:2689-2715,2771-2776,2811-2828`; `Sources/Pebble/TextEntryAccessibilityM.swift:5-52` ->** Dropping an element from bridge arrays does not revoke an external AX client's retained reference; the object can continue serving its cached text,
  range, help, enabled state, and stale focusability. The in-place refresh list also omits role and
  focusability, while current focus authorization reads the element's immutable old descriptor.
  Make commit all-or-nothing after validating the complete bounded candidate and combined text/RPG
  ID/object set. Treat role or focusability changes as structural. Before replacement/invalidation,
  synchronously mark every removed element retired, deny every getter/action/focus operation, scrub
  value/help/character count/range/focus/enabled/parent/children, then atomically republish the new
  roots. Add external old-reference tests proving value/range/help are unavailable as well as focus
  denied after close, replacement, generation change, role change, and bridge collision/failure.
- **[HIGH] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:2720-2746`; `pebble:70-85` ->** The gate requires the launched ad-hoc-signed executable SHA-256 to equal the pre-sign `.build/release/Pebble`, but bundle signing replaces the executable's embedded signature and therefore cannot be bound by that whole-file equality. The current packager also converts signing failure into a warning. Extract one reusable fail-closed packager. Verify the regular non-symlink staged executable equals the resolved release executable **before** signing; sign successfully; verify `codesign --verify --deep --strict`, expected bundle ID and sealed resources; record the post-sign executable SHA-256 and CDHash; then prove the launched PID's canonical executable path, bundle path/ID, SHA-256, and CDHash equal that post-sign artifact. Rehash the release input after the run to detect races. Never accept a signing warning, a prior installed bundle, or pre-sign/post-sign hash equality as identity evidence.
- **[HIGH] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:2726-2745,2773-2780,2829-2835` ->** The trusted-session driver can send keys or Paste to another application if focus changes, overwrite or materialize the user's general pasteboard, leave the launched app/provider/temp home running after failure, and retain raw logs containing input. Require fail-closed Accessibility/event-post trust preflight with finite deadlines; exact PID, canonical path, frontmost app, key window, and target-element revalidation immediately before every CGEvent/menu action; and abort before posting on any mismatch. Use a bounded in-memory, never-logged, lossless general-pasteboard save/restore with explicit prerequisite failure when preservation is impossible, plus a lazy fixed-sentinel provider to prove zero pre-read and exactly one approved read. On EXIT/INT/TERM, restore pasteboard, terminate only the recorded process identity, remove driver/app/home artifacts, and emit only redacted exception counts/hashes/generic errors—never raw unified logs, clipboard bytes, or entered values.
- **[MEDIUM] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:2744-2746,2782-2796`; `.githooks/pre-push:16-45`; `scripts/pipeline.sh:16-47` ->** The plan mandates the interactive gate in pre-push/pipeline but forbids edits outside a list that omits those files, and both current machine paths run `security-scan.sh` before the warning-free release build. Explicitly authorize and name `.githooks/pre-push`, `scripts/pipeline.sh`, `scripts/security-scan.sh`, the reusable packager, and documentation. Keep headless source security separate; place the mandatory AppKit stage after one warning-free release build and pass its exact hash into a no-rebuild integration run. Locked/noninteractive sessions or missing TCC must fail promptly with a prerequisite error, never hang, skip, or pass; CI requires an explicitly provisioned interactive macOS runner. Prove both pre-push and pipeline invoke the stage in the required order and propagate every nonzero/timeout/cleanup failure before deploy.

### Review boundary

Reviewed: invalid-selector removal; exactly-once routing and replay/DoS risk; retained and merged AX
identity, stale generation, privacy and focus authority; publication/notification/readiness/Paste
reentrancy; external CGEvent/AX/TCC/pasteboard/log trust; package/sign/hash/PID identity; and current
pre-push/pipeline ordering. Prior bounded text, Sign authority, read-only live AX values, and zero-LAN
requirements remain binding.

Not reviewed: implementation of this revision, compiled artifacts produced from it, live driver/TCC
behavior, or renewed installed results. No implementation was edited and no build or deployment was
performed by this reviewer.

**Security (plan) verdict: FAIL.** The runtime root causes are credible, but collision-safe event
identity, stale AX value revocation, achievable signed-artifact identity, safe external-driver
cleanup/privacy, and executable machine-gate ordering must be incorporated and receive a fresh
Security (plan) PASS before Build.

## Architecture Security Remediation Revision — runtime gate closure

This revision closes the five Security-plan findings at plan SHA-256
`aad453319b8a15f02261164e132463135eb870fa6bacf5375858a64f55d08a76`. It supersedes the Runtime
Failure Revision where that revision proposed general keyboard fingerprint equality, dynamic AX
replacement without object retirement, pre-sign/post-sign executable equality, an underspecified
external driver, or an incomplete edit/gate list. No LAN behavior or schema enters scope.

### 1. Collision-safe cross-entry keyboard correlation

Remove `RPGPureInputRouter`'s 250 ms general `consumedFingerprints` suppression and do not compare
independent `keyDown` events by key code/timestamp/window/modifiers/repeat/origin. Every AppKit
callback receives a checked, nonzero, monotonically increasing `deliverySerial`; Core routes or
consumes that serial at most once. Two distinct `keyDown` callbacks therefore always receive
different identities and both route, even when every observable event field and timestamp is equal.

`AppInputRouter` owns a separate bounded `HandledEquivalentLedger` used only across callback entry
points. After—and only after—`performKeyEquivalent` actually handles an event, store a marker that
strongly retains that exact `NSEvent` object, its keyboard-safe fields, monotonic insertion time, and
the handled callback serial. At a subsequent `keyDown`, consume one marker only when the retained
event is identical by `===` and all safe fields agree; then consume that callback without dispatch.
An unhandled equivalent records nothing. Strong retention prevents pointer reuse; one-shot removal
prevents a marker suppressing more than one callback. Bound the ledger to 16 markers and 250 ms,
expiring oldest first. Capacity failure drops the oldest marker, never a `keyDown`; correlation is a
replay defense, not authorization.

Physical key delivery serials and synthesized modifier deliveries use disjoint enum cases
`.keyboard(UInt64)` and `.modifier(UInt64, terminal)`, each with a checked counter. Exhaustion latches
that namespace, clears equivalent markers, consumes future events fail-closed, and requires app
restart; it cannot wrap into another identity. `NSEvent.eventNumber` is read nowhere. Tests cover
same-signature distinct keyDowns, exact-object handled replay, equal-fields/different-object input,
unhandled equivalent then keyDown, repeats, F11, binding capture, marker expiry/capacity, modifier
edges/namespaces, and both exhaustion paths.

### 2. Atomic retained-AX retirement and publication

`GameView` is the sole owner of a main-actor `CombinedAccessibilitySnapshot`. Before mutation it
validates the complete bounded text+RPG candidate: count caps, globally unique stable IDs/object
keys, stable order, current screen/generation, finite frames, valid bounded attributes, and valid
parentage. Role or focusability change is structural and requires a replacement object; value,
range, label/help, enabled/focused state, and finite-frame changes refresh the same object. Candidate
collision, overflow, malformed structure, or construction failure publishes no partial tree.

Every `TextEntryAccessibilityElement` and RPG counterpart gains a one-way `retired` state checked by
all getters, attribute exposure, actions, and focus methods. Before a valid structural replacement,
close, invalidation, generation change, or fail-closed empty publication, synchronously retire each
removed object and scrub its label, value, help, character count, selected range, enabled/focused
state, parent, children, and actions; mark it ignored/non-element. A retained external reference then
returns no value/help/range, no focus/action, and no parent/children. It cannot consult its cached
descriptor. Candidate validation failure retires the current affected tree and atomically publishes
empty roots rather than leaving stale content.

After full validation and preparation, perform the scrub/update/replacement and exactly one
`setAccessibilityChildren(textRoots + rpgRoots)` synchronously on the main actor, then publish the
approved notifications. No AX request can interleave this non-suspending transaction. Membership,
order, identity, generation, role, or focusability changes republish roots; ordinary value/caret/
frame/focus updates reuse retained objects and do not reset group navigation. Text roots remain in
visual/focus order before stable RPG roots. External tests hold old references through close,
replacement, generation and role/focusability changes, collision, and failed commit, requiring
value/range/help/parent/action unavailability as well as focus denial.

### 3. Achievable signed-artifact identity

Extract `scripts/package-app.sh` as the single fail-closed packager used by integration,
`pebble install`, and pipeline. It accepts an explicit resolved release executable and output path.
Before signing it requires both input and staged executable to be regular non-symlink files, resolves their
canonical paths, byte-compares them, and records equal pre-sign SHA-256 values. It then signs the
bundle; signing failure is fatal, never a warning. After signing it requires `codesign --verify
--deep --strict`, expected identifier `com.briangao.pebble`, and a valid sealed-resource report, then
records the post-sign executable SHA-256 and CDHash. Post-sign equality with the unsigned input is
neither expected nor used.

The packager emits a private temporary manifest containing canonical release/stage paths, pre-sign
input/staged hashes, post-sign executable hash, CDHash, bundle ID, and resource-seal success. The
driver accepts that manifest once. After launch it proves the recorded PID/start identity resolves
to the canonical staged bundle/executable, `NSRunningApplication` reports the expected bundle ID,
and on-disk SHA-256/CDHash equal the post-sign manifest. It rejects `/Applications/Pebble.app`, a
symlink, path substitution, different PID/start identity, and any prior bundle. After the run it
rehashes the original release input and requires the original pre-sign hash, detecting build/sign
races.

### 4. External driver trust, privacy, and cleanup

Before packaging or launch, the driver verifies an unlocked interactive console session,
fail-closed Accessibility trust and event-post/Input Monitoring trust. Every preflight and wait has
a fixed monotonic deadline; missing TCC, locked/headless state, or unavailable APIs exits promptly
with a generic prerequisite error. Immediately before every mouse/key/menu action it revalidates the
recorded process identity, canonical paths/hashes, frontmost application, exact key window, and the
expected current group/target AX identity. Any mismatch aborts before posting.

Paste proof snapshots every item/type of the general pasteboard losslessly into bounded in-memory
Data before changing it; a missing/unreadable promised type or aggregate over the documented test
cap is a prerequisite failure. It installs a fixed nonsensitive sentinel through a lazy
`NSPasteboardItemDataProvider`, records no bytes, proves provider reads remain zero before readiness,
and requires exactly one read for the approved menu Paste. The original pasteboard is restored on
every exit path before the saved bytes are released.

One idempotent EXIT/INT/TERM cleanup owns the saved pasteboard, exact PID+start identity, provider,
temporary bundle/home/manifest, and log handles. It restores the pasteboard, terminates only that
still-matching process (bounded graceful wait, then same-identity kill), removes all temporary
artifacts, and reports cleanup failure as gate failure. Diagnostics emit only stage names, counts,
hashes/CDHash, roles, and generic errors. Raw unified logs, clipboard/entered values, positions,
usernames, and AX text are neither printed nor retained; exception checking reduces the PID-scoped
stream to redacted counts before deletion.

### 5. Authorized files and machine-gate order

Builder may edit exactly: `Sources/Pebble/AppInputRouterM.swift`,
`Sources/PebbleCore/Game/InputChords.swift`, `Sources/Pebble/TextEntryAccessibilityM.swift`,
`Sources/Pebble/RPGAccessibilityM.swift`, `Sources/Pebble/UIManagerM.swift`,
`Sources/Pebble/main.swift`, their focused pure/source tests, new non-shipping
`Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/` and `scripts/appkit-text-entry-integration.sh`, new reusable
`scripts/package-app.sh`, `pebble`, `scripts/security-scan.sh`, `scripts/pipeline.sh`,
`.githooks/pre-push`, `Package.swift` only if required for the non-product driver, and the directly
affected `README.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `SECURITY.md`, and this plan. No LAN,
world, save, Sign protocol, or unrelated product file is authorized.

`scripts/security-scan.sh` remains the prompt-free headless source/pure security stage and may run
before build; it must not launch UI, request TCC, or treat source-string checks as runtime proof.
Both `.githooks/pre-push` and `scripts/pipeline.sh` use this fail-closed order:

1. headless source security and prerequisite files;
2. one warning-free `swift build -c release`, then capture the resolved regular executable SHA-256;
3. release-surface/binary verification against that hash;
4. the mandatory AppKit script with `--no-build`, exact executable path/hash, outer finite timeout,
   and post-build TCC/interactive preflight; any prerequisite, timeout, assertion, or cleanup failure
   exits nonzero;
5. XCTest and the 457-check release `pebsmoke`, followed by a release-input rehash; and
6. pipeline only: fail-closed package/install of that same rehashed release input, installed binary/
   signing verification, and installed acceptance. Pre-push stops after step 5.

The AppKit script never rebuilds or silently selects an installed binary. Pipeline deployment may
not call the current rebuilding `pebble install`; it passes `--no-build` plus the captured hash to
the shared packager/deployer. A locked/noninteractive local session or missing TCC fails with a
bounded prerequisite error, never skip/pass/hang. CI can pass only on an explicitly provisioned,
logged-in interactive macOS runner with required grants; there is no environment skip flag. Tests
inspect both scripts' stage order and nonzero propagation before deploy.

### Updated risk-to-test map

- Equivalent replay versus legitimate collisions: exact-object one-shot ledger and equal-signature
  distinct-object/independent-keyDown tests.
- Serial/cap exhaustion denial: bounded ledger, disjoint identity namespaces, expiry and fail-closed
  overflow tests.
- Stale AX disclosure/capability: external retained-reference scrub tests across every retirement and
  failed combined commit.
- Partial/colliding AX trees: all-or-nothing combined candidate, stable-order/unique-ID/property tests,
  plus real external AX counts.
- Artifact substitution and sign mutation: pre-sign byte/hash proof, mandatory signing/seal, post-sign
  hash/CDHash/PID canonical-path proof, and release-input rehash.
- Misdelivery/TCC/hang: per-action PID/frontmost/key-window/target checks, trust/lock preflight, inner
  deadlines and outer watchdog tests.
- Clipboard/log privacy and abandoned state: unreadable/oversize/race/provider-count cases, injected
  EXIT/INT/TERM failures at every phase, exact restoration/process cleanup, and redaction scans.
- Gate bypass/order drift: executable tests of pre-push/pipeline ordering, `--no-build` hash pinning,
  failure propagation, and deploy denial.

### Revised Conditions for Builder

1. Preserve the verified runtime fixes and all prior bounded ingress, readiness, clipboard, Sign
   authority, read-only AX, generation, and zero-Sign-LAN invariants.
2. General keyDown equality suppression is forbidden; only one exact retained handled-equivalent
   object may suppress one matching cross-entry replay.
3. No removed AX object remains readable/actionable, no partial combined tree is published, and no
   notification precedes the completed root/object transaction.
4. Signing, seal, path, hash, CDHash, PID/start identity, TCC, focus, pasteboard preservation,
   redaction, timeout, and cleanup checks all fail closed without warning/skip fallbacks.
5. The black-box gate must run the hashed freshly signed release app after one release build and
   prove actual CGEvent edits and external AX behavior; pure/source tests remain supplemental.
6. Both machine gates must enforce the exact order above. No deploy, commit, or push proceeds after
   any nonzero, timeout, cleanup, Security, Design, Test, pipeline, or installed failure.

**Architecture Security Remediation Revision verdict: PASS.** Each Security-plan blocker now has an
implementable identity/ownership algorithm, exact mutation boundary, fail-closed lifecycle, allowed
file list, and empirical risk test. Build remains blocked until renewed Design Review and Security
(plan) independently PASS this revision.

## Design Review/Revision rerun — Security Remediation (2026-07-11)

Reviewed the Architecture Security Remediation Revision through plan SHA-256
`00c26f6b6bf487366088158a9bacebf27609605223bf5167ea444e84d84236e6`. Collision-safe delivery,
fail-closed driver identity/cleanup, and explicit AX retirement preserve the approved interaction.
These corrections are binding:

1. A generation-safe structural replacement may retire the old AX object, but a same-screen
   resize/rebuild must atomically publish the replacement with the same stable descriptor ID and
   truthful logical focus, then hand VoiceOver focus to that exact replacement after layout
   publication. Never transfer focus across close, screen replacement, collision/failure, or merely
   matching IDs. Ordinary value/caret changes retain the object and never reset root children.
2. The prior semantic contract remains exact: root label `Pebble menus and actions`, text children
   in stable visual order before RPG children, Recipe search role, guest Sign static role, read-only
   value/range, and targeted notifications only after the complete retained-tree transaction.
   Retirement must not move focus to the group or announce scrubbed content.
3. Event correlation and the black-box driver must preserve the first character after click and
   reactivation, independent same-signature key presses, repeat, arrows/Backspace, and separate Paste
   exactly once. Trust/path/pasteboard prerequisites fail promptly with concise actionable setup copy;
   cleanup and redaction may not create a visible app state or alter the user's clipboard.
4. The safe black-box New World gate is a regression prerequisite, not a substitute for installed
   Design Sign-off. The complete twelve-field, Recipe/Sign/Chat/slash/guest, 360x224/520x330/700x420,
   Standard/High Contrast, VoiceOver, exact-world-name/seed, clipping/status, and RPG glyph/layout
   matrix from the prior Design Review remains mandatory on the newly installed signed bundle.

**Design Review/Revision rerun verdict: PASS.** With these four corrections binding, the remediation
closes the runtime/security gaps without sacrificing stable VoiceOver context, ordinary key behavior,
or the complete installed experience. Security (plan) must independently PASS before Build.

## Security (plan) rerun — Runtime Security Remediation (2026-07-11)

Reviewed the Architecture Security Remediation Revision and binding Design rerun at plan SHA-256
`132276b48a07657d2eec6cd5a1247ae231e2d42a8b728b3067ece510de47d6a7`. Collision-safe cross-entry
correlation, all-or-nothing combined AX publication with retired-object scrubbing, achievable
pre-sign/post-sign/PID identity, and explicit pre-push/pipeline file scope and ordering close four of
the five prior findings without weakening bounded ingress, notification-before-readiness, Sign
authority, read-only live AX values, generation checks, or zero-Sign-LAN scope.

### Finding

- **[MEDIUM] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:2993-3012,3061-3063` ->** The clipboard lifecycle is still
  not fail-safe. Reading every representation into `Data` necessarily materializes an arbitrary
  promised type before the driver can learn that representation's size, so the stated cap does not
  bound allocation or provider side effects. Unconditionally restoring the original snapshot also
  overwrites a newer clipboard value if the user or another process changes the pasteboard during the
  gate. Replace snapshotting with a non-materializing prerequisite: require the general pasteboard to
  contain no items before the test (or run in a separately authenticated disposable login session
  whose pasteboard is isolated). Record the initial `changeCount`; after installing the fixed lazy
  sentinel, track the exact test-owned counts. Cleanup may clear/restore only while the current count
  and provider identity are still test-owned. If an external change occurs, leave that newer content
  untouched, fail the gate, and report only a generic race. Test empty/nonempty prerequisite,
  pre-read zero, one approved read, external replacement before/during cleanup, every signal/failure
  phase, no overwrite, no retained bytes, and exact provider/temp-process cleanup.

### Review boundary

Reviewed all five prior findings and the revised Builder conditions. Not reviewed: implementation,
compiled artifacts, TCC/runtime behavior, or installed evidence. No implementation was edited and no
build or deployment was performed.

**Security (plan) rerun verdict: FAIL.** The event, AX, signing, and machine-path remediations are
approved at plan level, but the external driver's clipboard prerequisite/race algorithm must be made
non-materializing and non-overwriting, then receive a fresh Security (plan) PASS before Build.

## Architecture Clipboard Safety Amendment — non-materializing ownership

This amendment closes the remaining finding at plan SHA-256
`59898c54ce272b5fedbc7055f7e9ac12d6d8bae64e23d3faaf73762a9c682848`. It supersedes every prior
instruction to snapshot, read, size, serialize, or restore arbitrary general-pasteboard items or
promised types. The driver never materializes preexisting clipboard representations.

### Prerequisite and ownership state

In an ordinary login session, read only `changeCount` and item-count metadata, bracketed by a second
`changeCount`; proceed only when both counts match and the general pasteboard contains zero items.
A nonempty or racing pasteboard fails before packaging, launch, clearing, provider installation, or
event posting. The generic actionable error is: `AppKit gate requires an empty clipboard. Preserve
anything needed, clear the clipboard, and rerun.` Documentation provides an explicit opt-in setup
command using `NSPasteboard.general.clearContents()`; the gate never clears user content itself.

Alternatively, an explicitly provisioned, separately authenticated disposable macOS login session
may attest its expected console UID/session identity. In that isolated mode only, the driver may
call `clearContents()` without enumerating or reading prior items, verify zero items, and take the
resulting `changeCount` as its empty baseline. A temporary HOME alone, environment claim, headless
process, or shared login is not isolation and must fail the prerequisite.

From the empty baseline the driver creates one retained `NSPasteboardItem` and one retained lazy
provider. The item carries a private, random, in-memory ownership nonce under a private UTI and a
fixed nonsensitive `.string` supplied lazily. After `writeObjects`, record the resulting owned
`changeCount`, exact item object identity, provider identity/nonce, and provider read count. None is
logged. Before focus/readiness proof the provider count must be zero; the one approved menu Paste
must change it to exactly one. Any earlier, duplicate, or foreign read fails.

### Race-safe cleanup

The idempotent EXIT/INT/TERM cleanup may clear the sentinel only if all ownership checks still hold:
the current `changeCount` equals the recorded owned count, the pasteboard contains exactly the
retained test item, and its private nonce/provider identity is still the retained test ownership.
It then calls `clearContents()`, verifies zero items, releases the provider/nonce, and continues
process/artifact cleanup. Because the prerequisite was empty, there is no prior value to restore.

If the count, item, nonce, or provider differs, another process owns newer clipboard state. Cleanup
must not read, clear, rewrite, or restore it. It preserves that external value, records only a generic
`clipboard changed during AppKit gate` failure, releases its own references, and still terminates the
exact test process/removes temporary artifacts. Gate result is FAIL even if all UI assertions passed.
Checked count arithmetic and identity comparison prevent wrap or same-type substitution from being
treated as ownership.

### Tests, setup, and amended conditions

Driver state-machine tests use an isolated/named pasteboard and adversarial lazy providers to cover:
initial empty success; ordinary nonempty prerequisite rejection with zero provider reads/mutation;
isolated-session clear without materialization; baseline race; zero reads before readiness; exactly
one approved read; duplicate/foreign read; external replacement before Paste, after Paste, and
during every cleanup phase; same-types/different-item/provider/nonce substitution; change-count
change and exhaustion; EXIT/INT/TERM at every phase; owned clear to empty; unowned preservation;
no retained bytes/provider/process/temp artifacts; generic-only diagnostics; and nonzero propagation
through pre-push/pipeline. The real black-box gate repeats empty prerequisite, zero/one provider
counts, owned cleanup, and an injected external-replacement cleanup test without exposing content.

`README.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, and gate prerequisite output must explain two supported setups:
preserve any needed clipboard data and explicitly clear it before a normal local run, or use the
documented separately authenticated disposable interactive macOS account with TCC grants. Missing
either setup is a prompt, bounded prerequisite failure—not a skip, hang, or PASS.

Revised Builder condition 4 is: never materialize, snapshot, or overwrite a preexisting/general
external clipboard value. Install the sentinel only from a verified empty or attested isolated
baseline; clear it only while exact count+item+provider ownership remains current; otherwise preserve
the external replacement and fail. All other Security Remediation conditions, risk tests, allowed
files, gate ordering, privacy/redaction, cleanup, zero-LAN scope, and downstream blocks remain
binding.

**Architecture Clipboard Safety Amendment verdict: PASS.** The AppKit gate now has a feasible,
non-materializing prerequisite and an exact ownership-aware cleanup algorithm that never overwrites
newer external clipboard state. Build remains blocked until renewed Security (plan) independently
PASSes this amendment.

## Design Review — Clipboard Safety Amendment (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`a5c3eedee812b8d2695d32518c3f3c58a5438234518b64a4e1a611eaace81a67`. The exact empty-clipboard
error is concise, actionable, and appears before launch or mutation; the documented manual-clear and
separate disposable-login options make the prerequisite understandable without silently discarding
developer data. Ownership-aware cleanup also preserves a newer external clipboard unchanged.

The amendment does not weaken product Paste proof: the lazy nonsensitive sentinel must remain unread
before readiness, be read exactly once by the approved menu Paste, produce the exact bounded field/AX
result required by the black-box gate, and then be cleared only while still test-owned. Generic-only
diagnostics and the full installed Paste matrix remain binding.

**Clipboard Safety Design Review verdict: PASS.** No further Design correction is required; renewed
Security (plan) must PASS before Build.

## Security (plan) rerun — Clipboard Safety Amendment (2026-07-11)

Reviewed the Architecture Clipboard Safety Amendment and binding Design Review at plan SHA-256
`8e3fcb2ae1d6d2f8e3f5faebac1bdb765a03f92819cd9eb303a0be2a448ee370` against the sole remaining
