clipboard finding and all previously approved runtime-remediation contracts.

### Findings

None.

The amendment closes the blocker with an implementable fail-closed lifecycle: ordinary sessions
inspect only stable item-count/change-count metadata and require an empty clipboard before any
packaging, launch, clear, provider install, or event; an isolated-session clear requires authenticated
session identity rather than HOME/environment claims. The gate never materializes preexisting
representations. Its random private ownership nonce, retained item/provider, exact owned change count,
and lazy fixed string distinguish the sentinel and prove zero pre-read plus exactly one approved
Paste read. Cleanup clears only while every ownership check remains current; any race or replacement
is left untouched and fails generically while exact process/artifact cleanup continues. The adversarial
matrix covers nonempty/racing prerequisites, provider/read anomalies, substitution, count exhaustion,
external replacement, every signal/failure phase, redaction, and machine-gate failure propagation.

Earlier collision-safe key delivery, retired/scrubbed AX identity, notification-before-readiness and
Paste denial, pre-sign/post-sign/PID identity, TCC/focus checks, machine-path ordering, bounded text,
Sign authority, read-only live AX, generation, and zero-Sign-LAN requirements remain unchanged.

Review boundary: plan only. Implementation, compiled artifacts, live TCC/pasteboard behavior, and
installed evidence were not reviewed. No implementation was edited and no build/deploy was run.

**Security (plan) rerun verdict: PASS.** The final clipboard blocker is closed without a new security
or product regression. Build may proceed under the complete revised Conditions for Builder; all
Security (code), Design Sign-off, Test, pipeline, deployment, commit, and push gates remain mandatory.

## Clipboard Gate Feasibility Revision — separate unattended regression from deliberate Paste proof

Builder verification established that `NSPasteboard.general.writeObjects([lazyItem])` returns false
on a stable empty general pasteboard unless `clearContents()` is called first. AppKit exposes no
compare-and-clear or compare-and-write primitive, so another process can win between any metadata
check and that destructive clear. No isolated disposable login session exists, and this work must
not create one. Therefore the prior unattended sentinel algorithm is not implementable without a
clipboard-clobber race and is superseded.

### Mandatory black-box gate

The pre-push/pipeline `scripts/appkit-text-entry-integration.sh` must not read, enumerate, clear,
write, restore, provide data to, or invoke Paste on the general pasteboard. It retains the genuine
freshly signed `NSWindow`/`GameView` regression proof for the installed failure: first ordinary
letter after click, independent letters, Left/Right, Backspace, caret/range, field switching,
application resign/reactivate, retained external AX World Name/Seed elements, resize, old-element
denial, artifact/PID/frontmost/key-window identity, no AppKit exception, finite deadlines, cleanup,
and redacted output. Removing menu Paste from this unattended gate does not weaken the verified
keyboard/AX root-cause proof.

`scripts/security-scan.sh` and driver tests must reject general-pasteboard mutation or Paste
invocation in the mandatory black-box driver. Named/private pasteboards may be used only for driver
state tests; dependency injection, a production launch hook, or a private test pasteboard cannot be
presented as proof of the shipping menu path.

### Paste verification split

Executable `PebbleTextPasteIngressAdapter` tests remain mandatory and cover the security contract:
blocked readiness performs zero read; owner capture precedes read; exact owner/generation is
revalidated after read; decode/raw-size limits fail closed; valid bounded Paste dispatches once;
invalid, stale, reentrant, over-limit, and clipboard-error cases produce no text/world/template
fallback or replay. These use controlled closures/data and never touch `NSPasteboard.general`.

The real AppDelegate/Edit-menu Paste path moves to explicit user-controlled installed Design
Sign-off after deployment. The proof gives this actionable sequence:

1. Warn the user to preserve any clipboard content they need. The user deliberately replaces it
   with the documented fixed nonsensitive sentinel (for example with `pbcopy`) outside the driver.
2. The observer records only the current `changeCount`, verifies the installed Pebble PID/path/hash/
   CDHash, frontmost app, key window, ready target field and pre-Paste AX value/range, then prompts on
   an interactive TTY. It does not read or alter the clipboard.
3. The user manually invokes Pebble's Edit -> Paste or Command-V. No synthetic Paste event is posted
   by test tooling. Afterward the observer requires unchanged `changeCount`, the exact single
   sentinel insertion and bounded AX range/value transition, and no exception/crash. Equality is
   checked in memory; entered/sentinel text is not logged.
4. Any count/focus/PID/target/value mismatch fails without reading, clearing, restoring, or otherwise
   touching the clipboard. The deliberate sentinel remains for the user to clear or replace.

This proof has no noninteractive confirmation flag or environment bypass. Pipeline may build, run
the no-Paste black-box gate, test, package, and install autonomously, but deployment completion,
installed Design Sign-off, commit, and push remain blocked until the user-controlled Paste proof and
the rest of the installed matrix pass. CI can prove the adapter and keyboard/AX surfaces but cannot
claim installed Paste completion.

### Tests, documentation, and risk amendment

Tests prove the mandatory driver performs zero general-pasteboard calls and still fails on every
keyboard/AX/artifact/TCC/cleanup regression; executable adapter tests cover zero-pre-read, owner,
bounds, decode, race, and exactly-once Paste. Installed-proof tests cover TTY/explicit-confirmation
requirements, change before prompt, change during/after manual Paste, focus/PID/target replacement,
timeout/signal cleanup, exact one insertion, generic redaction, and preservation of the deliberate
sentinel. Source/gate-order tests ensure pre-push/pipeline never require empty clipboard or create an
isolated login, while final deployment status cannot become complete without recorded manual proof.

`README.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `SECURITY.md`, and the installed sign-off checklist must distinguish
the unattended keyboard/AX gate, executable Paste security tests, and explicit installed Paste
proof. They must explain why the driver cannot safely acquire the general pasteboard and provide the
fixed-sentinel preparation/cleanup steps without claiming automatic restoration.

The allowed-file list also narrowly authorizes reviewed pin-only updates in
`scripts/verify-pebble-storage-release-surface.sh` for the new `PebbleCore.o`, `Pebble`, and
`pebsmoke` SHA-256 values produced by the approved `InputChords` and App runtime changes.
`PebbleStorage.o` and `PebbleTextInput.o` pins must remain byte-for-byte unchanged. Builder must
present before/after hashes, rerun the verifier against the final release build, and obtain PASS;
no symbol allowlist, API/capability manifest, product, dependency, verifier logic, or storage/text-
input surface may be broadened under this authorization.

Risk mapping is now: unattended clipboard overwrite/materialization is eliminated by zero access;
Paste security regressions are caught by executable production-adapter tests; AppKit menu wiring is
caught by the explicit installed proof; clipboard races during that proof are detected by
`changeCount`/focus/value checks and never repaired destructively; false completion is prevented by
blocking final sign-off/commit/push on the manual evidence.

### Revised Conditions for Builder

1. Do not create an isolated login and do not add any general-pasteboard read/write/clear/restore,
   provider, or Paste action to the mandatory pre-push/pipeline black-box driver.
2. Keep the no-Paste black-box gate mandatory for real signed-app keyboard, reactivation, AX,
   artifact identity, timeout, redaction, and cleanup behavior.
3. Keep production Paste adapter tests executable and exhaustive; source strings or a private
   pasteboard alone cannot satisfy them.
4. Require a human-controlled fixed-sentinel menu Paste on the newly installed app before installed
   Design Sign-off or deployment completion. Test tooling observes only and never restores/clobbers.
5. Limit release-verifier edits to reviewed `PebbleCore.o`/`Pebble`/`pebsmoke` hash pins, retain the
   exact Storage/TextInput pins and surface rules, and rerun the verifier successfully.
6. All prior event-correlation, AX retirement, signing/PID, readiness, bounded text, Sign authority,
   zero-LAN, Security, Test, installed-matrix, and no-commit/push-on-failure conditions remain binding.

**Clipboard Gate Feasibility Revision verdict: PASS.** No race-free unattended general-pasteboard
mutation exists in the current session, so the mandatory machine gate now proves the actual
keyboard/AX regression without touching it, while executable adapter tests and explicit installed
human proof preserve complete Paste coverage. Renewed Design and Security review must accept this
material gate split before Builder resumes.

### Artifact Pin Authorization addendum

The reviewed final release build authorizes pin-only edits in
`scripts/verify-pebble-storage-release-surface.sh` to `PebbleCore.o`
`39e37366bcba82ba65131a1112ad0b262f562279e363cf621542a804bd3ae028`, `Pebble`
`4f62d01f93d4c9dfd47c8c410a92abd95deaf39d5e34cb7bc3d50e04367d2179`, and `pebsmoke`
`d989c030656377ea1dca57b563408e2f6ae739e391904bde8be25f4f1d6dde2f`. The unchanged pins remain
`PebbleStorage.o` `ed37590e383037968b25905cb7ecd1d29e8faa43ba1f62a4919baebf9aabc6ba` and
`PebbleTextInput.o` `547f0c44a950be0b7287ab73b918b6a3f486502e7ba57d7897e2d751b4d040ac`. Builder must rerun the
verifier against these exact final artifacts and obtain PASS. This authorization permits no change
to verifier logic, symbol/type allowlists or denylists, API/capability manifests, products,
dependencies, or storage/text-input surface.

## Design Review — Clipboard Gate Feasibility Revision (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`04b72740701ccfb85d08d7560965ef55133296a3b8e1a8866cee1dad69516262`. The no-Paste unattended
gate is truthful and developer-safe: it still proves the real signed-app keyboard/AX regression and
never conditions routine pre-push/pipeline work on surrendering clipboard contents. The installed
prompt must plainly state the fixed sentinel, that the user's prior clipboard must be preserved by
them, that Pebble/tooling will not restore it, how to clear it afterward, and how to cancel/timeout
without posting Paste or claiming completion.

The manual New World sentinel transition is the minimum real AppDelegate/Edit-menu wiring proof, not
a substitute for the complete installed matrix. Using that same deliberate sentinel, installed
Design Sign-off must still verify bounded Paste routing on all practical TextFields and Recipe,
local/host Sign, Chat/slash, plus guest/non-owner no-Paste behavior, alongside the full text-entry,
adaptive, contrast, VoiceOver, and RPG checks already binding. CI and pin-only verifier updates may
never claim those human-observed states; the exact authorized pins change no user-facing contract.

**Clipboard Gate Feasibility Design Review verdict: PASS.** With the clarification above binding,
the split preserves complete user-facing Paste evidence and provides actionable, non-destructive
developer UX. Security review must accept the material split before Build resumes.

## Security (plan) — Clipboard Gate Feasibility Revision (2026-07-11)

Reviewed the Clipboard Gate Feasibility Revision, Artifact Pin Authorization, and binding Design
clarification at plan SHA-256
`e2436cf8ec039ce35464037840f336cad708b8905ae2c5e46d85abdf06b4ab95`.

The unattended boundary is sound: its real signed-app keyboard/AX driver has zero general-pasteboard
or Paste activity, while executable production `PebbleTextPasteIngressAdapter` tests retain blocked-
read, owner-before-read, exact post-read revalidation, byte/decode bounds, exactly-once dispatch, and
no fallback/replay coverage. Real AppDelegate/menu wiring and all practical owner/non-owner behavior
remain explicitly assigned to user-controlled installed proof. The release-verifier authorization is
properly pin-only for `PebbleCore.o`, `Pebble`, and `pebsmoke`; Storage/TextInput pins and every
verifier rule, manifest, product, dependency, and surface remain frozen.

### Finding

- **[HIGH] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:3320-3335,3349-3368` ->** “Blocked until recorded manual
  proof” is not yet an enforceable completion state. The plan defines neither the receipt schema nor
  how pipeline/pre-push distinguishes a successfully installed but still-pending bundle from a fully
  signed-off one. The current pipeline can therefore return its ordinary success after install, and
  a later process can claim deployment/commit/push completion without proving that the receipt belongs
  to the unchanged source and installed artifact. Define a two-phase machine contract: preparation
  installs the verified artifact but ends explicitly `PENDING_INSTALLED_SIGNOFF` and never prints or
  exits as clean/deployed completion; the TTY observer emits a private, redacted receipt bound to the
  authorized implementation-file digest, plan/evidence revision, release and installed SHA-256/CDHash,
  bundle ID/signing result, PID/start identity, TTY confirmation, fixed checklist version, Paste
  equality/count/range assertions, full installed-matrix result, and timestamp. A finalizer must
  reverify that receipt, current source digest, installed signature/hashes, and all downstream gates
  before the pipeline may return success. Any rebuild, source/install/hash/checklist change, timeout,
  cancellation, partial matrix, or reused receipt invalidates it. Pre-push—and a pre-commit gate if
  local commit is claimed blocked—must require the matching finalized receipt with no environment/
  noninteractive bypass. Add adversarial missing/partial/stale/copied/tampered/replayed receipt,
  changed-source/artifact, cancelled TTY, and premature-success tests. The receipt must contain no AX
  text, clipboard bytes, sentinel, usernames, or raw logs.

Review boundary: plan only. No implementation, build, verifier artifact, installed proof, deployment,
or source-control state was reviewed or changed.

**Security (plan) verdict: FAIL.** The zero-paste unattended split, adapter coverage, explicit human
proof boundary, and pin-only surface are approved, but durable artifact-bound two-phase completion
must prevent false deployment/commit/push claims before Build resumes.

## Two-Phase Installed Sign-off Receipt Revision — durable completion authority

This revision closes the remaining false-completion finding at plan SHA-256
`cc7abe6043d9ddb91e7b613926a3eb52e54936a19de5971ad0fdc72dfff789eb`. Automated preparation may
install a verified bundle, but only a matching finalized private receipt authorizes pipeline success,
commit, or push.

### Canonical content identity and private schema

`scripts/installed-signoff-content-v1.txt` is a reviewed manifest of every build/gate input:
`Package.swift`, `Sources/**`, `Tests/**`, `packaging/**`, `scripts/**`, `.githooks/**`, and `pebble`.
The digest enumerator includes tracked and untracked regular files under those roots, rejects
symlinks/duplicates/missing manifest roots, sorts raw relative UTF-8 paths, and hashes a versioned
NUL-delimited stream of path, executable mode, byte length, and per-file SHA-256. It excludes `.git`,
`.build`, installed artifacts, private evidence, and documentation-only sign-off narrative. Thus a
commit that changes only Git metadata leaves `contentDigest` unchanged, while any implementation,
test, package, hook, checklist, or gate-script byte/mode change invalidates it. The fixed semantic
`planRevision`, `evidenceRevision`, and checklist digest are separate required fields.

Receipts live only at mode 0600 beneath
`~/Library/Application Support/Pebble/InstalledSignoff/<canonical-repo-root-digest>/`; the directory
is 0700 and outside the repository. `.gitignore` defensively excludes any local receipt/evidence
name, and tests fail if one is staged, committed, copied into the worktree, or printed. Each atomic
JSON receipt is HMAC-SHA256 authenticated with a 256-bit per-user/per-canonical-repository key stored
in macOS Keychain; there is no environment/file key or unsigned mode.

Schema `PebbleInstalledSignoffReceiptV1` contains only:

- schema/state/receipt random IDs; canonical repository-root digest; `contentDigest`; manifest,
  plan, evidence, and checklist versions/digests;
- build epoch plus release executable path identity, stat identity, SHA-256, and prior gate-evidence
  digests/statuses for source security, warning-free build, release surface, binary scan, black-box
  keyboard/AX, XCTest, and 457-check `pebsmoke`;
- staged and installed canonical bundle/executable paths, bundle ID, signing result, sealed-resource
  result, executable SHA-256, CDHash, and installed-vs-staged equality;
- observed installed PID and process-start identity; interactive-TTY confirmation boolean/challenge
  digest; fixed-sentinel Paste equality/count/range booleans; full checklist total/passed/failed/
  omitted counts and Designer verdict; redacted evidence-file hashes; and prepare/observe/finalize
  UTC plus monotonic timestamps and expiry;
- optional commit-arm/committed/push-arm state described below, followed by the HMAC.

It contains no AX/clipboard/entered text, sentinel, username, TTY path, screen position, raw log,
environment, or private evidence bytes.

### Two-phase state machine

The only states are `prepared -> observed -> finalized -> commitArmed -> committed -> pushArmed`,
plus terminal `invalidated`/`cancelled`/`expired`. Transitions use locked, write-temp/fsync/rename
updates and reverify the HMAC/current state before mutation.

1. `scripts/pipeline.sh --prepare-installed-signoff` performs the approved single release build and
   every automated gate, packages and installs the exact verified artifact, verifies the installed
   signature/hash/CDHash, writes `prepared`, then exits **75** with exact status
   `PENDING_INSTALLED_SIGNOFF`. Exit 0, `PASS`, `deployed`, or cleanup of the pending receipt is
   forbidden at this point.
2. The TTY-only installed observer accepts only the matching unexpired `prepared` receipt. It
   revalidates content/artifact/process identity, obtains explicit confirmation, observes the
   user-controlled fixed-sentinel Paste, and executes every versioned installed checklist item.
   Only zero failed/omitted items and Designer `PASS` atomically produce `observed`; cancel, timeout,
   partial matrix, changed identity, or failed assertion produces a terminal non-success state.
3. `scripts/finalize-installed-signoff.sh` never builds or changes source. It authenticates
   `observed`, recomputes the current canonical content/checklist digests, rechecks all signed prior-
   gate evidence hashes/statuses, release artifact stat/hash/build epoch, installed canonical path,
   signature/seal/bundle ID/SHA/CDHash, timestamps/expiry, PID/start observation, TTY confirmation,
   Paste booleans, and full matrix/Designer result. Exact agreement writes `finalized` and prints
   `INSTALLED_SIGNOFF_COMPLETE`; only this command may return installed pipeline success.

Any source/mode/manifest/checklist/plan revision, rebuild or release stat/hash/epoch, installed
bundle/signature/hash/CDHash, evidence, timeout, cancellation, partial result, receipt copy/path,
HMAC, or state mismatch atomically invalidates the receipt. A reused old receipt, prior installed
bundle, or second finalize/observation cannot recover it; the workflow restarts at preparation.

### Commit and push enforcement

Add fail-closed `.githooks/pre-commit`, `.githooks/post-commit`, and strengthen
`.githooks/pre-push`; none has an environment, CI, noninteractive, or receipt-creation bypass.
`core.hooksPath=.githooks` is verified by preparation/finalization and source-control handoff;
`--no-verify` is prohibited for this workflow.

- Pre-commit requires an unexpired matching `finalized` receipt, recomputes the working-tree content
  digest, and independently computes the staged/index version of the same manifest. It requires the
  index and worktree digests to equal the receipt—no unstaged or omitted build/gate input—then writes
  `commitArmed` with index-tree digest and parent metadata. It does not bind the receipt's validity to
  HEAD, so the commit operation itself cannot invalidate content.
- Post-commit rechecks HMAC, content, index/tree content digest, artifact and installed identity, and
  requires the new commit tree to contain that exact manifest content. It writes `committed` with the
  resulting commit ID. A failed commit may retry only with the unchanged armed index; any content/
  index change invalidates and requires preparation.
- Pre-push requires `committed`, exact current content/artifact/install identity, and the outgoing
  local SHA equal the recorded committed ID. It writes `pushArmed` for that one exact local/remote-ref
  tuple. Retry is allowed only for the identical tuple within receipt expiry; a later commit, ref,
  artifact, source, or copied/replayed receipt is rejected. Hook failures propagate and push does not
  start. Project automation and this task never use `--no-verify`.

The private receipt remains ignored and uncommitted throughout; commit records only project content.
Receipt authenticity is local release authority, not a shipped/runtime capability.

### Exact allowed files and adversarial evidence

This revision authorizes only new `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift`,
`scripts/installed-signoff-content-v1.txt`, `scripts/installed-signoff-checklist-v1.json`,
`scripts/finalize-installed-signoff.sh`, installed-observer/evidence scripts, changes to
`scripts/pipeline.sh`, `.githooks/pre-commit`, `.githooks/post-commit`, `.githooks/pre-push`,
`.gitignore`, focused non-product tests/fixtures, and directly affected `README.md`,
`CONTRIBUTING.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `SECURITY.md`, and this plan. Pin-only verifier authority and
all prior product/runtime file scope remain unchanged; no receipt code enters a shipping product.

Adversarial tests cover missing/unsigned/tampered/truncated/copied/wrong-root/wrong-key receipts;
every skipped or illegal state transition; missing/partial/failed/reordered checklist; cancelled or
non-TTY confirmation; Paste mismatch; timestamp/expiry/rollback; changed implementation/mode,
manifest, plan/checklist, evidence, rebuilt-same-bytes stat/epoch, release/installed hash/CDHash/
signature/bundle/path, PID/start; prior installed bundle; finalize replay; concurrent observers/
finalizers; crash between temp/fsync/rename; staged subset, unstaged change, commit failure/retry,
post-commit mismatch, later commit, copied branch, altered ref, repeated/different push; hook/config
absence; receipt/evidence staging; redaction; and exact propagation of pending exit 75/nonzero before
any deployment-complete/commit/push claim.

### Current end-to-end sequence

For this run, finish implementation and all renewed code reviews first, then execute preparation once.
It builds/tests/installs and intentionally stops `PENDING_INSTALLED_SIGNOFF` with the private
`prepared` receipt. The user deliberately prepares and invokes Paste; Designer completes the entire
installed matrix through the observer, producing `observed`. The finalizer revalidates unchanged
content, gates, release and installed artifacts and produces `finalized` without rebuilding. Stage
the already-verified project bytes; pre-commit/post-commit advance the receipt while the canonical
content digest remains unchanged across the commit; pre-push verifies the exact committed receipt
and pushes. No repo-controlled file, checklist, build, bundle, or evidence revision changes between
prepare and push; Designer evidence is private/redacted receipt evidence rather than a late source
edit. Any required correction restarts from preparation.

**Two-Phase Installed Sign-off Receipt Revision verdict: PASS.** The installed-but-pending state is
now explicit and non-successful, final completion is authenticated to unchanged content/artifacts and
the full human matrix, and commit/push are mechanically gated without binding validity to HEAD.
Renewed Design and Security review must PASS before Builder resumes.

## Design Review — Two-Phase Installed Sign-off Receipt (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`7ed360bf94cbdb997b3903a070549562e78a961db53f0523facbb7d79663842f`. The prepared/observed/
finalized split makes “installed but not signed off” unmistakable and preserves the deliberate Paste
plus complete installed matrix as the only path to final success. These UX clarifications are
binding:

1. `PENDING_INSTALLED_SIGNOFF` output must name the exact next observer command, receipt expiry/time
   remaining, clipboard preparation/no-restoration warning, and the implementation/checklist changes
   that force restart. It must read as an intentional pending handoff, never as deployment success.
2. The TTY observer guides one versioned checklist item at a time, shows redacted completed/remaining
   counts, confirms before the first destructive clipboard replacement, and states before cancel that
   cancellation/timeout invalidates the run. Failure names only the generic stage and exact restart
   command; no partial matrix or Paste result is presented as success.
3. Finalization prints the exact verified state and subsequent stage/commit/push commands. Hook
   failures explain the current receipt state and a non-destructive recovery. If Git creates the exact
   matching commit but the post-commit receipt update is interrupted, an idempotent resume command may
   reverify and record only that unchanged commit; users must not be told to reset, amend, bypass, or
   create a dummy commit. Different content/ref/artifacts still restart preparation.

The observer remains non-mutating except for the user's explicit Paste action, and the full
twelve-field/custom-owner/adaptive/contrast/VoiceOver/RPG matrix remains mandatory. Private receipts
may authorize workflow state but never replace screenshots, AX checks, or the Designer verdict.

**Two-Phase Installed Sign-off Design Review verdict: PASS.** With these clarifications binding, the
pending-to-final workflow is understandable, cancellation/recovery is honest, and valid commit/push
handoff is usable without weakening artifact-bound completion. Security review must PASS before Build.

## Security (plan) — Two-Phase Installed Sign-off Receipt (2026-07-11)

Acting solely as the adversarial Security reviewer, independently reviewed the receipt revision and
binding Design clarification at plan SHA-256
`d812b069b8a374a5d3c61eb47611233d304a50207260a85db3b45b26fc56781e`. The explicit exit-75 pending
state, HMAC/path/artifact binding, full observed/finalized split, staged-versus-worktree comparison,
and commit-stable content identity substantively close the original false-success path. Two later-
transition gaps still permit a matching receipt to authorize different policy/evidence than was
actually reviewed.

### Findings

- **[HIGH] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:3461-3469,3519-3522,3551-3557` ->** The claimed canonical
  content identity excludes `README.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `SECURITY.md`, this
  plan, and any other repository-controlled file outside the selected build roots, yet the invalidation
  rule says a plan revision or any repo-controlled change forces restart. A semantic `planRevision`
  string does not detect a changed plan/security contract unless the changed code voluntarily bumps
  it; pre-commit can therefore accept a receipt while committing altered security instructions or
  sign-off narrative. Canonicalize all tracked and nonignored untracked regular repository files,
  including these documents and the reviewed plan, excluding only `.git`, build/dist products and
  the already-external private receipt/evidence. Alternatively bind an exhaustive reviewed path/hash
  manifest that includes every committable file and fail on an extra path. Use that same full set for
  worktree, index, commit-tree, post-commit and pre-push comparison. This remains valid across commit
  because bytes/modes—not HEAD/index metadata—are the identity. No repository file may be appended
  after preparation; Designer results remain private evidence as already specified.
- **[MEDIUM] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:3482-3490,3512-3517,3531-3544,3559-3567,3601-3607` ->**
  Finalization verifies private evidence files, but pre-commit, post-commit and pre-push recheck only
  receipt/content/artifact identity. Deleting or replacing the screenshots/AX reports after finalize
  leaves the HMAC receipt summary usable even though Design explicitly says the receipt never replaces
  that evidence. Require every transition through `commitArmed`, `committed` and `pushArmed` to verify
  every private evidence file's canonical path, regular-file/mode, length and hash, with missing/extra/
  changed evidence invalidating the receipt. Also define and authorize the Design-required idempotent
  post-commit recovery command: it may advance only `commitArmed -> committed` when HEAD's parent,
  complete tree/content digest, index, receipt, evidence, release and installed identities exactly
  match the armed attempt and no later/amended commit exists. It cannot create/amend/reset a commit
  or recover any other state. Add deletion/substitution and interrupted-post-commit/resume races to
  executable tests.

### Review boundary and verdict

Reviewed: pending/final success separation; closed receipt fields and privacy; content and artifact
identity; HMAC/storage/replay states; TTY/full-matrix authority; commit/push transitions; Design
recovery clarification; allowed files; and adversarial test map. Prior event, AX, Paste split,
signing/PID, bounded text, Sign authority and zero-LAN conditions remain accepted.

Not reviewed: implementation, Keychain access behavior, generated receipts/evidence, built or
installed artifacts, hook execution, deployment, commit or push. No implementation was edited.

**Security (plan) verdict: FAIL.** The two-phase receipt prevents ordinary premature pipeline
success, but its digest does not yet bind all committable policy/content and its later source-control
states can outlive the evidence they summarize. Architecture must close both exact conditions and
receive another Security-plan PASS before Builder resumes.

## Two-Phase Receipt Remediation Amendment — exhaustive content and durable evidence

This Architecture amendment closes the two Security findings at plan SHA-256
`37abc1cb9f7c3fc19484a9ca612df7bb888c2a008bff9d374483f5607738e393` and supersedes the selective
content roots and finalize-only evidence checks.

### Exhaustive commit-stable repository digest

The canonical worktree enumerator is now exactly the union of `git ls-files -z` and
`git ls-files --others --exclude-standard -z` from the canonical repository root. It includes every
tracked and nonignored untracked regular repository file—source, tests, packaging, hooks, scripts,
assets, `README.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `SECURITY.md`, every plan, and future
committable paths. The only exclusions are explicit noncommittable roots `.git/`, `.build/`,
`.swiftpm/`, `dist/`, and the already-external private receipt/evidence directory. An ignored path is
not included; any nonignored extra path is. Symlinks, gitlinks/submodules, conflict stages, duplicate/
noncanonical paths, unsupported modes, or files escaping the root fail closed.

For each sorted raw path, hash a versioned NUL-delimited record of path, mode (`100644`/`100755`),
length, and SHA-256 bytes. Before commit, the index enumerator reads every stage-0 entry and hashes
the staged blob bytes with the same format; it rejects conflicts and requires its path/mode/hash set
to equal both worktree and receipt, which forces every nonignored file to be staged and forbids
unstaged drift. Post-commit/resume hash the complete commit tree identically. Pre-push requires
worktree, index, recorded commit tree, and receipt digests/path sets all equal. Git HEAD/index/ref
metadata is never hashed, so the exact commit transition remains valid; changing any repository byte,
mode, path, plan, policy, or extra file after preparation invalidates the receipt. Designer results
remain external private evidence—no repo file is appended after preparation.

### Frozen evidence through every authority transition

`prepared` seals a sorted automated-evidence manifest. The observer may add files only inside the
receipt's fresh private installed-evidence directory; before `observed`, it atomically seals the
union as the final evidence manifest. Each entry contains a canonical descendant path, regular-file
identity, mode, length and SHA-256; roots are 0700, files 0600, symlinks/hard-link aliases/extra or
escaping paths are rejected. No evidence contents enter the receipt.

Finalizer, pre-commit, post-commit, post-commit recovery, and pre-push must re-enumerate the private
evidence roots and require the exact frozen path/mode/length/hash set before any state transition.
Missing, added, replaced, relinked, unreadable or changed screenshots, AX reports, redacted command
evidence, or Designer evidence atomically invalidates the receipt. Thus the HMAC summary never
substitutes for the evidence it authenticates, and evidence must remain intact through push arming.

### Exact idempotent post-commit recovery

Authorize `scripts/resume-installed-signoff-commit.sh`; `.githooks/post-commit` invokes the same
single recovery implementation. `commitArmed` additionally records the exact parent commit, armed
index/tree/content digests, arm timestamp/expiry, and the current HEAD reflog tail/count/digest.

The recovery may perform only `commitArmed -> committed`. It authenticates and locks the receipt,
rehashes the exhaustive worktree/index/current commit tree and frozen evidence, and revalidates the
release/installed signature/hash/CDHash. It then requires: current HEAD differs from the recorded
parent; HEAD has exactly one parent equal to it; its tree equals the armed index and receipt content;
and exactly one new reflog transition follows the recorded tail, from that parent to current HEAD,
within the arm interval, with an ordinary `commit` action—not amend, merge, reset, rebase, checkout or
another update. No later reflog entry or commit may exist. Exact success atomically records that HEAD
as `committed`; rerunning against the same already-`committed` receipt is an idempotent success with
no mutation. Every other state/shape invalidates and requires preparation. The command never runs
Git commit/amend/reset/checkout, edits source/index, or manufactures a dummy commit.

Tests interrupt post-commit before/after each receipt write/fsync/rename, then prove exact recovery;
they also reject no commit, two commits, amend with identical tree, copied commit/tree, merge,
rebase/reset/checkout, reflog truncation/insertion, changed index/worktree/evidence/artifact, expiry,
concurrent recovery, and reuse after `committed` other than the exact idempotent query.

### Allowed files and revised conditions

Add `scripts/resume-installed-signoff-commit.sh` and its non-product fixtures/tests to the prior exact
allowed-file list. The digest enumerator/receipt helper, finalizer, observer, pipeline,
`.githooks/pre-commit`, `.githooks/post-commit`, `.githooks/pre-push`, `.gitignore`, checklist,
directly affected docs and this plan remain authorized; product/LAN scope does not expand.

Revised Builder conditions are: (1) one exhaustive path-set/digest algorithm must serve worktree,
index, commit and push; selective roots or semantic-only plan versions are forbidden; (2) no
repo-controlled file changes after preparation; (3) the frozen private evidence set must revalidate
at finalize and every commit/push transition; (4) post-commit and manual resume share the exact
non-mutating recovery algorithm above; and (5) all prior receipt privacy/HMAC, artifact, TTY/matrix,
pending exit, no-bypass workflow, event/AX/Paste, bounded text, Sign authority, zero-LAN and
downstream gate conditions remain binding.

**Two-Phase Receipt Remediation Amendment verdict: PASS.** Receipt authority now binds every
committable repository byte and cannot outlive or summarize-away its private evidence; interrupted
post-commit state has one narrowly verifiable, non-mutating recovery. Renewed Security (plan) must
independently PASS before Builder resumes.

## Design Review — Two-Phase Receipt Remediation (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`e5fc5afc0c824e5b206d436fe7a8a704d7851e4268068a0a46cc00096c73db35`. Exhaustive content/evidence
binding and the idempotent post-commit resume preserve the approved pending/manual-proof/finalization
workflow. The following usability clarification is binding: preparation must show the repository-
relative committable path count/digest and flag every nonignored untracked or unexpected path before
expensive gates, never stage it silently; status output must show receipt state, expiry, protected
evidence location, exact next command, and that moving/editing evidence forces restart.

If post-commit recording is interrupted, the hook and status command must name
`scripts/resume-installed-signoff-commit.sh` as the sole non-destructive recovery and clearly report
idempotent success or the exact restart requirement—never suggest reset, amend, bypass, or a dummy
commit. All prior guided checklist, cancellation warning, deliberate Paste/full installed matrix,
private evidence, finalizer, and commit/push UX conditions remain binding.

**Two-Phase Receipt Remediation Design Review verdict: PASS.** The amendment closes evidence/content
drift while keeping recovery understandable and non-destructive. Security (plan) must PASS before
Builder resumes.

## Security (plan) rerun — Two-Phase Receipt Remediation (2026-07-11)

Acting solely as the adversarial Security reviewer, independently reviewed the Remediation Amendment
and binding Design clarification at plan SHA-256
`034b2021396d6d3615365c594424810ab1923113b14f7cbda47c7e0b3083997f` against the two prior findings
and the complete receipt trust boundary.

### Findings

None.

The exhaustive union of tracked and nonignored untracked regular files now binds every committable
path, mode and byte—including plans/security documentation—through one worktree/index/commit-tree/
pre-push algorithm. Git metadata is excluded, so an exact content-preserving commit remains valid,
while an extra, omitted, unstaged, changed, unsupported or noncanonical path fails closed. The Design
requirement to enumerate unexpected paths before expensive gates prevents silent staging.

Automated and installed evidence now becomes a frozen private path/mode/length/hash set and is
re-enumerated at finalization, pre-commit, post-commit/recovery and pre-push. Missing, extra, linked,
replaced or changed evidence invalidates the receipt, so its authenticated summary cannot outlive the
screenshots/AX/review evidence it represents. The sole interrupted-post-commit recovery is narrowly
bound to `commitArmed`, the recorded parent/reflog tail, one ordinary child commit, exact armed tree/
index/content, unchanged evidence and artifacts, and expiry; amend, merge, reset, later commit,
copied tree or state replay cannot advance it. Its idempotent already-committed query is non-mutating.

The earlier exit-75 pending state, TTY/full-matrix authority, HMAC/private receipt, signing/hash/
CDHash/PID identity, redaction, deliberate Paste split, event/AX fixes, bounded ingress, Sign
authority and zero-LAN requirements remain binding. The revised adversarial matrix covers both prior
findings and the recovery/atomicity consequences without adding a shipping capability.

### Review boundary and verdict

Reviewed: plan algorithms, path/evidence identity, receipt states, hook/recovery transitions, Design
status requirements, allowed files and adversarial tests. Not reviewed: implementation, Keychain or
filesystem runtime behavior, generated evidence/receipts, builds, installed app, hooks, deployment,
commit or push. No implementation was edited.

**Security (plan) rerun verdict: PASS.** Both prior receipt findings are closed with implementable,
fail-closed content and evidence authority and no new plan-level blocker. Builder may resume under
the complete revised Conditions for Builder; Security (code), installed Design Sign-off, Test,
pipeline finalization, commit and push remain mandatory downstream gates.

## Builder Implementation and Verification Record (2026-07-11)

Builder implemented the approved text-entry runtime, Accessibility, packaging, real-window gate,
and two-phase installed-signoff receipt revisions. No deployment, receipt preparation, commit, or
push was performed in this phase; those actions remain downstream of independent Security (code).

### Implemented surface

- `Sources/Pebble/AppInputRouterM.swift` and `Sources/PebbleCore/Game/InputChords.swift` now assign
  one delivery identity per physical/synthesized input, deduplicate only handled-equivalent delivery,
  never use `NSEvent.eventNumber`, and fail closed when physical or modifier serial space is
  exhausted. Ordinary text follows the canonical key-down ingress after protected/global routing.
- `Sources/Pebble/UIManagerM.swift`, `Sources/Pebble/TextEntryAccessibilityM.swift`,
  `Sources/Pebble/RPGAccessibilityM.swift`, and `Sources/Pebble/main.swift` now publish text and RPG
  Accessibility children as one committed retained generation. Old retained elements are retired and
  scrubbed before replacement; focus/value/range notifications follow committed state; external old
  references fail closed; the game-view label remains exactly `Pebble menus and actions`.
- The text-input support target and all shipping text-owner adapters retain bounded scalar/UTF-8
  policies, grapheme-safe editing, capability-bound focus, owner capture/revalidation for Paste, and
  one insertion path for Recipe, Sign, Chat, slash-command, template-name, and ordinary fields.
- `scripts/package-app.sh`, `pebble`, `scripts/appkit-text-entry-integration.sh`, and
  `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift` use one fail-closed package/sign/manifest path. The
  real-window driver binds the exact staged PID/start/path/signature/hash/CDHash, performs no
  pasteboard access, exercises mouse/keyboard/AX focus and editing through Create New World, proves
  retained-element invalidation across resize, and verifies exact cleanup with no staged process.
- `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift`, the observer/finalizer/resume scripts, pipeline, and Git
  hooks implement `prepared -> observed -> finalized -> commitArmed -> committed -> pushArmed`.
  Receipt authority is Keychain-HMAC authenticated, locked, atomically published, bound to the
  exhaustive tracked plus nonignored-untracked content set, exact release/installed identities, and
  frozen private evidence. Evidence directories/files are verified as real `0700`/`0600` entries;
  installed/release files are independently revalidated as regular nonsymlink identities; ordinary
  post-commit recovery requires the exact one-child reflog transition and time interval; even the
  idempotent committed query revalidates content, evidence, index, commit, and artifacts.
- `.githooks/pre-push` composes receipt authorization with the complete existing gate in enforced
  order: receipt, source-security scan, warning-free release build, release-surface verifier, binary
  scan, real AppKit gate, XCTest, 457-check `pebsmoke`, then receipt revalidation. It does not replace
  or weaken the standard pre-push gate.
- The authorized verifier change is exactly the `Pebble` product pin
  `6c770799df8b85344994541408a1c7e23b199a21188300fc2cdf264faa51b700`.
  Storage, Core, TextInput, smoke, verifier logic, manifests, and symbol policy remain unchanged.

### Iteration record

The first final full XCTest run executed 986 tests with one failure: the composed pre-push rewrite
had preserved the XCTest command but dropped the storage suite's machine-checked `stage "xctest"`
ordering marker. Builder restored the standard stage interface and strengthened the assertion to
prove the full receipt/security/build/verifier/binary/AppKit/XCTest/pebsmoke/receipt order. The exact
failed test then passed 1/1, and the subsequent full run passed 986/986 with a separately persisted
exit status of zero.

The real-window gate initially exposed staged-process cleanup identity races. Builder replaced the
transient cleanup lookup with exact `/bin/ps` command identity, made every success/failure path clean
up explicitly, and added a final no-process-under-staging-root assertion. The final run passed and an
independent process scan found zero staged Pebble processes.

Receipt review found and closed four additional fail-closed details before handoff: actual evidence
mode verification, retry-plus-fallback installed executable lookup, strict regular/nonsymlink
release/installed identity, and exact committed/reflog/status revalidation. The receipt self-test
increased from 12 to 16 cases, and the affected source test plus security scan were rerun after those
edits.

### Empirical evidence

- `swift test --filter 'PebbleTextInputTests|InputChordTests|AppInputRouterSourceTests|TextEntrySourceTests|RPGAccessibilitySourceTests|SignCommitTokenTests'`
  -> 58 tests passed, 0 failed (21 support-target plus 37 Core/source/runtime tests).
- `swift test --filter PebbleStorageAdversarialTests.testReleaseSurfaceVerifierIsExecutableFailClosedAndIntegrated`
  -> 1 passed, 0 failed after composed-hook repair.
- `/bin/bash -o pipefail -c 'swift test > /tmp/swift-test-text-entry-exit.log 2>&1; ...'`
  -> persisted status `0`; 986 tests passed, 0 failed in 230.821 seconds. After the later receipt-only
  hardening, `swift test --filter TextEntrySourceTests` passed 11/11; the independent downstream Test
  gate must renew the full-suite result after Security (code), as required by the ordered protocol.
- `swift scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift self-test` -> 16 cases passed; exhaustive preflight
  enumerated 293 committable paths before this final documentation append and reported the digest.
- `bash scripts/security-scan.sh` -> PASS after the final receipt hardening;
  `sqlite-boundary-scan` passed all 127 production Swift files.
- `scripts/appkit-text-entry-integration.sh --no-build --executable ... --expected-hash ...` -> PASS,
  `fields=2`, `clipboard_access=0`, `cleanup=verified`, release SHA-256
  `6c770799df8b85344994541408a1c7e23b199a21188300fc2cdf264faa51b700`, signed SHA-256
  `9368c99544b49d434e750f94d2518a9ef1302eba2385d80d4f58c1386f5a645c`, CDHash
  `d8b05502ae5c1fa9f0e98730d5426e323685eef5`; post-run staged-process count was zero.
- `swift run -c release pebsmoke` -> 457 passed, 0 failed; the production build log contained no
  warning/error diagnostics.
- `scripts/verify-pebble-storage-release-surface.sh` -> exit `0`, verified; binary security scan ->
  exit `0`, passed. Exact release hashes after the final production build were:
  `Pebble` `6c770799df8b85344994541408a1c7e23b199a21188300fc2cdf264faa51b700`,
  `pebsmoke` `d989c030656377ea1dca57b563408e2f6ae739e391904bde8be25f4f1d6dde2f`,
  `PebbleStorage.o` `ed37590e383037968b25905cb7ecd1d29e8faa43ba1f62a4919baebf9aabc6ba`,
  `PebbleCore.o` `39e37366bcba82ba65131a1112ad0b262f562279e363cf621542a804bd3ae028`,
  and `PebbleTextInput.o` `547f0c44a950be0b7287ab73b918b6a3f486502e7ba57d7897e2d751b4d040ac`.
- `git diff --check` -> PASS.

### Builder boundary and verdict

Builder reviewed and exercised the implementation listed above. Builder did not independently
approve Security (code), perform the complete human Paste/twelve-field/custom-owner/adaptive/
contrast/VoiceOver/RPG installed matrix, invoke pipeline preparation, create a private receipt,
finalize deployment, stage/commit, or push. Those remain mandatory in canonical order; any finding
or source correction invalidates the affected evidence and restarts from the earliest gate.

**Builder verdict: PASS.** The approved implementation is complete and locally verified for Builder
handoff. Independent Security (code) is the next gate; deployment preparation remains blocked until
that gate passes.

## Security (code) — Final Runtime and Receipt Implementation (2026-07-11)

Acting solely as the independent adversarial Security reviewer, inspected the actual final runtime,
AX, AppKit driver, packager, receipt, hooks, pipeline, verifier, tests, and Builder record at plan
SHA-256 `d72dfa4868672626e505b3f411e200db3a97b01d9cce4668973783ce8f37b856`.

### Findings

- **[HIGH] `.githooks/pre-push:1-42`; `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift:137-159`; `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/PebbleStorageAdversarialTests.swift:485-514` ->** The required pre-push hook is mode `0644`, not executable, so Git ignores the entire receipt/security/build/AppKit/XCTest/smoke gate. The submitted tests inspect only source tokens/order and miss hook mode. After restoring `0755`, its `swift build | tee` under `/bin/sh` also observes only `tee`'s status and can continue after a failed build. Restore executable mode, make build failure propagation explicit without a non-pipefail pipeline, and require executable-mode tests for all three hooks plus a fixture whose build command exits nonzero while output succeeds.
- **[MEDIUM] `Sources/Pebble/AppInputRouterM.swift:32-44` ->** Once the keyboard delivery counter latches exhausted, `performKeyEquivalent` returns `false` before classifying F11 or active binding capture. Those protected events can escape to `super.performKeyEquivalent` rather than being consumed fail-closed as the approved exhaustion contract requires. On exhaustion, continue returning `false` only for ordinary unhandled equivalents; consume eligible F11/capture equivalents and every keyDown without dispatch. Add executable AppKit-adapter exhaustion tests, not source assertions.
- **[MEDIUM] `Sources/Pebble/RPGAccessibilityM.swift:102-155`; `Sources/Pebble/main.swift:219-231` ->** Every RPG semantic commit constructs a completely new element set, retires all current elements, and republishes the combined root array even when membership, order, screen/generation, role, and focusability are unchanged. This violates the binding retained-identity contract and resets VoiceOver context on ordinary focus/value/selection updates. Implement the same structural comparison/in-place bounded refresh used for text elements; replace/republish only for a structural identity change, while preserving retirement and fail-closed combined validation. Add executable retained-object identity tests across nonstructural RPG updates and replacement tests for structural changes.
- **[HIGH] `scripts/observe-installed-signoff.swift:63-116`; `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift:446-499` ->** Installed observation is caller-asserted, not authenticated evidence. A caller can create a mode-0700 directory containing an arbitrary `installed-signoff.json` with all booleans `true`, pass any 64-hex challenge and matching live PID/start to the public `observe` command, and have the receipt helper HMAC-sign `observed`. The challenge is caller-supplied, not minted by the prepared receipt. The guided observer itself seals only that summary JSON: it collects and requires no screenshots, AX reports, command evidence, or independent Designer evidence despite the approved frozen-evidence contract. Mint the observation challenge in `prepared`; bind it and the exact observer/checklist executable hashes in the HMAC payload; make one controlled observer-to-receipt transition that rejects caller-authored summaries; and require a nonempty per-checklist evidence manifest with actual regular `0600` screenshots/AX reports/redacted command evidence plus a distinct Designer attestation before `observed`. Test direct-command forgery, arbitrary JSON, reused challenge, omitted/swapped evidence, and observer substitution.
- **[HIGH] `scripts/pipeline.sh:79-83`; `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift:399-443` ->** Automated gate evidence is equally forgeable. Pipeline's digest is only the release hash plus constant labels, while `prepare` accepts any 64-hex value, creates `automated-gates.json` with hardcoded `PASS`, and independently sets `automatedGatesPassed = true`; it never authenticates actual command logs, exit statuses, test counts, or verifier/AppKit outputs. Seal real per-gate command identity, status, counts, artifact hashes and redacted log hashes in a private pipeline-created evidence manifest, validate the closed schema in `prepare`, and reject direct preparation without exact evidence. Derive the gate digest from those sealed bytes rather than labels.
- **[HIGH] `scripts/package-app.sh:31-81`; `scripts/pipeline.sh:65-83`; `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift:399-440` ->** The receipt does not bind the installed bundle to the release executable that passed the gates. Pipeline rehashes before packaging, but never after package/install; `prepare` records whatever release bytes exist then and receives neither the captured release hash nor package pre-sign hash. A replacement after packaging can therefore make the receipt validate a different untested release while the installed executable remains the earlier staged bundle. Pass the captured release SHA and the fail-closed package manifest into `prepare`; require its pre-sign input/staged hashes equal that SHA, the current release still equals it after install, and installed post-sign SHA/CDHash equal the same manifest. Rehash again after receipt publication and cover replacement at every boundary.
- **[HIGH] `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift:261-287,335-366,640-675` ->** HMAC authenticates bytes but provides no rollback protection. `load()` does not require `current.json` to be the canonical regular non-symlink `0600` file, and no external monotonic anchor records the latest receipt ID/state/transition. Replacing it with an earlier still-unexpired HMAC-valid copy replays an older state and defeats the promised copied/replayed-receipt rejection. Make the canonical state a Keychain-backed latest receipt ID plus monotonic transition sequence/state/MAC (or store the authoritative receipt there), require canonical `0700` roots and regular nonlinked `0600` receipt/lock files with no-follow opens, and define crash recovery across file/anchor publication. Test replay of every prior valid state, copied/symlinked/hard-linked receipts and locks, interrupted anchor/file writes, and concurrent transitions.
- **[HIGH] `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift:607-637`; `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift:174-199` ->** The claimed 16-case adversarial receipt test is a false green. It exercises stable worktree enumeration, string/path/SHA/reflog parsing and a standalone HMAC primitive, but never executes receipt save/load authentication, Keychain behavior, state transitions, evidence sealing, prepare/observe/finalize, hooks, commit recovery, push arming, tamper, replay, concurrency, or fault injection. The supplemental XCTest merely searches source strings. Extract a testable nonshipping receipt/state implementation and add real temporary-repository/private-evidence/ephemeral-key integration tests for the full approved adversarial matrix, including injected failures before and after every fsync/rename/state-anchor transition. Make the machine security gate execute them.

### Fresh evidence and boundary

Fresh reviewer execution: the focused command passed **58/58** tests; the receipt self-test reported
**16/16**; `bash scripts/security-scan.sh` passed including the 127-production-file SQLite scan; the
release-surface verifier passed; the package graph exposed only `Pebble` and `pebsmoke`; all five
release hashes exactly matched their pins; zero Sign-specific LAN symbols were found; and
`git diff --check` passed. These green results are explicitly insufficient because the findings above
show the hooks and receipt authority they purport to verify are not exercised. The Builder's real
AppKit no-Paste result was inspected but not rerun by this reviewer after blockers were confirmed.

Confirmed clean within the reviewed boundary: bounded/typed-versus-Paste text adapters and
notification-time readiness denial; MainActor one-shot Sign commit; unattended driver source has zero
general-pasteboard/Paste calls; package signing is fail-closed; current release hashes and pin-only
verifier values are exact; Storage/TextInput/verifier policy and product topology are not broadened;
and Sign-specific LAN scope remains zero.

Not reviewed or performed: complete independent full XCTest renewal, human installed Paste/full
matrix, receipt preparation/finalization, deployment, commit, push, or GitHub state. No implementation
file was edited by this reviewer.

**Security (code) verdict: FAIL.** Event exhaustion and RPG retained identity need correction, and
the non-executable/masked pre-push plus forgeable, replayable, largely untested receipt/evidence
authority make false deployment/commit/push completion possible. Security (code), Design Sign-off,
Test, pipeline preparation/finalization, deployment completion, commit and push remain blocked until
Builder fixes every finding and a fresh independent Security (code) rerun passes.

## Security (code) Remediation Architecture — authoritative state and executable proof

This Architecture revision closes all eight Security-code findings at plan SHA-256
`93347eed83ef9dc1b6f0cdbbb7cda09d0d84f2bd1de5dec89def61d0e56d6ec9`. It replaces caller-asserted
receipt JSON with one authoritative Keychain state machine, requires real evidence at every gate,
and keeps product behavior limited to the approved keyboard/AX fixes.

### Authoritative receipt/store simplification

Extract the closed state model, canonical codec, content/evidence hashing and transition validation
into a normal non-product `PebbleReleaseGate` target. Production uses
`KeychainReceiptStateStore`; tests use only an explicitly injected ephemeral store. One generic-
password Keychain item per canonical repository root contains the complete authoritative canonical
payload: schema, receipt ID, checked monotonic transition sequence, state, content/checklist/gate/
evidence digests, challenges, artifact identities, commit/push fields, expiry and an internal MAC.
Every command acquires the canonical no-follow 0600 lock, loads this latest Keychain value, validates
the expected sequence/state, computes the next checked sequence, and performs one atomic
`SecItemUpdate` (or initial `SecItemAdd`). Keychain state is authority; `current.json` is a derived
0600 nonsymlink cache/status view that is never accepted as transition input.

Crash order is: fully write/fsync required evidence, atomically advance Keychain, then regenerate the
cache by temp/fsync/rename. A crash before Keychain update leaves the prior state and orphan evidence
is removed after hash/path checks; a crash after update regenerates cache from Keychain. Old copied,
HMAC-valid JSON cannot roll back sequence/state. Canonical 0700 roots and regular single-link 0600
locks/cache/evidence are opened no-follow and revalidated before and after use. Exhaustion,
concurrent stale sequence, Keychain failure, path/link mismatch or unknown fields fail closed.

### Integrated observation and real installed evidence

Delete the public caller-asserted `observe`/JSON/boolean/challenge interface. `prepared` mints a
random one-shot observation challenge inside authoritative Keychain state and binds the exact
observer/helper executable hashes and checklist digest. The only transition is
`observe-interactive`: the authoritative helper verifies an interactive TTY, consumes no caller
summary, revalidates the prepared sequence/challenge internally, drives each fixed checklist item,
and advances to `observed` once. Reuse, different helper/checklist, partial/cancelled observation or
direct CLI field injection fails and terminally consumes/invalidates the attempt as specified.

Each checklist item must produce its schema-declared private evidence beneath the receipt's 0700
directory: at least one real Pebble-window PNG screenshot (validated PNG header/dimensions/window
identity), one external AX JSON report where applicable (closed roles/counts/focus/range/settable/
frame schema), and one redacted command-result record with command ID, exit status, counts and log
hash. Evidence files are regular single-link 0600 files. Visual-only items still require screenshot
plus redacted observation record; unavailable states are failures, not omissions. After all items,
a separate Designer TTY attestation uses a second receipt-minted one-shot challenge and records only
checklist counts, evidence-manifest digest, `PASS`, and timestamp. It cannot be supplied in the
observer summary or inferred from checks. Receipt/evidence retain no clipboard bytes or logged AX
text; private screenshots/reports are hashed and remain required through push.

### Authentic automated gates and artifact continuity

Pipeline creates a fresh private evidence directory and runs each real gate through one
`run_and_record_gate` primitive. It redirects the actual command output to a 0600 log while teeing a
redacted display copy, captures the command's direct exit status without a masking pipe, parses only
the gate's closed expected counts/status, and records command ID/version, status, counts, relevant
artifact hashes and log SHA-256 in `AutomatedGateEvidenceV1`. Required entries are source security,
warning-free release build, release-surface verifier, binary scan, no-Paste real AppKit gate, XCTest,
and 457-check `pebsmoke`; duplicates, unknown/missing fields, hardcoded labels without files, nonzero
status or wrong counts fail. `prepare` accepts only this exact private manifest, reopens and hashes
every evidence file, and derives `gateDigest` from the canonical sealed bytes—never caller booleans
or an arbitrary 64-hex value.

The release build step captures the regular nonsymlink release executable SHA before packaging.
`scripts/package-app.sh` emits its fail-closed signed manifest; pipeline passes both captured hash and
manifest to `prepare`. Prepare requires package pre-sign input/staged hashes equal the captured
release hash, current release bytes still equal it after install, and installed post-sign SHA/CDHash/
bundle/signature/seal equal the package manifest. After the authoritative Keychain `prepared` update,
pipeline rehashes release and installed artifacts again; mismatch immediately advances to
`invalidated` and exits failure. Every later transition retains the same revalidation.

### Hook, keyboard and RPG AX corrections

- Restore `.githooks/pre-commit`, `.githooks/post-commit`, and `.githooks/pre-push` to executable
  `0755`; source-control tests verify index mode, filesystem mode and `core.hooksPath`. Pre-push runs
  the existing ordered receipt/security/build/verifier/binary/AppKit/XCTest/smoke/receipt gates.
  Its build writes to a log without a pipeline (`if ! swift build ... >log 2>&1`) so the Swift exit
  status cannot be replaced by `tee`; it displays the log afterward. A temporary-repository fixture
  places a failing fake `swift` on PATH and proves a successful output consumer cannot mask failure.
- When the delivery counter is exhausted, `AppInputRouter` still classifies entry source before
  returning: every `keyDown` is consumed without dispatch; an active capture or F11
  `performKeyEquivalent` is consumed; only an ordinary unhandled equivalent returns false to AppKit.
  `flagsChanged` remains nondispatching. Put this production exhaustion disposition in the executable
  input support adapter and test protected/ordinary equivalents, keyDown, modifiers and latch/reset;
  no source-string assertion substitutes.
- `RPGAccessibilityBridge` computes a structural key from screen identity, root membership/order,
  descriptor IDs, roles, parentage, action/focusability and generation. An unchanged key refreshes
  the existing retained element objects in place with bounded value/help/enabled/selected/focused/
  frame/revision data and posts targeted notifications without `setAccessibilityChildren`.
  Structural change validates the whole combined candidate, retires/scrubs removed objects and
  republishes once. Use the production generic retention adapter in executable tests asserting `===`
  identity across nonstructural updates and replacement/old-reference denial for each structural
  dimension.

### Real integration tests and exact files

Add `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/**` and `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/**`; Package.swift exposes no new
product. Tests create real temporary Git repositories, private evidence trees and ephemeral secure
stores, invoke the actual state/codec/evidence APIs and shell hooks, and cover every state from
prepare through push. They include malformed/tampered/old cache, replay of every previously valid
state, link/path/mode attacks, direct observe/JSON/challenge forgery, helper/checklist substitution,
missing/swapped evidence, gate-manifest forgery, artifact replacement at every boundary, concurrent
transitions, sequence exhaustion, and injected failures before/after every evidence fsync, Keychain-
store update and cache rename. Commit/reflog/resume and hook failure fixtures use actual temporary
repos. `scripts/security-scan.sh` must execute this suite and fail if it is absent or skipped.

Authorized edits are limited to `Package.swift`, `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/**`,
`Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/**`, `Sources/Pebble/AppInputRouterM.swift`,
`Sources/Pebble/RPGAccessibilityM.swift`, `Sources/Pebble/main.swift`, the shared production input/
retention support target and its tests, `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift`, the observer/
finalizer/resume scripts and checklist, `scripts/pipeline.sh`, `scripts/package-app.sh`,
`scripts/security-scan.sh`, all three `.githooks`, focused existing source/integration tests, and
directly affected docs/this plan. Verifier edits remain the already approved Pebble pin only;
Storage/Core/TextInput/smoke pins, verifier logic/surfaces, product topology, LAN and Sign protocol
scope remain frozen.

### Risk-to-test map and Conditions for Builder

- Hook omission/masked build -> executable-mode assertions plus real failing-build hook fixture.
- Exhausted input escape -> executable production disposition tests for every AppKit entry class.
- VoiceOver identity churn -> retained-object identity and structural replacement/retirement tests.
- Forged observation -> Keychain-minted one-shot challenge, integrated observer, per-item evidence,
  distinct Designer attestation and direct-forgery/substitution tests.
- Forged automated gates/artifact TOCTOU -> actual logs/status/counts/hash manifest, closed prepare
  validation and release/package/install rehash tests at every boundary.
- Receipt rollback/link/concurrency -> authoritative monotonic Keychain state, disposable cache,
  no-follow identities and fault/concurrency/replay tests.
- False self-test -> temporary-repository/ephemeral-store end-to-end transitions executed by the
  machine security gate, not source-token assertions.

Builder conditions are: (1) no file/JSON/caller field is receipt authority; only monotonic Keychain
state advances; (2) no public observe-summary/challenge path remains; (3) every checklist and gate
has actual validated private evidence and Designer is a distinct attestation; (4) release identity is
captured before packaging and rehashed after install, after receipt write and at every transition;
(5) hooks are executable and propagate actual command failures; (6) exhaustion consumes protected/
keyDown input fail-closed and nonstructural RPG AX updates preserve identity; (7) the real integration
suite passes under `security-scan.sh`; and (8) zero unattended clipboard/Paste access, explicit
installed manual Paste, bounded ingress, AX retirement, receipt privacy, pin-only release surface,
zero LAN, and all downstream review/Test/deploy/commit/push gates remain binding.

**Security (code) Remediation Architecture verdict: PASS.** All eight findings now have simplified,
implementable ownership boundaries and executable closing evidence. Build remains blocked until
renewed Design Review and Security (plan) PASS this revision.

## Design Review — Security-code Remediation Architecture (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`9a84b93ce251cbc60a8e056c0d0725314657986f942b421b54268f2477bc00b4`. The authoritative store,
integrated evidence, hook fixes, input-exhaustion behavior, and retained RPG objects preserve the
approved product and release workflow. These clarifications are binding:

1. Nonstructural RPG updates retain exact objects and VoiceOver position. When a same-screen
   generation change legitimately replaces them, atomically publish same-ID replacements and hand
   focus only to the truthful replacement after layout; never hand focus across screen/collision/
   failure. Text-before-RPG ordering, `Pebble menus and actions`, roles, and retirement privacy remain
   exact.
2. The integrated observer guides and observes the full matrix but never performs the manual Paste,
   reads clipboard data, or infers Designer approval. It preserves the prior item-by-item progress,
   expiry, cancel/restart and no-restoration prompts; screenshots/AX/redacted records stay private and
   sufficient for independent visual review, while receipt/status output contains no entered text.
3. Keychain authority and derived-cache recovery must remain actionable: every command reports only
   the current state, expiry, exact next or resume command, and restart condition. Cache regeneration,
   observer/finalizer interruption, and post-commit recovery are non-destructive and never suggest
   bypass/reset/amend/dummy commits. Manual Paste, the complete installed matrix, and final Designer
   attestation remain mandatory regardless of automated evidence.

**Security-code Remediation Design Review verdict: PASS.** With these conditions binding, the
remediation closes false authority and test gaps without sacrificing VoiceOver stability, private
human evidence, or understandable pending/recovery UX. Security (plan) must PASS before Build.

## Security (plan) — Security-code Remediation Architecture (2026-07-11)

Independently reviewed the remediation Architecture and binding Design corrections at plan SHA-256
`36c6b67626319a1ba73c1c96547f5614168c27fd57c7e7000e79c1ce3d09b3b4` against all eight Security-code
findings and the earlier bounded-ingress, AX privacy, receipt, pin-only, and zero-LAN invariants.

The proposed monotonic Keychain authority, derived nonauthoritative cache, evidence-before-state
crash order, no-follow private identities, integrated one-shot observation, distinct Designer
attestation, per-item installed evidence, closed automated-gate schema, release/package/install
rehashing, executable hooks with direct status propagation, protected exhaustion disposition, and
structural RPG retention algorithm are implementable and close their corresponding code defects.
They do not weaken the unattended zero-Paste boundary, manual installed Paste/full matrix, Sign
authority, product topology, or frozen verifier surfaces.

### Finding

- **[MEDIUM] `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md:4017-4032,4099-4113` ->** The authoritative store is the
  central security boundary, but the plan says tests use only an injected ephemeral store. That can
  prove the state machine while leaving the production `KeychainReceiptStateStore` query identity,
  access class, add/update/load persistence, duplicate handling, cross-process visibility, and error
  mapping completely unexecuted. A broken or accidentally file-backed production store could pass
  the entire proposed suite. Add a mandatory macOS integration test that exercises the actual
  `KeychainReceiptStateStore` with a random test-only service/account that can never equal the
  canonical production account: add, reload from a second process, checked sequence/state update,
  stale update rejection under the canonical lock, access-class verification, delete/recreate,
  unavailable/locked/failure behavior, and cleanup verification. It must never read, update, or
  delete production receipt state, must fail if test residue remains, and must run from
  `security-scan.sh` alongside the ephemeral fault-injection matrix. Keep ephemeral injection for
  exhaustive crash/fault tests; it does not substitute for this production-adapter proof.

Review boundary: plan only. No implementation, Keychain item, build, runtime evidence, deployment,
or source-control state was created or changed.

**Security (plan) verdict: FAIL.** Seven remediation boundaries are approved, but the production
Keychain authority itself needs mandatory isolated integration evidence before Build. After adding
that exact test boundary, renew Security (plan); all Security (code), Design Sign-off, Test, pipeline,
deployment, receipt, commit, and push gates remain blocked.

## Production Keychain Store Test Amendment

Add `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/KeychainReceiptStateStoreIntegrationTests.swift` and the smallest
test-only helper executable under `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/Fixtures/KeychainStoreProbe/`. The
test must instantiate the actual production `KeychainReceiptStateStore`, never an ephemeral-store
substitute, with a cryptographically random, nonproduction service/account namespace carrying an
explicit test prefix. The store must reject a test identity equal to or sharing the canonical
production service/account, and the test may not enumerate, read, update, or delete any production
Keychain item or key.

Using separate helper processes, the mandatory integration test proves: initial add and load;
cross-process visibility; checked monotonic sequence/state update; rejection of a stale sequence
under the canonical lock without changing the stored value; the required Keychain accessibility
class; delete, confirmed absence, and clean recreate; deterministic mapping of duplicate,
unavailable/locked, authorization, malformed-data, and persistence failures; and final deletion with
an independent confirmed-absence check. Test setup first removes only its newly generated identity,
and a `defer`/termination cleanup path always removes only that identity on success, assertion
failure, thrown error, timeout, or child-process failure. Residue is a test failure and is reported
without secret payloads.

`scripts/security-scan.sh` must build and execute this macOS-only production-adapter integration test
in addition to the ephemeral store fault/crash matrix, fail closed if it is skipped or unsupported on
the release host, and propagate its real exit status. No production receipt may exist as a
prerequisite, and the test must remain safe when production state does exist. Exhaustive fault
injection continues to use the ephemeral adapter; it does not replace this test.

Conditions for Builder: keep production/test Keychain identities structurally disjoint; pass random
identity only through the test-only initializer or dependency boundary; expose no product command
for arbitrary service/account access; use the production codec, locking, access class, error mapping,
and `SecItemAdd`/load/checked-update/delete paths unchanged; ensure every child inherits only the
random test identity; guarantee bounded timeout and cleanup; and add no new product, LAN, clipboard,
Paste, receipt-bypass, or verifier surface. Exact authorized files are
`Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/KeychainReceiptStateStoreIntegrationTests.swift`, its test-only fixture,
the minimum test-only initializer/dependency seam in `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/**`, `Package.swift`
only if the helper target requires it, and `scripts/security-scan.sh`.

**Production Keychain Store Test Amendment verdict: PASS.** This mandatory isolated integration
boundary closes the sole Security-plan gap; Build remains blocked until Security (plan) is renewed
and passes this amendment.
