## Builder Remediation Implementation and Evidence (2026-07-11)

Builder implemented the approved remediation for all eight Security-code findings. This record
supersedes the earlier Builder PASS. No pipeline preparation, production receipt, installed human
matrix, deployment, commit, or push was performed; independent Security (code) is next.

### Finding closure

1. All three hooks and their release-build helper are mode `0755`. Pre-push calls
   `scripts/prepush-release-build.sh`, which captures `swift build` directly without a pipeline,
   displays its log only after status capture, and blocks nonzero status or warnings. A real fake-
   `swift` fixture exits 42 while printing output and proves the hook helper remains nonzero.
2. `PebbleAppSupport` owns executable exhausted-input classification. Exhausted `keyDown`, F11, and
   active capture are consumed without dispatch; ordinary unhandled equivalents alone pass through;
   modifier changes remain nondispatching. Matrix tests execute the production function.
3. `RPGAccessibilityBridge` now computes a structural key from screen/layout generation, ordered
   IDs, roles, parentage, actionability, and focusability. Equal structure refreshes exact retained
   objects in place and posts targeted notifications without root publication. Structural changes
   replace once, retire/scrub old objects, and preserve combined text-before-RPG validation.
4. Caller-authored `observe`, summary JSON, boolean, PID/start, and challenge arguments are deleted.
   The same stable compiled release-gate binary owns Keychain and `observe-interactive`, verifies its
   own binary/source/checklist digests and Keychain-minted one-shot observer/Designer challenges,
   performs no Paste, and advances once. Fourteen checklist items each require a real Pebble-window
   PNG, a private command/hash record, and external AX role/count/focus/settable/frame evidence where
   declared; a separate Designer challenge writes the final attestation.
5. Pipeline runs seven gates through one direct-status recorder and seals real private redacted logs,
   command IDs, statuses, counts, artifact hashes, and log hashes. Closed-schema validation requires
   security, warning-free build, release surface, binary scan, two-field no-Paste AppKit proof,
   nonzero XCTest count, and exactly 457 smoke checks; arbitrary digests/labels cannot prepare.
6. Preparation accepts the captured pre-package release hash, fail-closed package manifest, and gate
   evidence directory. It requires release/pre-sign/staged continuity, signed installed hash/CDHash/
   bundle/seal identity, then rehashes release and installed bytes after authoritative publication.
7. `PebbleReleaseGate` makes one generic-password Keychain item authoritative. Receipt ID, checked
   monotonic sequence/state, closed payload, challenges, digests, artifacts, expiry, commit, and push
   fields live there; JSON is regenerated cache only. A stable integrated binary preserves default
   current-application ACL ownership. Lock/cache/evidence identities use `O_NOFOLLOW`, `0700`/`0600`,
   regular-file and single-link checks. Restart advances rather than resets sequence; stale,
   concurrent, malformed, replayed-cache, linked, mode, fault, and exhaustion cases fail closed.
8. The false string/HMAC self-test is removed. `PebbleReleaseGateTests` executes the real codec,
   ephemeral store, transitions, cache replay, concurrency, injected before/after-update failures,
   link/mode attacks, gate evidence, package continuity, and hook fixture. The mandatory real
   Keychain test compiles one random test-only stable probe and launches separate instances for
   delete/absence, add/load, checked update, stale rejection, an actual Security-framework match on
   `kSecAttrAccessibleAfterFirstUnlockThisDeviceOnly` (`cku`), delete/recreate, final confirmed
   absence, and cleanup. Production identity cannot be selected by the test seam.

The runtime kernels live in non-product `PebbleAppSupport`, preserving the frozen
`PebbleTextInput.o` pin. `PebbleReleaseGate` is also non-product. `swift package describe` exposes
exactly `Pebble` and `pebsmoke`; zero LAN or Sign protocol surface was added.

## Security (code) rerun — Complete Remediation Diff (2026-07-11)

Acting only as the independent adversarial Security reviewer, reinspected the current runtime,
Accessibility, release-gate, Keychain, observer, evidence, packaging, hook, pipeline, test, product,
LAN, verifier, and Builder-record bytes at plan SHA-256
`58e8aa8f28670af059d6db9fc86f3ae2f8ae8ff2809cebc7fb30f4c7b331b96b`.

### Findings

- **[HIGH] `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/ReleaseGate.swift:416-452`;
  `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift:184-197`;
  `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/ReleaseGateTests.swift:169-183` ->** Automated gate evidence is still
  caller-forgeable. The validator authenticates only caller-written bytes, caller-written status and
  caller-written counts; it records no closed executable/argv/tool-version identity and has no
  runner-minted one-shot authority. Its own passing test creates seven arbitrary files containing
  only `redacted <id> PASS`, supplies invented counts/status, and proves they are accepted. A caller
  can therefore invoke public `prepare` with fabricated `0600` logs without running any gate. Move
  gate execution and sealing behind one authoritative runner transition (or mint and consume a
  Keychain-held one-shot gate challenge), bind the exact reviewed argv/tool/build identities and
  direct exit/count parsers, and make `prepare` reject a merely schema-conforming caller directory.
  Add an actual direct-prepare forgery test using the current fake-log construction and require it to
  fail.

- **[HIGH] `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/ReleaseGate.swift:464-486`;
  `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift:147-159,184-219` ->** Package/artifact continuity is not
  closed. `PackageManifest.validate` never requires `executable_path` to equal the canonical
  `/Applications/Pebble.app/Contents/MacOS/Pebble` beneath its declared bundle, so a forged closed-
  field manifest can combine a valid signed Pebble bundle with an unrelated file whose hash becomes
  authoritative. Subsequent `fileDigest` calls use path-following `Data(contentsOf:)` without
  no-follow open/fstat/revalidation, permitting file/symlink substitution between identity checks.
  Canonically derive the installed executable from the fixed bundle instead of accepting it, reject
  every path/link/type/mode mismatch, hash stable no-follow descriptors with pre/post identity checks,
  and test unrelated executable, symlink/hard-link, rename and byte replacement at every capture,
  package, install, prepare, publication and later-transition boundary.

- **[HIGH] `scripts/observe-installed-signoff.swift:37-49,97-110,112-172` ->** The integrated observer
  does not bind the observed *running process* to the prepared installed identity. It accepts an
  active application by bundle ID and bundle URL, while `validateCurrent` hashes only current disk
  bytes; a stale process launched before bundle replacement can supply all screenshots/AX evidence.
  The purported independent Designer attestation is also collected in the same observer process and
  terminal from whoever just completed observation, and each command record hardcodes status `0`
  without the required real command counts/log hash. Bind PID, process start, canonical executable,
  dynamic code-signing identity/CDHash and installed receipt identity before every capture; make the
  Designer consume the second one-shot challenge through a distinct invocation/identity after the
  observer exits; and validate the closed evidence schema and actual per-item results before the
  authoritative transition. Test stale live process, PID reuse, observer substitution, same-session
  Designer forgery, reused challenges and missing/swapped evidence.

- **[MEDIUM] `Sources/Pebble/AppInputRouterM.swift:106-132`;
  `Tests/PebbleAppSupportTests/PebbleAppSupportTests.swift:4-10` ->** Exhaustion remains dispatchable
  through the real `flagsChanged` entry point. That method never checks `routingSerialExhausted` and
  can synthesize and route modifier edges after the latch; the pure four-row helper test does not
  execute this production state or prove latch/reset behavior. Guard the production modifier path so
  exhaustion updates only nondispatching modifier bookkeeping, extract an executable stateful adapter
  if needed, and test the actual latch across protected/ordinary equivalents, keyDown, modifiers,
  release/reset and repeated post-exhaustion calls.

- **[MEDIUM] `Sources/Pebble/RPGAccessibilityM.swift:42-56,141-152,239-249`;
  `Tests/PebbleAppSupportTests/PebbleAppSupportTests.swift:11-25` ->** The nonstructural RPG refresh
  retains object allocation but not the promised stable Accessibility relationship. Every refresh
  first reparents every nested child to `GameView`, then calls `setAccessibilityChildren` and reparents
  it again; this is exactly the publication/reset operation the approved in-place path forbids and can
  disturb VoiceOver context. The executable test compares only value-type structural keys and never
  asserts retained AppKit object identity or parent/child publication behavior. Preserve the existing
  parent graph without any child publication for an equal structural key and add real bridge tests
  proving `===` identity, stable parentage/no root-or-child publication on nonstructural changes, plus
  replacement and retired-reference denial for every structural dimension.

- **[HIGH] `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/ReleaseGateTests.swift:41-220`;
  `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/KeychainReceiptStateStoreIntegrationTests.swift:37-87`;
  `scripts/security-scan.sh:104-109` ->** The prior false-green test finding is only partially closed.
  The suite has no executable prepare/observe/finalize/precommit/postcommit/resume/prepush path, no
  direct observation or gate-forgery test, no helper/checklist/evidence substitution, no artifact
  replacement boundary matrix, no temporary-repository commit/reflog recovery, and no injected fault
  before/after each evidence fsync, rename and cache publication. The Keychain test now exercises real
  add/load/update/delete across processes, but stale update is sequential and outside the canonical
  coordinator lock, while unavailable/locked/authorization/malformed/persistence cases merely call
  the private status mapper rather than inducing production-adapter behavior. Implement the complete
  approved executable matrix and make `security-scan.sh` fail closed on every category, not merely on
  these eleven tests existing.

### Fresh evidence and review boundary

All three hooks and `scripts/prepush-release-build.sh` are currently `0755`; the direct failing-build
fixture passes, so the original hook/status-masking finding is closed in the reviewed bytes. The
exhausted keyDown/F11/capture pure disposition and RPG structural-key separation pass, but the real
adapter/bridge gaps above remain. The release-surface verifier and binary scan pass. Exact hashes are
`Pebble` `f5eb66575e17a7bf1ea31427c2021968c633b7e779c8f9fcba9329a7de33ac90`,
`pebsmoke` `d989c030656377ea1dca57b563408e2f6ae739e391904bde8be25f4f1d6dde2f`,
`PebbleStorage.o` `ed37590e383037968b25905cb7ecd1d29e8faa43ba1f62a4919baebf9aabc6ba`,
`PebbleCore.o` `39e37366bcba82ba65131a1112ad0b262f562279e363cf621542a804bd3ae028`,
`PebbleTextInput.o` `547f0c44a950be0b7287ab73b918b6a3f486502e7ba57d7897e2d751b4d040ac`,
and evidence-only `PebbleAppSupport.o`
`57d91481a1947e0798ceed302c8cd66e288675ad9b8298896fac50b228957d63`. Package products remain exactly
`Pebble` and `pebsmoke`; the remediation targets add no LAN/Sign protocol symbol, so pin-only product
topology and zero-LAN scope pass.

During this review, `KeychainReceiptStateStore.accessibilityClass()` changed after a fresh focused run
had failed its two accessibility assertions. That mutation invalidated both the failed snapshot and
the Builder's earlier evidence. Against the new bytes, a fresh focused run passed ReleaseGate/actual
Keychain **11/11** and AppSupport **2/2**, and a fresh `bash scripts/security-scan.sh` passed including
127 production Swift files. These green results do not exercise or close the findings above. The
verifier pass predates only the nonproduct Keychain-method edit and remains product-relevant; every
broader downstream Test/build claim must nevertheless be renewed after the implementation stabilizes.
`git diff --check` passed.

Review boundary: no implementation, installed observation, production receipt, deployment, commit,
or push was performed by Security. No implementation file was edited by this reviewer.

**Security (code) rerun verdict: FAIL.** Hook execution, current Keychain happy-path integration,
pin-only products and zero-LAN scope pass, but the automated and installed evidence authorities remain
forgeable, artifact/process continuity is incomplete, exhaustion and retained-AX contracts are not
fully implemented, and the mandatory adversarial suite remains materially absent. Design Sign-off,
Test/pipeline receipt preparation, deployment, commit and push remain blocked until all findings are
fixed and a fresh independent Security (code) rerun passes against stable bytes.

## Security-code Remediation Revision II — self-executing authority and stable runtime identity

This Architecture revision closes the six findings above by removing caller-created proof from every
authoritative transition. The release-gate binary executes and observes its own fixed commands;
installed observation and independent Designer approval become separate states and processes; stable
file, process, input, and Accessibility identities are required rather than inferred.

### Self-executing prepare and canonical artifact continuity

`PebbleReleaseGate` owns one `run-prepare-gates` transition. Starting from no receipt, its authoritative
binary acquires the canonical coordinator lock, mints the Keychain-held attempt/challenge, and itself
executes exactly seven reviewed gates in fixed order: source security scan; warning-free clean release
build; release-surface verifier; binary security scan; real no-Paste AppKit integration; full XCTest;
and 457-check `pebsmoke`. It constructs each argv from compiled closed command IDs, resolves and
records executable identity/version, launches with a sanitized environment and canonical repository,
captures direct termination status, parses the closed expected counts, writes/fsyncs private logs,
and hashes the logs internally before atomically advancing to `prepared`. No public `prepare`, log
directory, digest, PASS boolean, status/count, argv, executable, or evidence-manifest input remains;
schema-correct caller files have no authority and cannot trigger a transition. Pipeline only invokes
this command and displays its redacted status.

Before packaging, the same transition opens the canonical clean release executable no-follow,
requires a regular single-link executable file, hashes a stable descriptor with matching pre/post
device/inode/size/mtime identity, and binds that digest. `scripts/package-app.sh` returns its manifest
directly to the still-running authority. The installed bundle is fixed exactly at
`/Applications/Pebble.app`; the installed executable is derived, never accepted, as the canonical
strict child `/Applications/Pebble.app/Contents/MacOS/Pebble`. Canonicalization, parent identity,
regular-file/single-link/mode, bundle signing, requirement, CDHash, package pre/post-sign hashes and
captured release digest must all agree. Every prepare publication and later transition repeats stable
no-follow descriptor hashing before and after the Keychain update; symlink, hard link, rename, mount/
parent change, unrelated path, byte replacement or package-manifest disagreement invalidates the
attempt. Exact implementation files: `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/ReleaseGate.swift`,
`scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift`, `scripts/pipeline.sh`, and `scripts/package-app.sh`.

### Bound live observation and independent Designer transition

`observe-interactive` verifies before every checklist capture that the live Pebble PID has the same
start identity, has not been reused, resolves to that exact canonical installed executable, and has
the expected bundle/signing requirement, dynamic code-signing identity, CDHash and installed SHA from
the prepared receipt. Each screenshot, AX report and command record is generated by a fixed internal
operation with its real status/count/log hash; no hardcoded result or caller summary is accepted.
After closed-schema evidence validation and challenge consumption, it advances only to
`observedPendingDesigner`, exits, and cannot attest design.

A later `designer-attest` invocation requires a new interactive TTY, distinct process identity and
the second Keychain-held one-shot challenge. It revalidates artifacts and receipt sequence, opens and
reviews the sealed checklist/evidence set, records a closed PASS/FAIL attestation without entered
text, and alone advances `observedPendingDesigner` to `observed`. Same-process/session assertion,
challenge reuse, observer/helper/checklist substitution, missing/swapped evidence, stale/PID-reused
process or changed signature/hash/CDHash fails closed. Exact files are
`scripts/observe-installed-signoff.swift`, its shell wrapper, the checklist, the receipt helper/core,
and a separate `scripts/designer-attest-installed-signoff.swift` plus shell wrapper. Finalize, resume,
and hooks accept only authoritative `observed`. Manual Paste remains a human installed-app action;
both unattended helpers retain zero clipboard/Paste access.

### Exhausted input and stable RPG Accessibility

`Sources/Pebble/AppInputRouterM.swift` must consult the same latched state at every entry point.
After exhaustion, `flagsChanged` may update only bounded internal modifier bookkeeping and must never
synthesize or dispatch an edge; keyDown and protected F11/capture equivalents remain consumed without
dispatch, ordinary unhandled equivalents alone return false, and only the defined reset releases the
latch. The stateful production adapter, not a value-only table, is exercised through actual entry
methods in `Tests/PebbleAppSupportTests/PebbleAppSupportTests.swift`.

For an equal RPG structural key, `Sources/Pebble/RPGAccessibilityM.swift` changes only bounded scalar
Accessibility attributes on the already retained objects. It must not call any parent/children/root
setter, reparent descendants, rebuild root arrays, or publish children; existing root, parent and
child identities remain untouched. Structural changes alone build and validate a complete candidate,
publish once, retire old references and deny their actions/focus. Real bridge seams in
`Sources/PebbleAppSupport/**` and AppSupport tests assert `===` object identity, unchanged parent/
children/root publication counters for every nonstructural field, and replacement plus retired-
reference denial for every structural dimension.

### Mandatory executable adversarial matrix

Expand `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/ReleaseGateTests.swift` with an actual injectable command runner
that executes temporary helper programs, and add temporary-repository integration tests that invoke
the real receipt commands, observers, finalizer, resume script and all three hooks. Required cases are
the seven successful real command transitions; nonzero/missing/wrong-version/wrong-argv/wrong-count/
timeout runner failures; direct fake-log/digest/PASS/public-prepare forgery; observer/checklist/helper/
Designer/challenge/evidence substitution; stale process and PID reuse; unrelated/symlink/hard-link/
rename/byte-replaced artifacts at capture, package, install, prepare, state publication and every later
transition; commit/reflog/precommit/postcommit/prepush recovery in real temporary Git repositories;
concurrent stale transitions; and injected failure before/after each log/evidence fsync, rename,
Keychain update and derived-cache publication with deterministic recovery.

`KeychainReceiptStateStoreIntegrationTests.swift` must additionally induce production-adapter stale
concurrency under the real coordinator lock, malformed payload, duplicate, unavailable/locked,
authorization and persistence failures through injectable Security-operation boundaries—not by
calling a status mapper—and confirm cross-process visibility, access class, identity-only cleanup and
no residue after every failure. `scripts/security-scan.sh` executes every named category and fails
closed if any test, fixture, subprocess, cleanup assertion or category is absent/skipped.

### Risk map and Conditions for Builder

- Caller-forged gates -> one binary executes fixed seven argv sets and mints/seals all evidence.
- Artifact/path TOCTOU -> fixed bundle/executable, canonical strict-child validation, stable no-follow
  descriptors, package/signature/CDHash binding and pre/post-transition rehashes.
- Forged or stale human proof -> live process identity per capture, pending-Designer intermediate
  state, separate TTY/process/challenge and sealed-evidence review.
- Exhaustion escape -> all actual AppKit entries share one latch; modifiers never dispatch exhausted.
- VoiceOver reset -> equal structural key permits attribute mutation only and zero tree publication.
- False-green coverage -> actual runners, scripts, repositories, process/artifact forgeries, faults,
  recovery and production Keychain failure/cleanup are machine-enforced.

Builder conditions: (1) delete every caller-evidence prepare interface; (2) keep command identities,
argv, versions, parsers, statuses, counts and log hashes internally generated and closed; (3) derive
the sole installed path from exact `/Applications/Pebble.app` and use stable no-follow identities;
(4) require `observedPendingDesigner` and a later distinct Designer process before `observed`; (5)
permit only scalar attribute mutation during nonstructural RPG refresh and no exhausted modifier
dispatch; (6) make every adversarial category execute under `security-scan.sh`; (7) preserve the
authoritative monotonic Keychain state, disposable cache, executable hooks, private evidence, manual
installed Paste, zero unattended clipboard/Paste, bounded ingress, Sign one-shot authority, frozen
pin-only verifier/product surface and zero LAN; and (8) any product hash delta beyond the approved
final Pebble pin, or any Storage/Core/TextInput/pebsmoke delta, returns to Architecture and Security.

Authorized edits are limited to the files named above, `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/**`,
`Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/**`, `Sources/PebbleAppSupport/**`, the directly affected AppSupport
tests, receipt/observer/designer/finalize/resume/pipeline/package/security scripts, all three hooks,
and this plan. No new shipping product, protocol, LAN path or verifier surface is authorized.

**Security-code Remediation Revision II verdict: PASS.** All six findings now have one implementable
authority and executable closing evidence. Build remains blocked until renewed Design Review and
Security (plan) PASS this revision; all later gates remain mandatory.

## Design Review — Security-code Remediation Revision II (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`aa7e3a87cbeb6bd194dde5dc45d4df94d07b39bc76d23168277cd31d02424fdd`. Scalar-only RPG refresh
and the separate `observedPendingDesigner` transition preserve the approved experience with these
binding clarifications:

1. Equal-key RPG updates retain object, root, parent, child, VoiceOver focus and navigation context
   with zero tree publication. Structural replacement retains the prior atomic same-screen/same-ID
   focus handoff after layout and never transfers focus across screens or failure.
2. `observedPendingDesigner` is visibly pending, never success. Observer exit names the exact
   Designer command, remaining expiry and sealed evidence location. Separation requires a later
   process and one-shot challenge, not a different macOS user/login or unnecessary new terminal; the
   same authenticated console may perform the deliberate sequential handoff.
3. The observer still guides item progress, cancel/restart consequences and the genuinely manual
   Paste without reading or posting clipboard content. Designer attestation presents a navigable
   redacted evidence index with private screenshots/AX reports, never asks the user to repeat passed
   actions, and records no entered text. FAIL, interruption and recovery state the exact safe restart
   or resume command. The complete installed matrix remains mandatory before `observed`.

**Security-code Remediation Revision II Design Review verdict: PASS.** The revision preserves stable
VoiceOver identity and makes observation versus independent Designer approval clear, private and
recoverable. Security (plan) must PASS before Build.

## Security (plan) — Security-code Remediation Revision II (2026-07-11)

Independently reviewed Revision II and its Design Review at plan SHA-256
`692d937e04f420170b83fa6f5568757f079594ce2c22612f896f5aa92cc50cb2` against all six blocking
Security-code findings, the authoritative monotonic-Keychain boundary, and the Pebble ordered-gate
requirements.

### Findings

None.

The self-executing authority removes caller-authored gate logs, status/counts, argv and manifests from
the transition boundary; its fixed seven-command runner, Keychain-held attempt, internally sealed
evidence and direct status/count parsing are implementable and close the prepare forgery. Fixed bundle
and derived strict-child executable paths, no-follow stable descriptors, package/signature/CDHash
binding and pre/post-transition identity checks close the artifact and path races. The intermediate
`observedPendingDesigner` state, per-capture live process/signature identity, closed installed evidence
and later one-shot Designer process close stale-process and same-process attestation authority without
automating Paste or exposing clipboard bytes.

The same-latch actual-entry contract closes exhausted modifier dispatch. Scalar-only equal-key RPG
refresh with zero parent/child/root publication closes the retained VoiceOver relationship reset. The
mandatory runner, CLI, temporary-repository, process/artifact substitution, transition/fault/recovery
and production-Keychain integration matrices provide executable closing evidence for the prior false-
green gap. The plan preserves monotonic sequence across attempts, private evidence and disposable
cache behavior, executable hooks, bounded ingress, Sign one-shot authority, the one approved Pebble
pin, all other frozen artifacts/products, and zero LAN scope.

Conditions for Build are the Revision II conditions exactly as written: no caller-evidence prepare
surface; no caller-selected command/path/result; no state transition before durable evidence and
stable pre/post identity checks; no direct `prepared -> observed` path; no equal-key Accessibility tree
setter; no post-exhaustion modifier dispatch; and no skipped adversarial category. The Keychain
`preparing`/attempt recovery must advance rather than reset authority, clean only identity-bound orphan
evidence, and deterministically resume or invalidate after interruption. Any relaxation, new product,
LAN/protocol surface, verifier expansion, or frozen hash delta invalidates this approval.

Review boundary: plan and implementability only. No implementation, build, Keychain item, installed
observation, deployment, commit, or push was performed by Security.

**Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** Revision II provides bounded, implementable fixes and mandatory
machine evidence for all six findings. Builder may proceed only within the authorized files and
conditions; fresh Security (code), installed Design Sign-off, independent Test, pipeline/receipt,
deployment, commit and push gates remain mandatory against stable final bytes.

## Revision II Stale Test-contract Reconciliation Amendment

The five reproducible failures at `PebbleStorageAdversarialTests.swift:480` and
`TextEntrySourceTests.swift:18,140,184,194` assert superseded implementation ownership. They must be
replaced one-for-one with the stronger Revision II contracts below; every other passing assertion in
both tests remains unchanged. No test may be deleted, skipped, renamed out of a gate, or weakened to
mere token presence when an ordering/absence invariant is specified.

### Exact assertion replacements

1. In
   `PebbleStorageAdversarialTests.testReleaseSurfaceVerifierIsExecutableFailClosedAndIntegrated`,
   retain the executable/verifier-content and complete pre-push ordering assertions. Replace only the
   obsolete pipeline `swift build -> verifier -> binary` block. Assert that `scripts/pipeline.sh`
   contains exactly one `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.sh run-prepare-gates`, ordered before
   `installed-signoff-receipt.sh verify-current`, `PENDING_INSTALLED_SIGNOFF`, and `exit 75`; contains
   no direct `swift package clean`, release verifier, binary scanner, AppKit driver, `swift test`,
   `pebsmoke`, `pebble install`, `RELEASE_HASH=`, caller log/digest/count/status, or PASS input; and
   uses `set -euo pipefail`. Read `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift` and assert its
   `closedGateSpecs` range contains exactly seven command IDs in this exact order:
   `source-security`, `release-build`, `release-surface`, `binary-scan`, `appkit-text-entry`,
   `xctest`, `pebsmoke`. In `runAuthoritativePrepare`, require ordering of `runner.run` before
   nonzero-status rejection, count parsing, private log write and sealed entry; all seven entries and
   gate evidence validation before install; and install/artifact validation before
   `preparing -> prepared`. Thus integration remains fail-closed even though the pipeline no longer
   owns individual gates.

2. In `TextEntrySourceTests.testCanonicalKeyDownOwnsOrdinaryTextAndPreflightDoesNotFingerprintIt`,
   replace the stale `pebbleExhaustedInputDisposition(` expectation. Require AppInputRouter's owned
   `private let exhaustionLatch = PebbleAppInputExhaustionLatch()`; require
   `exhaustionLatch.disposition` before the protected-equivalent branch; require
   `exhaustionLatch.recordFlagsChanged` before either release or `modifierSynthesizer.update` in the
   `flagsChanged` span; require `routingSerialExhausted = true` before `exhaustionLatch.exhaust()` on
   serial overflow; and require the defined reset to clear both the serial flag and
   `exhaustionLatch.resetInputSession()`. Assert the router does not call the old stateless helper.
   Preserve the existing ordinary-keyDown, protected-equivalent, unmapped-text, identity-ledger and
   bounded-ingress assertions.

3. Replace the stale direct-build portion of
   `TextEntrySourceTests.testMachineGatesPinOneReleaseBuildBeforeAppKitAndDeploy` with the same
   ownership split, while retaining its hook assertions. In pipeline, assert exact
   `run-prepare-gates -> verify-current -> pending message -> exit 75` ordering and absence of direct
   gate/deploy/evidence inputs. In the receipt helper, assert `case "run-prepare-gates"` accepts
   exactly one argument and calls `runAuthoritativePrepare`; assert no public `case "prepare":`
   exists. Within the authoritative function require exact seven-command order, direct status
   rejection and count parsing, release capture after `release-build`, the captured hash injected
   into AppKit, sealed evidence validation, package/install validation, bound live process, then
   `preparing -> prepared`, followed by release and installed revalidation and `validateCurrent`.
   These source-order checks supplement, and never replace, the executable command-runner tests.

4. At `TextEntrySourceTests.swift:184`, replace only the obsolete comment token
   `post-publication artifact race`. Starting after `let prepared = try gate.transition`, assert exact
   order of `from: .preparing, to: .prepared`, `StableFileHasher.revalidate(releaseIdentity)`,
   `StableFileHasher.revalidate(installed)`, and `validateCurrent(prepared)`. Also require the enclosing
   catch to call `gate.invalidate()`. Retain all worktree/index, no-caller-observer, PackageManifest,
   automated-evidence, Keychain/no-follow, checked-update and stale-sequence assertions. This proves
   the fail-closed race invariant from executable statements rather than a removable comment.

5. At `TextEntrySourceTests.swift:194`, move Designer assertions to their actual owner. The observer
   must still contain capture, AX, observation/designer challenge, sealed evidence digest and exact
   `prepared -> observedPendingDesigner` transition, then print the separate Designer command; it
   must not write `designer-attestation.json`, execute `designer-attest`, or transition
   `observedPendingDesigner -> observed`. Read `scripts/designer-attest-installed-signoff.swift` and
   require interactive TTY, authoritative `.observedPendingDesigner`, observer PID/start inequality
   from the current process identity, current artifact/live-process validation, sealed checklist and
   evidence-digest validation, the one-shot `designerChallenge`, a closed PASS attestation written as
   `designer-attestation.json`, and exact `observedPendingDesigner -> observed` transition followed by
   current/live revalidation. Require the observer and Designer wrappers to invoke only their
   respective commands. Finally, require `ReleaseGateCoordinator.allowed` to include the two-step
   transitions and exclude any direct `prepared -> observed` edge.

### Risks, tests, and Conditions for Builder

- Pipeline ownership drift is caught by delegation/absence assertions plus exact authoritative
  seven-gate order, result parsing, evidence sealing and transition order.
- Input exhaustion escape is caught at every actual router entry/reset owner, while executable
  AppSupport latch tests remain mandatory.
- Artifact publication races are caught by ordered post-transition stable revalidation, not prose.
- Same-process Designer forgery is caught by explicit pending state, separate file/wrapper/process,
  sealed evidence and forbidden direct transition.
- False-green source checks remain mitigated by the mandatory real command-runner, temporary-repo,
  receipt/observer/Designer, artifact substitution, recovery/fault, Keychain and installed tests.

Builder may edit only the two named test files for this reconciliation. Preserve their existing test
membership and every unrelated assertion; factor a local ordered-range helper only if it makes these
checks unambiguous. A missing stronger marker is an implementation failure, not permission to loosen
the test. Closing evidence is: the two formerly failing focused commands pass with 0 failures;
AppSupport and ReleaseGate executable suites pass; `security-scan.sh`, full XCTest, clean warning-free
release, exact frozen hashes, binary scan and verifier pass; then fresh Security (code) and all
installed/downstream gates. No production, pin, product, verifier logic/surface, clipboard/Paste, LAN,
receipt-authority or Accessibility behavior change is authorized.

**Revision II Stale Test-contract Reconciliation verdict: PASS.** All five obsolete assertions have
stronger exact replacements that prove current ownership and fail-closed ordering without deleting
coverage. Build may resume only with the specified test-only edits and renewed downstream evidence.

## Design Review — Revision II Final Pebble Pin Amendment (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`35c54996ce7e29354da0e75450352eb15e7ac9430558794c47e5b71e366f1c5b`. The one-pin amendment does
not itself alter visible copy, layout, controls, text semantics or Accessibility structure. It
preserves the approved experience only while the executable delta remains exactly the authorized
exhaustion-latch and scalar-only retained-tree remediation, with these binding conditions:

1. Exhaustion remains a rare fail-closed degraded state: existing entered text, caret and visible
   status stay intact; no key or modifier edge is duplicated, leaked into gameplay or dispatched
   after exhaustion. Protected F11/capture equivalents and `keyDown` are consumed as specified,
   ordinary unhandled equivalents alone may continue to AppKit, and only the defined reset clears
   the latch. This must be proved through real text-entry/latch/reset behavior, not inferred from the
   new product hash.
2. Equal-structure RPG refresh retains the exact root, group, parent, child and focused object
   identities and publishes no relationship or root setter, so VoiceOver focus and navigation do not
   reset. Structural replacement retains the approved atomic same-screen/same-ID focus handoff after
   layout, with no cross-screen or failure handoff. Installed VoiceOver inspection remains mandatory.
3. The pin edit is evidence bookkeeping only. It cannot authorize additional product, Accessibility,
   input-routing or verifier behavior, cannot advance any other frozen artifact, and cannot replace
   the complete installed text-entry and VoiceOver Design Sign-off matrix on the final bytes.

**Revision II Final Pebble Pin Amendment Design Review verdict: PASS.** Exact one-pin authorization
preserves the approved text-entry, exhaustion and stable RPG/VoiceOver interaction contracts under
the binding conditions above. Builder may make only the named pin edit; all downstream installed
Design Sign-off and verification gates remain mandatory.

## Design Review — Revision II Stale Test-contract Reconciliation (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`899f611006ebfac75e7d25422bc542d61a224871af23243390327776c74cb492`. Replacing stale source
assertions with checks at their actual owners preserves the approved user experience only under
these binding conditions:

1. The shared exhaustion latch remains one input-session behavior across every router entry point,
   not merely a collection of source markers. Existing text, caret and visible status remain intact;
   exhausted keys and modifier edges do not duplicate, leak or reach gameplay; ordinary unhandled
   equivalents alone may continue to AppKit; and only the defined session reset restores dispatch.
   Executable and installed text-entry proof remains mandatory.
2. `observedPendingDesigner` is visibly and semantically pending, never presented as observation
   success or final approval. The observer may identify the exact next Designer command and preserve
   completed evidence, but a later interactive TTY and distinct live process must perform the
   one-shot Designer decision before `observed`. Failure, cancellation or stale evidence must remain
   recoverable without misreporting success or requiring the user to repeat already valid actions.
3. Paste remains a genuinely manual installed-app action. Observation may retain a private, redacted
   result and sealed evidence reference, but must not synthesize Paste, read, log or reproduce
   clipboard contents, or expose entered text in terminal output, gate state or attestation.
4. The test-only reconciliation authorizes no RPG or Accessibility change. Equal-structure RPG
   refresh must continue to retain exact root/group/parent/child/focus identities with zero tree
   publication, and structural replacement must retain the approved same-screen focus handoff.
   Installed VoiceOver Design Sign-off on final bytes remains required and cannot be replaced by
   source-order assertions.

**Revision II Stale Test-contract Reconciliation Design Review verdict: PASS.** The amendment aligns
tests with the approved shared-latch, pending-then-Designer and private manual-Paste experiences
without changing RPG/VoiceOver behavior. Builder may make only the named test edits; the executable
and installed design evidence above remains binding.

## Security (plan) — Revision II Stale Test-contract Reconciliation (2026-07-11)

from a removed stateless token to the actual shared latch, modifier entry and paired reset order;
release ownership moves from direct pipeline commands to closed `run-prepare-gates`, sealed evidence,
install and `preparing -> prepared` order while preserving hooks; the removable race comment becomes

### Condition

- **[MEDIUM] `Revision II Stale Test-contract Reconciliation Amendment`, replacement 5 ->** The

## Observer/Designer Privacy Test Amendment

At plan SHA-256 `c8174be1c2043cd4b6e4e927e80da8ccc69e70eb995196c8e7e634449375ee9f`,
extend replacement 5 only in
`Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift`. The test must read both Swift helpers and both
wrappers as four distinct sources:

- `scripts/observe-installed-signoff.swift`
- `scripts/designer-attest-installed-signoff.swift`
- `scripts/observe-installed-signoff.sh`
- `scripts/designer-attest-installed-signoff.sh`

For every source, assert absence of clipboard read/write APIs and commands, including
`NSPasteboard`, `generalPasteboard`, `pasteboardWithName`, `pbcopy`, `pbpaste`, `readObjects`,
`canReadObject`, `writeObjects`, `clearContents`, `declareTypes`, clipboard `data(forType:)`/
`string(forType:)`, and pasteboard `setData`/`setString`. Assert absence of synthetic Paste or
Command-V mechanisms: `NSApp.sendAction`, `sendAction(`, `#selector` Paste selectors, `paste:`,
`performKeyEquivalent`, `CGEventCreateKeyboardEvent`, keyboard-event `CGEvent`, `NSEvent.keyEvent`,
`osascript`, `System Events`, `keystroke`, `key code 9`, `Command-V`, and `⌘V`. The wrappers must also
reject arbitrary command construction by containing no `eval`, `sh -c`, `bash -c`, or `"$@"`
forwarding; they may invoke only their fixed receipt commands.

Machine-check the three serialized schemas by isolating their dictionary/key-set source ranges, not
by scanning the whole file. `PebbleInstalledCommandEvidenceV1` is limited exactly to `schema`,
`commandID`, `captureStatus`, `axStatus`, `windowID`, `screenshotSHA256`, `axReportSHA256`, and
`operationLogSHA256`. `PebbleInstalledAXEvidenceV1` is limited exactly to `schema`, `itemID`,
`windowCount`, `elementCount`, `focusedCount`, `settableValueCount`, `finiteFrameCount`, and
`roleCounts`. `PebbleDesignerAttestationV2` is limited exactly to `schema`, `verdict`,
`checklistPassed`, `checklistTotal`, `sealedEvidenceSHA256`, `designerPID`, `designerProcessStart`, and
`timestamp`. In those ranges assert absence of fields or values named `text`, `enteredText`, `value`,
`selectedText`, `clipboard`, `pasteboard`, `sentinel`, `axValue`, `description`, `label`, or `help`.
Only aggregate counts, identifiers, hashes, process identity, status and fixed schema/version values
may be serialized; no free-form AX attribute value may enter command, AX, operation-log, receipt, or
attestation evidence.

The sole permitted entered-value read is the fixed manual-Paste proof. Isolate
`verifyManualPaste` from its declaration through the next function boundary and require exactly one
`observerAXValue(..., kAXValueAttribute ...)` read, used directly in `as? String == sentinel` with no
intermediate storage. Require that span to contain no `print`, `privateWrite`, JSON serialization,
operation log, hash, interpolation of `sentinel`, or return of the value. Require the sole call site to
pass `checklist.fixedPasteSentinel`; load the checklist's fixed sentinel and assert that literal is
absent from both Swift helpers and both wrappers. The helper may report only pass/fail by control flow;
it must never print or persist the sentinel.

For terminal-output privacy, collect each Swift `print` call and each wrapper `echo`/`printf` line and
assert none references or interpolates `sentinel`, `fixedPasteSentinel`, `observerAXValue`,
`kAXValueAttribute`, a read-line `answer`, clipboard/pasteboard data, AX attribute results, or entered
field text. Fixed instructions may say that Paste is manual and clipboard bytes are not accessed, but
no dynamic user/AX/clipboard value may reach an output sink. Preserve the existing exact pending-
Designer state, separate-process/challenge, closed evidence, wrapper ownership, and forbidden direct
transition assertions.

These source assertions do not substitute for executable proof. The existing mandatory helper tests
must still run the observer and Designer through test seams with canary entered/clipboard/AX strings,
prove the canaries never appear in stdout, stderr, logs, schemas, receipts, attestations or private
non-screenshot evidence, prove no clipboard or keyboard-event adapter is invoked, and binary/source
scan the compiled helpers for forbidden clipboard/Paste symbols. `security-scan.sh`, focused
`TextEntrySourceTests`, ReleaseGate integration, installed manual Paste, Security (code), Design
Sign-off and full downstream gates remain required.

Builder conditions: modify only `TextEntrySourceTests.swift`; add explicit per-file negative checks,
closed-range schema checks, fixed-sentinel equality/use-count checks and output-sink checks; do not
remove any earlier replacement-5 assertion or relax exact field sets. A current helper violation is a
code finding, not permission to weaken/exclude a token. No production/helper/wrapper/checklist/pin,
clipboard, Paste, AX, receipt, product, verifier or LAN change is authorized by this amendment.

**Observer/Designer Privacy Test Amendment verdict: PASS.** The Security-plan condition is closed at
the architecture contract: all four helper surfaces now have explicit machine privacy assertions,
while executable and installed proof remains mandatory before Security (code) can pass.

## Design Review — Observer/Designer Privacy Test Amendment (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`1b126533d1e1e60d17a083576511dcc9044ea5e71f7e61c2a83c6af83a34db11`. The amendment protects a
usable manual Paste check and a truthful two-stage review without turning entered text into evidence,
under these binding conditions:

1. The observer gives a short, navigable instruction for the single manual Paste action, identifies
   the field and expected completion signal without displaying the sentinel, and waits for deliberate
   user completion. It must not synthesize Paste, inspect the clipboard, echo field contents or make
   the user repeat a successfully sealed action merely to hand off to Designer.
2. Progress copy and exit behavior distinguish checklist capture, `observedPendingDesigner`, final
   `observed`, failure and stale-evidence invalidation. Pending output names the exact next Designer
   command and sealed evidence location; interruption or rejection names the safe resume/restart path
   and never implies that observation or Design Sign-off passed.
3. The one direct AX value comparison is ephemeral pass/fail control flow only. Entered text, the
   fixed sentinel and free-form AX values never reach stdout, stderr, logs, schemas, receipts,
   attestations or non-screenshot evidence. Any screenshot capable of showing entered text remains
   private and redacted before Designer indexing or durable retention.
4. Aggregate AX schemas and hashes are privacy-preserving corroboration, not sufficient Design
   Sign-off by themselves. The distinct Designer must still inspect the installed app with VoiceOver
   and the fixed checklist for discoverable names/roles/states, focus order, retained focus/navigation,
   finite frames and actionable controls. That live inspection may record only closed pass/fail or
   aggregate results; it must not persist spoken labels, field values or other entered content.

**Observer/Designer Privacy Test Amendment Design Review verdict: PASS.** The closed-source and
executable privacy checks preserve manual Paste usability, honest pending/recovery semantics and
adequate installed Accessibility review without capturing entered text. Builder may make only the
named test edit, and the live installed Designer inspection remains mandatory.

receipt transitions or tests. If the exact safe copy does not satisfy the existing privacy test,
