## Strict codesign Designated-requirement Parser Remediation

At plan SHA-256 `41f8239b64cc8eefb2733b55bfdade81c51bcb63a68b5a268f97a5550049622a`,
read-only execution of `/usr/bin/codesign -d -r- /Applications/Pebble.app` succeeded and emitted:

```text
Executable=/Applications/Pebble.app/Contents/MacOS/Pebble
# designated => cdhash H"cbc0e038d072ccf610c573850d66aaae8f1abb23"
```

`scripts/package-app.sh:57` accepts only the older unprefixed `designated => ` form, causing the real
package gate to reject valid current macOS output. The identical unprefixed-only assumption at
`scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift:162-164` would fail the later installed-artifact check, so
both trust-boundary parsers require the same minimal grammar correction.

### Exact accepted grammar and binding

Capture output directly from a successful `/usr/bin/codesign -d -r- <canonical bundle>` invocation;
no caller-supplied output or PATH-selected executable is authoritative. After allowing one terminal
newline only, require exactly two records in exact order: one
`Executable=<expected canonical bundle>/Contents/MacOS/Pebble` record and one designated-requirement
record at column zero. The second record may begin with exactly either `designated => ` or
`# designated => `. The optional prefix is exactly one codesign-owned hash and one ASCII space; do
not treat general comments as requirements. Strip only the optional exact `# ` and the exact
`designated => ` marker, preserving the remaining requirement byte-for-byte.

Require exactly one nonempty payload of at most 4096 UTF-8 bytes with no leading/trailing whitespace,
CR, LF, NUL, other control character or `#` comment/injection marker. Reject a missing requirement;
empty payload; duplicate same-form or mixed-form requirements; extra `Executable=` or requirement
records; a different executable; extra output/comment lines; leading indentation; tabs; `#designated`,
`## designated`, `#  designated`, altered spacing/case/arrows; trailing comments; embedded line or
manifest-field injection; nonzero `codesign` status; and decode failure. Failure produces no manifest
and no receipt transition.

`scripts/package-app.sh` writes the normalized payload exactly once as the closed manifest's
`designated_requirement` value. `PackageManifest` must continue to reject duplicate/unknown fields and
must additionally reject empty or control/newline-bearing requirement values. During prepare and every
later artifact validation, the receipt helper parses fresh `/usr/bin/codesign` output with the same
two-form grammar and requires byte-for-byte equality among the package manifest, receipt artifact
identity and Security-framework designated requirement. The `# ` output decoration is never stored;
only the exact canonical requirement expression is bound.

### Tests, risks, and digest consequences

Add table-driven executable parser tests for both shell/package and Swift/receipt paths. Passing
fixtures are the observed `# designated => cdhash H"..."` output and the legacy unprefixed equivalent;
both must return the identical exact payload. Failing fixtures cover every rejection above, including
zero/duplicate/mixed candidates, malformed prefix/spacing/comments, empty/whitespace payload,
alternate executable, nonzero status, CR/NUL/control bytes, extra lines, and newline/duplicate-key
manifest injection. Add seeded single-byte/line mutation coverage proving only the two exact forms are
accepted. A real packaging integration test must run current `/usr/bin/codesign`, parse the generated
bundle, validate the closed manifest, and prove its stored requirement equals a fresh receipt-parser
result and `SecRequirementCopyString`; `security-scan.sh` must execute these tests fail-closed.

- Compatibility risk -> exact two-form fixtures plus the real current-macOS package run.
- Parser broadening/comment spoof -> column-zero exact grammar, one candidate, exact output envelope,
  mutation tests and rejection of every other comment form.
- Manifest injection/substitution -> control/newline rejection, closed unique fields, canonical
  executable binding and byte-exact three-way requirement comparison.
- Partial fix -> both package and receipt parsers share the same fixture corpus and installed check.

Changing either parser changes the exhaustive repository-content digest; changing the receipt helper
also changes the compiled authority/observer digest. Every earlier preparing, prepared, pending-
Designer, observed or later receipt and package manifest is stale and must be invalidated through the
monotonic authority. Builder must rerun the authoritative clean pipeline, package/install, challenges,
observation, Designer review and all evidence. These are nonshipping script/test changes: Pebble,
pebsmoke, Storage, Core, TextInput and evidence-only AppSupport hashes and every verifier/product/LAN
surface must remain unchanged.

Builder conditions: edit only `scripts/package-app.sh`, the designated-requirement parser in
`scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift`, the minimum shared/nonshipping parser test fixture if
needed, `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/ReleaseGateTests.swift`, focused source-contract tests, and
`scripts/security-scan.sh` only to enforce the new executable category. Do not relax strict signing,
canonical path/no-follow hashing, CDHash/identifier/seal checks, closed manifest fields, receipt
sequence/state, observer privacy or any prior gate. Capture `/usr/bin/codesign` status separately so a
pipe/parser cannot mask failure. If current output requires any form beyond the two exact prefixes,
return to Architecture rather than accepting general comments or trimming arbitrary text.

**Strict codesign Designated-requirement Parser Remediation verdict: PASS.** The observed macOS form
is admitted by one exact optional decoration while duplicates, malformed comments, empty/injected
requirements and artifact substitution remain fail-closed. Build may resume only after renewed Design
Review/Security (plan), and all downstream evidence must be regenerated.

## Design Review — Strict codesign Designated-requirement Parser Remediation (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`06290a139757789319931bb9a2a418f9d1b25390420eb66101d2d9501077d460`. This is not marked Design
N/A because it changes the developer-visible package/sign-off outcome from a false rejection of valid
current macOS output to successful continuation. The narrowly bounded parser correction preserves the
approved interaction under these binding conditions:

1. It adds no prompt, choice, output copy or new manual step. Malformed, mismatched or ambiguous
   signing output still fails before install or observation and cannot present prepared, pending,
   observed or Designer-approved progress.
2. The installed sign-off sequence remains authoritative prepare, installed observation,
   `observedPendingDesigner`, then distinct Designer approval. Digest invalidation must reject every
   stale attempt and direct the operator through the existing truthful fresh-prepare/recovery path;
   no passed manual item is reused from invalid evidence.
3. The approved observer line remains exactly:

   `Navigate to Create New World, focus the empty World Name field, perform one manual Paste, then return to this terminal after the field updates.`

   Parser work may not change that copy, manual Paste behavior, entered-text privacy, Accessibility
   checklist, VoiceOver evidence, app UI or RPG interaction. Fresh installed Design Sign-off remains
   mandatory after the helper/content digests change.

**Strict codesign Designated-requirement Parser Remediation Design Review verdict: PASS.** Exact
two-form compatibility fixes the package workflow without changing the approved installed/manual
experience. All stale-proof, truthful-state, privacy and fresh installed-sign-off conditions remain
binding.

## Security (plan) — Strict codesign Designated-requirement Parser Remediation (2026-07-11)

Independently reviewed the amendment, Design Review, both current parser call sites and live
`/usr/bin/codesign`/Security.framework output at plan SHA-256
`72de26b822e0b5836a6469ae2d578f996f4b5855a6655264679a95146599c500`.

The accepted requirement forms, canonical bundle/executable binding, exact payload preservation,
4096-byte/control/comment limits, duplicate/extra/malformed/injected-record rejection, strict command
status, closed manifest, byte-equal package/receipt/Security-framework comparison, mutation corpus,
real packaging integration, digest invalidation and nonshipping scope are otherwise fail-closed and
implementable. Live Security.framework output is exactly
`cdhash H"cbc0e038d072ccf610c573850d66aaae8f1abb23"`, matching the normalized codesign payload.

### Condition

- **[MEDIUM] parser capture contract ->** Live macOS does not emit the two records on one ordered
  stream: `Executable=/Applications/Pebble.app/Contents/MacOS/Pebble\n` is written to **stderr** and
  `# designated => cdhash H"cbc0e038d072ccf610c573850d66aaae8f1abb23"\n` is written to
  **stdout**. The plan's current "exactly two records in exact order" wording is ambiguous and a
  merged `2>&1` parser cannot securely assign a total order across descriptors; the current Swift
  implementation captures only stderr and therefore cannot see the requirement. Amend the contract
  so both shell and Swift paths capture raw stdout and stderr separately from the fixed
  `/usr/bin/codesign` process, wait for and require status zero, then require stderr to contain exactly
  the single canonical `Executable=` record and stdout to contain exactly the single accepted
  designated-requirement record, each with exactly one terminal LF and no other byte. Parse from raw
  files/Data before any shell command substitution or lossy `String(decoding:)`, so NUL, invalid UTF-8,
  CR and other control bytes can actually be rejected. The shared fixture corpus must model the two
  channels and reject swapped channels, either record on both channels, duplicate/extra records,
  missing terminal LF, extra LF, partial writes, nonzero status with valid-looking bytes, and both
  possible process write timings. Builder/Architecture owns this condition; closing evidence is the
  amended two-channel contract plus passing shell, Swift, mutation and real package/Security-framework
  integration tests.

All original conditions remain binding: one normalized payload only; no caller/PATH authority;
canonical path and fresh signature/CDHash/requirement revalidation; no manifest or receipt transition
on any parse/status failure; monotonic invalidation of all prior digests/evidence; and no shipping,
privacy, product, verifier, hash or LAN change.

Review boundary: plan and read-only command execution only. No parser, test, receipt, build,
deployment, commit or push was changed or performed by Security.

**Security (plan) verdict: CONDITIONAL PASS.** Grammar, injection resistance, tests, invalidation and
scope are sound, but Build remains blocked until stdout/stderr ownership and raw-byte decoding are
made explicit as above. After that exact amendment, both parsers may be implemented and every
downstream gate must be regenerated on the final helper/content digests.

## Codesign Two-channel Raw-capture Architecture Revision

At plan SHA-256 `a36f090d732f909086df15e22a69642cba5d8f513ea5d775461b5dc3a9790cd8`,
raw read-only capture confirms current `/usr/bin/codesign -d -r- /Applications/Pebble.app` exits zero
and writes exactly one LF-terminated requirement record to stdout and exactly one LF-terminated
executable record to stderr. This revision supersedes the earlier merged-stream/two-record ordering
language for both package and receipt parsers; no merge or cross-descriptor ordering is authoritative.

### Fixed process and raw channel ownership

Both `scripts/package-app.sh` and `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift` must launch exactly
`/usr/bin/codesign` with argv `-d`, `-r-`, and the already validated canonical bundle path. No PATH
lookup, shell command string, caller executable/output, `2>&1`, merged pipe, or parser pipeline is
allowed. Before launch, open distinct private regular single-link `0600` stdout and stderr capture
files no-follow under a `0700` temporary directory; attach both descriptors to the one child before
start. The child writes both channels concurrently without pipe backpressure. Wait for process exit,
record its direct status, synchronize and close both descriptors, then read each complete file as raw
`Data`/bytes. Always remove only these identity-bound captures. Two concurrently drained pipes are an
acceptable equivalent only if their drains start before wait, complete after exit, propagate read
errors, and are proven not to deadlock or truncate; sequential pipe draining is forbidden.

Require direct status `0` before accepting either channel. Before shell command substitution,
`readLine`, `String(decoding:)`, trimming, splitting or any other lossy transformation, validate each
raw byte stream: strict UTF-8 via a failable decoder; nonempty; no NUL, CR or control byte except the
one final LF; exactly one LF and it is the final byte; bounded size; and no unread/trailing byte.
Invalid status, read/sync/close failure, invalid UTF-8, truncation or byte violation produces no
manifest and no receipt transition. Shell may assign the normalized payload only after raw validation
has proved that command substitution cannot erase evidence.

The channel contracts are independent and exact:

- stderr bytes equal exactly `Executable=<expected canonical executable>\n`, where the expected
  executable is derived from the canonical bundle (`Contents/MacOS/Pebble`), never parsed as a new
  authority. No decoration, second record, requirement, whitespace or other output is accepted.
- stdout contains exactly one record beginning at byte zero with either `designated => ` or exact
  codesign-owned `# designated => `, followed by the prior nonempty bounded payload and one LF. No
  `Executable=`, duplicate, general comment, extra/missing LF or other byte is accepted.

Only after both channel contracts pass may the parser remove the single optional `# ` and exact
`designated => ` marker. It preserves the payload bytes exactly and retains every prior nonempty,
4096-byte, no-leading/trailing-whitespace, no-comment/control/injection rule. Package writes that
normalized value exactly once to the closed manifest. Receipt parsing must yield the identical value
and retain byte-equality with the manifest, receipt artifact identity and
`SecRequirementCopyString`; the output-channel decoration is never persisted.

### Exact executable tests

Use one shared channel fixture model `(status, stdout Data, stderr Data, expected executable)` against
both shell/package and Swift/receipt parsing. Accept the current decorated stdout and legacy
unprefixed stdout only when stderr is the exact executable record. Reject: swapped channels; either
record copied to both channels; missing stdout or stderr; duplicate records on either channel; extra
record, byte, comment or LF; missing terminal LF; CRLF; NUL/control/invalid UTF-8; empty or malformed
payload; alternate executable; partial/truncated final record; and nonzero status with otherwise
valid-looking bytes.

Runner tests must use real helper processes that (1) write valid stdout first then delayed stderr,
(2) write valid stderr first then delayed stdout, and (3) fragment both valid records across multiple
writes before exit; all three must capture complete exact bytes and accept independent of timing.
Additional helpers write more than pipe capacity to both channels concurrently, close one channel
early, terminate mid-record, or exit nonzero after valid bytes; capture must complete without hang,
timeout or truncation and the parser must reject. Tests assert direct child status cannot be replaced
by a reader/parser status. The real current-macOS integration must verify raw channel bytes, normalized
manifest value and Security-framework equality. Seeded channel/byte mutation coverage and
`security-scan.sh` enforcement remain mandatory.

### Risks, scope, and Conditions for Builder

- Channel confusion/spoofing -> exact stderr executable ownership and exact stdout requirement
  ownership; swapped/duplicate/both-channel fixtures reject.
- NUL/CR/invalid-UTF8/injection loss -> raw validation before decode, split or shell substitution.
- Deadlock/truncation -> two file descriptors or genuinely concurrent drains plus large-output,
  partial-write, early-close and opposite-timing process tests.
- False success -> direct `/usr/bin/codesign` status zero is necessary but not sufficient; both exact
  raw channels and normalized Security equality must also pass.

Authorized edits remain limited to `scripts/package-app.sh`, the codesign runner/parser in
`scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift`, the minimum nonshipping shared raw-channel parser/capture
seam in `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/**`, `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/**`, focused source-contract and
process fixtures, and `scripts/security-scan.sh` only to enforce the category. Builder must not use
command substitution before validation, merge descriptors, sequentially drain bounded pipes, trim
unknown bytes, accept extra diagnostics, relax canonical path/signature/CDHash/seal/manifest/state
checks, or expose a caller-selected production runner.

All prior normalized-requirement equality, closed-manifest, digest invalidation, monotonic-state,
privacy, observer copy, product/pin/hash and zero-LAN conditions remain binding. Parser/receipt bytes
change content and authority/observer digests, invalidating every old package, receipt and evidence;
rerun the complete authoritative pipeline, install, observation, Designer approval and downstream
gates. Shipping artifact hashes and verifier surfaces must remain unchanged.

**Codesign Two-channel Raw-capture Architecture Revision verdict: PASS.** The sole Security-plan
condition is closed with exact channel ownership, pre-decode byte validation and deadlock-safe process
capture. Build remains blocked until renewed Security (plan) passes this revision; no implementation
is authorized outside the files and conditions above.

## Design re-review — Codesign Two-channel Raw-capture Architecture Revision (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`723830625e1338c9d70fd5e238c9220b162c5149f38c8f13093e967312f8d041`. Separate raw-channel
capture preserves the approved developer and installed-sign-off experience under these binding
conditions:

1. Valid current-macOS output proceeds without a new prompt, choice or manual step. A nonzero
   codesign result, unreadable capture or invalid channel envelope exits nonzero before manifest,
   install or receipt transition and emits one concise stderr failure identifying the package/signing
   phase and a safe category such as command failure or invalid output. It must not dump raw channel
   bytes, designated-requirement payloads, paths beyond the already approved canonical target, or
   suggest that prepare/observation succeeded.
2. Failure and stale-digest recovery continue through the existing truthful fresh-prepare path. The
   workflow must never offer observation, pending Designer or resume of manual evidence created from
   old helper/content digests; status output continues to name the actual next authorized command.
3. The installed sequence remains authoritative prepare, installed observation,
   `observedPendingDesigner`, then distinct Designer approval. The approved observer line remains
   exactly:

   `Navigate to Create New World, focus the empty World Name field, perform one manual Paste, then return to this terminal after the field updates.`

   Two-channel parsing may not alter this copy, manual-Paste/privacy behavior, Accessibility evidence,
   app UI or RPG/VoiceOver interaction. Fresh installed Design Sign-off remains mandatory.

**Codesign Two-channel Raw-capture Architecture Revision Design re-review verdict: PASS.** Exact
channel ownership makes the package gate reliable without adding interaction burden or changing the
installed/manual flow. Safe truthful failure output and every prior privacy/sign-off condition remain
binding.

## Security (plan) rerun — Codesign Two-channel Raw-capture Revision (2026-07-11)

Independently reviewed the Architecture revision and Design re-review at plan SHA-256
`195e773f9120b4bcc038c93b4c73bfe58d655bda26da18cf27c6ac130f0e6c16` against the prior MEDIUM
channel condition, parser trust boundary, process failure modes, privacy and authorized scope.

### Findings

None.

The condition is fully closed. Both callers must launch fixed `/usr/bin/codesign` with closed argv and
the prevalidated canonical bundle; capture stdout and stderr into distinct private no-follow identities
before launch; require the direct child status; synchronize/close/read complete raw bytes; and reject
all I/O, status, UTF-8, NUL/control, size, LF or trailing-byte failures before any shell substitution,
split, trim or lossy decode. Exact stderr ownership of the derived canonical `Executable=` record and
exact stdout ownership of one accepted requirement record eliminate merged-channel ordering and
comment/record injection ambiguity.

The shared two-channel fixture corpus is complete for the reviewed risk: accepted current and legacy
forms; swapped/copied/missing/duplicate/extra channels; malformed prefix/payload/path; CRLF, NUL,
control and invalid UTF-8; truncation and partial records; and valid-looking bytes with nonzero status.
Real opposite-order, fragmented, over-pipe-capacity, early-close, mid-record and nonzero helpers prove
capture completion, direct-status ownership and absence of deadlock/truncation. Seeded mutation, real
current-macOS packaging and byte-equal Security-framework comparison remain mandatory and
`security-scan.sh` fails closed on the category.

No raw diagnostic or requirement bytes may reach user output. Failures remain concise, non-successful
and pre-transition; all canonical path, CDHash/identifier/seal, closed-manifest, three-way normalized-
requirement equality, monotonic state, observer privacy/copy and installed sign-off conditions remain
unchanged. Parser/helper byte changes invalidate all prior content/authority/observer digests and
require a fresh authoritative pipeline, install, observation and Designer approval. The work is
nonshipping: product hashes, verifier/pins, package products, protocols and LAN surface remain frozen.

Review boundary: plan only. No parser, test, capture, receipt, build, deployment, commit or push was
changed or performed by Security.

**Security (plan) rerun verdict: PASS.** Separate raw-channel ownership, strict pre-decode validation,
direct status and deadlock/fault coverage close the sole prior condition without broadening grammar or
scope. Builder may proceed only within the named files and conditions; fresh Security (code), parser/
privacy suites, installed Design Sign-off, independent Test and every downstream gate remain mandatory
against stable final bytes.

## Real AppKit Reactivation/Input-identity Failure Diagnosis and Remediation

At plan SHA-256 `5d3a4a9d443ecd0dfab946fa79ddf5e45a4232a3e37ecbbe8a96c380f5c0f5ce`,
the exact no-build AppKit gate was rerun against release SHA-256
`406929c1592d50f964c8dcc0976358dbe1c1c89885aff0c5f6027beda4f3f347`. Every run used a newly
packaged/signed temporary app, unique temporary HOME/CFFIXED_USER_HOME and cleanup. Failures moved
among `title-navigation: GameView group`, `reactivation-and-middle-edit: middle edit`,
`reactivation-and-middle-edit: middle edit range`, and the reported
`field-switching: World Name end range`. A concurrent read-only AX observer captured the reported path
without printing field contents: World Name length/range/focus progressed
`0/0/false -> 0/0/true -> 1/1/true -> 3/1/true -> 3/1/false -> 3/1/true -> 3/2/true`; the final
range was actually `2`, not the required `3`.

Stale state/binary is excluded: the script verifies the exact release hash, creates a fresh profile,
starts with both fields empty, binds the temporary signed executable and cleans it. Deterministic text
model/AX corruption is not supported: the current full suite passed 986/986; the driver,
`TextEntryAccessibilityM.swift` and `UIManagerM.swift` predate a successful 17:59 execution of this
same gate; and identical current-byte runs fail at different asynchronous stages.

### First-principles causes

There is one real product identity defect. `AppInputRouter.resetPressedBindings()` resets
`nextPhysicalRoutingSerial` to zero and releases the exhaustion latch, but leaves
`RPGPureInputRouter.consumedIdentities` and `handledEquivalents` from the old application-active input
session intact. The first new-session deliveries therefore reuse `.keyboard(1...)` identities that
the retained router may classify as `.consumedDuplicate`. Resetting one half of an identity namespace
while retaining its duplicate ledger violates uniqueness and can drop legitimate post-reactivation
keys. The fingerprint hypothesis is disproved by current code: `RPGPureInputRouter.route` explicitly
ignores `fingerprint` and `nowMilliseconds`; duplicate authorization uses the independently incremented
`PebbleInputDeliveryIdentity.keyboard(routingSerial)`. Reintroducing `NSEvent.eventNumber`, accepting
nonpositive timestamp exceptions or weakening duplicate checks is neither necessary nor authorized.

The driver also has an independent automation race. It waits a fixed three seconds while Pebble's
launch-fullscreen logic may retry at 1.2-second intervals up to five times, and it treats
`NSRunningApplication.isActive` plus logical AX focus as proof that the fullscreen/key-window/
first-responder/reactivation transaction is settled. It then posts six global HID key pairs with no
per-event observable barrier and later posts three Right pairs while checking only the final range.
The moving failure stages and captured `range=2` are exactly consistent with acting during these
unacknowledged transitions. Retrying the whole gate until green would hide both defects and is
forbidden.

### Minimal remediation in dependency order

1. In `Sources/Pebble/AppInputRouterM.swift`, make `resetPressedBindings()` one MainActor input-session
   reset. Before reusing serial zero or clearing exhaustion, replace `router` with a fresh
   `RPGPureInputRouter()` and clear `handledEquivalents`; then clear the configured-press and modifier
   ledgers, reset the physical serial/exhaustion flag, and reset the latch. Old duplicate identities
   and equivalent markers must never coexist with the restarted serial namespace. Within a session,
   the existing bounded 16-identity duplicate ledger and monotonic serial remain unchanged; exhaustion
   stays fail-closed until this defined lifecycle reset.
2. In `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, replace the fixed launch sleep with a bounded
   semantic barrier: exact active app/PID/bundle, exactly one focused AX window, launch fullscreen
   true and stable, stable finite geometry, and the unique GameView group over consecutive samples.
   After Finder reactivation, require the same focused-window barrier and deliberately invoke the
   existing World Name AX focus action to re-establish readiness before posting input.
3. Keep global `.cghidEventTap` keyboard delivery so the test remains real AppKit input. Serialize the
   fixed edit sequence with a bounded AX acknowledgement after every down/up pair: `a -> ab -> abc ->
   abcd`, ranges `4 -> 3 -> 2`, then Backspace to fixed `acd`/range `1`. After Seed and World Name AX
   focus switches, assert the preserved World Name range is still `1`; acknowledge Right to `2`, Right
   to `3`, and one saturation Right remaining `3`. A failure may report stage, expected range and
   observed aggregate length/range/focus only—never entered text. Do not switch ordinary keys to
   PID-targeted delivery, add sleeps as correctness, or add automatic retries.

### Tests, risks, and Conditions for Builder

- Add `InputChordTests` coverage proving a duplicate serial is consumed within one router, while a
  deliberately fresh input-session router accepts the reused serial; preserve the bounded ledger.
- Strengthen `AppInputRouterSourceTests`/`TextEntrySourceTests` to require router and handled-equivalent
  reset before serial reuse, paired exhaustion reset, and continued absence of `event.eventNumber` and
  timestamp/fingerprint authorization exceptions. Source checks do not replace the real gate.
- The driver must prove first post-reactivation input, every edit/range transition, AX field switching,
  retained element identity, resize retirement, cleanup and zero clipboard access. Run five
  consecutive cold-profile no-build gates against one exact release hash; any failure fails the
  evidence set, with no retry-discard. Then run full XCTest, `security-scan.sh`, pebsmoke, binary scan,
  verifier and installed Design/Security/Test gates.

Risks map as follows: old-event/new-session collision -> clear ledger and markers atomically before
serial reuse plus executable reactivation test; duplicate leakage within a session -> retain the same
bounded router ledger and same-event tests; automation false red -> semantic fullscreen/reactivation
barriers and per-event acknowledgements; false green -> no retry masking and explicit intermediate
states; privacy regression -> aggregate-only failures and unchanged zero-clipboard driver.

Builder may edit only `Sources/Pebble/AppInputRouterM.swift`,
`Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, the focused InputChord/AppInputRouter/TextEntry tests and
this plan's directly affected source contract. Do not change Core delivery-identity shape, text model,
AX setters, RPG AX, AppSupport latch, UI copy, clipboard/Paste behavior, package/receipt/parser,
products, LAN or verifier logic. The shipping AppInputRouter edit will change only the Pebble product
hash; stop after a clean warning-free build for a fresh exact one-pin Architecture/Security review.
Storage/Core/TextInput/pebsmoke and evidence-only AppSupport hashes must remain frozen.

**Diagnosis verdict: FAIL.** The current product can discard valid post-reactivation keys because its
serial namespace and duplicate ledger reset inconsistently, and the current real-AppKit verifier can
race launch/reactivation and key delivery. **Remediation Architecture verdict: PASS.** The bounded
session reset plus semantic/per-event black-box barriers closes both causes without weakening duplicate
protection or reintroducing `NSEvent.eventNumber`; Build requires renewed Design Review and Security
(plan) before implementation.

## Design Review — AppKit Reactivation/Input-identity Remediation (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`6cfd86afdf9d6fa30dcf87f0ab28579c2e94d3b4f283a25b7fbb9117d73d4d69`. The atomic routing-ledger
reset is the correct product boundary: application deactivation may clear pressed-input bookkeeping,
duplicate identities and exhaustion, but must not alter either field's text, selected range, retained
AX element identity, logical focus or visible caret. It must also prevent a stuck modifier and accept
the first post-reactivation key exactly once.

The driver revision needs one blocking correction before it can prove that experience rather than
repair it:

1. After the semantic reactivation/window barrier and **before** invoking any AX focus action, require
   the original World Name element identity to remain in the current group, its value and selected
   range to remain at the exact pre-deactivation state, its logical/AX focus to remain true, and Seed
   focus to remain false. Only then may the driver invoke the World Name AX focus action exactly once,
   require identity/value/range to remain unchanged, and post the first new key. Otherwise that action
   can mask a real focus, caret or reactivation-readiness regression.
2. Every keyboard or AX action is issued exactly once. Polling may observe a bounded semantic
   acknowledgement but may not repost activation, focus, keyDown/keyUp or field-switch actions. Assert
   each intermediate value length and zero-length caret range: `a/1`, `ab/2`, `abc/3`, `abcd/4`,
   Left `3`, Left `2`, Backspace `acd/1`; Seed focus makes World Name unfocused while preserving
   `acd/1`, Seed input reaches length/range `1/1`; one World Name focus action restores World Name
   `acd/1` with Seed unfocused; Rights reach `2`, `3`, then remain saturated at `3`.
3. Failures report only stage plus aggregate expected/observed length, range and focus. They must not
   print values or retry/discard a failed pass. All five fresh-profile runs use the same measured
   release hash; each is one first attempt and any failure invalidates the entire five-run set.
4. The AX driver corroborates, but does not replace, final installed VoiceOver inspection of field
   names/roles, focus announcements, caret navigation, focus switching, retained identity and resize
   retirement. No UI copy, Paste/clipboard behavior or RPG Accessibility behavior may change.

**AppKit Reactivation/Input-identity Remediation Design Review verdict: FAIL.** The product reset and
semantic/per-key direction preserve the intended interaction, but Build remains blocked until the
pre-focus-action reactivation assertions and exact no-retry intermediate-state contract above are
adopted, followed by renewed Security (plan).

## AppKit Reactivation/Input-identity Remediation Architecture Revision

At plan SHA-256 `062c502145b7057d1ce172599601ab3c240e9a0c0a39acb91404f29913190ef8`,
Architecture adopts every binding Design correction. The product remediation is unchanged:
`AppInputRouter.resetPressedBindings()` must clear the pure router duplicate ledger and handled-
equivalent markers before restarting the serial namespace/exhaustion latch on the MainActor. No
fingerprint, timestamp, `NSEvent.eventNumber`, Core identity shape or duplicate-policy exception is
authorized.

### Preservation proof before any repair action

Immediately before activating Finder, capture only fixed-test expectations and retained identities:
the original World Name AX element, current GameView group/window identity, World Name length `1`,
selected range `{1,0}`, focused `true`, and Seed focused `false`. Issue Finder activation exactly once,
observe resignation, then issue Pebble activation exactly once. The reactivation wait is observation-
only and requires the exact app/PID/bundle, one focused stable fullscreen AX window, stable finite
geometry and the unique current GameView group.

Before calling any AX setter, clicking, posting a key, or otherwise attempting repair, require all of
the following simultaneously: the original World Name object remains a member of the current group;
there are still exactly two text fields; its value is exactly the fixed pre-deactivation `a` test
value with length `1`; its selected range is exactly `{1,0}`; it remains logically/AX focused; Seed is
unfocused; and window/group identities are unchanged. Failure ends the run. It must not trigger a
focus action, fallback activation or retry.

Only after that preservation proof may the driver issue exactly one World Name AX focused setter as
an idempotent readiness action. Poll without reposting and require the same object identity, fixed
value/length, `{1,0}` range and World Name/Seed focus states to remain unchanged. Thus the AX action is
tested as non-mutating and cannot hide a reactivation focus, identity or caret regression.

### One action, then observation-only acknowledgement

Introduce a bounded driver-only one-shot action ledger keyed by fixed action IDs. Every external
activation, navigation click, AX setter and keyboard down/up pair must enter its ID exactly once before
execution; duplicate entry fails. Semantic wait helpers accept predicates only and cannot contain or
invoke activation, click, AX-setter or key-post closures. `validateBeforeAction` becomes validation
only and may never reactivate the app. A failed acknowledgement ends that first attempt; no action is
reposted and no state is repaired.

Keep global `.cghidEventTap` delivery and require these exact fixed-test states after each single
action:

1. Initial World Name input: value `a`, length/range `1/{1,0}`, World Name focused, Seed unfocused.
2. After the preservation proof and one idempotent AX focus action: the identical state and retained
   World Name object.
3. One key each: `b` -> `ab` and `2/{2,0}`; `c` -> `abc` and `3/{3,0}`; `d` -> `abcd` and
   `4/{4,0}`; first Left -> `{3,0}`; second Left -> `{2,0}`; Backspace -> `acd` and `3/{1,0}`.
4. One Seed AX focus action: World Name becomes unfocused but retains the same object, `acd` and
   `{1,0}`; Seed becomes focused. One Seed key produces length/range `1/{1,0}`.
5. One later World Name AX focus action: World Name is focused with the same object, `acd` and
   `{1,0}`; Seed is unfocused. Three separately identified Right actions produce `{2,0}`, `{3,0}` and
   saturated `{3,0}` respectively.

Polling may read only the already approved identity, length, selected range, focus, finite geometry
and fixed-value equality needed by the test. Any failure output is limited to action/stage ID plus
aggregate expected/observed length, range and focus booleans; it must not print or hash field values,
AX free-form values, entered text, clipboard content or event payloads.

### Evidence, tests, and Builder conditions

Strengthen the driver/source tests to prove the pre-action reactivation snapshot occurs before the
first post-reactivation AX setter, the setter occurs once, and its postcondition is unchanged. Prove
the one-shot ledger covers every activation/AX/key ID, polling helpers are action-free, every exact
intermediate state above is checked, and aggregate-only diagnostics contain no value interpolation.
Retain the executable router-session collision test, within-session duplicate test, source reset-order
checks, exhaustion behavior, absence of event-number exceptions, field identity/resize retirement,
cleanup and zero clipboard access.

Closing AppKit evidence is five fresh-profile, cold first attempts against one premeasured release
SHA-256. Each attempt packages/launches independently but must validate that same release hash. Record
all five outcomes in order; success requires five passes and zero failures. No failed attempt may be
discarded, retried, replaced or excluded, and the gate has no retry-until-green path. Then renew the
full suite, security, smoke, binary/verifier, exact-hash/pin and installed gates.

The automated AX proof remains corroboration only. Final Design Sign-off must inspect the installed
Pebble app with VoiceOver and prove field names/roles, focus announcements, caret navigation, World
Name/Seed switching, retained identity/context through reactivation, and resize retirement. It must
preserve manual Paste and entered-text privacy and may record only the previously approved private/
aggregate evidence. No UI copy, text/AX model, Paste/clipboard or RPG Accessibility change is
authorized.

Authorized files and the pin stop remain exactly those in the prior remediation: shipping changes are
limited to `Sources/Pebble/AppInputRouterM.swift`; nonshipping changes to
`Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift` and focused InputChord/AppInputRouter/TextEntry tests.
Storage/Core/TextInput/pebsmoke/AppSupport hashes and all verifier logic, product and LAN surfaces stay
frozen; the changed Pebble hash requires fresh exact one-pin Architecture/Security approval.

**AppKit Reactivation/Input-identity Remediation Architecture Revision verdict: PASS.** The revised
driver first proves reactivation preservation, then performs each action once with observation-only
acknowledgement and no repair/retry masking, while the product ledger reset remains bounded and
correct. Build remains blocked until renewed Security (plan) passes this revision; all final installed
VoiceOver and downstream evidence remains mandatory.

## Design re-review — AppKit Reactivation/Input-identity Remediation (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`5c70a8728228a72275b890dbbee87da5e6f8dffee7091a44e802bd27a424da55`. The revision closes every
prior Design blocker:

1. Before any repair-capable action, it proves the same World Name object, text, `{1,0}` caret,
   logical/AX focus, Seed nonfocus, window and group survive reactivation. The later single AX setter
   is therefore an explicit idempotence/readiness test rather than a way to hide lost focus or caret.
2. The one-shot action ledger and action-free polling establish one activation, focus switch and
   key pair per intended action. Every text/caret transition, Seed switch, World Name return and
   saturated Right is observable at the exact intermediate state; a failed acknowledgement ends the
   attempt without repair or reposting.
3. Diagnostics remain aggregate-only. Five ordered cold-profile runs use one measured release hash,
   require five first-attempt passes and retain every failure, so neither UI timing nor dropped input
   can be made green by retry selection.
4. The product session reset remains invisible to field text/caret/focus and prevents stale duplicate
   identity or modifier state. Automated AX proof remains subordinate to installed VoiceOver review
   of names, roles, announcements, caret navigation, field switching, reactivation context and resize
   retirement. UI copy, manual Paste/privacy and RPG Accessibility remain unchanged.

**AppKit Reactivation/Input-identity Remediation Design re-review verdict: PASS.** The revised
architecture now proves the intended human-visible and Accessibility behavior without retry masking.
Build may proceed only after renewed Security (plan), with final installed VoiceOver Design Sign-off
and all downstream gates still mandatory.

## Security (plan) — AppKit Reactivation/Input-identity Remediation final review (2026-07-11)

Independently reviewed the diagnosis, revised Architecture, Design PASS and current router/driver
owners at plan SHA-256
`a583e4c1b647bf8141d7c064730245998755891e3f90e6795962550d6f2610be` against delivery-identity,
session-exhaustion, lifecycle, automation false-green, privacy and frozen-scope risks.

### Findings

None.

The product root cause is valid: deactivation currently resets `nextPhysicalRoutingSerial` and the
exhaustion latch while retaining `RPGPureInputRouter.consumedIdentities` and
`handledEquivalents`, allowing a new-session serial to collide with a prior consumed delivery identity.
The ordered MainActor reset closes that boundary by replacing the pure router and clearing equivalent
markers before serial zero/latch reuse, then clearing configured-press and modifier ledgers as the same
input-session transaction. Within a session the monotonic serial, bounded sixteen-identity duplicate
ledger, exact AppKit-event marker and fail-closed exhaustion semantics remain unchanged; only the
defined lifecycle reset can release exhaustion, with no event-number/timestamp authorization escape.

The revised driver does not repair the product before proving it. It first binds the exact app, PID,
bundle, release hash, one stable fullscreen window and unique GameView; after one Finder/Pebble
activation pair it proves the original field/group/window identity, fixed test value length/range and
focus state before any setter. The later one-shot AX setter is explicitly nonmutating. A fixed action-
ID ledger covers every activation, click, AX setter and global HID down/up pair; waits are predicate-
only, cannot invoke actions, and every edit, caret, field-switch and saturated-Right intermediate state
is acknowledged before the next action. Failure ends that first attempt: no repost, repair, retry,
PID-targeted substitution, sleep-as-correctness or retry-until-green path is authorized.

Diagnostics are limited to stage/action plus aggregate length, range and focus; no entered value,
free-form AX value, event payload or clipboard bytes may be printed or hashed. Zero clipboard access,
retained element/resize retirement, cleanup and installed manual-Paste/VoiceOver proof remain binding.
Closing evidence is exactly five ordered cold fresh-profile first attempts against one premeasured
release hash, requiring five passes and zero discarded/replaced failures, followed by the full test,
security, smoke, binary/verifier, exact-hash/pin and installed gates.

Authorized shipping scope remains only `Sources/Pebble/AppInputRouterM.swift`; driver and focused tests
are nonshipping. Core delivery identity, text/AX/RPG models, AppSupport latch, UI copy, Paste/clipboard,
receipt/parser, products, verifier logic and LAN stay frozen. The resulting Pebble hash must stop for
the required exact one-pin Architecture/Security review; Storage/Core/TextInput/pebsmoke and evidence-
only AppSupport hashes may not move.

Review boundary: plan and read-only source inspection only. No source, test, build, AppKit run,
installed state, deployment, commit or push was changed or performed by Security.

**Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** The atomic session reset and observation-only, exactly-once driver
close the diagnosed collision and automation races without weakening duplicate protection,
exhaustion, privacy or scope. Builder may proceed only within the named files and conditions; fresh
Security (code), five-run AppKit evidence, installed Design Sign-off, independent Test and every
downstream gate remain mandatory against stable final bytes.

## AppKit Reactivation Final Pebble Pin Amendment (2026-07-11)

Reviewed the final release artifacts and approved remediation scope at plan SHA-256
`a94429b087ca549e6222d085dc19bf9f956594fec2ae94ec263ac95b37db63fe`. The only authorized shipping
change is the ordered MainActor input-session reset in `Sources/Pebble/AppInputRouterM.swift`: it
replaces the pure router and clears handled-equivalent state before serial-zero and exhaustion-latch
reuse, then clears configured-press and modifier state in the same transaction. Driver and focused-
test changes are nonshipping. The resulting release `Pebble` SHA-256 is
`a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998`, replacing only the previously
reviewed product pin `406929c1592d50f964c8dcc0976358dbe1c1c89885aff0c5f6027beda4f3f347`.

The focused AppInputRouter/text-entry suite reports 33/33 passing, the AppKit driver typecheck and
scope-diff checks are green, and the warning-free release build completed in 116.27 seconds. The
release binary security scan passes. The storage-surface verifier reaches its intended one-pin stop
only because it still contains the old `Pebble` product hash; every preceding frozen check passes.
The other four verifier-pinned artifact hashes remain exact:

- `pebsmoke`: `d989c030656377ea1dca57b563408e2f6ae739e391904bde8be25f4f1d6dde2f`
- `PebbleStorage.o`: `ed37590e383037968b25905cb7ecd1d29e8faa43ba1f62a4919baebf9aabc6ba`
- `PebbleCore.o`: `39e37366bcba82ba65131a1112ad0b262f562279e363cf621542a804bd3ae028`
- `PebbleTextInput.o`: `547f0c44a950be0b7287ab73b918b6a3f486502e7ba57d7897e2d751b4d040ac`

The evidence-only `PebbleAppSupport.o` hash also remains exact at
`d543fc8af3427cd5fcfea8d6ed113e32212b28f7040dfbbc957db0ba21a4e1f5`; this amendment does not add it
to the verifier surface. These unchanged artifacts, the shipping/nonshipping diff boundary and the
passing binary scan establish that the new product digest is caused by the approved input-session
repair rather than Storage, Core, TextInput, smoke, AppSupport, verifier-surface or product-surface
drift.

### Exact scope, risks, tests and Conditions for Builder

Builder may edit only the existing `EXPECTED_PEBBLE_PRODUCT_SHA256` value in
`scripts/verify-pebble-storage-release-surface.sh`, changing it from the old digest above to the exact
new digest above. No verifier logic, path, product, surface, error behavior or other expected hash may
change. No source, test, AppSupport pin/surface, receipt/parser, product or LAN edit is authorized by
this amendment.

The remaining risks are a falsely attributed product digest, a lifecycle reset regression masked by
automation, or post-approval artifact drift. Close them respectively with exact diff-scope and frozen-
artifact checks plus the binary scan; the already-green focused 33/33 suite followed by the mandatory
five ordered cold first-attempt AppKit runs; and a fresh clean warning-free release build, rehash and
unchanged-verifier run after the pin edit. The pin edit does not satisfy or replace any downstream
gate.

After the exact one-line pin change, Builder must rerun the clean warning-free release build, confirm
the five verifier hashes plus the evidence-only AppSupport hash above, rerun the binary scan and prove
the otherwise unchanged verifier passes. The same final `Pebble` bytes must then pass the full XCTest,
security-scan and `pebsmoke` gates; five ordered cold fresh-profile AppKit first attempts with zero
failures, retries, replacements or discarded outcomes; fresh Security (code); installed VoiceOver
Design Sign-off; the release pipeline, receipt/deployed-bundle verification and authorized deployment.
Any other hash, source, verifier, product, receipt or LAN delta invalidates this amendment and requires
renewed review before continuing.

**AppKit Reactivation Final Pebble Pin Architecture verdict: PASS.** The exact one-pin update from
`406929c1592d50f964c8dcc0976358dbe1c1c89885aff0c5f6027beda4f3f347` to
`a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998` is causally justified and
authorized. Architecture performed no source, test or verifier-pin edit. Full runtime, Security,
installed-design, Test, deployment and source-control closure remains incomplete and mandatory.

## Design Review — AppKit Reactivation Final Pebble Pin Amendment (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`ad5f4a7c8c029ca4e7b781f93367fbb330175e4ad7080e5e15ef20959831b4cc`. The exact product-pin
advance is bookkeeping for the already approved session-reset bytes; it introduces no new visible or
Accessibility behavior and preserves the approved design only under these binding conditions:

1. The reset remains one MainActor session boundary that clears duplicate/equivalent/pressed/modifier
   bookkeeping before serial and exhaustion reuse. It must not replace either text field or change
   entered text, zero-length caret range, logical/AX focus, visible caret, VoiceOver context or the
   first post-reactivation key's exactly-once result.
2. Focused 33/33 and a warning-free build are preliminary evidence only. The exact final product hash
   must complete five ordered, cold, independently packaged fresh-profile AppKit first attempts with
   five passes, zero failures and no retry, replacement or discarded outcome. Each run retains the
   pre-action identity/text/caret/focus proof, one-shot action ledger, action-free waits and every
   exact intermediate field/range acknowledgement.
3. The pin edit cannot substitute for installed Design Sign-off. On the same final bytes, VoiceOver
   must confirm names/roles, focus announcements, caret navigation, World Name/Seed switching,
   retained focus/context through reactivation and resize retirement. The approved manual-Paste copy,
   entered-text privacy, UI and RPG Accessibility behavior remain unchanged.
4. Only the named Pebble product hash may advance. Any additional source, verifier behavior, frozen
   hash, receipt, product or LAN delta invalidates this Design approval and all later evidence.

**AppKit Reactivation Final Pebble Pin Amendment Design Review verdict: PASS.** Exact one-pin
bookkeeping preserves the approved text, caret, focus and VoiceOver contracts; five undiscarded cold
first attempts and installed VoiceOver Design Sign-off remain mandatory before completion.

## Security (plan) — AppKit Reactivation Final Pebble Pin Amendment (2026-07-11)

`ed37590e383037968b25905cb7ecd1d29e8faa43ba1f62a4919baebf9aabc6ba`, `PebbleCore.o`

## Cold Attempt-1 ArrowLeft Failure Diagnosis and Driver Remediation (2026-07-11)

Architecture reviewed the stopped five-run evidence set at plan SHA-256
`0eb1bef8ff48d0723994e6aa3e5eedb73717dbc7ace1e89ffd5617af74fdd4fd`. Attempt 1 used exact release
SHA-256 `a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998`; packaging produced signed
executable SHA-256 `aab45756fd0643dd0c443a748298513e9c934d09d33b9ad6d2a93f7390f68133`
and CDHash `7546586f6c278efd2bb4fee016c5671ee025019f`. Cleanup passed and attempts 2-5 were correctly not run.
The binding failure was:

`reactivation-and-middle-edit: world-name left 1 ... expected length 4/range 3/focus true, observed length 4/range 4/focus true`.

This was not the first post-reactivation key. The driver's exact ordering proves that `b`, `c` and `d`
each crossed its own value/range/focus acknowledgement before `world-name.key.left-1` was entered in
the one-shot ledger and its one global HID down/up pair was posted. The failure preserves the field's
text length, logical focus and end caret; it is a missing first-Left semantic transition, not text,
identity or focus corruption.

### Runtime-flow findings

The current evidence cannot claim that ArrowLeft reached `AppInputRouter`: neither product nor driver
records an ingress receipt. If a ready ArrowLeft reaches `routeToScreen`, `Screen.onKeyEvent`
synchronously calls `TextField.moveCaret(by: -1)`, and the same call synchronously commits the new AX
descriptor through `notifyTextAccessibilityValueChanged`. Therefore a routed-ready event should expose
range `3`; persistent AX range `4` strongly indicates either that the event never reached `GameView`,
or that it reached the router while text ingress was not ready and was intentionally consumed. There
is no evidence for a separate internal-caret/AX-range split.

The repaired pure-router ledger is not a supported cause. After the resignation reset, the accepted
`b`, `c` and `d` deliveries use distinct monotonically increasing routing identities in the fresh
router; first Left receives the next distinct identity. No callback on this path restarts the serial
without also replacing the router, and no retained handled-equivalent applies to an unmodified Left.
The MainActor reset ordering is therefore correct and must not be changed or weakened.

The driver does have a confirmed activation-validation defect. Its reactivation wait accepts one
instantaneous `app.isActive`/AX-window/field sample. `validateBeforeAction` then checks another
instantaneous `app.isActive` value and a window, while `validateFieldBeforeAction` adds only the
custom logical AX-focus value. Neither requires the exact `NSWorkspace.frontmostApplication`, a
consecutive stable key-window surface, or preserves activation identity in the post-action field
barrier. Apple's SDK contract in `AppKit.framework/Headers/NSRunningApplication.h` explicitly marks
time-varying running-application properties as race-prone and states that a successful activation
request neither means immediate activation nor guarantees activation at all. The current one-sample
assumption contradicts that contract.

One separately labeled diagnostic execution—not a retry, replacement or member of the failed
five-run set—used the same exact `a25cabb...` release and reproduced the same signed package identity,
then failed in `reactivation-and-middle-edit` at `action identity`. At that guard the expected PID,
bundle URL and executable hash were fixed and independently unchanged; `app.isActive == false` is the
only mutable predicate capable of producing that exact error. The temporary app was cleaned and the
release hash remained exact. This confirms activation loss at an action boundary. It strongly supports
an active-app/global-HID time-of-check/time-of-use cause for the original Left loss, but does not
retroactively prove an unobserved router receipt.

### Minimal remediation in dependency order

1. Change only `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift` to add one observation-only active-surface
   barrier. Success requires several consecutive samples spanning a defined minimum stability window,
   not a single sample or fixed sleep: `app.isActive`; exact
   `NSWorkspace.shared.frontmostApplication` object/PID/bundle/executable; exactly one AX window and
   that exact focused window; window geometry matching the owning screen's captured stable fullscreen
   geometry; unique retained GameView group; and the expected retained field identities, aggregate
   values/ranges and focus state. A mismatching sample resets only the sample counter; it performs no
   action.
2. Use that barrier after the one Pebble activation and before the preservation proof, immediately
   before and after the one idempotent AX focus setter, and before every keyboard/AX action. Inside
   each existing one-shot ledger closure, perform one final non-waiting active/frontmost/focused-
   window/field identity check immediately before posting or setting. No run-loop turn or unrelated
   work may occur between that final check and the single external action.
3. Extend every per-action acknowledgement to require the exact app remains active/frontmost and the
   original focused window/group remain current in addition to the existing field state. Report the
   action ID and aggregate booleans such as active/frontmost/focused-window exactness separately from
   length/range/focus. Do not print app names, paths, field values, event payloads or free-form AX
   content. This will distinguish activation loss from a routed-but-unapplied key without product
   instrumentation or global keyboard capture.
4. Keep exactly one Finder activation, one Pebble activation and one execution of each existing
   action ID. Do not reactivate after a failed barrier, repost any event, use PID-targeted delivery,
   add retry-until-green, use a fixed sleep as the success condition, weaken the global HID proof, or
   add event-number/timestamp/fingerprint exceptions. A failed stability/action/postcondition check
   ends that attempt and remains evidence.

### Tests, risks and Conditions for Builder

Strengthen `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift` to require exact frontmost identity,
consecutive semantic stability, the final in-ledger pre-action check, active-surface postconditions,
action-scoped aggregate diagnostics and the existing action-free waits/one-shot completeness. Retain
the focused router-session tests, driver typecheck, privacy scan, zero clipboard access, retained AX
identity/resize retirement and cleanup checks. No passive global event tap or product debug logging is
authorized: either would widen input-observation/privacy scope while still failing to prove AppKit
dispatch to `GameView`.

The failed attempt-1 log and stopped-set summary must be retained. After the reviewed driver correction,
start a distinctly named new evidence set against the same exact release bytes. It must contain five
ordered, independently packaged cold first attempts with five passes and zero retry, replacement,
discard or selection. If any active-surface barrier or semantic action fails, stop the set and return
the exact aggregate evidence to Architecture; do not continue simply to collect a later pass.

Builder scope is limited to `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, the directly affected
`TextEntrySourceTests` contract and this plan. No shipping source, AppInputRouter, Core/TextInput/
AppSupport, verifier/pin, package/receipt/parser, product or LAN change is authorized. Consequently the
release `Pebble` hash must remain `a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998`
and every frozen hash/pin remains exact. Any product-byte or broader verifier delta invalidates this
plan. Build is blocked until renewed Design Review and Security (plan) approve this proof correction;
after implementation, fresh Security (code), the focused/full/security/smoke gates, five-run set,
installed VoiceOver Design Sign-off, pipeline, receipt/deployed-bundle proof and deployment remain
mandatory.

**Cold attempt-1 diagnosis verdict: FAIL.** The stopped set is not deployment evidence, and the log
cannot establish an ArrowLeft router receipt. **Driver Remediation Architecture verdict: PASS.** A
driver-only sustained active-surface barrier, final in-ledger identity check and action-scoped
postcondition close the confirmed activation race without altering product behavior, weakening
exactly-once input, observing user keystrokes or masking the failed attempt.

## Design Review — Cold Attempt-1 Driver Remediation (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`9822880816bf3aee3d88e8d892b60e5e11285cef577864cc3c05a20db482cc1d`. Exact frontmost,
focused-window, fullscreen, group, field-identity and field-state readiness is the correct observable
surface for global-HID usability and Accessibility proof. The final in-ledger check, aggregate-only
diagnostics, retained failed set and prohibition on reactivation/repost/sleeps/product logging are
also correct. One no-masking defect remains in the barrier contract:

1. Only the first barrier immediately following the single Pebble activation may reset its consecutive
   sample count while the requested activation settles within a fixed bound. Once that barrier and
   the pre-repair preservation proof establish the ready surface, readiness is a sustained invariant
   for the rest of the attempt. Every later pre-action barrier must start from the already-ready epoch;
   its first active/frontmost/focused-window/fullscreen/group/field identity or focus mismatch ends the
   attempt. It may not reset a counter and wait for the surface to recover.
2. Per-action acknowledgement may poll for the expected text length or caret transition, but every
   sample during that poll must retain the exact active surface. Any active/frontmost/window/group/
   field identity or focus loss is immediately fatal even if the requested text/caret state later
   appears. Otherwise a transient focus loss followed by recovery can still be selected away as
   timing and make an unusable first attempt pass.
3. The final identity check remains inside the already-entered one-shot ledger immediately before the
   one external action, with no run-loop turn or repair between them. Failure records only action ID
   and aggregate readiness/length/range/focus booleans, performs cleanup, retains the outcome and
   stops the newly named five-run set. Installed VoiceOver Sign-off remains mandatory and unchanged.

**Cold Attempt-1 Driver Remediation Design Review verdict: FAIL.** The driver-only direction is sound,
but Build remains blocked until post-settlement readiness loss is fail-fast rather than allowed to
reset and recover inside later barriers or action acknowledgements. That correction is required to
prove first-attempt usability without masking focus instability.

## Cold Attempt-1 Driver Remediation Architecture Revision (2026-07-11)

Architecture adopts the blocking Design corrections at plan SHA-256
`48917d1a0637ddab0a360eb582c8534bf7ed352509e46f01ba53a7f1ede0ab46`. This revision supersedes only
the recovery behavior of the prior active-surface barrier. The confirmed activation diagnosis,
driver-only scope, one-shot actions, adjacent in-ledger check, aggregate-only diagnostics, retained
failed set and unchanged product bytes remain binding.

### One settling barrier, then a fail-fast ready epoch

Model reactivation verification as a one-way driver state transition. Immediately after the single
`pebble.reactivate` action, and only there, the driver is in `settling`. Within one fixed deadline, an
observation-only barrier may require consecutive exact samples and reset that consecutive-sample
count when a sample is not yet ready. It performs no activation, focus, keyboard, mouse or other
repair action. Each successful sample requires the exact app/PID/bundle/executable and
`NSWorkspace.shared.frontmostApplication`, exactly one exact focused AX window, captured stable
fullscreen geometry, the unique retained GameView group, both retained field identities, and the
pre-repair World Name/Seed value-length/range/focus state.

After the settling barrier succeeds, run the existing preservation proof before any setter or key.
Only if that proof independently confirms the original window/group/field identities, fixed World
Name `1/{1,0}` state and focus, and Seed `0/{0,0}` nonfocus does the driver enter the `ready` epoch.
There is no transition from `ready` back to `settling` within an attempt.

Every later pre-action barrier is a single fail-fast assertion against the already-ready epoch. Its
first active/frontmost/focused-window/fullscreen-geometry/group/field-identity or action-expected
focus mismatch fails the attempt immediately. It must not reset a counter, consume a timeout waiting
for recovery, call the initial settling helper or establish a new ready epoch. This applies before the
idempotent reactivation AX setter and before every later AX or keyboard action.

The existing final check remains inside the already-entered one-shot ledger closure, directly adjacent
to the single external action. It repeats the exact ready-epoch and action-specific field assertion
without running the event loop. On failure, the action remains recorded as attempted, no event/setter
is issued, cleanup runs, the aggregate result is retained and the five-run set stops.

### Fail-fast surface during semantic acknowledgement

An action acknowledgement may poll only the action's explicitly expected semantic value/length or
zero-length caret-range transition. Before accepting or continuing every individual poll sample, it
must synchronously assert the exact active/frontmost/focused-window/fullscreen/group/retained-field
surface and the focus state required after that action. Any surface or focus mismatch latches failure
and exits immediately; a later recovered sample can never make the acknowledgement pass.

For ordinary keys, the focused field and every non-target field property remain invariant while only
the expected value/range target may settle. An AX field-switch action is synchronous: its first
post-action sample must have the exact new World Name/Seed focus assignment while retaining both
identities and all non-target values/ranges. The final fullscreen/resize action retains its previously
approved explicit identity-retirement contract; only that named action may transition from the exact
pre-resize surface to its exact renewed window/group/field surface, and no app/frontmost/focused-window
loss, ambiguous/missing surface or return to an earlier state may be sampled away as part of that
transition.

Diagnostics remain bounded to stage/action ID and aggregate active/frontmost/window/fullscreen/group/
field-identity/focus/length/range booleans. They do not print app names or paths, field values, event
payloads, free-form AX data or clipboard content. The driver must not add product logging, a passive
keyboard tap or any other input observation to infer router receipt.

### Tests and Conditions for Builder

Update the directly affected source contract to prove there is exactly one counter-resetting settling
barrier and it occurs only after the single Pebble activation; the preservation proof precedes the
irreversible ready-epoch transition; no later pre-action path calls the settling helper, resets a
counter or waits for readiness recovery; every one-shot action contains the adjacent final assertion;
and every semantic poll sample calls a fail-fast active-surface assertion before checking the target
value/range. Add a negative source/driver test that a first post-ready mismatch is terminal even when a
later supplied sample would be exact. Retain all prior action-ledger, ordering, aggregate-diagnostic,
privacy, cleanup, AX identity/resize and router-session coverage.

Builder scope remains only `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, the directly affected
`Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift` contract and this plan. No shipping source,
AppInputRouter, verifier/pin, package/receipt/parser, product or LAN edit is authorized. The failed
attempt-1 log and stopped set remain immutable evidence. Do not reactivate, repost, retry, replace or
discard an outcome; do not use PID-targeted input or a fixed sleep as correctness. After implementation
and renewed downstream review, start one distinctly named new five-first-attempt set against unchanged
release SHA-256 `a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998`.

**Prior Driver Remediation Architecture verdict: SUPERSEDED.** Its repeatable stability barrier could
hide post-ready focus loss and does not authorize Build. **Revised Driver Remediation Architecture
verdict: PASS.** Only initial activation settling may use consecutive recovery samples; the preserved
ready epoch, every later pre-action assertion and every semantic sample are fail-fast. Build remains
blocked until renewed Design Review and Security (plan) pass this revision, followed by fresh
Security (code), independent Test, the undiscarded five-run set, installed VoiceOver Design Sign-off
and every remaining deployment gate on unchanged product bytes.

## Design re-review — Cold Attempt-1 Driver Remediation Revision (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`c057a3238b25b13ab197c14590f46f13bac2485e06242266d55b1c1b70f91b41`. The revision adopts the
ready-epoch correction exactly:

1. Only the single post-reactivation `settling` barrier may reset consecutive observations. The
   independent preservation proof must succeed before the irreversible `ready` transition, and no
   later code path may re-enter settling or wait through loss of the user-visible active surface.
2. Every later pre-action check is fail-fast, then repeated inside the one-shot ledger immediately
   adjacent to its sole external action. Every acknowledgement sample preserves active/frontmost,
   focused-window, fullscreen, group, retained-field identity and action-specific focus; a mismatch
   is terminal even if text or caret would later recover.
3. Field switches and resize have explicit synchronous surface transitions rather than generic retry
   tolerance. Aggregate-only diagnostics, immutable failed evidence, same-hash five cold first
   attempts, zero repost/reactivation/retry/discard, and zero product logging/input observation remain
   binding.
4. This driver proof remains corroboration. Installed VoiceOver must still verify focus announcements,
   caret navigation, field switching, retained reactivation context and resize retirement on the
   unchanged final product bytes; no UI, manual-Paste/privacy or RPG Accessibility behavior changes.

**Cold Attempt-1 Driver Remediation Revision Design re-review verdict: PASS.** The prior no-masking
blocker is closed: post-settlement readiness loss can no longer be sampled away. Build may proceed
only after renewed Security (plan), with the undiscarded five-run set and installed VoiceOver Design
Sign-off still mandatory.

## Security (plan) — Cold Attempt-1 Driver Remediation final review (2026-07-11)

Independently reviewed the confirmed diagnosis, revised Architecture, Design PASS and current driver
owners at plan SHA-256
`69e1e11a47c5441648c3e824e8ad259d844eb37341f5d0e51015f2326dae577f` against readiness recovery,
global-HID time-of-check/use, action masking, evidence selection, privacy and frozen-scope risks.

### Findings

None.

The revised lifecycle is one-way and fail-closed. Exactly one post-`pebble.reactivate` `settling`
barrier may reset only its consecutive observation count within a fixed deadline and may perform no
action. The independent preservation proof then binds the unchanged app/PID/bundle/executable,
frontmost app, focused fullscreen window/geometry, GameView group, retained fields and pre-repair
length/range/focus state before the irreversible `ready` transition. No later path can re-enter
settling or wait through active-surface recovery.

After `ready`, every pre-action barrier is a single fail-fast assertion. The same exact surface and
action-specific field focus is checked again inside the already-entered one-shot ledger immediately
adjacent to its sole global-HID or AX action, with no run-loop turn or repair opportunity. Every
semantic poll checks that surface before examining its expected target transition; the first mismatch
latches terminal failure even if a later sample would recover. AX field switches and resize have
explicit synchronous old/new surfaces, and resize alone may retire identities without tolerating app,
frontmost or focused-window loss. No activation, focus, key or mouse action is reposted; no PID-
targeted delivery, fixed-sleep correctness, passive keyboard tap, product logging or retry-until-green
path is authorized.

Diagnostics remain limited to stage/action and aggregate readiness/length/range/focus booleans; app
names/paths, entered values, event payloads, free-form AX data and clipboard content are forbidden.
The negative later-recovery fixture, one-shot/action-free-wait source contracts, privacy/typecheck,
cleanup, retained-identity/resize and router-session tests remain mandatory. The original failed
attempt-1 log and stopped set are immutable. A distinctly named replacement evidence set uses the same
exact release SHA-256 `a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998`,
contains five ordered independently packaged cold first attempts, requires five passes, and stops at
the first failure with no retry, replacement, discard, exclusion or later-pass selection.

Authorized edits remain driver and its direct source-contract test only. Shipping source,
AppInputRouter/Core/TextInput/AppSupport, verifier/pin, package/receipt/parser, product and LAN surfaces
remain frozen; the release hash may not move. Fresh Security (code), focused/full/security/smoke
testing, the undiscarded five-run set, installed VoiceOver Design Sign-off, pipeline, receipt/deployed-
bundle verification and deployment remain required on unchanged final product bytes.

Review boundary: plan and read-only source inspection only. No driver, test, build, AppKit run,
installed state, deployment, commit or push was changed or performed by Security.

**Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** The one-settling-then-ready state machine, adjacent one-shot checks
and per-sample fail-fast surface close the activation race without sampling away instability or
weakening privacy/evidence integrity. Builder may proceed only within the named nonshipping files and
conditions; all final-byte downstream gates remain mandatory.

## Ready-Epoch Cold-Five v1 Initial-Settling Failure Diagnosis (2026-07-11)

Architecture reviewed the immutable `ready-epoch-cold-five-v1` stopped set, current driver and source
contract at plan SHA-256 `aa4d7c8cea738935c80316b8a3558bf937b39bb8625a730cac887b4c2c05fefe`.
Attempt 1 retained exact release SHA-256
`a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998`, signed executable SHA-256
`aab45756fd0643dd0c443a748298513e9c934d09d33b9ad6d2a93f7390f68133` and CDHash
`7546586f6c278efd2bb4fee016c5671ee025019f`. Cleanup passed, the set stopped after attempt 1, and no
setter or post-ready key ran. Its binding failure was
`reactivation-preservation: initial active-surface settling`.

### Exact false predicate and diagnostic-contract failure

`activeSurface` currently defines frontmost exactness as
`NSWorkspace.shared.frontmostApplication === app` plus matching PID, bundle and executable. The
`app` object comes from the `openApplication` completion; `frontmostApplication` may return a distinct
`NSRunningApplication` wrapper for the same process. Pointer identity is not application identity.
Apple's `NSRunningApplication` SDK contract directs callers to object equality for process comparison,
and its wrappers are not documented as pointer-canonical.

Two separately labeled read-only diagnostics confirm this is deterministic rather than a three-second
timing shortage. For the same stable process, frontmost versus PID/list wrappers reported pointer
identity false, object equality true and PID equality true. More decisively, the exact packaged Pebble
launch-completion object reported at both launch and Finder/Pebble reactivation:

`active=true pointer=false equal=true pid=true bundle=true executable=true`.

Across the full reactivation sampling period, at least one complete logical-identity sample was
present while no pointer-identity sample was ever present. The diagnostic app and temporary package
were cleaned, and the release hash remained exact. Because `surface.exact` conjunctively requires the
always-false pointer predicate, `ReadyEpoch.settle` resets its consecutive count on every sample and
cannot succeed in three seconds or any longer timeout. The same `frontmost === app` defect also exists
in the resize loop and would deterministically fail that later gate if settling were bypassed.

The driver then violates the approved aggregate-diagnostic contract. `ActiveSurfaceObservation`
constructs safe aggregate booleans, but `ReadyEpoch.settle` accepts only `() -> Bool`, discards the
observation and on timeout throws only `initial active-surface settling`. The immutable log therefore
cannot identify which predicate was false. This is a verifier defect, not evidence of product
activation, router, caret, field or Accessibility failure.

### Constraint audit

Frontmost pointer identity is overconstrained and must be removed everywhere. Exact PID, bundle and
executable checks remain defense in depth, but they accompany `NSRunningApplication` value equality
(`==`/`isEqual`), never Swift reference identity (`===`). Window, group and field preservation is not
overconstrained: those checks use AX `CFEqual`, retain the exact published elements intentionally and
implement the approved reactivation identity contract. They remain mandatory.

The predicate named `fullscreenGeometry` is not the false predicate and is not currently an absolute
fullscreen proof. It only proves that the captured AX point/size remain exact and a screen with the
captured AppKit frame still exists. Rename this observation to `geometryRetained` so diagnostics do
not claim evidence they lack. Establish fullscreen once before Finder activation using one uniquely
matching active-display bound in the same Quartz global coordinate space as the AX window rectangle;
then settling and the ready epoch retain that captured display identity and exact rectangle. This
separates `fullscreenEstablished` from `geometryRetained`, avoids mixing AppKit bottom-left and AX/
Quartz coordinates, and preserves rather than weakens the fullscreen requirement.

### Minimal remediation architecture

1. In `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, replace both `frontmost === app` checks—the active-
   surface observation and resize transition—with value equality plus the existing exact PID/bundle/
   executable checks. No code path may compare `NSRunningApplication` wrappers by pointer identity.
2. Preserve the one-way ReadyEpoch contract unchanged: only initial settling may reset consecutive
   samples; preservation then enters ready; every later surface mismatch remains immediately fatal.
   Do not extend the timeout, weaken frontmost identity, or add reactivation, retry, repost, fixed
   sleep, PID-targeted delivery or product logging.
3. Make the settling sample structured rather than Boolean-only. At minimum it carries the safe active-
   surface observation, `fieldStateExact`, and aggregate length/range/focus evidence. `settle` retains
   the last sample, total sample count and maximum consecutive exact count. On timeout it reports the
   stage plus each predicate's safe `last`, `ever_true` and `ever_false` booleans, sample counts and
   the existing aggregate field lengths/ranges/focus. This identifies an always-false predicate and a
