   fluctuating predicate without recording names, paths, values, AX free-form content or events.
4. Split combined predicates enough to diagnose their boundary: app not-terminated/PID/bundle/
   executable; active; frontmost present/value-equal/PID/bundle/executable; AX window count/retained-
   identity/focused-identity; fullscreen established/display retained/geometry retained; group count/
   identity; field count/name identity/seed identity; and name/seed focus. `exact` remains the
   conjunction. Missing AX values become false booleans, never raw error or object output.
5. Keep each later fail-fast and action acknowledgement on the same structured observation so future
   failures use the same safe predicate vocabulary. The explicit resize transition may change only
   its already-approved window/group/field identity state; it still uses value-based frontmost
   application identity and fails immediately on active/frontmost/focused-window ambiguity.

### Tests and Conditions for Builder

Update `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift` to forbid `frontmost === app` and any other
frontmost `NSRunningApplication` pointer comparison; require value equality plus PID/bundle/executable
in both active-surface and resize paths; prove structured settling diagnostics reach the timeout error;
and require every named predicate, sample count and maximum-consecutive count in aggregate output.
Add a deterministic wrapper fixture or source contract proving distinct wrappers with value identity
are accepted while any PID/bundle/executable mismatch remains rejected. Retain the one-settling-only,
first-post-ready-mismatch-terminal, adjacent one-shot, action-free wait, privacy, cleanup, AX identity,
resize and router-session tests.

Builder scope remains only `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, the directly affected
`Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift` contract and this plan. No shipping source,
AppInputRouter, verifier/pin, package/receipt/parser, product or LAN edit is authorized. Preserve the
immutable failed `ready-epoch-cold-five-v1` logs; they cannot become a pass or be omitted. After renewed
Design Review, Security (plan), Builder work and Security (code), start one distinctly named cold-five
set on unchanged `a25cabb...` bytes. It must again stop at its first failure with no retry, replacement,
discard or later-pass selection.

**Ready-Epoch Cold-Five v1 evidence verdict: FAIL.** The stopped set is not product evidence, and its
generic failure omitted required diagnostics. **Initial-settling diagnosis verdict: PASS.** Noncanonical
`NSRunningApplication` pointer identity is the confirmed always-false predicate; three seconds is not
the cause. **Value-identity and aggregate-diagnostic Remediation Architecture verdict: PASS.** The
minimal nonshipping correction restores the intended exact frontmost contract and auditable failure
evidence without weakening the ready epoch or masking an action. Build remains blocked pending renewed
Design Review and Security (plan), followed by every unchanged downstream gate.

## Design Review — Ready-Epoch Cold-Five v1 Initial-Settling Remediation (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`a99fe8555b00ba6842e328877455f156da6e53f8f8b3a25f9031461bb798d545`. The nonshipping correction
restores a truthful interaction verifier under these binding conditions:

1. Frontmost application exactness uses `NSRunningApplication` value/process equality together with
   the existing PID, bundle and executable checks; equality alone is never sufficient. Pointer
   identity is forbidden only for application wrappers. Window, GameView group and both field objects
   continue to use retained AX `CFEqual` identity so focus/context replacement cannot be sampled away.
2. `fullscreenEstablished` is proved once before Finder activation from one unambiguous active-display
   identity and exact window/display rectangle in one documented Quartz coordinate space. After that,
   `geometryRetained` preserves that same display and rectangle; it cannot re-establish fullscreen,
   choose a different display or wait through a mismatch. Post-ready mismatch is immediately fatal,
   except for the one already approved explicit resize identity transition.
3. Initial settling diagnostics may expose only fixed predicate names, `last`/`ever_true`/
   `ever_false` booleans, total/maximum-consecutive sample counts and aggregate field lengths, ranges
   and focus. They must not expose app/display names, paths, object descriptions, field values,
   free-form AX data, event data or clipboard content. The same safe vocabulary applies to later
   fail-fast errors.
4. The one-way settling -> preservation -> ready contract, adjacent one-shot checks, per-sample
   fail-fast acknowledgements and prohibition on timeout extension, reactivation, repost, retry,
   sleep-as-correctness and product/input logging remain unchanged. The failed v1 set stays immutable;
   a new same-hash cold-five set stops at its first failure with no replacement or selection.
5. Automated AX evidence remains corroboration. Installed VoiceOver Design Sign-off must still prove
   names/roles, announcements, caret navigation, field switching, retained reactivation context and
   resize retirement; no product UI, manual-Paste/privacy or RPG Accessibility behavior changes.

**Ready-Epoch Cold-Five v1 Initial-Settling Remediation Design Review verdict: PASS.** Value/process
identity removes the false verifier predicate without weakening retained AX identity, and the safe
structured diagnostics improve failure usability without masking recovery or leaking entered data.
Renewed Security (plan), the undiscarded cold-five set and installed VoiceOver proof remain mandatory.

## Security (plan) — Ready-Epoch Cold-Five v1 Initial-Settling Remediation (2026-07-11)

Independently reviewed the diagnosis, remediation Architecture, Design PASS and current application/
AX identity owners at plan SHA-256
`698120eb06ed0269748187f4f1c3d89e068b4e8fd3eac39e0193303ae7585cfe` against process substitution,
surface replacement, recovery masking, diagnostic privacy and evidence-selection risks.

### Findings

None.

The confirmed false predicate is correctly isolated: distinct `NSRunningApplication` wrappers for the
same process are not pointer-canonical, while value equality, PID, bundle and executable identities
remain exact. Replacing only application-wrapper `===` with value/process equality is not a weakened
identity check: the original launch object must remain nonterminated, its fixed PID/bundle/canonical
executable and measured bytes remain bound, and the frontmost wrapper must be value-equal and repeat
the same PID/bundle/executable. Any termination, PID reuse through a replacement process, bundle/path/
hash substitution, missing wrapper or ambiguity fails. Pointer identity remains required where it is
meaningful: retained AX window, GameView group and field elements continue to use `CFEqual` and cannot
be replaced during settling or the ready epoch.

Fullscreen is established once before Finder activation by one unique active-display identity and an
exact AX-window/display rectangle in one documented Quartz coordinate space. Later samples may only
prove `geometryRetained` for that captured display and rectangle; they cannot choose a new display,
re-establish fullscreen or wait through a mismatch. The already approved resize action alone has an
explicit old-to-new identity transition and still cannot tolerate active/frontmost/focused-window
loss.

Structured settling retains the last sample, total count and maximum consecutive exact count and
reports fixed predicate names with `last`/`ever_true`/`ever_false` plus aggregate field length/range/
focus only. App/display names, paths, object descriptions, values, free-form AX/event data and
clipboard content remain forbidden. Deterministic distinct-wrapper fixtures accept only full value+
PID+bundle+executable identity, reject every component mismatch, and prove diagnostic propagation.

The one-way contract is unchanged: only initial settling may reset a consecutive count; preservation
precedes irreversible ready; every later pre-action and acknowledgement sample is fail-fast; no
timeout extension, reactivation, repost, repair, retry, sleep-as-correctness, product logging or input
observation is authorized. The failed `ready-epoch-cold-five-v1` set remains immutable. A distinctly
named new set uses unchanged release SHA-256
`a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998`, stops at its first failure,
and requires five ordered cold first-attempt passes without replacement, discard or later-pass
selection.

Authorized edits remain the nonshipping driver and direct source-contract test only. Shipping source,
product bytes, verifier/pin, receipt/parser, package, AppInputRouter/Core/TextInput/AppSupport and LAN
surfaces remain frozen. Fresh Security (code), focused/full/security/smoke tests, the undiscarded five-
run set, installed VoiceOver Design Sign-off, pipeline, receipt/deployed-bundle proof and deployment
remain mandatory on the same hash.

Review boundary: plan and read-only source inspection only. No driver, test, build, AppKit run,
installed state, deployment, commit or push was changed or performed by Security.

**Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** Value/process identity removes the noncanonical pointer predicate
without allowing application substitution, while retained AX/display identities, privacy, one-way
readiness and immutable same-hash evidence remain fail-closed. Builder may proceed only within the
named nonshipping files and conditions; all downstream gates remain mandatory.

## Value-Identity Cold-Five v2 Presentation Failure Diagnosis (2026-07-11)

Architecture reviewed the immutable `value-identity-cold-five-v2` stopped set, current presentation
predicate and shipping launch behavior at plan SHA-256
`3c34d1e099597efeab16146427aa2ba8cb575ff67698ad0071b9731051dd1493`. Attempt 1 retained exact
release SHA-256 `a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998`,
signed executable SHA-256 `aab45756fd0643dd0c443a748298513e9c934d09d33b9ad6d2a93f7390f68133`
and CDHash `7546586f6c278efd2bb4fee016c5671ee025019f`. Cleanup passed and the set stopped
at attempt 1 before Finder reactivation. Its binding failure was
`name-focus-and-edit: fullscreen_established=false matching_display_count_exact=false`.

### Read-only presentation evidence

A separately labeled diagnostic launched the exact packaged bytes and observed only aggregate
presentation state. The active Quartz display bound was `x=0, y=0, width=1728, height=1117`. The
corresponding `NSScreen.frame` was identical, `visibleFrame` was
`x=58, y=0, width=1670, height=1084`, and backing scale was `2.0`. Once the launch transition settled,
Pebble had one exact focused AX window with stable rectangle
`x=0, y=33, width=1728, height=1084`.

The window's published AX attribute names include `AXFullScreen` and `AXFullScreenButton`.
`AXFullScreen` was present and Boolean `true`; the fullscreen button was present. The state remained
active/frontmost with one focused window and the same rectangle across more than five seconds. The
matching on-screen CoreGraphics owner window used the same `x=0, y=33, width=1728, height=1084`
rectangle; a separate auxiliary owner window was `x=0, y=33, width=1728, height=44`. No active
display bound equalled the main AX window rectangle at any sample.

This is not a windowed fallback and not a product fullscreen defect. AppKit reports the authoritative
fullscreen state as true while publishing a stable system-inset window rectangle. AX and
CoreGraphics agree on that rectangle, so scale or coordinate conversion is not the cause. The
33-point inset is deliberate system-managed framing, not floating-point noise for which an equality
tolerance would be appropriate. The verifier's assumption that fullscreen requires
`AX window rectangle == CGDisplayBounds` is false.

Shipping startup also has a legitimate supported second mode. The window starts hidden, requests
fullscreen after 0.1 seconds, retries at 1.2-second intervals, and after five checks deliberately sets
alpha to one and reveals a usable windowed app if fullscreen never enters. The text-entry verifier
must not classify that documented fallback as a product failure or accept an unrevealed/transitional
window as ready.

### Presentation contract revision

The AppKit text-entry gate verifies usable global-HID text entry and reactivation in the presentation
mode Pebble actually exposes. It must bind exactly one stable initial presentation mode rather than
require display-filling geometry:

- `fullscreen`: the exact AX window publishes a present Boolean `AXFullScreen == true`.
- `windowedFallback`: the same attribute is present and false, and the uniquely matched on-screen
  CoreGraphics owner window is visibly revealed after the shipping fallback rather than merely
  published during a pending fullscreen attempt.

Mode selection occurs once in a bounded, observation-only launch-presentation barrier before any
navigation, field click or key. It may use consecutive semantic samples while launch settles, but it
performs no activation, fullscreen toggle, focus, mouse or key action. Each successful sample requires
the exact measured app frontmost, one exact focused AX window, a finite stable rectangle, one unique
containing active display, and one unique layer-zero CoreGraphics owner window matching the AX
rectangle. For windowed fallback, that owner window must be on-screen and visibly opaque; a false
`AXFullScreen` value alone cannot select fallback while the retry sequence may still be pending.

After selection, capture the mode, display identity, exact AX/CG window identity and rectangle as one
immutable presentation binding. Title navigation, field publication, reactivation settling, the
preservation proof, every ready-epoch check and every semantic acknowledgement must retain that exact
binding. They cannot reselect a mode, choose another display, wait through a mode/geometry mismatch or
upgrade a fallback to fullscreen. The already approved final F11 action is the sole presentation
transition: it must synchronously initiate and then establish the opposite AX fullscreen Boolean,
with its separately approved window/group/field identity renewal and no active/frontmost/focused-
window loss.

### Minimal remediation architecture

1. In `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, replace exact display-bound equality with a typed
   `LaunchPresentationMode` and a fail-closed `AXFullScreen` Boolean reader. Attribute absence, wrong
   type, multiple windows or ambiguous mode fails with aggregate predicates; geometry must never infer
   fullscreen when the AX state is available.
2. Map the finite AX window rectangle to exactly one active Quartz display that contains it and to
   exactly one on-screen layer-zero CoreGraphics window for the bound PID whose rectangle matches.
   Containment binds display ownership; it does not claim the window fills the display. Do not compare
   against `NSScreen.visibleFrame`, add a 33-point magic constant or use an epsilon to turn an inset
   rectangle into a display-filling rectangle.
3. Move initial presentation establishment ahead of `title-navigation`. Fullscreen may bind after
   consecutive stable `AXFullScreen == true` samples. Windowed fallback may bind only after consecutive
   stable `AXFullScreen == false` samples plus the uniquely matched CoreGraphics window's on-screen and
   opaque evidence. If that visibility evidence is unavailable or ambiguous, fail closed; do not wait
   a fixed sleep and assume fallback.
4. Replace `fullscreenEstablished` in `ActiveSurfaceObservation` with safe predicates for AX
   fullscreen attribute presence/type, bound-mode equality, display containment/identity, AX/CG
   rectangle equality and geometry retention. Keep exact application value/process identity and AX
   window/group/field `CFEqual` identity unchanged. Reactivation's initial settling is still the only
   later recovery-sampling barrier; after preservation, every mismatch remains immediately fatal.
5. Revise the final F11 acknowledgement to require the opposite bound mode, not merely a different
   size. Starting fullscreen must produce stable windowed state; starting in documented fallback must
   produce stable fullscreen state. Identity retirement, field text/range/focus preservation and all
   aggregate-only privacy constraints remain unchanged.

Safe diagnostics may report numeric AX/display/screen rectangles and backing scale when explicitly
capturing presentation, plus fixed booleans such as `ax_fullscreen_present`, `ax_fullscreen_value`,
`presentation_mode_bound`, `containing_display_count_exact`, `cg_window_count_exact`,
`cg_window_onscreen`, `cg_window_opaque`, `geometry_retained` and `opposite_mode_established`. They may
not report app/display names, paths, object descriptions, entered values, free-form AX data, events or
clipboard content.

### Tests and Conditions for Builder

Update `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift` to forbid
`CGDisplayBounds($0) == fullscreenRectangle`, `fullscreenRectangle == fullscreenDisplayBounds` and any
geometry-derived fullscreen Boolean. Require presentation binding before title navigation, typed AX
fullscreen state, unique containing-display and matching-visible-CG-window checks, and exact bound-mode
retention through reactivation. Add deterministic fixtures for the observed inset fullscreen
rectangle (`AXFullScreen == true` must pass without display equality), stable visible windowed fallback,
false/unrevealed transitional window rejection, missing or non-Boolean AX state, ambiguous displays/
windows, post-bind mode or geometry change, and both directions of the final F11 transition.

Retain structured safe aggregates, value/process frontmost identity, one-way ReadyEpoch semantics,
adjacent one-shot checks, first-mismatch failure, action-free waits, privacy, cleanup, field identity/
resize and router-session coverage. The immutable v1 and v2 failed sets remain evidence and cannot be
relabelled, omitted or selected away.

Builder scope remains only `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, the directly affected
`Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift` contract and this plan. No shipping source,
AppInputRouter, verifier/pin, package/receipt/parser, product or LAN edit is authorized. Product and
all frozen hashes remain unchanged. After renewed Design Review, Security (plan), Builder work and
Security (code), begin one distinctly named cold-five set on the same exact release bytes; it must
stop at its first failure with no retry, replacement, discard or later-pass selection.

**Value-Identity Cold-Five v2 evidence verdict: FAIL.** Its display-equality failure is a verifier
false negative and not product evidence. **Product presentation diagnosis verdict: PASS.** The exact
app was stably fullscreen according to its authoritative AX state; the 33-point inset is legitimate,
and shipping intentionally supports a revealed windowed fallback. **Bound-presentation Remediation
Architecture verdict: PASS.** Binding one stable fullscreen-or-documented-fallback mode before any
user action preserves exact reactivation evidence without conflating text-entry correctness with
display-filling geometry or sampling away a transition. Build remains blocked pending renewed Design
Review and Security (plan), followed by every unchanged downstream gate.

## Design Review — Bound-presentation Verifier Remediation (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`52bfc9b3657d2e89e5b8efc80e5699092111c695873f4897630f4c5edfbcf2d0`. The revision follows macOS
presentation conventions by treating typed `AXFullScreen` state as authoritative, accepting AppKit's
system-managed inset, and recognizing only Pebble's deliberately revealed fallback. It preserves
adaptive interaction and no-masking evidence under these binding conditions:

1. Presentation binds before title navigation. The observation-only launch deadline is derived from
   Pebble's documented fullscreen-attempt/fallback schedule plus a fixed bounded margin; it may sample
   transition state but may not sleep, toggle, activate or extend itself. `windowedFallback` requires
   stable `AXFullScreen == false` and one layer-zero, on-screen, fully opaque matching owner window;
   false state while hidden, translucent, ambiguous or still transitioning never qualifies.
2. Exactly one containing active display, one focused AX window and one matching CoreGraphics owner
   window bind the mode, identities and finite rectangle. Fullscreen does not require display-bound
   equality, magic insets or tolerance. Windowed fallback must remain visible and usable within its
   bound display. After binding, mode/display/window/geometry are immutable through navigation and
   reactivation; no display reselection, mode upgrade or recovery wait is allowed.
3. F11 is issued once and is the sole mode transition. Its acknowledgement may observe the explicit
   old-mode -> transition -> opposite-mode lifecycle, but active/frontmost/focused-window continuity
   is fail-fast, a return to the old mode is terminal, and only the approved window/group/field
   retirement and renewal may occur. Field text, caret, focus and Accessibility context must survive
   both fullscreen-to-windowed and fallback-to-fullscreen directions.
4. Diagnostics may include the fixed presentation predicates and numeric geometry/backing scale
   needed to explain platform framing, but no names, paths, object descriptions, field values,
   free-form AX/event data or clipboard content. The v1/v2 failures remain immutable and the new
   same-hash cold-five set stops at its first failure without retry or selection.
5. Automated presentation/AX checks remain corroboration. Installed VoiceOver Design Sign-off must
   inspect discoverability, focus announcements, caret navigation, field switching and renewed AX
   context across the actual bound mode and its F11 opposite; no product UI, copy, manual-Paste/privacy
   or RPG Accessibility change is authorized.

**Bound-presentation Verifier Remediation Design Review verdict: PASS.** The typed mode contract
accepts legitimate AppKit framing and the documented usable fallback without weakening mode, display,
focus or AX identity proof. Platform-adaptive presentation, safe diagnostics and one-way no-retry
evidence remain binding through installed VoiceOver Sign-off.

## Security (plan) — Bound-presentation Verifier Remediation (2026-07-11)

Independently reviewed the diagnosis, remediation Architecture, Design PASS and current presentation
owners at plan SHA-256
`dc40614e2bbedaabfef6b4590bfd610e310747902908aeec6467008a1377dcbd` against mode spoofing,
window/display substitution, transient fallback acceptance, recovery masking, diagnostic privacy and
same-hash evidence integrity.

### Findings

None.

Typed, present Boolean `AXFullScreen` is the correct authoritative mode signal and removes the false
display-filling geometry assumption without accepting inferred fullscreen. Binding still requires the
exact non-substitutable app/process, one focused retained AX window with a finite stable rectangle, one
unique containing active Quartz display, and one unique layer-zero CoreGraphics owner window for the
bound PID whose exact rectangle equals the AX rectangle. No visible-frame comparison, magic inset,
epsilon, rounding or geometric mode inference is authorized.

`windowedFallback` is fail-closed: `AXFullScreen == false` alone is insufficient. The exact matching CG
owner must be on-screen and fully opaque across consecutive samples within the one bounded launch
deadline derived from the shipping attempt/fallback schedule. A hidden, translucent, off-screen,
ambiguous, unmatched or transitional window cannot bind. Fullscreen and fallback capture one immutable
typed mode, display ID, AX/CG window identity and exact rectangle before title navigation. Navigation,
field publication, reactivation settling, preservation, ready checks and every semantic sample retain
that binding; none can reselect a display/mode, wait through loss, or upgrade fallback.

The one F11 global-HID action is the sole presentation transition. Its acknowledgement may observe the
explicit old-mode/transitional/opposite-mode lifecycle, but app/frontmost/focused-window continuity is
fail-fast, return to the old mode is terminal, and stable opposite `AXFullScreen` plus the approved
renewed window/group/field binding is mandatory in both directions. No repost, second toggle, repair,
retry, discarded outcome or size-only success is allowed; field text/range/focus and Accessibility
context must survive.

Diagnostics are restricted to fixed presentation booleans and numeric rectangles/backing scale; app/
display names, paths, object descriptions, entered values, free-form AX/event data and clipboard bytes
remain forbidden. Deterministic inset-fullscreen, revealed-fallback, hidden/transitional, missing/wrong-
type AX, ambiguous owner/display, post-bind drift and bidirectional-F11 fixtures remain mandatory. The
immutable v1/v2 failures stay retained, and the distinctly named replacement cold-five set uses the
same exact release SHA-256
`a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998`, stops at its first failure,
and permits no retry/replacement/discard/selection.

Authorized edits remain the nonshipping driver and direct source-contract test only. Shipping source,
product bytes, verifier/pin, package/receipt/parser, AppInputRouter/Core/TextInput/AppSupport and LAN
surfaces remain frozen. Fresh Security (code), focused/full/security/smoke tests, undiscarded cold-five
evidence, installed VoiceOver Design Sign-off across the bound and opposite modes, pipeline,
receipt/deployed-bundle proof and deployment remain mandatory on unchanged bytes.

Review boundary: plan and read-only source inspection only. No driver, test, build, AppKit run,
installed state, deployment, commit or push was changed or performed by Security.

**Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** Authoritative AX mode plus exact CG owner/display binding admits the
real inset fullscreen and documented revealed fallback without tolerance, substitution or transition
masking. Privacy, one-way evidence, opposite-mode proof and same-hash scope remain fail-closed.

## Bound-Presentation Cold-Five v3 Reactivation-Handoff Diagnosis (2026-07-11)

Architecture reviewed the immutable `bound-presentation-cold-five-v3` stopped set, exact driver
ordering and current activation/presentation queries at plan SHA-256
`aff6f53562575b7742a03bd97fa138510da3123b9c624cfa2f1884afbc67a6f3`. Attempt 1 retained release
SHA-256 `a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998`,
signed executable SHA-256 `aab45756fd0643dd0c443a748298513e9c934d09d33b9ad6d2a93f7390f68133`
and CDHash `7546586f6c278efd2bb4fee016c5671ee025019f`. Cleanup passed and the set stopped
without retry at attempt 1.

The reactivation settling failure reported 24 samples and maximum exact consecutive count `1`.
Product identity/hash, AX fullscreen mode, display and geometry binding, GameView group, both fields,
focus and complete field state were true in every sample. Only `app.isActive`, frontmost value/PID/
bundle/executable, AX window list count/retained identity, matching on-screen CoreGraphics owner count/
identity and AX/CG rectangle equality fluctuated, and most ended false. The retained focused AX object
itself remained available. This pattern does not support text, router, presentation-mode, field-
identity or product reactivation-state corruption.

### Timestamped read-only handoff evidence

A separately labeled diagnostic reproduced Finder -> exact packaged fullscreen Pebble activation and
registered workspace activation/deactivation notifications before either action. It recorded only
monotonic timestamps, fixed app categories, PIDs and aggregate AX/CG booleans:

- Finder PID `1620` activated and Pebble PID `45029` deactivated at `t=1.593`.
- The single Pebble activation request produced a real Pebble-activate/Finder-deactivate pair at
  `t=1.669`/`1.670`.
- At `t=1.723`, Pebble was active and frontmost with one retained AX window, while the exact matching
  on-screen CG rectangle was temporarily absent.
- At `t=1.776`, sequential queries disagreed: `app.isActive` was false while the frontmost wrapper
  still categorized Pebble; AX window count and on-screen CG owner count were zero although the
  retained AX object still reported the bound fullscreen state.
- At `t=2.971`, Finder really activated again and Pebble emitted deactivation. This was a Finder
  bounce, not merely a stale property read.
- At `t=3.484`, Safari PID `1611` activated and Finder deactivated; Safari remained frontmost. This is
  confirmed third-application interference in the shared interactive console session.

The immutable v3 log lacks a workspace-notification/category ledger, so it cannot identify whether
its final non-Pebble state was Finder, Safari or another application. It must not be retroactively
labelled a product failure from the separate diagnostic. The diagnostic does establish all relevant
classes of behavior present in this environment.

### Root-cause classification

1. **Fullscreen Space animation/query non-atomicity: confirmed.** AX, `NSRunningApplication`,
   `NSWorkspace.frontmostApplication` and `CGWindowListCopyWindowInfo(.optionOnScreenOnly)` are
   independent sequential queries. During a Space handoff, the bound window can be absent from the
   on-screen CG list and the AX application window list even while its retained AX object, mode,
   geometry and content remain valid. One cross-framework sample is not an activation transaction.
2. **Finder bounce: confirmed in the diagnostic and strongly consistent with v3, but unclassified in
   v3 itself.** A successful `activate(options:)` request and even a Pebble activation notification do
   not guarantee activation will remain sticky. A later Finder activation is a real loss and may not
   be sampled away.
3. **Environment interference: confirmed.** Safari activation is external to the authorized Finder/
   Pebble sequence. Any such third-app activation invalidates the first attempt immediately.
4. **Product activation bug: not supported.** Pebble entered active state, retained its exact
   fullscreen presentation and field state, and its `applicationDidBecomeActive` path has no authority
   over which macOS Space remains frontmost. The failure is in the OS/automation handoff or shared
   environment, not a demonstrated shipping input defect.
5. **Driver overcoupling: confirmed.** The driver uses one sequential conjunction of activation truth
   and transitional AX/CG visibility as both handoff authority and surface readiness. The current
   recoverable settling counter cannot distinguish harmless query lag from an actual deactivation,
   Finder bounce or unrelated-app takeover.

### Minimal remediation architecture

1. In `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, register a bounded
   `WorkspaceActivationHandoffLedger` before the Finder action. It records only activate/deactivate,
   monotonic offset and category `pebble | expectedFinder | unexpectedOther`, with expected-PID/value-
   identity booleans. It never records application names, paths, arbitrary bundle identifiers or
   object descriptions, and its observers are removed during every cleanup path.
2. Split the reactivation gate into an event-authority phase and a surface-settlement phase. The
   event phase requires the one Finder action to produce Pebble-deactivate plus expected-Finder-
   activate, then the one Pebble action to produce expected-Finder-deactivate plus Pebble-activate.
   Notification order inside either system pair may vary, but no duplicate action or extra activation
   is allowed. Request acceptance alone is not success.
3. Any `unexpectedOther` activation is terminal environment interference, including during initial
   settling. After the Pebble-activate commit, any Pebble-deactivate or expected-Finder-activate is a
   terminal non-sticky reactivation/Finder-bounce failure. Neither may reset a counter and later pass.
   Preserve the event category and offset, clean up and stop the cold-five set.
4. Only after the Pebble activation event commits may the observation-only Space-surface barrier seek
   consecutive exact snapshots. While no terminal event is latched, transient `app.isActive`/
   frontmost property disagreement, AX window-list absence or on-screen CG-owner absence may reset
   this one pre-ready counter because those queries are non-atomic during the Space animation. Final
   success still requires the complete bound app/frontmost/AX/CG/presentation/group/field surface for
   the existing consecutive duration. No component is dropped from the final ready snapshot.
5. The event ledger is checked before and after each expensive AX/CG sample so a notification arriving
   mid-snapshot cannot be hidden by a stale exact conjunction. Preservation then enters the existing
   irreversible ReadyEpoch; every later event or predicate mismatch remains immediately fatal exactly
   as already approved.
6. Keep the current fixed handoff/surface deadlines unless empirical isolated evidence proves they are
   insufficient. Do not extend a timeout to turn v3 green, issue a second activation, replace the
   activation API, reactivate after a bounce, repost input, use PID-targeted delivery or retry a set.
   If the same Finder bounce recurs on a dedicated idle console with no `unexpectedOther` event, stop
   and return to Architecture before considering a different one-shot activation mechanism.

Diagnostics on failure include the bounded categorized event ledger, whether each expected event pair
committed, first terminal event category/offset, total surface samples, maximum consecutive exact
count and the existing fixed predicate aggregates. Actual Pebble/Finder PIDs may be reported as
numeric process evidence; unexpected applications remain `unexpectedOther` without name, bundle or
path. No field values, AX free-form data, CG window titles, event payloads or clipboard content are
authorized.

### Tests and Conditions for Builder

Add deterministic handoff-state tests for: both legal notification orders within each expected pair;
AX/CG/on-screen zeroes followed by a stable surface without any terminal notification; value-property
lag that recovers before ready; missing Pebble activation; Finder bounce after Pebble commit;
Pebble deactivation after one exact sample; unexpected-other activation before and after commit;
notification arriving during an expensive surface sample; duplicate/out-of-order events; cleanup
observer removal; and post-ready first-mismatch termination. Prove Finder bounce and unexpected-other
events cannot reset settling and later pass. Retain bound-presentation, value/process identity,
structured predicate, one-shot, privacy, field identity/resize and router-session coverage.

Run the real cold-five gate only in an unlocked, otherwise idle interactive console session. The
driver does not suppress user activity or other applications; it detects and fails interference.
An interference failure remains immutable and stops the set rather than authorizing automatic retry.
The immutable v1, v2 and v3 logs remain retained and may not be relabelled, omitted or selected away.

Builder scope remains only `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, the directly affected
`Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift` contract and this plan. No shipping source,
AppInputRouter, verifier/pin, package/receipt/parser, product or LAN change is authorized; release and
all frozen hashes remain exact. After renewed Design Review, Security (plan), Builder work and Security
(code), begin one distinctly named same-byte cold-five set and stop at its first failure without retry,
replacement, discard or later-pass selection.

**Bound-Presentation Cold-Five v3 evidence verdict: FAIL.** The stopped set proves neither product
reactivation success nor a product defect because it omitted activation-event ownership. **Handoff
diagnosis verdict: PASS.** Non-atomic fullscreen-Space queries, a real Finder bounce and shared-session
third-app interference are confirmed; shipping state remained intact. **Event-authority/Space-surface
Remediation Architecture verdict: PASS.** Separating authoritative activation events from recoverable
pre-ready surface publication prevents both false reds from query lag and false greens from focus
recovery, without adding an action or weakening the final surface. Build remains blocked pending
renewed Design Review and Security (plan), followed by every unchanged downstream gate.

## Design Review — v3 Activation-handoff Driver Remediation (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`f6bfd78e21d9aeeddd38df87aef2d7f761caf0767e0d8e6d9bba7338b38434f8`. Event authority cleanly
separates an expected macOS Space handoff from user-visible loss of frontmost/focus state and preserves
first-attempt proof under these binding conditions:

1. The bounded ledger is installed before Finder and records an immutable monotonic sequence. It
   accepts exactly one Finder-activate/Pebble-deactivate pair and one Pebble-activate/Finder-deactivate
   pair, allowing order only within each pair. Missing, duplicate, out-of-order, `unexpectedOther`,
   post-commit Pebble-deactivate or Finder-activate events are terminal and can never be cleared by a
   later exact surface.
2. AX/CG visibility and time-varying value properties may settle only after the Pebble activation
   event commits and only before `ready`. Every expensive sample binds the ledger sequence before and
   after the query and performs a final synchronized terminal check before counting that sample; an
   event arriving or being delivered during the query invalidates it. Observer delivery/removal must
   be race-safe and cleanup must prevent a stale callback from entering later evidence.
3. Recoverable pre-ready samples may reset only the surface-publication counter while the event ledger
   remains nonterminal. Final settling still requires consecutive complete bound presentation,
   active/frontmost, focused-window, group, retained-field and focus snapshots. Preservation then
   enters the existing one-way fail-fast ready epoch; no later event or predicate mismatch may be
   waited through, reactivated, reposted or retried.
4. Diagnostics may report fixed event categories, monotonic offsets, expected numeric PIDs, pair-
   commit booleans, sample counts and existing aggregate predicates. They must not report unexpected
   app identity, bundle/path/name, field values, free-form AX/CG content, input payloads or clipboard
   data. `unexpectedOther` truthfully means environment interference, not a product or Accessibility
   failure.
5. The cold-five run uses a dedicated unlocked idle console but does not suppress user activity. Any
   interference, Finder bounce or non-sticky activation remains immutable and stops the set. Automated
   AX evidence remains corroboration; installed VoiceOver must still prove focus announcements, caret
   navigation, field switching, reactivation context and presentation-mode retirement on unchanged
   product bytes.

**v3 Activation-handoff Driver Remediation Design Review verdict: PASS.** Exact lifecycle-event pairs
permit harmless pre-ready AX/CG publication lag without sampling away a real app switch or focus loss.
Safe diagnostics, one-way readiness, no retry and installed Accessibility proof remain binding.

## Security (plan) — v3 Activation-handoff Driver Remediation (2026-07-11)

Independently reviewed the diagnosis, event-authority remediation, Design PASS and current activation/
surface owners at plan SHA-256
`7553feb56ac18d965fac2e6e68dea4589acc08f81300ce2f6d4e3e741a0ba11c` against notification spoof/
ordering, observer lifetime, query race, interference masking, diagnostic privacy and evidence-
selection risks.

### Findings

None.

The bounded ledger is authoritative only after installation before the first Finder action and binds
events to the fixed Pebble and uniquely selected Finder value/PID identities. It accepts exactly one
Pebble-deactivate plus Finder-activate pair for the one Finder action, followed by exactly one Finder-
deactivate plus Pebble-activate pair for the one Pebble action; only order within each system-generated
pair may vary. Action boundaries, monotonic sequence/offset and one-shot ledger ownership prevent stale
pre-action events from satisfying a pair. Missing, duplicate, extra or out-of-order events, an
`unexpectedOther` activation, post-commit Pebble deactivation, Finder reactivation/bounce or any event
after ready is terminal and cannot be cleared by later surface recovery.

Observer lifetime is fail-closed: callbacks and state transitions must use one synchronized ledger/
generation; cleanup atomically closes that generation before removing every observer on success,
failure, timeout or termination; a queued/stale callback after close cannot mutate, commit or enter a
later attempt's evidence. Tests cover both legal pair orders, missing/duplicate/out-of-order and mid-
sample events, every terminal category, cleanup removal and stale-callback denial.

Activation authority and query settling are correctly separated. No AX/CG settling begins until the
Pebble activation pair commits. While and only while the ledger remains nonterminal before ready,
non-atomic `isActive`/frontmost/AX-window-list/on-screen-CG publication lag may reset the surface sample
counter. The ledger sequence and terminal state are checked before and after every expensive sample
and once more synchronously before counting it, so a notification delivered during the query cannot be
hidden by stale exact values. Final settlement still requires the complete bound presentation,
application/process, focused-window, group, retained-field and focus surface; preservation then enters
the existing irreversible ready epoch, where the first event or predicate mismatch fails immediately.

Diagnostics expose only bounded event categories, monotonic offsets, expected numeric PIDs, pair-
commit booleans, counts and fixed aggregate predicates. Unexpected applications remain the literal
category `unexpectedOther`; names, bundle IDs, paths, object descriptions, field/free-form AX/CG
values, input payloads and clipboard content are forbidden. The driver neither suppresses user
activity nor reclassifies interference as product failure: any third-app activation remains immutable
and stops the set.

No activation, event, input or attempt is reposted or retried. Query recovery is pre-ready surface
publication only, never event-authority recovery. Immutable v1/v2/v3 failed sets remain retained. The
new distinctly named cold-five set uses unchanged release SHA-256
`a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998`, stops at its first failure,
and permits no replacement, discard or later-pass selection. Authorized edits remain the nonshipping
driver and direct source-contract test only; shipping/product, verifier/pin, receipt/parser, package,
AppInputRouter/Core/TextInput/AppSupport and LAN surfaces remain frozen. Fresh Security (code), full
tests, installed VoiceOver Design Sign-off, pipeline/receipt/deployed-bundle proof and deployment remain
mandatory on the same bytes.

Review boundary: plan and read-only source inspection only. No driver, test, observer, AppKit run,
build, installed state, deployment, commit or push was changed or performed by Security.

**Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** Exact one-shot lifecycle pairs provide activation authority, race-
safe observer cleanup prevents cross-attempt contamination, and separately bounded pre-ready surface
settling tolerates query lag without masking a real bounce or interference. Privacy, no-retry evidence
and same-hash scope remain fail-closed.

## Handoff-Authority Cold-Five v4 Finder-Space Overlap Diagnosis (2026-07-11)

Architecture reviewed the immutable `handoff-authority-cold-five-v4` attempt, exact event offsets and
current action ordering at plan SHA-256
`c4802009b1b8163e5c6e5920937980fdb81abc89caffedba8789610d33bdc67c`. Attempt 1 retained release
SHA-256 `a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998`,
signed executable SHA-256 `aab45756fd0643dd0c443a748298513e9c934d09d33b9ad6d2a93f7390f68133`
and CDHash `7546586f6c278efd2bb4fee016c5671ee025019f`. Cleanup passed and no action or
attempt was retried.

The event ledger is exact:

- Finder activate at `+19 ms`; Pebble deactivate at `+20 ms`.
- Pebble activate and Finder deactivate at `+44 ms`.
- Finder activate and Pebble deactivate at `+1446 ms`, terminal after commit.

The driver calls `awaitFinderPair`, then immediately enters the one Pebble activation action. It has
no observation between Finder notification commit and the reverse request. Thus the reverse activation
was issued only about `24 ms` after the first system pair. The delayed Finder completion arrived about
`1426 ms` after the original Finder activation, consistent with the prior timestamped diagnostic's
fullscreen Space handoff and delayed Finder bounce. A workspace activation notification proves an app
status transition; it does not prove the corresponding Space/window transaction has settled.

### Root cause

The driver overlaps two opposing fullscreen Space transactions. It requests Finder, treats the first
activation/deactivation notification pair as complete presentation settlement, and requests Pebble
while Finder's Space migration is still in flight. When the earlier Finder transaction finishes, it
wins frontmost ownership and produces the correctly terminal postcommit pair at `+1446 ms`.

This is a driver sequencing defect, not evidence of product activation or text-entry failure. It is
also distinct from a retry or timeout problem: making the post-Pebble surface deadline longer would
only wait after the conflict was already created. The correct dependency is to finish the first
Space presentation transaction before issuing the sole reverse activation.

### Finder-surface settlement contract

After the exact Finder pair commits and before entering `beginPebbleAction`, add one bounded,
observation-only `FinderSurfaceSettlement`. It may reset consecutive samples only in this pre-Pebble-
action phase. Every sample requires the exact value/PID/bundle/executable-bound Finder to be active
and frontmost, Pebble to be inactive, the fixed handoff ledger to remain in `finderCommitted`, and no
unexpected or additional activation event before or after the sample.

Window proof is mode-specific and uses the already bound Pebble presentation rather than assuming
Finder owns an AX window:

- For bound `fullscreen`, query the exact Pebble `CGWindowID` without the on-screen-only filter and
  require that same PID/window/rectangle to be present but `kCGWindowIsOnscreen == false`. This proves
  the Finder Space has actually displaced the fullscreen surface. The retained Pebble AX window may
  remain queryable and must retain its mode/geometry identity, but AX application-window-list absence
  is diagnostic only because it is non-atomic across Spaces.
- For bound `windowedFallback`, no Space displacement is expected. Require the exact Pebble CG window
  to remain on-screen, opaque and rectangle-identical behind the exact active/frontmost Finder, while
  Pebble itself is inactive. Requiring it to disappear would falsely reject the documented shared-
  desktop mode.

Success requires the complete mode-specific Finder surface over the existing minimum consecutive
duration inside a fixed deadline. No elapsed-time threshold, sleep or magic `1.4 s` delay constitutes
success. A mismatch resets only this pre-action surface counter; a ledger event, identity mismatch,
unexpected application or timeout is terminal and prevents the Pebble action from entering its
one-shot ledger.

Only after Finder-surface settlement succeeds may the driver enter `beginPebbleAction` directly
inside the existing final check adjacent to the one `app.activate` request. The exact Pebble/Finder
event pair, postcommit terminal-event rule, Space-surface settlement, preservation and irreversible
ReadyEpoch then remain unchanged. A later Finder bounce still fails immediately and cannot trigger a
second activation or renewed Finder settling.

### Tests and Conditions for Builder

In `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, add the bounded mode-aware Finder-surface observation
and a bound-window query capable of reporting the exact CG window while it is off-screen. Do not
enumerate or report unrelated window titles/content. Extend
`Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift` to enforce this order:

`awaitFinderPair -> settleFinderSurface -> activateOnce("pebble.reactivate") -> awaitPebblePair`.

Add deterministic tests for: notification pair committed while fullscreen Pebble remains on-screen
must not allow the reverse action; exact off-screen bound fullscreen window across consecutive samples
does allow it; windowed fallback remains exact/on-screen behind Finder; Finder not active/frontmost;
Pebble still active; missing/substituted CG ID/PID/rectangle; an event during an expensive sample;
unexpected-other activation; a Finder-surface timeout; and a post-Pebble Finder bounce. Prove timeout
or terminal failure leaves `pebble.reactivate` unperformed, while a successful barrier permits it
exactly once. Retain both notification orders within each pair, observer cleanup/generation, bound-
presentation, ready-epoch, adjacent one-shot, aggregate privacy, field identity/resize and router-
session tests.

Diagnostics may report fixed booleans for Finder identity/active/frontmost, Pebble inactive,
bound-CG-window present/identity/rectangle/on-screen-expected, presentation mode, total samples,
maximum consecutive count and ledger event sequence/offset. They may not report unrelated app/window
names, bundle IDs, paths, titles, field values, free-form AX/CG content, event payloads or clipboard
data.

Builder scope remains only the AppKit driver, its directly affected `TextEntrySourceTests` contract
and this plan. No shipping source, activation behavior, AppInputRouter, verifier/pin, package/receipt/
parser, product or LAN edit is authorized; all product hashes remain exact. Do not change activation
API, add a second activation, sleep, retry, repost or extend a deadline to force green. If a delayed
Finder bounce recurs after this barrier on an idle console, preserve it and return to Architecture.
The immutable v1-v4 sets remain retained. After renewed Design Review, Security (plan), Builder work
and Security (code), start one distinctly named same-byte cold-five set and stop at its first failure
without replacement, discard or selection.

**Handoff-Authority Cold-Five v4 evidence verdict: FAIL.** The terminal bounce is real, but was caused
after the verifier overlapped opposing Space transactions and is not product evidence. **Finder-Space
overlap diagnosis verdict: PASS.** The `24 ms` reverse request preceded the first transaction's
approximately `1426 ms` delayed completion. **Finder-surface Remediation Architecture verdict: PASS.**
A mode-aware, observation-only settlement barrier restores dependency order without masking, sleeping
or adding an action. Build remains blocked pending renewed Design Review and Security (plan), followed
by every unchanged downstream gate.

## Design Review — v4 Finder-surface Settlement Remediation (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`fac3a9145298e8bdf09926b01e992888d584c40604a8c7dd5dcb3fb2f40252ea`. The added dependency
models macOS presentation correctly: Finder's activation events authorize the handoff, while the
mode-specific bound-window state proves that the first presentation transaction is actually ready
before the sole reverse activation. It preserves usability and no-masking evidence under these
binding conditions:

1. Ordering remains exact: Finder action -> exact event pair -> bounded Finder-surface settlement ->
   adjacent final check and sole Pebble activation -> exact reverse pair. Elapsed time, request
   acceptance, one sample or a fixed delay is never readiness. Timeout, identity mismatch, ledger
   event or interference prevents the Pebble action from entering its one-shot ledger.
2. In bound fullscreen mode, the exact previously bound Pebble PID/`CGWindowID`/rectangle must remain
   present in the unfiltered query and be off-screen over consecutive semantic samples; retained AX
   mode/geometry identity remains exact. In documented windowed fallback, that exact layer-zero
   window instead remains on-screen, fully opaque and rectangle-identical while exact Finder is
   active/frontmost and Pebble inactive. The verifier may not force one mode's Space convention onto
   the other, substitute a window, or inspect unrelated titles/content.
3. Only non-authoritative Space-publication mismatch may reset the counter during this one pre-Pebble
   phase. The handoff-ledger generation and terminal state are checked before, after and immediately
   before accepting each expensive CG/AX sample. Any event, app/process/window identity change or
   `unexpectedOther` is terminal rather than recoverable.
4. After settlement, the Pebble activation occurs exactly once with no run-loop gap after its final
   check. There is no return to Finder settlement. A later Finder activation, Pebble deactivation or
   other app activation is a terminal retained bounce/interference result; no reselect, recovery,
   API swap, sleep, repost, retry or deadline extension is permitted.
5. Diagnostics remain fixed aggregate booleans, presentation mode, counts and monotonic ledger
   sequence/offset only. No unrelated identities, names, bundle/path/title, field value, free-form
   AX/CG data, event payload or clipboard content may be emitted. The immutable v1-v4 sets remain.
6. This barrier performs no user or Accessibility action. The reverse handoff must still preserve the
   bound window/group/field identities, text, caret and focus into the fail-fast ready epoch, and final
   installed VoiceOver Sign-off remains mandatory on unchanged product bytes.

**v4 Finder-surface Settlement Remediation Design Review verdict: PASS.** The mode-aware semantic
barrier respects fullscreen Spaces and shared-desktop fallback behavior without turning time into
success or hiding a real bounce. Safe diagnostics, exactly-once interaction and installed
Accessibility proof remain binding.

## Security (plan) — v4 Finder-surface Settlement Remediation (2026-07-11)

Independently reviewed the v4 evidence, overlap diagnosis, Finder-surface Architecture, Design PASS
and current bound-window/event owners at plan SHA-256
`0cb56caee5c4c18037b2d4ca3a4eeb5e62d575fef61aa25a97d49959d408cc31` against window substitution,
Space-transition masking, event/query race, action reordering, diagnostic privacy and evidence-
selection risks.

### Findings

None.

The dependency order is exact and one-shot: Finder action, committed Finder notification pair,
bounded Finder-surface settlement, adjacent final check plus sole Pebble activation, then committed
reverse pair. Request acceptance, elapsed time, one sample or fixed delay is never presentation
readiness. Timeout, terminal ledger event, identity mismatch or interference prevents
`pebble.reactivate` from entering its action ledger; success permits it exactly once with no later
return to Finder settlement.

The mode-aware window proof preserves the already bound CG authority rather than selecting a new
surface. In fullscreen, the unfiltered CG query must contain the exact prior Pebble PID,
`CGWindowID`, layer, rectangle and owner identity while that same window is explicitly off-screen over
consecutive samples; the retained AX window/mode/geometry remains exact. In documented windowed
fallback, the same bound layer-zero window must instead remain on-screen, fully opaque and rectangle-
identical behind exact active/frontmost Finder while Pebble is inactive. Missing, substituted or
ambiguous PID/window ID/rectangle is an identity failure, not a candidate for reselection; unrelated
titles/content are neither queried as authority nor emitted.

Only non-authoritative on-screen/Space publication mismatch may reset this one pre-Pebble surface
counter. The synchronized handoff-ledger generation/terminal state is checked before and after every
expensive CG/AX sample and immediately before accepting it. An event delivered mid-query, unexpected
application, app/process/window identity change or postcommit bounce is terminal and cannot be sampled
away. After the reverse pair, the existing preservation and irreversible ReadyEpoch remain fail-fast;
no reactivation, second activation, API substitution, repost, retry, sleep-as-correctness or deadline
extension is authorized.

Deterministic tests cover on-screen fullscreen rejection, exact off-screen fullscreen acceptance,
on-screen opaque fallback, Finder/Pebble state, missing/substituted CG identity/rectangle, mid-sample
events, interference, timeout and later bounce, plus explicit proof that failures leave the reverse
action unperformed. Source ordering enforces
`awaitFinderPair -> settleFinderSurface -> activateOnce("pebble.reactivate") -> awaitPebblePair`.
Diagnostics remain fixed booleans, bound mode, sample/count data and monotonic ledger sequence/offset;
unrelated identities/names/bundles/paths/titles, field values, free-form AX/CG data, event payloads and
clipboard content are forbidden.

Immutable v1-v4 failed sets remain retained. The new distinctly named cold-five set uses unchanged
release SHA-256 `a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998`, stops at its
first failure and permits no retry, replacement, discard or later-pass selection. Authorized edits
remain the nonshipping driver and direct source-contract test only; shipping/product bytes,
AppInputRouter, verifier/pin, package/receipt/parser and LAN remain frozen. Fresh Security (code), full
tests, installed VoiceOver Design Sign-off, pipeline/receipt/deployed-bundle proof and deployment remain
mandatory on the same bytes.

Review boundary: plan and read-only source inspection only. No driver, test, AppKit run, build,
installed state, deployment, commit or push was changed or performed by Security.

**Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** Exact bound-window semantics finish the Finder Space transaction
before the sole reverse activation without substituting a surface, treating time as success, or
masking ledger interference. Action order, privacy, immutable failures and same-hash scope remain
fail-closed.

## Finder-Surface Cold-Five v5 Launch-Publication Diagnosis (2026-07-11)

Architecture reviewed the immutable `finder-surface-settlement-cold-five-v5` attempt, current launch
ordering and presentation binder at plan SHA-256
`bae0af6cae0b120ed96a7487ab53676b5cce4345e6a8856e870f647e6b5a52ff`. Attempt 1 retained release
SHA-256 `a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998`,
signed executable SHA-256 `aab45756fd0643dd0c443a748298513e9c934d09d33b9ad6d2a93f7390f68133`
and CDHash `7546586f6c278efd2bb4fee016c5671ee025019f`. Cleanup passed and the failure
occurred in `launch-settle` before any Finder action or handoff barrier.

The generic launch report contains 15 samples and ends with exact app identity, active and frontmost
all true, while AX window count/focus/finite rectangle/typed fullscreen, containing display and
matching on-screen CG owner are false. Unlike the structured reactivation report, the launch binder
reports only the final sample, not `ever_true`/`ever_false`, maximum candidate duration or reset
reason; the immutable log cannot establish that every earlier sample had the same values.

### Read-only launch lifecycle evidence

A separately labelled exact-package diagnostic reproduced the relevant publication lifecycle:

- `t=0.324`: Pebble active/frontmost; AX windows `0`; PID-owned layer-zero CG windows `0`.
- `t=0.479`: AX windows `1`, role `AXWindow`, subrole `AXUnknown`, `AXFullScreen=false`, rectangle
  `0,0,1728,1117`. The exact PID owned seven layer-zero CG windows in the unfiltered list and one
  on-screen full-display transition window.
- `t=1.465`: AX windows `1`, role `AXWindow`, subrole `AXStandardWindow`, `AXFullScreen=true`,
  rectangle `0,33,1728,1084`. The same PID owned the stable main and auxiliary on-screen CG windows.
- `t=1.802`: the AX application window list and focused-window publication became absent while Pebble
  remained active/frontmost and the same two PID-owned layer-zero CG windows remained on-screen.

A second run queried one cached and one newly created `AXUIElementCreateApplication(pid)` on every
sample. Both reported the same sequence: AX `0`; AX `1/fullscreen=false`; AX `1/fullscreen=true`; both
AX lists `0` at `t=4.578`; both AX lists restored to `1/fullscreen=true` at `t=5.853`, while CG windows
remained present and Pebble stayed active/frontmost. Recreating the AX application element therefore
does not repair or change the observation.

This excludes wrong process ownership, a stale cached AX application object and multiple competing AX
windows. The auxiliary CoreGraphics windows are normal launch/fullscreen surfaces; exact PID/layer/
rectangle filtering identifies the main candidate when AX publishes it. The product reaches a real
standard fullscreen window and its CG surface persists. The failure is launch AX publication churn,
not a demonstrated product window or fullscreen defect.

The shipping `windowDidEnterFullScreen` callback is process-local and cannot be observed directly by
the external driver without prohibited product instrumentation. Its source contract sets `fsEntered`
and reveals the window; the black-box transition from `AXUnknown/fullscreen=false` to
`AXStandardWindow/fullscreen=true` plus matching visible CG ownership is the appropriate external
semantic evidence.

### Root cause

The driver first waits for any nonempty AX window list, so it can return on the temporary
`AXUnknown` launch window. It then starts a separate binder that assumes repeated `kAXWindows` and
focused-window queries are a stable presentation clock. Fullscreen publication can retire or
temporarily withdraw that list while the actual PID-owned CG window remains visible. The binder has no
explicit lifecycle distinction between unpublished, transition-window, final candidate and bound
states, and its final-only diagnostic obscures which state occurred.

The binder also couples every candidate sample to expensive cross-framework enumeration and a full
executable rehash. In v5 only 15 samples fit inside the launch deadline, so a four-sample requirement
becomes a variable multi-second condition rather than the stated minimum semantic duration. Rehashing
17,368,120 immutable executable bytes measures about 0.04 seconds alone; it is not the primary AX
withdrawal, but it does not belong in every presentation sample and increases cadence coupling.

### Minimal remediation architecture

1. Replace the preliminary `wait(active && AX windows nonempty)` plus separate binder with one bounded
   `LaunchPresentationSettlement` beginning after exact launch/process identity. It owns explicit
   `unpublished -> transitioning -> candidate -> bound | failed` phases. AX window-list absence is
   expected only before binding and resets the candidate; it is never success.
2. A transition window—non-`AXWindow`, non-`AXStandardWindow`, missing/wrong-type fullscreen state,
   nonfinite geometry, ambiguous focus or unmatched CG owner—cannot become a candidate. A fullscreen
   candidate requires one focused `AXWindow/AXStandardWindow`, typed `AXFullScreen=true`, unique
   containing display and unique exact on-screen PID/layer/rectangle CG owner. Windowed fallback keeps
   its previously approved false-mode plus visible/opaque terminal requirements.
3. Separate fast AX publication observation from the full binding snapshot. First require the same
   eligible AX object/mode/rectangle across observations bracketing the approved minimum stable
   duration, with any AX window/focus/role/mode/geometry change resetting the candidate. Then take one
   full display/CG/app-identity snapshot and immediately revalidate the AX candidate before committing
   it. A failed full snapshot returns to `transitioning`; it does not select a new identity implicitly.
4. Keep the premeasured executable hash and bound file identity outside the hot polling loop. During
   settlement, use a cheap fail-closed file-identity tuple (device/inode/type/size/mtime) against the
   already hashed regular nonsymlink file; any change fails immediately. Rehash immediately after
   presentation binding and retain the existing end-of-gate race hash. A metadata or rehash mismatch
   is terminal, never a candidate reset.
5. Add a target-only AX observer or equivalent synchronized invalidation ledger for window-created,
   focused-window, moved/resized and destroyed/publication changes. Notifications may only invalidate
   a candidate and improve observability; they cannot establish success without the exact read-back
   snapshot. Observer generation and cleanup follow the existing handoff-ledger fail-closed pattern.
6. Once presentation binds, later AX application-window-list absence or identity/mode/geometry change
   remains immediately fatal before navigation and throughout the ready epoch. The remediation permits
   launch publication to settle; it does not mask a post-bind Accessibility outage.

Do not add product logging, infer the process-local delegate callback, recreate AX application objects
as a repair, lengthen the launch deadline to force green, sleep, toggle fullscreen, activate again,
retry a set or weaken exact final presentation/field identity.

### Diagnostics, tests and Conditions for Builder

Structured launch failure evidence must include total samples, maximum eligible-candidate consecutive
count/duration, last and `ever_true`/`ever_false` for app/active/frontmost, AX count, fixed role/subrole
eligibility, focused identity, typed mode, finite rectangle, containing display, CG owner match/
on-screen/opaque, file identity and final rehash, plus a bounded candidate-reset reason category.
Numeric rectangles/backing scale remain allowed; titles, app/display names, arbitrary bundles/paths,
object descriptions, field values, free-form AX/CG content, input and clipboard data remain forbidden.

Update `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift` with deterministic lifecycle cases: no AX
window before publication; `AXUnknown` transition rejection; standard fullscreen candidate; candidate
withdrawal to AX zero; restored same and replacement identities; cached/fresh application-element
equivalence; exact PID main-plus-auxiliary CG ownership; missing/ambiguous matching owner; observer
invalidation during a full snapshot; metadata mutation; post-bind rehash mismatch; deadline with full
structured diagnostics; and post-bind AX withdrawal terminality. Prove no navigation/click/key occurs
before `bound` and that launch settlement invokes neither Finder nor Pebble activation.

Retain typed presentation/fallback, Finder-surface ordering, handoff event authority, one-shot actions,
ReadyEpoch, privacy, field identity/resize and router-session coverage. Builder scope remains only
`Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, the directly affected `TextEntrySourceTests` contract and
this plan. No shipping source, AppInputRouter, activation behavior, verifier/pin, package/receipt/
parser, product or LAN edit is authorized; all product hashes remain exact. The immutable v1-v5 sets
remain retained. After renewed Design Review, Security (plan), Builder work and Security (code), start
one distinctly named same-byte cold-five set and stop at its first failure without retry, replacement,
discard or selection.

**Finder-Surface Cold-Five v5 evidence verdict: FAIL.** The launch failure is verifier evidence only,
not a product defect. **Launch-publication diagnosis verdict: PASS.** Temporary/withdrawn AX window
publication is confirmed while exact CG product surfaces persist; stale AX objects, wrong PID and
multiple AX windows are excluded. **Lifecycle-aware launch Remediation Architecture verdict: PASS.**
A unified semantic binder accepts only a stable final standard window, decouples fast publication
observation from expensive identity proof and preserves fail-fast post-bind Accessibility. Build
remains blocked pending renewed Design Review and Security (plan), followed by every unchanged
downstream gate.

## Design Review — v5 Lifecycle-aware Launch Binder Remediation (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`a6592639848609b4490a9ceb57112a7390b59ee63638d28cec2dba356e178841`. The one-way unpublished /
transition / candidate / bound lifecycle correctly rejects `AXUnknown`, requires a typed final
`AXWindow/AXStandardWindow`, decouples fast Accessibility publication from expensive proof, and makes
every post-bind AX gap terminal. One no-masking ambiguity remains blocking:

1. The statement that a failed full display/CG/**app-identity** snapshot returns to `transitioning`
   is too broad. Only target AX/CG publication churn, observer invalidation or a non-authoritative
   active/frontmost property disagreement may reset a pre-bind candidate, and only while exact launch
   process, PID, bundle, executable, file identity and prior hash authority remain unchanged. Any app/
   process identity, termination, bundle/executable, file tuple or rehash mismatch is terminal and
   cannot later recover into a bound presentation.
2. Install a bounded launch-lifecycle observer immediately after exact process identity and before
   settlement (or extend the existing synchronized activation ledger to cover this phase). Any
   `unexpectedOther` activation or Pebble deactivation after current Pebble activation is terminal
   environment/non-sticky-launch evidence. Without that event authority, an external app can briefly
   take frontmost focus, allow the candidate to reset, and later be sampled away before `bound`.
   Property lag without a corresponding terminal event may remain a recoverable pre-bind reset.
3. Record the reset-versus-terminal classification in fixed categories and test that a later exact AX/
   CG surface cannot pass after any terminal identity or lifecycle event. Observer generation must be
   closed race-safely during cleanup and before the later Finder/Pebble handoff ledger starts, so no
   callback crosses phases or attempts.
4. All other Design conditions are approved: candidate stability is measured semantically rather
   than by sleep; `AXUnknown` and wrong/missing typed mode never qualify; the full snapshot plus
   immediate AX revalidation and post-bind rehash are mandatory; windowed fallback remains strictly
   visible/opaque; diagnostics remain fixed aggregates with no identities/paths/values; after bind any
   AX identity/mode/geometry gap is terminal. Installed VoiceOver proof remains mandatory.

**v5 Lifecycle-aware Launch Binder Remediation Design Review verdict: FAIL.** The launch-state model
is sound, but Build remains blocked until full-snapshot failures are partitioned into recoverable
publication churn versus terminal authority loss and launch-time app switching is event-authoritative.
Those corrections are required to prevent pre-bind recovery from masking a real interaction loss.

## v5 Lifecycle-aware Launch Binder Architecture Revision (2026-07-11)

Architecture adopts the blocking Design corrections at plan SHA-256
`73eba9d0537c4a1313c4b7f14d375c6be873138e052d7770bc35820c2db3d892`. The unified launch
lifecycle, `AXUnknown` rejection, typed standard-window mode, fast publication stability, full
display/CG snapshot, post-bind rehash and fail-fast post-bind behavior remain unchanged. This revision
supersedes only the prior ambiguity about which pre-bind failures may recover.

### Immutable launch authority and explicit dispositions

Immediately after launch returns the exact application and before presentation settlement, create an
immutable `LaunchAuthority` containing the original `NSRunningApplication` value identity, PID,
canonical bundle and executable URLs, regular nonsymlink file device/inode/type/size/mtime, premeasured
SHA-256 and nonterminated state. Every fast observation and full snapshot produces exactly one typed
disposition:

- `candidate(surface)` only when all publication predicates are exact.
- `reset(LaunchResetReason)` only for pre-bind non-authoritative publication/property lag while all
  launch authority remains exact.
- `terminal(LaunchTerminalReason)` for any authority or lifecycle loss. Terminal is sticky and no
  later sample can return to `transitioning`, `candidate` or `bound`.

Allowed reset reasons are fixed and narrow: AX window list unpublished; transition/nonstandard AX
role or subrole; focused-window property lag; typed mode not yet published; AX position/size
publication change; target AX observer invalidation; containing-display or exact PID/layer/rectangle
CG publication absent/ambiguous; and `isActive`/frontmost property disagreement with no corresponding
terminal workspace event. These reasons clear only the current pre-bind candidate and stability clock.

Terminal reasons are fixed: process termination; original app value/PID/bundle/executable mismatch;
file device/inode/type/size/mtime mismatch; pre-bind or post-bind rehash mismatch; workspace lifecycle
unexpected-application activation; Pebble deactivation after the launch-active baseline; observer
generation/capacity corruption; or a terminal event delivered during a sample. A full snapshot may
reset for AX/CG publication churn, but it may never reset for any app/process/file/hash predicate.

### Launch lifecycle event authority

Install a bounded workspace lifecycle ledger after exact process/file/hash identity and an exact
active/frontmost Pebble baseline, before the first launch-settlement sample. During its launch phase,
any non-Pebble activation is categorized `unexpectedOther` and terminal; any Pebble deactivation is
terminal. It records only fixed category, activate/deactivate, monotonic offset and expected-identity
booleans. Names, arbitrary bundle identifiers and paths remain forbidden.

Prefer extending the existing synchronized handoff ledger into one `WorkspaceLifecycleLedger` that
spans launch, bound navigation and the later Finder/Pebble sequence. It installs observers once and
transitions atomically from `launch` to `finderAction` inside the adjacent final check before the one
Finder action. Thus there is no observer-removal/installation gap in which an application switch can
escape both phases. If separate ledgers are retained, the replacement generation must be installed
and armed before the launch generation closes, with one synchronized ownership transfer that permits
no callback to commit to both or neither.

Every fast observation and full snapshot binds the lifecycle sequence before querying, checks terminal
state and unchanged sequence after querying, then performs one final synchronized check before
counting or binding. A lifecycle event during the query is terminal, not a recoverable observer
invalidation. Target-only AX publication notifications remain reset-capable before bind; workspace
activation/deactivation events do not.

At `bound`, atomically commit the exact presentation only if authority, lifecycle generation and event
sequence remain unchanged and the mandatory rehash still equals the premeasured hash. After that
transition, AX/CG publication loss, property disagreement, target-observer invalidation and every
authority/lifecycle mismatch are all terminal. No post-bind reset category exists.

### Tests and Conditions for Builder

Make reset-versus-terminal classification machine-enforced with exhaustive switches and deterministic
fixtures. For each allowed reset category, prove an initial mismatch clears the candidate and a later
exact surface may bind only while process/file/hash and lifecycle authority remain exact. For every
terminal category—termination; PID, value, bundle, executable or file-tuple mismatch; pre/post hash
mismatch; unexpected activation; Pebble deactivation; mid-snapshot lifecycle event; generation/
capacity failure—feed a later exact AX/CG surface and prove binding remains impossible.

Also test active/frontmost property lag without an event as recoverable pre-bind; the same property
lag with a workspace event as terminal; target AX invalidation versus workspace lifecycle event;
observer event arriving between final snapshot and bind; queued callback after generation transfer;
atomic launch-to-Finder ownership transfer; cleanup close on every outcome; and that the expected
Finder event is accepted only after the one-shot Finder action boundary is armed. Retain all prior
transition-window, standard-mode, file-mutation, full-snapshot, post-bind outage, presentation,
Finder-surface, handoff, ReadyEpoch, privacy, field identity/resize and router-session tests.

Diagnostics add only fixed `reset_reason` or `terminal_reason`, sticky-terminal Boolean, lifecycle
phase/generation, bounded event category/offset and the prior safe aggregate predicates/counts. They
must not expose app names, arbitrary bundles/paths, window titles, object descriptions, field values,
free-form AX/CG data, input or clipboard content.

Builder scope remains only `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, the directly affected
`Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift` contract and this plan. No shipping source,
AppInputRouter, activation behavior, verifier/pin, package/receipt/parser, product or LAN edit is
authorized; all product hashes remain exact. The immutable v1-v5 sets remain retained. Do not extend
the deadline, add product logging, sleep, activate again, repost or retry. After renewed Design Review,
Security (plan), Builder work and Security (code), start one distinctly named same-byte cold-five set
and stop at its first failure without replacement, discard or selection.

**Prior Lifecycle-aware Launch Remediation Architecture verdict: SUPERSEDED.** Its full-snapshot reset
language could allow authority loss to recover. **Revised Lifecycle-aware Launch Remediation
Architecture verdict: PASS.** Only bounded target AX/CG publication and event-free property lag may
reset before bind; process/file/hash and workspace lifecycle authority are sticky-terminal, and the
observer phase transfer is race-closed. Build remains blocked pending renewed Design Review and
Security (plan), followed by every unchanged downstream gate.

## Design re-review — v5 Lifecycle-aware Launch Binder Revision (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`793935ca846be2bd9bd90e67d47f4434c624afbec1dd6378e6aaa9c8e9b4e56b`. The revision closes the
reset-versus-terminal classification blocker: AX/CG publication and event-free property lag alone may
reset before bind; process, app, file, hash, lifecycle and observer-authority failures are sticky
terminal; target AX invalidation cannot substitute for read-back; and every post-bind gap is fatal.

One lifecycle masking gap remains:

- **[BLOCKING]** The launch ledger is specified to install **after** an exact active/frontmost Pebble
  baseline. An app activation or Pebble deactivation can occur between that baseline read and observer
  installation, leave no lifecycle event in the new generation, then be treated as recoverable
  event-free property lag. Install and arm the ledger immediately after immutable process/file/hash
  identity and **before** establishing the active/frontmost launch baseline. Establish that baseline
