   `selfActivated` and `explicitActivation` are mutually exclusive, permanently retire the other
   action cardinality, and share the original fixed deadline. No second open/activate, force-focus
   option, in-process activation, sleep, deadline reset, repair or retry is permitted.
2. Explicit activation is authorized only by at least two event-free exact inactive samples on
   distinct common-mode turns spanning the approved minimum duration: completion/file/hash authority,
   `isFinishedLaunching`, regular activation policy, inactive target and the same exact live
   frontmost predecessor. The final synchronized check is adjacent to branch sealing and the one
   `app.activate(options: [])` call. A last-moment target-active observation selects self-activated
   and calls nothing.
3. Any exact target activity or first expected-pair member before explicit branch sealing permanently
   selects/disables the appropriate alternative; candidate samples never carry across branches. A
   partial or invalid event cannot authorize explicit activation or later fall back. Foreign,
   duplicate, wrong-predecessor, target-deactivation, ingress/capacity or authority failure remains
   sticky-terminal.
4. A false activation return is terminal. A true return is action acceptance only and still requires
   the exact target-activate/predecessor-deactivate pair, complete drainage and the unchanged
   consecutive finished/active/frontmost standard AX/CG presentation. Zero or partial callbacks,
   properties alone, or a later exact surface fail. Diagnostics must not claim causal attribution if
   the product's earlier request completes near the one action boundary.
5. Both branches retain the full snapshot, immediate AX revalidation, fresh rehash and atomic
   `boundNavigation` commit. Every post-bind loss remains fatal; later Finder/Pebble handoffs remain
   exact-pair-only and cannot consume the optional launch action slot.
6. Diagnostics remain fixed branch/action/result booleans, counts, durations, reasons and safe
   aggregates, with no identities, PIDs, names, bundles/paths, object descriptions or free-form AX/CG,
   field, input or clipboard content. Immutable v1-v8 evidence and the same-byte v9 first-failure stop
   remain mandatory.
7. Automated authority remains corroboration. Installed VoiceOver must still verify names/roles,
   focus announcements, caret navigation, field switching, reactivation context and presentation
   retirement; UI, manual-Paste/privacy and RPG Accessibility behavior remain frozen.

**v8 Conditional Activation Design Review verdict: PASS.** Stable finished-inactive proof permits one
standard activation without turning a failed implicit request into a retry loop, while self-activation,
partial events and every later handoff remain independently fail-closed. Platform UX, no-masking,
privacy and installed Accessibility conditions remain binding.

## Security (plan) — v8 Conditional Activation (2026-07-11)

Independently reviewed the immutable conditional-activation architecture and renewed Design Review
through plan SHA-256 `d30761f4f23eeae56cf2abb589ff7b056d81334acc338005a8f3c6cc775dc76d`.

### Findings

None.

Setting `OpenConfiguration.activates = false` cleanly separates completion-bound application launch
from focus authority without changing Pebble's shipping startup path. Static artifact authority,
pre-armed lifecycle collection, exact completion identity, process/file tuple capture and rehash
remain prerequisites rather than evidence reconstructed after the action.

The `selfActivated` and `explicitActivation` branches are mutually exclusive and permanently retire
the other action cardinality. The self-activated branch accepts only the already-approved zero-event
surface or exact launch-pair surface, calls no driver activation and cannot later consume the optional
action slot. First expected-pair activity selects that path; a partial pair cannot be repurposed as an
inactive baseline or fall through to explicit activation.

The explicit branch is authorized only after finished-launch authority and a stable exact zero-event
inactive baseline: multiple consecutive samples on distinct common-mode turns over a minimum duration,
the same live non-Pebble frontmost predecessor, no pending ingress, overflow or terminal condition, and
unchanged immutable application/process/file/hash authority. The final synchronized inactive snapshot
is adjacent to branch sealing and optional-slot claim. A target-active observation at that boundary
selects self-activation and issues no action; admitted callbacks are drained and accounted without
holding the synchronization lock across `activate(options: [])`.

The optional action is exactly one standard `app.activate(options: [])` call. A false return is
terminal; a true return is only action acceptance. The explicit branch still requires exactly one
target activation and one exact action-predecessor deactivation, in either order, plus complete ingress
drain. Zero, partial, duplicate, third, foreign, wrong-predecessor or otherwise invalid events are
sticky-terminal and cannot be repaired by later properties, callbacks or surface publication. No
causal overclaim, second activation, reopened launch, branch fallback, deadline extension or retry is
permitted.

Both branches retain the full immutable binding snapshot, immediate AX revalidation, fresh executable
rehash, atomic `boundNavigation` commit and fatal post-bind mismatch rules. The original deadline stays
fixed; later Finder/Pebble handoffs remain exact-pair-only and cannot reuse the launch action slot.
Diagnostics remain bounded aggregates and fixed booleans/reasons, excluding identities, PIDs, names,
bundles, paths, object descriptions and free-form AX/CG, input, field or clipboard content. The product
hash, verifier/pin, receipt/parser and shipping source remain unchanged, and same-byte v9 cold-five
evidence must stop on its first failure without replacement, discard, repair, retry or selection.

Review boundary: plan and test obligations only; no implementation or runtime evidence was approved at
this gate. Build remains blocked on implementing the reviewed contract and rerunning every downstream
gate against the actual diff and installed target.

**Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** Conditional activation preserves fail-closed lifecycle, focus,
artifact and presentation authority while adding only one completion-bound action under an exact stable
inactive proof. Branch exclusivity, terminal partial/invalid evidence, synchronized boundary accounting,
full snapshot/rehash/post-bind checks, privacy, fixed deadline and no-retry/same-byte requirements are
complete and binding.

## Immutable v9 Activation Eligibility and Reveal Architecture (2026-07-11)

Architecture diagnoses immutable v9 against input plan SHA-256
`6b4a0c6257725089164de0bfb7afb35a70f6ea90dcbdd77c7ca36e1dd225886f`, the macOS 27.0
`26A5378j` runtime and Xcode/SDK 27.0. This amendment supersedes the driver's conditional external
activation. It distinguishes window ordering, focus-transfer eligibility and the product's own launch-
readiness request; those are separate platform contracts.

### Immutable v9 and controlled probe evidence

V9 selected the exact explicit branch under the approved finished-launching, regular-policy,
zero-event inactive baseline. Its sole exact `NSRunningApplication.activate(options: [])` returned
false, with no activation/deactivation callbacks. Because the API rejected the request before any
postcondition, lifecycle or AX/CG settlement could not repair it and the stop-on-first-failure behavior
was correct.

Architecture then ran four predeclared, separately labeled, clean-launch single-call probes against
fresh packages built from exact pre-sign release SHA-256
`a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998` (packaged executable SHA-256
`aab45756fd0643dd0c443a748298513e9c934d09d33b9ad6d2a93f7390f68133`). Every target was exact,
finished launching, activation policy `regular`, nonhidden, inactive and not frontmost. No probe made
more than one activation call, no failed run was retried and all outcomes were retained:

| Probe | Exact call point | Options | Return | Final active/frontmost | Target callbacks |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `emptyTransition` | shipping CG alpha `0`, fullscreen setup | `[]` | `false` | `false` / `false` | `0` / `0` |
| `allWindowsTransition` | shipping CG alpha `0`, fullscreen setup | `.activateAllWindows` | `false` | `false` / `false` | `0` / `0` |
| `emptyAfterFallback` | shipping fallback CG alpha `1` | `[]` | `false` | `false` / `false` | `0` / `0` |
| `allWindowsAfterFallback` | shipping fallback CG alpha `1` | `.activateAllWindows` | `false` | `false` / `false` | `0` / `0` |

The AX reads themselves succeeded. During transition the exact target published one focused AX window
and one on-screen layer-zero CG owner, but the AX surface was not standard, typed or finite and CG
alpha was `0`. Without any second activation call, shipping fallback later made that same one CG owner
fully opaque (`alpha == 1`); the target still remained inactive. The post-fallback probes then returned
the same false result. Thus invisibility/fullscreen transition did not cause the API rejection, and
window reveal does not itself grant activation eligibility. One temporary inline-probe compile failure
occurred before any `openApplication` or activation call and is not runtime evidence or a consumed set.

### SDK contract and root cause

The SDK header and Apple's
[`activateAllWindows`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/appkit/nsapplication/activationoptions/activateallwindows)
documentation say only that all windows are brought forward instead of the default main/key windows.
The target already had one main/key window. This option neither changes activation policy nor grants
focus-transfer authority, and identical false returns before and after reveal confirm it is irrelevant.
It must not replace `[]` or be combined with another option as a speculative repair.

Apple's
[`cooperative activation`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/appkit/passing-control-from-one-app-to-another-with-cooperative-activation)
contract makes activation a request and says the active app first yields to the target; the system then
decides whether to grant focus. `NSWorkspace` handles that context when an active app opens another
app. The current integration driver is an unbundled command-line executable, not an active regular
AppKit application; the SDK explicitly gives unbundled executables without an Info.plist a prohibited
activation policy. A read-only caller probe likewise reported a nonregular, inactive caller. It cannot
yield the active user's application and cannot manufacture user intent. Passing the observed
predecessor to
[`activate(from:options:)`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/appkit/nsrunningapplication/activate%28from%3Aoptions%3A%29)
would not fix this: Apple requires that predecessor to call `yieldActivation(to:)` first, which an
external driver cannot do.

The driver-side conditional activation is therefore an invalid authority design on this platform,
not an option-bit bug. The four false returns do not prove that a Finder/Dock/user launch of Pebble
fails; they prove only that this inactive command-line caller cannot force a transfer. Likewise,
Accessibility raise, synthesized Cmd-Tab, AppleScript, deprecated ignore-other-apps flags or repeated
open/activate calls would manufacture focus or retry around platform policy and remain forbidden.

There is also a product compatibility defect in the explicit source intent. Pebble calls deprecated
`NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)` while its window is transparent and before fullscreen
readiness. On current AppKit the supported target-side API is `NSApp.activate()`, which is still only a
request and deliberately does not steal focus. The existing early forced call is neither a reliable
readiness signal nor aligned with the cooperative activation contract. This defect is independent of
the driver's inability to yield and requires a new product hash if corrected.

**`.activateAllWindows` remediation verdict: FAIL.** It changes post-activation window ordering only
and produced no runtime difference in either window state. **Driver external-activation verdict:
FAIL.** The inactive unbundled driver lacks cooperative focus authority. **Shipping activation timing/
API diagnosis: PASS.** The product's one launch request should use the modern target-side API only at
the first actual reveal, without force or retry.

### Shipping one-shot reveal request

In `Sources/Pebble/main.swift`, remove the early
`NSApp.activate(ignoringOtherApps: true)` call. Add one main-thread-only launch activation owner with
closed states `pendingReveal -> systemActivated | requestIssued -> complete`. It may issue at most one
`NSApp.activate()` request for the process lifetime and exposes no external trigger or product log.

At the first actual reveal point, after setting the exact window alpha to `1`:

- `windowDidEnterFullScreen` marks the fullscreen reveal; its existing fade invokes the one-shot
  method from the `NSAnimationContext` completion only after revalidating the same window and exact
  final alpha `1`, not immediately after scheduling the animator; and
- the `fsChecks >= 5` branch marks the supported opaque windowed fallback reveal.

Both sites invoke the same idempotent `requestLaunchActivationAtRevealIfNeeded`. If `NSApp.isActive`
is already true, transition to `systemActivated` and never request activation. Otherwise transition to
`requestIssued` before calling `NSApp.activate()` exactly once. The state transition must precede the
call so synchronous callbacks or reentrant window delegate paths cannot duplicate it. Repeated
fullscreen callbacks, fallback checks or a later mode transition cannot reopen `pendingReveal`.

This is a focus request, not an assertion. The product must remain a usable visible window if the
system denies it; do not hide it again, force focus, loop, poll and retry, synthesize input or change
the fullscreen fallback schedule. Product launch success does not depend on a callback pair. A real
user may click the visible fallback window if system context does not grant the request. Existing
application-active/resign handling remains unchanged.

Calling only at reveal avoids requesting focus for an invisible alpha-zero window and uses the exact
point where the product claims it is ready for interaction. It does not guarantee fullscreen entry:
normal Finder/Dock/active-launcher context may activate Pebble before fullscreen and therefore select
`systemActivated`; an initially denied background launch reaches the documented visible fallback and
makes its single modern request there. No activation request may be added to each fullscreen retry.

### Driver and proof architecture after the product hash changes

Delete the v8 external `app.activate(options:)` branch and its optional action slot. The nonshipping
driver must not claim it can create focus authority. Use `OpenConfiguration.activates = true` again so
an eligible active launcher can establish the system's normal cooperative context, retain the prearmed
observer and exact completion/file/hash binding, and accept only the v7 initial-launch current-surface
authority or exact emitted launch pair. A command-line cold run that remains inactive is a valid gate
failure, not permission to call activate.

Automated command-line proof alone cannot distinguish a denied focus transfer from a product launch
defect. Final Design Sign-off must include a fresh installed, user-initiated Finder/Dock launch on the
real app and record exact active/frontmost plus typed standard AX/CG surface after one of the two reveal
paths. This is the authority for normal launch UX. If fully automated focus transfer is required, build
a separately reviewed regular foreground helper app: while it is genuinely active, it must yield to
the exact target and then make one cooperative activation request. Merely packaging the current driver
without proving it active, or invoking `activate(from:)` without the helper's prior yield, is not
sufficient. The helper is outside this minimal product fix and requires its own Design/Security plan.

The same limitation applies to later Finder/Pebble handoff claims. Keep their exact pair requirements,
but do not execute them from an ineligible command-line source and interpret false activation as a
product failure. Installed/manual proof or a reviewed active yielding helper must own those deliberate
actions. No zero-event/property fallback weakens a deliberate handoff.

Because shipping bytes change, immutable v1-v9 sets remain historical failure evidence but cannot
authorize the new product. Rebuild release/package hashes, rerun Security (code), full tests, smoke,
pipeline, installed VoiceOver and deployment evidence on one new exact hash. Do not edit verifier pins
or receipts by hand; regenerate only through their reviewed workflows after tests pass.

### Tests, risks and Conditions for Builder

Shipping source tests must prove:

- the deprecated `activate(ignoringOtherApps:)` call is absent and `.activateAllWindows` is absent;
- `NSApp.activate()` has one syntactic owner and is reachable only through the idempotent reveal
  method;
- alpha becomes exactly `1` before the method is invoked in both fullscreen and fallback paths;
- already-active reveal performs zero request calls; inactive fullscreen reveal performs one; inactive
  fallback reveal performs one; both callbacks/repeated checks together still perform at most one;
- denial leaves the window visible and schedules no retry; and
- fullscreen retry cadence, fallback threshold, mode binding, input ownership and accessibility
  publication remain unchanged.

Driver/source-contract tests must remove `launch.activate`, `app.activate(options:)` and the v8
explicit branch, require `configuration.activates = true`, retain exact completion/file/hash and
launch-surface binding, and classify inactive timeout without an external action. Keep deterministic
fixtures showing `.activateAllWindows` cannot satisfy activation authority and `activate(from:)`
without prior exact yield is forbidden.

Runtime testing must preserve all four labeled v9 probe outcomes as diagnosis, not pass evidence. On
the new hash, test user-launched already-active/fullscreen reveal, inactive-to-fullscreen request,
inactive-to-windowed-fallback request, system denial with visible fallback, repeated/reentrant reveal,
termination during request and post-bind focus loss. Installed VoiceOver must re-prove names/roles,
focus announcement, caret navigation, field switching, reactivation context and presentation
retirement. Security must review focus-stealing, phishing/window-fronting and unintended background-
launch risks; the absence of force flags and the one-shot visible-only request are binding invariants.

Builder scope expands only to `Sources/Pebble/main.swift`,
`Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, the directly affected
`Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift` contract and this plan. No RPG, storage, LAN,
package/parser/receipt, verifier-pin or unrelated product edit is authorized. After renewed Design
Mock/Architecture review, Design Review and Security (plan), build one new release hash and run a
distinctly named v10 cold-five/install set that stops at its first failure without replacement,
discard, retry or selection.

**Conditional Activation Architecture verdict: SUPERSEDED.** Exact inactive proof cannot grant an
ineligible command-line caller cooperative focus authority. **Activation Eligibility and Reveal
Architecture verdict: PASS.** Window-order options and retries are rejected; the product makes one
modern request only when its exact window is visibly ready, while normal user/cooperative context—not
the verifier—remains the source of activation authority. Build is blocked pending renewed human-
visible Design Mock, Architecture adoption/re-review, Design Review and Security (plan) for the
shipping behavior and expanded hash-changing scope, followed by every downstream gate.

## Design Mock — Activation Eligibility and First Reveal (2026-07-11)

Reviewed input plan SHA-256
`347c051931278a578f80fa2379f4fbec8f1d8860c181121fadda93887f7c2700`. The human-visible contract
for the shipping activation change is:

1. A Finder/Dock/user launch never requests focus while Pebble is transparent, transitional or not
   ready for interaction. Fullscreen reveal is the completed fade with the same window at alpha `1`;
   fallback reveal is the documented opaque windowed state. No intermediate frame is presented as
   ready.
2. At first reveal, already-active Pebble makes zero activation requests. Otherwise it makes one
   modern cooperative `NSApp.activate()` request after closing the one-shot state first. System denial
   leaves the exact usable window visible and inactive so the user may click it; there is no force,
   flash/hide cycle, repeated prompt, retry, fullscreen-loop request or later F11 request.
3. Activation changes no text, caret, logical focus or retained AX identity. Before system activation,
   Pebble must not claim focused Accessibility state. After a granted user/cooperative launch,
   VoiceOver receives one standard-window/focus context and can navigate the same fields and controls;
   fallback remains visible, finite and usable at adaptive window/display sizes.
4. No new copy, dialog, permission request, telemetry, product logging or entered-text capture is
   introduced. Existing manual-Paste privacy and RPG Accessibility contracts remain unchanged.
5. Acceptance requires real installed Finder and Dock launches for already-active, fullscreen reveal
   and windowed fallback; a controlled denied request with visible click-to-activate recovery; repeated/
   reentrant reveal; and VoiceOver names/roles/announcement/caret/focus proof. Command-line automation
   may verify bytes and surfaces but cannot substitute for those user-authority cases.

**Activation Eligibility and First Reveal Design Mock verdict: PASS.** The contract removes hidden-
phase focus stealing, aligns activation with the first usable frame and keeps denial recoverable
without retry or false focus.

## Design Review — Activation Eligibility and Reveal Architecture (2026-07-11)

The current Architecture is substantively aligned with the Design Mock: it removes deprecated forced
activation, centralizes one idempotent modern request after exact reveal, preserves visible fallback
on denial, removes driver-created focus authority and requires installed Finder/Dock/VoiceOver proof.
However, the mandatory gate order is not yet complete because Architecture predates this renewed
human-visible Design Mock and has not adopted/re-reviewed its exact acceptance criteria.

**Activation Eligibility and Reveal Architecture Design Review verdict: FAIL.** Build remains blocked
until Architecture explicitly adopts the five Design Mock conditions above, including no AX focused-
state claim before activation, denial-visible click recovery, adaptive fallback and the exact installed
Finder/Dock/VoiceOver matrix. Designer must then re-review that revision before Security (plan) or
Build. No product, driver or test edit is authorized by this Design gate.

## Activation Eligibility and Reveal Architecture Revision (2026-07-11)

Architecture explicitly adopts all five binding Design Mock conditions at input plan SHA-256
`cf685768ac4115d9148b42be625199bf3a75926d50212d029e49e436ddaf82a5`. The prior SDK/runtime
diagnosis, rejection of `.activateAllWindows` and external command-line activation, one modern
target-side request, cooperative user authority, product-hash change and downstream verification
requirements remain unchanged. This revision closes the exact reveal, denied-focus, Accessibility,
privacy and installed-proof details identified by Design Review.

### 1. No hidden, transitional or false-ready request

`LaunchActivationAtRevealCoordinator` is main-thread-only and begins `pendingReveal`. It accepts one
typed reveal token containing the exact retained `NSWindow` identity, a monotonically increasing
launch-reveal generation and one fixed kind: `fullscreen` or `windowedFallback`. A token is valid only
for the original launch window; a replacement, stale generation or ambiguous window is terminal for
the request owner and cannot issue activation.

Fullscreen reveal is not `windowDidEnterFullScreen` entry or animation scheduling. It is the existing
fade completion after re-reading the same window, `fsEntered == true`, fullscreen mode and exact
`alphaValue == 1`. Windowed fallback reveal is the `fsChecks >= 5` branch only after the same window is
not fullscreen, is directly set to alpha `1`, has finite positive geometry and retains one owning
screen. The product does not enumerate CoreGraphics windows to decide whether to request focus; the
external verifier must prove the corresponding exact layer-zero on-screen CG surface and reject any
AXUnknown/nonstandard publication. The product token itself is limited to exact window identity,
reveal kind, alpha, mode and finite owning-screen geometry; it does not poll AX/CG. No alpha-zero frame,
fullscreen retry, missing geometry or intermediate animation can mint a reveal token or reach the
activation owner.

The initial window-order path must not create a false focus claim. If Pebble is not active while the
window is constructed, order the window without making it key; retain the intended GameView responder
as application state but do not publish it as active Accessibility focus. Only an actual
`applicationDidBecomeActive` transition may make the same visible window key and restore that same
responder. If Pebble is already active, existing key-window behavior is retained. This separates
visible readiness from system-granted focus and prevents the inactive v9 surface from reporting a
focused AX window merely because `makeKeyAndOrderFront` ran early.

### 2. Exactly one modern request, with denial remaining usable

The valid reveal token enters one adjacent idempotent transition:

- if `NSApp.isActive == true`, close as `systemActivated` and call no activation API; or
- if inactive, close as `requestIssued(kind, generation)` before calling `NSApp.activate()` exactly
  once.

The closed state is immutable. Fullscreen and fallback paths race through the same owner; only the
first exact reveal can resolve it. Reentrant delegate callbacks, repeated fade completions, remaining
fullscreen checks, later F11 transitions, application reactivation, screen changes and teardown may
observe the state but cannot reopen it or request again. There is no deprecated force flag,
`.activateAllWindows`, external activation, loop, timer, retry, repost or failure recovery.

The request is void and nonauthoritative. Grant is established only by the real
`applicationDidBecomeActive` transition plus active/key/frontmost read-back. Denial leaves the exact
fallback window alpha `1`, finite, on-screen, ordinary-level, mouse-accepting and not hidden; it must
remain nonkey and inactive until the user clicks it. No denial path may flash, reorder behind a new
product window, clear the screen, disable controls, recapture the mouse, synthesize a click or claim
success. A real click may activate the same window through AppKit's normal user authority, after which
the ordinary activation callback establishes key/responder state. Denial is a visible click-to-
activate recovery, not an automated pass.

### 3. Text, caret and retained Accessibility identity are invariant

The reveal coordinator receives no UIManager, screen, field, pasteboard or RPG value and performs no
text/input mutation. It may update only its typed one-shot state and call the platform activation
request. Window reveal, request, grant and denial preserve the current screen instance, text values,
UTF-16 caret/selection, logical focus owner, presentation generations and retained AX element object
identities. Do not invalidate or rebuild Accessibility trees merely because application-active state
changes.

Inactive Pebble must report no custom element as Accessibility-focused and must reject an AX focus
setter as nonauthoritative. Conjoin actual application-active state in both retained bridges:

- `TextEntryAccessibilityBridge.isFocused` and `focus`; and
- `RPGAccessibilityBridge.isFocused` and `focus`.

Suppress `.focusedUIElementChanged` publication while `NSApp.isActive == false`, including text focus
transactions and RPG semantic commits. Layout/value updates may retain the exact cached elements but
cannot imply focus. On actual `applicationDidBecomeActive`, use one activation-epoch owner to restore
the same GameView responder and publish at most one focused-context notification for the single exact
retained logical target, without reminting elements or changing text/caret. Existing later
reactivation behavior uses new activation epochs but never reopens the launch activation request.
`applicationDidResignActive` continues to clear input readiness while retaining the approved logical
focus context and must make every custom `isAccessibilityFocused` read false.

The inactive top-level window must likewise remain nonkey. Exact grant/read-back must precede any
claim that the standard window or one of its retained children is focused. Automated AX discovery may
observe a visible window during denial, but `focused_window_exact` and focused-child predicates must
remain false until application activation. A false focused state is a product/Test FAIL, never a
recoverable publication lag.

### 4. Adaptive, private and copy-free behavior

The activation change does not select a display, resize/recenter the window, add fixed geometry or
alter the existing fullscreen/fallback choice. A fallback reveal is acceptable only with finite
positive geometry inside one active display and one exact on-screen opaque CG owner at the current
adaptive size/backing scale. Test compact and large display/window fixtures; if the existing launch
geometry is not fully reachable/clickable, return to Design/Architecture rather than adding a hidden
activation workaround.

No dialog, banner, status copy, permission request, sound, telemetry, product log or analytics event is
added for request, grant or denial. The typed coordinator and diagnostics contain only fixed state/
kind/generation and safe Booleans; never app/predecessor identity, PID, name, path, title, text, caret,
selection, clipboard, AX value or free-form event data. Manual Paste's bounded, user-initiated
pasteboard read and RPG Accessibility semantics remain byte-for-byte behaviorally unchanged.

### 5. Binding verification matrix

Machine tests must cover coordinator state exhaustively: alpha below one, animation scheduled but not
completed, stale/replacement window, nonfinite/off-display fallback and transitional AX never request;
already-active exact reveal calls zero times; inactive exact fullscreen and fallback reveal each call
once; both/repeated/reentrant callbacks still total one; denial remains visible/nonkey/clickable; F11,
later activation and teardown call zero additional times. Source contracts require one syntactic
`NSApp.activate()` owner and forbid the deprecated call, `.activateAllWindows`, driver activation and
reveal-path duplicates.

Accessibility fixtures must retain element `===` identity, screen/presentation generation, value,
caret range and logical focus across request, denial, grant, resign and reactivation. Prove inactive
`isAccessibilityFocused == false`, inactive setters reject without mutation, focused notifications are
suppressed while inactive, denial reports no focused AX window/child, and one real activation epoch
publishes one exact retained focus context. Retain manual-Paste no-read-before-action/privacy tests and
the complete RPG names/roles/actions/focus invariants.

Automated byte/surface checks are necessary but not sufficient. On one unchanged newly built product
hash, the installed matrix must include:

1. Finder launch and Dock launch where Pebble is already system-activated: exact first reveal, zero
   product activation requests and standard active/key/frontmost AX/CG surface.
2. Finder and Dock fullscreen reveal requiring the one modern request, with granted focus and no
   hidden/transitional request.
3. Finder and Dock windowed fallback at adaptive compact and large geometries, with the exact opaque
   usable surface and at most one request.
4. A controlled denied request: exact fallback remains visible, inactive, nonkey and AX-unfocused; a
   real user click activates that same window without another product request.
5. Repeated/reentrant fullscreen/fallback callbacks, later F11 and reactivation: request count remains
   one or zero according to the first branch.
6. VoiceOver on granted and denied-then-clicked paths: exact window and control names/roles, one focus
   announcement per activation epoch, stable caret navigation, field switching, retained reactivation
   context and presentation retirement.

Record request count/kind, reveal predicates and focus results only as safe signed attestation fields.
A command-line launch cannot substitute for Finder/Dock user authority, and screenshot/AX presence
cannot substitute for VoiceOver or the denied real-click case. Any code change after the matrix
invalidates all installed evidence.

### Files and Conditions for Builder

Authorized scope is now explicit:

- `Sources/Pebble/main.swift`: exact window ordering, reveal token/coordinator, modern one-shot request
  and application-active key/responder handoff;
- `Sources/Pebble/TextEntryAccessibilityM.swift` and `Sources/Pebble/RPGAccessibilityM.swift`:
  application-active focus read/set guards and retained focus-context publication only;
- `Sources/Pebble/UIManagerM.swift` only if required to gate the existing text focused notification or
  route the retained activation-epoch notification without changing text semantics;
- `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift` and directly affected source/integration tests for
  no external activation, no false focus and exact installed evidence; and
- this plan.

No gameplay, field value/caret algorithm, RPG semantic model, pasteboard ingress, storage, LAN,
package/parser/receipt, verifier-pin or unrelated product edit is authorized. Builder must preserve the
five Design Mock conditions as invariants, update durable Accessibility/launch documentation if file
inspection shows the new contract is otherwise undocumented, and rerun Security (code), full tests,
smoke, pipeline, installed VoiceOver and deployment proof on the new exact hash.

**Prior Activation Eligibility and Reveal Architecture verdict: SUPERSEDED FOR GATE ORDER.** Its
activation diagnosis and one-shot reveal direction were sound, but it predated the binding human-
visible mock and did not completely close inactive AX focus or the installed denial matrix.
**Activation Eligibility and Reveal Architecture Revision verdict: PASS.** All five Design Mock
conditions are now adopted as exact state, focus, privacy, adaptive and real-target verification
contracts. Build remains blocked pending renewed Designer re-review and Security (plan), then every
unchanged downstream gate.

## Design re-review — Activation Eligibility and Reveal Revision (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`0b6b511fe509f23695b8b621417cea35caa0670af6c3961d348999141461c595`. The revision adopts all
five Design Mock conditions and closes the prior gate-order blocker:

1. The exact retained window can mint one typed reveal only after alpha `1` and final fullscreen fade
   completion or the documented finite, on-screen fallback. The coordinator closes before any modern
   `NSApp.activate()` call, already-active reveal calls zero times, and no retry check, reentrant
   callback, later F11 or reactivation can reopen it.
2. Denial preserves the same ordinary-level, visible, nonkey, clickable window without flashing,
   hiding, disabling, recapturing input or claiming success. A real click uses normal AppKit user
   authority and does not cause another product request. Fullscreen/fallback choice, geometry and
   adaptive compact/large behavior are otherwise unchanged.
3. Application-active state is now part of both text and RPG Accessibility focus read/set authority.
   Inactive elements retain identity/text/caret/logical context but report unfocused, reject focus
   setters and emit no focused notification. One actual activation epoch makes the same window key,
   restores the same responder and publishes at most one retained focus context; resign clears the
   public focus claim without reminting or mutating content.
4. No new copy, prompt, telemetry, product log, pasteboard behavior or RPG semantics is introduced.
   Request count/kind in signed evidence must state its provenance: machine-enforced coordinator
   cardinality on the exact product hash plus the observed installed branch. It must not imply direct
   runtime observation through an unreviewed log, accessibility field or debug surface.
5. Command-line surface checks remain subordinate to the exact installed Finder/Dock matrix: already-
   active, granted fullscreen, adaptive fallback, controlled denial plus real click, repeated/
   reentrant reveal and VoiceOver on granted and denied-then-clicked paths. Each state proves standard
   window/control names and roles, truthful focus, stable caret/field switching, retained reactivation
   context and presentation retirement. Any later code change invalidates the matrix.

**Activation Eligibility and Reveal Revision Design re-review verdict: PASS.** The architecture now
aligns cooperative focus with the first usable frame, keeps denial visibly recoverable, prevents
inactive AX focus claims and preserves text/caret/VoiceOver identity without force or retry. Renewed
Security (plan), final installed Design Sign-off and every downstream gate remain mandatory.

## Security (plan) — Activation Eligibility and Reveal Revision (2026-07-11)

Independently reviewed the final Architecture revision, binding Design Mock and Designer re-review
through plan SHA-256 `7ea99792a0406da548f024984884c2b18b0882aca337760590e0a4d2bab29c47`.
The proposed edit sites were checked against the current retained text/RPG bridges, focus-notification
paths, AppKit launch delegate and integration driver; this gate approves only the plan contract, not
the current implementation.

### Findings

None.

The reveal owner is a closed, main-thread-only state machine with one exact retained-window and
generation authority. Neither an alpha-zero/transitional frame, stale/replacement window, invalid
fallback geometry nor a fullscreen animation merely being scheduled can reach activation. At the
first valid reveal it closes before any platform call: an already-active process issues zero requests,
and an inactive process issues one modern `NSApp.activate()` request. Fullscreen/fallback races,
reentrant delegate callbacks, repeated completions/checks, later F11, reactivation, screen changes and
teardown cannot reopen the state. The deprecated force API, `.activateAllWindows`, driver-created
activation, timers, polling, retries, reposts and synthetic input remain forbidden.

The request is nonauthoritative: only the real active callback plus active/key/frontmost read-back can
grant focus. The binding denial rule applies to either valid reveal kind: the same revealed window must
remain visible, finite, on-screen, mouse-accepting, inactive and nonkey, without a flash/hide/reorder,
new fronting window, disabled controls, mouse recapture or success claim. The installed controlled-
denial case deliberately uses the reproducible windowed fallback and a real user click; deterministic
state tests must still prove that denial cannot mutate or reopen the owner for either reveal token. A
click activates through ordinary user authority and cannot cause another product request.

Inactive focus authority is fail-closed at every relevant layer. The initial inactive window is ordered
without becoming key; both retained text and RPG bridges conjoin actual application-active state in
focus reads and focus setters, rejected setters perform no mutation, and focused-element notifications
are suppressed while inactive. Resign makes every custom focused read false while preserving logical
context. One real activation epoch may make the same window key, restore the same responder and publish
at most one retained focused context. It may not remint Accessibility elements, modify content or
reopen launch activation. AX-visible window presence during denial is explicitly not focused-window or
focused-child authority.

The coordinator has no UI manager, screen, field, RPG or pasteboard value path and can mutate only its
typed one-shot state. Screen identity, retained element `===` identity, presentation generation, text,
UTF-16 caret/selection and logical focus are invariant across request, denial, grant, resign and
reactivation. Diagnostics and signed attestations are restricted to fixed state/kind/generation,
Booleans, counts and reveal/focus predicates; they exclude identity, PID, path, title, text, caret,
selection, clipboard, AX value and free-form event data. Manual Paste no-read-before-user-action and
RPG Accessibility contracts remain mandatory regression coverage.

The proof model correctly separates provenance: coordinator cardinality is established by machine-
enforced source/state tests on the exact bytes, while the installed branch and focus outcome are
observations, not an unreviewed product log or debug/AX field. Command-line and screenshot/AX presence
cannot substitute for fresh installed Finder/Dock launch authority, adaptive compact/large fallback,
controlled denial plus real click, reentrant reveal or VoiceOver on granted and denied-then-clicked
paths. Termination, post-bind focus loss, stale tokens, false focus, notification multiplicity and
content/identity invariants remain explicit negative tests.

Shipping changes require one new exact release/package/product hash. Immutable v1-v9 results remain
historical only; verifier pins and receipts cannot be edited by hand, and any code change invalidates
the complete installed matrix. Scope is limited to the named launch, retained Accessibility,
notification-routing, driver/test, plan and necessary durable-documentation surfaces. Gameplay, RPG
semantics, text/caret algorithms, pasteboard ingress, storage, LAN, package/parser/receipt and unrelated
product behavior remain outside authorization. Security (code), full tests, smoke, pipeline, installed
VoiceOver, Design Sign-off and deployment proof must all be rerun on those same final bytes.

**Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** One visible-reveal-only modern request is idempotent and bounded,
cannot steal or retry focus, and treats denial as safe user-recoverable state. Active-state focus/setter/
notification guards, content and privacy invariants, evidence provenance, real installed coverage and
new-hash invalidation rules close the relevant focus-fronting, false-Accessibility-authority and data-
exposure risks. Build may proceed only within the reviewed scope and remains subject to every downstream
gate against the actual diff and installed artifact.

## Activation Reveal Final Pebble Pin Amendment (2026-07-11)

Architecture reviewed the actual reveal/focus implementation, clean release artifacts and unchanged
release-surface verifier through input plan SHA-256
`dc06e539ce6f62358651321e22bf88c37fabe7ac689475701e8b663eef6a72d6`. The frozen-artifact stop is
resolved by authorizing exactly one verifier value change: release `Pebble` SHA-256 advances from
`a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998` to
`648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`.

No other verifier value, logic, path, product, surface, failure rule or source edit is authorized by
this amendment.

### Exact source, binary and product-graph causality

The shipping delta is bounded to the reviewed activation-reveal contract:

- `Sources/Pebble/main.swift` owns the exact-window coordinator, inactive nonkey ordering, alpha-one
  fullscreen/fallback reveal, the sole modern `NSApp.activate()` call, real active/key/frontmost
  read-back and one retained focus notification per activation epoch;
- `Sources/Pebble/TextEntryAccessibilityM.swift` and
  `Sources/Pebble/RPGAccessibilityM.swift` add active/key guards to retained focus reads and setters and
  expose only the retained current focus target;
- `Sources/Pebble/UIManagerM.swift` suppresses focused-element notification while inactive; and
- `Sources/PebbleAppSupport/PebbleAppSupport.swift` adds the pure typed reveal token, predicates and
  fail-closed first-consume state machine used by the shipping coordinator.

The corresponding nonshipping driver and tests remove external activation, enforce exact source
ownership/order/absence and exercise invalid, already-active, inactive and reentrant reveal states.
Inspection found no activation option, deprecated force API, retry owner, new product or reveal-driven
gameplay/storage/LAN mutation.

The package graph is unchanged and contains exactly two products: `Pebble` and `pebsmoke`.
`PebbleAppSupport` is a source target, not a standalone product. It is linked directly into `Pebble`
and its own test target; `pebsmoke` depends only on `PebbleCore`. Thus the separately measured
`PebbleAppSupport.o` digest is corroborating evidence rather than a verifier pin, while its reveal
kernel is legitimately shipping code inside the changed Pebble executable. The final Pebble symbol
table contains the expected `PebbleLaunchActivationState`, `PebbleLaunchRevealToken`, predicates and
decision symbols, and the release AppSupport object exposes the same owners. This matches the source
dependency graph rather than an unexplained binary delta.

The release `PebbleAppSupport.o` evidence digest changed from
`d543fc8af3427cd5fcfea8d6ed113e32212b28f7040dfbbc957db0ba21a4e1f5` to
`12d4b146ea24da2457066590f0b2c54e331fa098f8f82f54849856e838b07383` because it now owns that
pure reveal state. It remains absent from `scripts/verify-pebble-storage-release-surface.sh`; this
amendment must not add it as a product or pin.

The four independently frozen release artifacts remain exact:

- `pebsmoke`: `d989c030656377ea1dca57b563408e2f6ae739e391904bde8be25f4f1d6dde2f`
- `PebbleStorage.o`: `ed37590e383037968b25905cb7ecd1d29e8faa43ba1f62a4919baebf9aabc6ba`
- `PebbleCore.o`: `39e37366bcba82ba65131a1112ad0b262f562279e363cf621542a804bd3ae028`
- `PebbleTextInput.o`: `547f0c44a950be0b7287ab73b918b6a3f486502e7ba57d7897e2d751b4d040ac`

These exact unchanged hashes exclude smoke, persistence, engine and text-ingress drift and agree with
the package graph: none consumes the changed AppSupport or Pebble AppKit sources. Running the unchanged
release-surface verifier against the clean artifacts checked all source and preceding Storage/Core/
TextInput object contracts, then stopped with exit `1` at `reviewed Pebble product hash drift`. Its
smoke comparison is ordered after the Pebble comparison and was therefore not executed in that run;
the exact smoke digest above was measured independently and must be rechecked by the passing verifier
after the one pin edit.

### Preliminary build and test evidence

The clean release build completed warning-free in 107.38 seconds and produced the exact new hashes
above. The focused activation-reveal/AppSupport/text-entry selection passed 29/29 with zero failures;
the AppKit driver typecheck passed; the binary security scan passed; and `security-scan.sh` passed,
including the 127-production-file SQLite boundary scan. These results establish implementability and
initial scope integrity only. They do not replace Security (code), full regression, installed Design
Sign-off, VoiceOver, denial-click, packaging, deployment or source-control closure.

### Risks and exact closing tests

The remaining risks are:

1. **False hash attribution.** An unrelated shipping delta could be hidden behind a broad product pin.
   Close with the named source scope, exact product graph, new reveal symbols, all four frozen hashes,
   evidence-only AppSupport digest and a clean rebuild that reproduces the exact Pebble hash.
2. **Reveal-state regression.** Invalid or transitional first consumption intentionally closes the
   state fail-closed and could prevent a later request; a loose implementation could instead retry.
   Close with exhaustive token/generation/window/predicate tests, exact alpha-one fullscreen/fallback
   tests, already-active zero-call proof, inactive exactly-one proof, reentrant/F11/later zero-additional
   proof and source absence checks for every alternative API.
3. **False focus or inaccessible denial.** A visible inactive window could claim key/AX focus, or denial
   could leave it unclickable. Close with retained element identity/text/caret tests, active/key focus
   read/set guards, inactive notification suppression, real denied visible/nonkey/clickable fallback,
   real click recovery and one focus context per activation epoch.
4. **Evidence-only target expansion.** AppSupport could accidentally become a product/verifier surface.
   Close by asserting the two-product graph, AppSupport's only consumers and continued absence of an
   AppSupport expected hash from the release verifier.
5. **Post-pin artifact drift.** A rebuild or late edit could invalidate causality. Close by rebuilding
   clean after the pin edit, rehashing all six measured artifacts and refusing any mismatch or code
   change without renewed review.

After the exact one-line pin edit, Builder must run, in order:

1. a clean warning-free release build and exact hashes for `Pebble`, `pebsmoke`, Storage, Core,
   TextInput and evidence-only AppSupport;
2. the otherwise byte-for-byte unchanged release-surface verifier, which must pass rather than merely
   reach a later failure, followed by the release binary security scan;
3. the focused 29-test suite, AppKit driver typecheck and full `security-scan.sh` with the 127-file
   boundary result;
4. full XCTest and release `pebsmoke`, with each test runner confirmed to have executed real
   tests/checks rather than a no-op;
5. fresh Security (code) against the actual diff;
6. the complete installed Finder/Dock, already-active, fullscreen, adaptive fallback, controlled
   denial plus real click, reentrant/F11/reactivation and VoiceOver matrix on the exact final bytes;
7. final Designer sign-off, pipeline/receipt, installed/deployed-bundle hash verification and the
   authorized deployment; and
8. source-control review, logical commit, push and exact local/remote synchronization proof.

Any failure stops the sequence. There is no regold, retry, replacement, discarded run, alternate hash
selection or manual receipt/pin adjustment beyond the one authorized value.

### Conditions for Pin Builder

Builder may edit exactly one existing assignment in
`scripts/verify-pebble-storage-release-surface.sh`:

`EXPECTED_PEBBLE_PRODUCT_SHA256='a25cabbf1da2bc4dacceb6bd2b02dead4dfa82f38dcf7ace9f956d7592aee998'`

becomes:

`EXPECTED_PEBBLE_PRODUCT_SHA256='648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8'`.

Do not edit any other expected hash, verifier command, scanner, manifest, source, test, package graph,
receipt/parser, hook, product, AppSupport surface, storage, RPG or LAN file under this amendment. Stage
that verifier file specifically; never use a blanket stage. If any artifact after the edit differs
from the six exact digests above, stop and return to Architecture/Security rather than advancing a
second pin.

**Activation Reveal Final Pebble Pin Architecture verdict: PASS.** The new product digest is causally
bounded to the approved shipping reveal/focus implementation, the AppSupport movement is explained by
its direct static dependency and remains separately evidence-only, and all other frozen artifacts are
exact. Exactly one Pebble verifier-pin value may advance. Architecture performed no source, test,
package, receipt or verifier-pin edit; every downstream code, runtime, installed, deployment and
source-control gate remains mandatory.

## Design Review — Activation Reveal Final Pebble Pin Amendment (2026-07-11)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`05ec91f490f409cd751c92952d90607bc07fe7e7d10f1e66259cca698a4f0201`. The exact product-pin
advance is bookkeeping for the adopted first-reveal and inactive-focus implementation; it introduces
no independent design authority. Approval is binding on these conditions:

1. The shipping delta remains exactly the reviewed reveal coordinator, inactive nonkey window
   ordering, active/key/frontmost read-back, retained text/RPG focus guards and inactive notification
   suppression. The AppSupport state machine is linked shipping code inside `Pebble`, but remains
   neither a new product nor a separately pinned verifier surface.
2. A request remains impossible before the same retained window reaches exact alpha `1` at completed
   fullscreen reveal or usable adaptive fallback. Already-active reveal performs zero calls; inactive
   reveal performs at most one modern cooperative call after closing state. Reentrant callbacks,
   F11, reactivation and denial cannot reopen it. Denial leaves the same visible, finite, on-screen,
   ordinary-level, nonkey, mouse-accepting window available for a real click without another request.
3. Inactive text and RPG elements retain object identity, values, UTF-16 caret/selection and logical
   context while reporting unfocused, rejecting focus setters and emitting no focused notification.
   One genuine activation epoch restores the same responder and publishes at most one retained focus
   context; resign removes public focus without reminting or content mutation.
4. The pin, focused 29/29 and preliminary scans do not satisfy the Design Mock. The exact final bytes
   must complete the installed Finder and Dock already-active/granted-fullscreen/adaptive-fallback,
   controlled denial plus real click, repeated/reentrant/F11/reactivation and granted/denied-then-
   clicked VoiceOver matrix. Request count/kind evidence must disclose its machine-cardinality plus
   observed-branch provenance and may not rely on an unreviewed product log or AX/debug field.
5. Only the named Pebble hash may advance. Any other source, behavior, product graph, verifier logic,
   frozen artifact, AppSupport surface, receipt, storage, RPG-semantic or LAN delta invalidates this
   Design approval and all installed evidence.

**Activation Reveal Final Pebble Pin Amendment Design Review verdict: PASS.** Exact one-pin
bookkeeping preserves the adopted reveal, denial-recovery, text/caret and VoiceOver/focus contracts;
the complete real installed matrix and final Designer Sign-off remain mandatory before completion.

## Security (plan) — Activation Reveal Final Pebble Pin Amendment (2026-07-11)

Independently reviewed the pin Architecture, Design PASS, current clean-release artifacts, resolved
SwiftPM graph, release-surface verifier and symbol evidence through plan SHA-256
`820baeab7e23901c6ba6b75984c08e55be34f2c732cbbee5525763de3068d6f4`. This is authorization of
the exact pin plan only; it is not Security (code), runtime, installed or deployment approval.

### Findings

None.

The exact currently measured release artifacts corroborate the amendment: `Pebble` is
`648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`; `pebsmoke`,
`PebbleStorage.o`, `PebbleCore.o` and `PebbleTextInput.o` retain the four stated frozen hashes; and
`PebbleAppSupport.o` is exactly
`12d4b146ea24da2457066590f0b2c54e331fa098f8f82f54849856e838b07383`. The current verifier still
contains the old Pebble value, contains no AppSupport expected hash and fails with exit `1` exactly at
`reviewed Pebble product hash drift`. It therefore has not silently accepted the new binary, skipped
the product comparison or consumed the unreviewed value early.

Hash causality is bounded by independent surfaces rather than asserted from the new product digest
alone. The reviewed shipping source scope owns launch reveal, active/key focus authority and the pure
reveal state; the final Pebble and AppSupport symbol tables contain the expected token/state/decision
owners. The unchanged smoke, Storage, Core and TextInput hashes exclude drift in their respective
product, persistence, engine and text-ingress surfaces. A clean rebuild after the pin must reproduce
all six digests; any source, package, toolchain-output or artifact mismatch invalidates this approval
and returns to Architecture/Security instead of authorizing another pin.

The resolved SwiftPM graph has exactly two executable products, `Pebble` and `pebsmoke`.
`PebbleAppSupport` is not a product or verifier surface: it has product membership only in `Pebble`, is
consumed directly by the Pebble target and separately by its test target, while `pebsmoke` remains
Core-only. Its object digest is evidence-only even though its code legitimately ships inside the
Pebble executable. The pin amendment may not add an AppSupport product, expected hash, verifier path,
receipt field or other externally trusted surface.

The mutation authority is singular: only the existing `EXPECTED_PEBBLE_PRODUCT_SHA256` assignment may
advance from the stated old digest to the stated new digest. No other expected value, comparison order,
failure rule, scanner, manifest, source/test file, package graph, receipt/parser, hook or product may
change under this amendment. The verifier file must be staged specifically, and its amendment-local
diff must prove the one assignment is the sole edit. A passing post-pin verifier must execute every
ordered comparison, including the independently measured smoke hash that the current intentional
Pebble failure has not yet reached.

Preliminary build, 29-test, typecheck and scan results remain implementability evidence only. After
the pin edit, the plan correctly requires a clean warning-free rebuild and six-hash proof; unchanged
verifier PASS and binary scan; focused tests, typecheck and full security scan; real full XCTest and
release smoke; fresh Security (code); the complete installed Finder/Dock, activation, adaptive
fallback, denial-click, reentrant/reactivation and VoiceOver matrix; final Design Sign-off;
pipeline/receipt; deployed-bundle hash verification and authorized deployment; then source-control
review, logical commit, push and exact local/remote synchronization. Failure stops the sequence with no
regold, retry, replacement, discarded run, alternate hash selection or second manual pin/receipt edit.

**Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** The new Pebble pin is causally constrained by exact source/symbol,
product-graph and frozen-artifact evidence; AppSupport remains a shipping dependency whose separate
digest is corroboration only; and exactly one existing verifier value may change. Full security,
regression, runtime, installed Accessibility, deployment and source-control gates remain mandatory on
one unchanged final artifact set before the hash can authorize release completion.

## Architecture — V10 Cooperative Activation-Denial Automation Recovery (2026-07-12)

This amendment is based on the immutable V10 black-box run and plan input SHA-256
`e3037e4b71a05f42ac26ddf42dc228d97cb115a54fe4a6e9140bad388cffd3ab`. All pre-runtime gates were
green and the exact reviewed product hash was revealed. Launch completed, the target process and
executable identity remained exact, `isFinishedLaunching` was true, and exactly one finite on-screen
Pebble-owned layer-zero CG window became visible. Pebble nevertheless remained inactive and was not
frontmost until timeout, while the prearmed workspace ledger observed zero activation/deactivation
events. Accessibility exposed one focused-window object, but its role, subrole, mode and finite frame
never became a typed eligible standard-window surface.

That combination is denial, not launch failure and not proof of focus. Cooperative AppKit activation
can leave a successfully revealed, mouse-eligible window inactive when another process retains user
authority on the shared console. An `AXFocusedWindow` object owned by an inactive application may
describe that application's intended internal focus, but it cannot establish global public focus and
must not be promoted to success while the application is inactive. The observed incomplete AX
surface is treated as transitional until a real key-window epoch exists. V10 therefore confirms the
Design Mock's visible-click denial state while exposing a verifier liveness gap: the driver can prove
the state, but currently has no bounded user-like action with which to continue the automated matrix.

The product remains immutable at SHA-256
`648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`. This amendment adds no
shipping activation request, retry, AX setter, focus claim or alternate product behavior. It
authorizes a nonshipping black-box-driver action only. Apple documents that
[`NSWindow.frameRect(forContentRect:styleMask:)`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/appkit/nswindow/framerect%28forcontentrect%3Astylemask%3A%29)
derives a frame rectangle from an exact style mask, and that a
[`CGEvent` mouse event](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/coregraphics/cgevent/init%28mouseeventsource%3Amousetype%3Amousecursorposition%3Amousebutton%3A%29)
uses a cursor position in global coordinates. Those APIs support deriving a standard titlebar band
rather than hard-coding chrome dimensions, then issuing one ordinary global left-button down/up pair
at a proven non-content point.

### Closed branch contract

Introduce a dedicated conditional launch-denial action state in the AppKit integration driver. It is
not another entry in the unconditional action list and must not add a generic action-retirement API.
Its closed states are exactly `unresolved`, `skippedSystemGranted` and `performedDeniedClick`; each run
must make one terminal transition and may make it only once.

1. **System-granted branch.** If Pebble becomes exact active/frontmost and publishes the exact typed
   standard AX surface before the denial action's final adjacent check, transition to
   `skippedSystemGranted`. Emit no mouse event. Continue with the existing settlement and navigation
   sequence without relaxing any lifecycle, CG, AX or text invariant.
2. **Proven-denial branch.** Only after the full eligibility predicate below remains exact across two
   distinct common-mode run-loop turns and the approved fallback interval may the action arm. Repeat
   the complete predicate immediately adjacent to posting. If it still holds, precreate both mouse
   events, arm the action/lifecycle boundary, transition exactly once, and post one down followed by
   one up at the same coordinate. There is no sleep, drag, double-click, second action, activation API
   or recovery retry.
3. **Terminal ambiguity.** Any other state is a failure without input. This includes timeout before a
   stable denial, mixed grant/denial evidence, a stale identity, an event already in flight, an unsafe
   coordinate, or a failed event allocation. A later valid AX surface cannot repair an invalid or
   partially observed action epoch.

The branch decision must be explicit in bounded diagnostics as
`launch_activation_path=systemGranted|syntheticDenialClick|terminal`. Diagnostics may disclose
booleans, bounded counts, transition names and safe geometry predicates. They must not disclose text,
paths, process identifiers, predecessor names, raw accessibility values or derived content hashes.

### Denial eligibility and exact ownership

All of these conditions are conjunctive at both stable samples and at the adjacent final check:

- launch completion, audit token, process start, executable identity, product hash, code identity and
  `isFinishedLaunching` remain exact;
- the launch workspace ingress was prearmed and completely drained, its sequence remains unchanged,
  and it has observed zero target activation/deactivation events;
- Pebble is inactive and is not the workspace frontmost application;
- the exact bound predecessor is still running, still frontmost and has not changed identity;
- exactly one Pebble-PID-owned, layer-zero, on-screen, alpha-exactly-one CG window exists; its window
  ID and finite rectangle are stable and equal the bound reveal identity;
- the rectangle has positive usable dimensions and is strictly contained by one active display. A
  display-filling, cross-display, off-screen, nonfinite or otherwise ambiguous rectangle is
  ineligible. Fullscreen denial is deliberately ineligible because it has no proven safe titlebar;
- no standard public AX surface is eligible: Pebble remains inactive, no product AX child claims
  `kAXFocused == true`, and no role `AXWindow` plus subrole `AXStandardWindow` plus typed mode plus
  finite-frame tuple has matured. A raw focused-window object may be recorded only as
  `inactive_intended_focus_present=true`; it is neither a public-focus claim nor success;
- the private bounded pre-action state snapshot is exact and unambiguous, including retained screen
  identity and every discoverable Pebble text field's object identity, value, UTF-16 selection/caret
  and editability. Values and hashes remain private; only counts and equality booleans may escape;
- the exact reviewed source and product prove the ordinary standard titled-window style
  `[.titled, .closable, .miniaturizable, .resizable]`; and
- Input Monitoring/CG-event preflight was granted before the test began. The driver must never open a
  permission prompt or reinterpret denied permission as product denial.

If Pebble becomes active/frontmost or the exact standard surface appears at the adjacent final check,
the driver selects `skippedSystemGranted` and does not click. If an unpaired, foreign, duplicate,
third-party or wrong-predecessor workspace event appears at any point, the run is terminal. Exact
properties may corroborate a valid event pair, but may never replace it.

### Proven non-destructive titlebar point

The click point is derived, never guessed. In a pure helper, use AppKit's standard frame/content
conversion with the exact reviewed style mask to derive the current titlebar/chrome thickness. Require
that thickness to be finite, positive and bounded by the bound window height. Derive the titlebar band
from the upper frame edge in CG global event coordinates, then choose the horizontal midpoint and the
vertical midpoint of that band. Validate the conversion explicitly rather than assuming identical
AppKit and CG origins, including displays with nonzero and negative origins.

The point is eligible only if all of these predicates are true at the final check:

- it is finite and strictly inside the exact bound CG rectangle, the single containing display and
  the derived titlebar band;
- it is outside the content rectangle and outside conservative left and right chrome exclusion zones,
  including all traffic-light controls, titlebar accessories and resize edges;
- the window is wide and tall enough for those exclusion zones not to overlap;
- converting between the tested AppKit and CG global coordinate spaces round-trips within the existing
  exact geometry tolerance; and
- both left-mouse events were successfully created before either can be posted.

Post exactly one `.leftMouseDown` followed by exactly one `.leftMouseUp` to the CG HID event tap, at
the same point, with left button, click count one and zero drag displacement. Never use the content
center, a field, button, close/minimize/zoom control, AX press/raise/focus action, AppleScript,
`activate`, keyboard event, Dock/Finder automation or a repeated click. If the down event is posted
but the precreated up event cannot be posted, the run is terminal and unsafe for reuse; it still may
not retry.

### Post-action proof and continuation

The click does not earn success by itself. From the prearmed action boundary, require exactly the
Pebble-activate and bound-predecessor-deactivate workspace events, once each, in either order. Zero,
partial, duplicate, foreign, third or wrong-identity events fail sticky even if later property reads
look correct. After the exact pair, require all of the following across the existing consecutive
settlement samples and again in the final snapshot:

- Pebble is exact active and workspace-frontmost;
- the same CG window ID, owner, layer, rectangle, display containment, on-screen state and alpha-one
  reveal identity remain exact;
- exactly one AX mapping describes that same window with role `AXWindow`, subrole
  `AXStandardWindow`, a present typed `AXFullScreen == false`, a finite equal frame and the exact
  focused-window identity;
- the retained screen/presentation/accessibility identity remains exact where published;
- every pre-action text value, object identity, UTF-16 caret/selection and editability tuple remains
  byte-for-byte/field-for-field equal. The title screen is expected to have zero editable text fields;
  a nonzero or ambiguous count is not silently accepted; and
- the product's one-shot reveal coordinator remains closed and its activation request cardinality does
  not increase in response to the click, key-window callback, reentrant callback, F11 or later
  reactivation.

Only after this proof may the existing driver continue to title navigation, text entry and the later
Finder/Pebble lifecycle epochs. No launch settlement rule is weakened. The click branch adds one
bounded prerequisite action and one exact event-pair epoch; it does not change the expected evidence
for any subsequent action.

### Evidence classification and remaining human gate

The synthetic CG pair is **automation-only evidence**. It proves that, with test-runner Input
Monitoring already granted, the immutable installed app can recover from the exact observed inactive
visible denial when it receives one user-like titlebar click. It is not product activation behavior,
not evidence that a person discovered the recovery, not a human click, not a Finder/Dock launch, not
VoiceOver evidence and not an authorization to ship global input generation in Pebble.

Accordingly, the Design Mock remains open until the same final installed bytes complete the manual
Finder and Dock matrix, controlled denial followed by one real human click on the visible window, and
the granted plus denied-then-clicked VoiceOver checks. The synthetic run may precede and diagnose that
manual evidence, but it may never populate, waive or substitute any human receipt field. Installed
Designer Sign-off must distinguish the two evidence classes by provenance.

### File and dependency order

Scope is restricted to nonshipping verifier surfaces:

1. `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift` — add the closed conditional action, pure titlebar
   coordinate derivation, double-sampled denial predicate, one down/up post and exact post-action
   lifecycle/CG/AX/state proof.
2. `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift` — enforce the closed branch cardinality, no
   generic retire path, no forbidden activation/AX/AppleScript/key APIs, no retry and immutable product
   boundaries.
3. Existing AppSupport tests may test a pure helper only if the helper already belongs to the
   nonshipping evidence boundary; no shipping target may acquire CoreGraphics event-generation
   authority.
4. `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md` — retain this architecture, downstream verdicts and exact evidence.

Do not edit Pebble shipping source, the activation coordinator, package product membership, product
hash pin, release verifier, receipt format or installed-signoff content under this amendment. If the
implementation cannot remain wholly nonshipping, return to Architecture and Security rather than
moving the authority into the app.

### Risk-to-test map

1. **Accidental input on grant.** Force activation immediately before every branch boundary and final
   check; require `skippedSystemGranted`, zero CG events and unchanged unconditional action counts.
2. **Misclick/clickjacking.** Cover zero/two CG windows, wrong PID/layer/ID/rectangle, alpha below one,
   off-screen/nonfinite/cross-display/display-filling/fullscreen geometry, predecessor drift, target
   activity and late workspace events. Every case must emit zero input and fail sticky.
3. **Coordinate error.** Seed fixtures for the standard style, scaled displays and positive, negative
   and nonzero display origins. Prove the point is inside window/display/titlebar, outside
   content/traffic-light/accessory/resize exclusions and round-trips across coordinate spaces.
4. **Repeated or malformed action.** Instrument posting to prove one left down and one left up, same
   coordinate, click count one, zero drag and no second call under reentrancy, F11, callback or timeout.
   Event-allocation failure before posting emits zero; any partial post is terminal and cannot retry.
5. **False lifecycle success.** Test both exact event orders as PASS. Zero, one, duplicate, foreign,
   third, wrong predecessor and stale-sequence events are FAIL and cannot be repaired by exact later
   properties.
6. **False surface success.** Require same CG identity and exact `AXWindow`/`AXStandardWindow`, typed
   nonfullscreen mode, finite equal frame and focused identity. Missing/wrong/duplicate mappings fail.
7. **Content mutation.** Compare private pre/post retained identities, values and UTF-16 ranges; title
   screen zero-field cardinality and subsequent initial empty-field state remain exact. No content
   value or hash may enter diagnostics.
8. **Authority escape.** Source scans forbid product CG event generation, activation APIs, AX setters,
   AX raise/press, AppleScript, keyboard switching, `func retire(` and retry loops. The product digest
   remains exactly `648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`.
9. **Automation mistaken for sign-off.** Receipt and sign-off tests reject synthetic provenance for
   Finder, Dock, real-click or VoiceOver human fields. Complete the installed manual matrix on the
   same bytes after automated proof.

Focused tests, driver typecheck, source scans and an immutable-product hash check precede the full
XCTest, security scan, release build, `pebsmoke`, pipeline and installed proof. Any failure or code
change invalidates later evidence and returns to the earliest affected gate. There is no regold,
replacement run, action retry or second product pin.

### Conditions for Builder

Builder may implement only after a distinct Design Review confirms that a non-content titlebar click
preserves the visible denial contract and a distinct Security (plan) review passes the new global-input
authority. Builder must preserve the exact product hash and keep all event creation/posting in the
AppKit integration executable. The conditional action must be a closed, typed state rather than an
unconditional action or generic retirement mechanism. Both events must exist before posting; every
eligibility predicate must be repeated immediately adjacent to the post; any ambiguity must fail
without input; and no state may authorize a second attempt. Initial tests must accompany the code in
the same pass. Security (code), actual installed Design Sign-off, full Test and Deploy remain
downstream and cannot be self-approved by Builder.

**V10 Cooperative Activation-Denial Automation Recovery Architecture verdict: PASS.** The immutable
product correctly exposes a visible click-recoverable denial state, while the verifier lacks one
bounded user-like continuation. A single nonshipping, evidence-gated CG HID titlebar down/up pair is
architecturally acceptable only on the proven windowed denial branch, with exact lifecycle, CG, AX
and text invariants afterward and no retry. System grant performs no click. This automation evidence
does not satisfy or replace the mandatory installed real-human-click, Finder/Dock or VoiceOver gates.

## Design Review — V10 Cooperative Denial-click Verifier (2026-07-12)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`10b33647fe8b2a45b18c6bafdb23afff31bfffe133c148d0c90e20ac65ef21c2`. A single synthetic
titlebar click is acceptable as nonshipping diagnostic evidence because the branch is closed around
an exact visible denial and every ambiguous state emits no input. Approval is binding on these
conditions:

1. `skippedSystemGranted` and `performedDeniedClick` are mutually exclusive terminal outcomes. Exact
   active/frontmost plus the standard AX surface always selects grant with zero CG events. Timeout,
   mixed evidence, fullscreen/display-filling geometry, stale identity, lifecycle race, unsafe point,
   allocation failure, permission failure or any ambiguity is terminal with zero input; no later
   surface can retire or repair the conditional action.
2. Denial must be stable across the two distinct semantic samples and the adjacent final check: exact
   product/process/file/code and workspace sequence, inactive target, unchanged exact frontmost
   predecessor, one alpha-one layer-zero windowed-fallback CG identity/rectangle/display, truthful
   unfocused AX state and an exact private pre-action screen/text/caret/editability snapshot. A raw
   inactive focused-window object is diagnostic only. A matured standard-but-inactive AX surface is
   not grant authority and, under this plan, remains terminal/no-click rather than being guessed safe.
3. The titlebar point is derived from the reviewed standard style and current window/display geometry
   in explicitly converted AppKit/CG coordinate spaces. It must round-trip, remain strictly inside the
   titlebar/window/display, outside content, traffic lights, accessories, title/drag exclusions and
   resize edges, and have nonoverlapping conservative exclusion zones. No hard-coded chrome height,
   content/control point, AX action or guessed coordinate is permitted.
4. Both click-count-one, zero-drag left events exist before either post. The final eligibility check
   is adjacent to exactly one HID down/up at the same coordinate. A partial post is terminal and can
   never retry. References to “force activation” in deterministic risk fixtures mean injected test-
   seam observations only; runtime verifier code may not invoke any activation API or synthesize a
   grant.
5. The click proves nothing until the exact target/predecessor event pair drains and the same CG
   window becomes one standard, typed nonfullscreen focused AX window with exact active/frontmost
   authority. Screen/presentation/AX identities, every private text value, UTF-16 caret/selection and
   editability tuple remain unchanged, and the product reveal-request cardinality cannot increase.
6. Diagnostics remain fixed branch/transition booleans, counts and safe geometry predicates; they
   exclude identities, PIDs, names/paths, raw AX/CG values, text/hashes, input and clipboard content.
   CG event generation stays entirely outside shipping targets and the exact product hash remains
   frozen.
7. Synthetic provenance cannot populate or waive Finder, Dock, real-click or VoiceOver receipt fields.
   Final installed Design Sign-off still requires one human click on the same visible denial plus the
   full Finder/Dock and granted/denied-then-clicked VoiceOver matrix on identical bytes.

**V10 Cooperative Denial-click Verifier Design Review verdict: PASS.** The exact titlebar-only branch
is non-destructive, fail-closed on grant or ambiguity and proves post-click continuity without
masquerading as human usability or Accessibility evidence. Security (plan), implementation review and
all manual installed gates remain mandatory.

## Security (plan) — V10 Cooperative Denial-click Verifier (2026-07-12)
