Independently reviewed the denial-click Architecture, binding Design PASS, current driver input
boundary, resolved product graph and immutable product artifact through plan SHA-256
`ab947efd37df2922214512fc811f3ea2c22b0bc82a99f1ed037b6d50a8780586`. This gate approves only
the nonshipping verifier plan; it does not approve implementation, automated causality, human
usability, installed Accessibility or deployment.

### Findings

None.

The binding branch predicates are fail-closed. `skippedSystemGranted` requires the complete exact
grant tuple—active target, workspace-frontmost target and one typed standard AX surface—and emits zero
input. Active/frontmost without that surface, or a standard-but-inactive surface, is mixed evidence and
therefore terminal/no-click. `performedDeniedClick` requires two distinct common-mode samples plus the
adjacent final check to retain exact launch/process/file/code/hash authority, unchanged zero-event
workspace sequence, inactive/nonfrontmost target, the same exact live frontmost predecessor, one stable
opaque layer-zero windowed-fallback CG identity and an exact private state snapshot. Fullscreen,
display-filling, cross-display, off-screen, nonfinite, duplicate/missing windows, stale identity,
partial AX maturity, a focused product child or any lifecycle/sequence ambiguity emits no input.

The titlebar coordinate is derived from the reviewed standard style and current bound geometry rather
than guessed. Explicit AppKit-to-CG conversion and reverse validation cover the opposing coordinate
conventions and displays with positive, negative or nonzero origins. Eligibility requires finite
positive chrome, strict containment in the one window/display/titlebar band, exclusion from content,
traffic lights, accessories, title/drag regions and resize edges, nonoverlapping conservative margins
and tolerance-bounded round-trip equality. The plan correctly refuses fullscreen or any geometry for
which a non-content point cannot be proven.

Global-input authority is both narrow and one-shot. `CGPreflightPostEventAccess()` must already pass at
test preflight and remain true at the final predicate; the verifier may never call the prompting
request API or reinterpret permission failure as product denial. Both click-count-one, zero-drag left
events are allocated before either post, the lifecycle/action boundary is prearmed, and state closes
before exactly one HID down followed immediately by exactly one HID up at the same coordinate. There
is no content/control click, drag, double-click, keyboard event, activation/AX/AppleScript action,
generic retirement, retry or second attempt. Allocation or pre-post ambiguity emits zero input; a
partial post or a lifecycle change admitted after the irreversible boundary is sticky-terminal and
cannot be repaired or retried.

The last synchronized predicate is the strongest available guard against a cooperative-grant race,
but it cannot establish exclusive causation across the OS boundary. Accordingly, post-click evidence
may report only that exact activation and continuity followed the bounded input epoch; it must not
claim that the synthetic click was the sole possible cause. Any extra, partial, foreign, duplicate,
wrong-predecessor or stale-sequence event remains terminal even if later properties mature. This
provenance limit is consistent with the plan's automation-only classification and prevents a racing
late system grant from being promoted into human or product evidence.

The action earns continuation only after the exact target-activate/predecessor-deactivate pair drains
and consecutive plus final snapshots prove exact active/frontmost authority, the same CG window and
display/alpha/rectangle, one equal typed nonfullscreen focused AX mapping, retained screen/
presentation/Accessibility identity and unchanged private text object/value/UTF-16 caret-selection/
editability tuples. Zero or ambiguous title-screen field cardinality is handled explicitly, and the
product reveal coordinator must remain closed with no increased request count. No property-only or
late-surface fallback weakens the lifecycle proof.

Privacy and scope remain bounded. Raw text, content hashes, object/process identities, paths,
predecessor names and raw AX/CG values stay in private ephemeral comparison state; diagnostics expose
only fixed branches/transitions, bounded counts, Booleans and safe geometry predicates. Synthetic
provenance cannot populate Finder, Dock, human-click or VoiceOver receipt fields. Event creation and
posting remain confined to the standalone AppKit integration driver, which is not a package product;
current shipping sources contain no CG event creation/posting authority, the two-product graph is
unchanged and the release `Pebble` remains exactly
`648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`. Any shipping, package,
pin, verifier, receipt or installed-signoff format change invalidates this review.

Focused state/coordinate/cardinality/race tests, driver typecheck, source scans and immutable hash
proof must precede full XCTest, security scan, warning-free release build, `pebsmoke`, pipeline and
installed proof. Security (code) must inspect the actual global-input implementation. The same final
bytes still require the manual Finder/Dock matrix, one real human denial click, granted and denied-
then-clicked VoiceOver, installed Designer Sign-off and Deploy. No automated result may replace those
human-authority gates, and any failure or code change returns to the earliest affected phase without
regold, replacement, discarded run, action retry or another product pin.

**Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** Exact grant and stable denial branches, converted non-content
geometry, precreated one-shot HID ownership, permission/race fail-closure, full lifecycle/CG/AX/text
postconditions, private diagnostics, explicit synthetic provenance and frozen nonshipping/hash scope
bound the new global-input risk without granting Pebble any event-generation or focus authority.

## Architecture — V11 Denial-Predicate Attribution and Sleeping-Display Remediation (2026-07-12)

This amendment diagnoses the immutable V11 `conditional_launch_timeout` against plan input SHA-256
`4dfa1d6ea38ebc1e3e2c6d6ed5b21a999d3118d6a871fe23d7534180a55da059`. The product remains the
exact reviewed release executable at SHA-256
`648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`. Architecture made no
source, test, pin, package, receipt or product edit.

### Exact-package diagnostic evidence

A separate read-only observer packaged that exact executable through `scripts/package-app.sh`,
launched it through `NSWorkspace.OpenConfiguration` with `activates = true` and the same private home
shape as the integration gate, posted no input, and sampled the complete denial predicate for 8.5
seconds. It produced this fixed evidence:

- package identity, single launch completion and `isFinishedLaunching` were true;
- Pebble was inactive/nonfrontmost, the exact predecessor remained frontmost, and target-activation
  and predecessor-deactivation event counts were both zero;
- AX trust, initial CG input preflight and final CG input preflight were true;
- AX window count was one, focused nonwindow child count was zero, typed standard inactive-surface
  count was zero, and the bounded private snapshot was capturable with exactly zero text fields;
- the PID-owned, on-screen, layer-zero CG window count was exactly one and its identity/geometry were
  stable across the final ten samples. The sole tuple was rectangle `(173, 95, 1440, 838)`, alpha
  `1.000`, area `1,206,720`, on-screen true;
- `CGGetActiveDisplayList` returned success with active display count zero. A separate bounded query
  also returned success/count zero, excluding a failed zero-capacity count probe;
- online display count and `NSScreen.screens.count` were both one. The main display was online and
  primary but inactive and asleep. The sole window had exactly one online containing display;
- using that online display only for diagnosis, the exact AppKit/CG coordinate conversion produced a
  finite positive standard chrome height and a geometrically valid titlebar point. It remained
  deliberately ineligible for HID posting because the containing display was asleep; and
- exact cleanup terminated the diagnostic app.

Apple defines
[`CGGetActiveDisplayList`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/coregraphics/cggetactivedisplaylist%28_%3A_%3A_%3A%29)
as the list of displays active for drawing. Apple separately documents that
[`CGDisplayIsAsleep`](https://developer.apple.com/documentation/coregraphics/cgdisplayisasleep%28_%3A%29)
means a display is sleeping and not drawable even though it remains part of global desktop space.
The observed success/count-zero result is therefore a truthful environmental state, not a Core
Graphics enumeration error and not evidence that online display geometry is safe for a global click.

### Root cause and rejected hypothesis

The current implementation at `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift:2400-2417` admits a CG
candidate only when `visibleCGOwnerWindows(pid:)` returns exactly one window, then derives the
containing display exclusively from `activeDisplayIDs()`. V11 had the one exact window, but zero active
displays, so `displayID` and `titlebar` remained nil. `LaunchDenialObservation.eligible` consequently
remained false until the shared presentation deadline. Its loop clears candidate history on every
ineligible sample and reports only `conditional_launch_timeout`, so the display predicate was lost.

The proposed main-plus-auxiliary topology is **not confirmed**. There was one, not multiple, visible
PID-owned layer-zero window. No maximal-area selection or auxiliary classification is authorized.
Adding a generic “largest window wins” rule would both fail to address V11 and weaken the reviewed
misclick boundary. If a future exact run observes multiple owner windows, it remains terminal until a
new evidence set proves stable identities, containment, noninteraction, bounded auxiliary dimensions
and a source-level owner relationship; only then may Architecture consider the narrowly described
unique-main/subordinate rule.

### Remediation contract

Preserve the existing active-display-only click authority. An online-but-inactive or sleeping display
may support diagnosis of stale desktop geometry, but may never bind a click coordinate. The verifier
must not synthesize user activity to wake a display, post a wake click, invoke `caffeinate`, substitute
`CGGetOnlineDisplayList`, or wait indefinitely for the display to change. Waking the console is user or
test-environment authority outside Pebble's black-box behavior.

Replace ambiguous array-return display enumeration with a typed, bounded observation that separates:

1. Core Graphics enumeration failure;
2. successful enumeration with zero active drawable displays;
3. one or more active displays, including exact per-candidate containment; and
4. corroborating online/sleep state used for redacted failure attribution only.

At the initial interactive preflight, require at least one active drawable display, exactly one
`NSScreen` mapping for every display that could contain the product window, and no sleeping state for
the eventually bound display. If the console is unlocked but all online displays are sleeping, fail
before launch or, if the transition occurs after launch, terminate the launch settlement immediately
with `conditional_launch_display_unavailable`. No CG event is created or posted. The failure is an
environment prerequisite failure, not product denial, and it does not consume or retire the
conditional click action.

If the display was active at preflight and becomes inactive/asleep during sampling or at the adjacent
check, fail sticky with zero input. If it later wakes, that same run is not resumed because display
configuration, window geometry, predecessor authority and event history may all have changed. A fresh
run must recapture every authority from the beginning. When the display remains active, the original
one-window, one-containing-display and exact titlebar contracts are unchanged.

### Predicate attribution without sensitive output

Give `LaunchDenialObservation` a fixed predicate list analogous to `LaunchFastObservation.predicates`
and a bounded field aggregate. The settlement loop records last, ever-true and ever-false values,
maximum safe counts and the final conditional-action state. At timeout or early display failure it
reports only these fixed names and bounded values:

- `authority_exact`, `finished_launching`, `inactive`, `predecessor_frontmost_exact`,
  `lifecycle_zero`, `lifecycle_unchanged`, `focused_children_zero`,
  `standard_inactive_surface_zero`, `input_preflight`;
- `owner_window_count`, `owner_window_unique`, `owner_geometry_finite`, `owner_onscreen`,
  `owner_alpha_one`, `owner_identity_stable`;
- `active_display_query_success`, `active_display_count`, `containing_active_display_count`,
  `online_display_query_success`, `online_display_count`, `containing_display_awake`,
  `nsscreen_mapping_count`, `titlebar_conversion_valid`;
- `private_snapshot_capture`, `private_text_count`, `private_text_empty`; and
- `denial_consecutive`, `conditional_state`, `total_samples` and
  `maximum_consecutive_samples`.

Do not place raw rectangles, coordinates, display/window identifiers, PIDs, paths, process names, AX
values, text or content hashes in ordinary driver failure output. The raw rectangle/area above was a
one-time local Architecture diagnostic required to attribute V11; the durable runtime diagnostic
must retain the preceding Security(plan) privacy boundary. Counts are capped before formatting, and
unknown/error is distinct from false/zero. Predicate formatting is deterministic and ordered so a
future run cannot fail with an unattributed aggregate.

### File and dependency order

1. `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift` — introduce typed active/online display observations,
   active-and-awake preflight/adjacent guards, the fixed denial predicate/field aggregate and the
   immediate redacted display-unavailable failure. Keep the existing unique-window rule exact.
2. `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift` — enforce ordered diagnostics, error-versus-zero
   distinction, sleeping-display no-input behavior, absence of an online-display click fallback and
   absence of maximal-area window selection.
3. Pure deterministic display/predicate fixtures may live in an existing nonshipping test support
   target only if that target remains outside every Pebble product. No shipping source or package
   membership changes.
4. Rerun the gate only after the physical/console display is independently active and awake. This is
   an execution prerequisite, not a code recovery step.

### Risk-to-test map

1. **Enumeration error hidden as zero.** Inject success/zero, success/nonzero and failure results;
   require distinct typed states and diagnostics. No branch may convert error into an empty list.
2. **Sleeping-display misclick.** Cover one online sleeping display with valid stale geometry and a
   valid titlebar conversion. It must fail before event allocation with zero down/up posts.
3. **Wake/configuration race.** Transition active-to-asleep, asleep-to-active, display-ID change and
   `NSScreen` mapping change at each sample and the adjacent check. Every transition is sticky-terminal
   with zero input and requires a fresh run.
4. **False V11 regression.** Fixture the exact evidence shape: one opaque finite owner window, zero
   focused children/standard surfaces/text fields, exact predecessor, input preflight true and active
   display count zero. Require `conditional_launch_display_unavailable`, not timeout.
5. **Predicate opacity.** Toggle each denial predicate independently and require last/ever values plus
   bounded counts to identify it. Test deterministic ordering, unknown states and count caps.
6. **Privacy regression.** Source/output fixtures reject rectangles, coordinates, identifiers, PIDs,
   paths, predecessor/product names, raw AX/text values and hashes from failure diagnostics.
7. **Window-selection weakening.** Multiwindow fixtures remain ineligible. Source tests reject
   maximal-area sorting/selection, `max(by:)`, “largest”, auxiliary thresholds and online-display
   substitution under this amendment.
8. **Happy denial path.** With one active awake containing display, one stable opaque owner window,
   exact titlebar conversion and all other predicates true, preserve the existing two samples,
   0.25-second stability, adjacent recheck, one down/up and exact postconditions unchanged.
9. **Immutable scope.** Rehash the release executable before and after focused tests; require the exact
   `648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8` digest and no shipping,
   pin, package, receipt or sign-off change.

### Conditions for Builder

Builder may change only the nonshipping driver and its source/deterministic tests after a distinct
Design Review of the developer-facing diagnostic contract and a fresh Security(plan) review of the
display/input boundary. Keep the one-window rule; do not implement maximal-area/auxiliary selection.
Keep active-and-awake display authority mandatory at preflight, stable samples and the adjacent final
check. A sleeping or inactive display must fail with zero event allocation/posting and cannot be woken
or retried by the verifier. Diagnostics must be fixed, ordered, capped and redacted. Initial tests
accompany the code. Security(code), full Test, an awake exact-package rerun and the mandatory manual
installed evidence remain downstream.

**V11 Denial-Predicate Attribution and Sleeping-Display Remediation Architecture verdict: PASS.**
V11 did not expose multiple Pebble windows or a product regression. It exposed a sleeping console
display: the sole online display was inactive/not drawable, correctly leaving the click's active
display and titlebar authority unavailable. The safe remedy is explicit typed attribution and early
zero-input failure, followed by a fresh run in an independently awake environment. Online geometry,
display waking, generic largest-window selection and retry remain forbidden.

## Design Review — V11 Sleeping-display Remediation (2026-07-12)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`fd06e212499ce9618836ef6c407b2eb974e2bf0664b4a88141acd791c1cda104`. Treating an asleep or
non-drawable display as an unmet environment prerequisite is the truthful, non-disruptive interaction
contract. Approval is binding on these conditions:

1. Display observation remains typed: enumeration failure, success with zero active displays and a
   nonzero active set are distinct. Online/asleep geometry is attribution only and can never supply a
   click display, coordinate or success predicate. No online fallback, largest-window/display choice,
   stale geometry tolerance or error-to-empty conversion is permitted.
2. Active-and-awake authority is required at preflight, every stable denial sample and the adjacent
   final check, with exact containing-display and `NSScreen` mapping. Zero active displays before
   launch fails before product/input action; loss or configuration change after launch is sticky-
   terminal before event allocation. A later wake cannot resume the same run or reuse its window,
   predecessor, lifecycle or conditional-action state.
3. The verifier may not wake or keep awake the display, post a wake click/key, call a prompting API,
   invoke `caffeinate`, wait indefinitely, select another display, extend a deadline or retry. A new
   set begins only after the user/test environment independently confirms an unlocked, active, awake
   console and recaptures all authority from the start.
4. Failure copy remains the fixed `conditional_launch_display_unavailable` environment category, not
   product denial, launch failure or Accessibility failure. Ordered capped diagnostics distinguish
   unknown/error from false/zero and expose only fixed predicates/counts; no rectangles, coordinates,
   IDs, PIDs, names/paths, raw AX/text values or hashes may appear.
5. The unique-window, exact titlebar, two-sample stability, adjacent check, one down/up and post-click
   lifecycle/CG/AX/text proof remain unchanged on an active awake display. Multiwindow ambiguity stays
   no-input; no main/auxiliary or maximal-area heuristic is authorized.
6. The sleeping-display failure is immutable diagnostic evidence only and cannot populate Finder,
   Dock, human-click, denial recovery or VoiceOver fields. The exact-product awake rerun and full
   manual installed/Accessibility matrix remain mandatory on identical bytes.

**V11 Sleeping-display Remediation Design Review verdict: PASS.** The verifier now fails early and
truthfully without disruptive wake/input automation, preserves every no-misclick invariant, and
requires a fresh independently awake run rather than sampling environmental recovery. Security
(plan), implementation review and all installed human/VoiceOver gates remain mandatory.

## Security (plan) — V11 Sleeping-display Remediation (2026-07-12)

Independently reviewed the V11 diagnostic evidence, remediation Architecture, binding Design PASS,
current display-query helper and immutable release artifact through plan SHA-256
`5791a6620cf9c57dc39dd57e4935b46c3d6b36812c03d984eb2faf0373499596`. This gate approves only
the nonshipping verifier plan; the existing implementation still requires Builder work and Security
(code) before any runtime claim.

### Findings

None.

The typed display model closes the observed attribution defect. Core Graphics query failure,
successful zero-active result and a nonempty active set remain distinct states; online enumeration and
sleep state are separate corroboration only. Bounded two-stage enumeration must preserve API errors,
count/capacity changes, truncation and mapping ambiguity as unknown/terminal rather than converting
them to an empty list. Only a successfully enumerated active drawable display with one exact
`NSScreen` mapping may provide containment or titlebar authority. An online, inactive or sleeping
display can explain stale desktop geometry but can never supply a coordinate, substitute display or
success predicate.

Active-and-awake authority is required before launch, throughout both stable denial samples and at the
adjacent final action check. Zero active displays at preflight fails before product launch or input
action; loss of activity, sleep, display-ID/configuration change, containing-display change or
`NSScreen` mapping change after launch is sticky-terminal before event allocation/posting. Multiwindow
or multicontainment ambiguity remains terminal. A later wake cannot resume the run, retain candidate
history, consume/retire the conditional action or reuse its process, window, predecessor, geometry or
lifecycle authority.

The verifier receives no wake authority. It may not call a prompting API, synthesize a wake click or
key, invoke `caffeinate` or power-management wake APIs, poll indefinitely, extend the deadline, select
an online/alternate display, tolerate stale geometry or retry the set. The physical console must be
made independently unlocked, active and awake, after which a genuinely fresh run recaptures every
authority from the beginning and rechecks the same active-and-awake predicates. This is an execution
precondition, not product recovery behavior.

Failure is early, zero-input and honestly classified as
`conditional_launch_display_unavailable`, not product denial, launch failure or Accessibility failure.
Fixed ordered diagnostics retain error/unknown versus false/zero, last/ever predicate states, capped
counts, bounded consecutive/sample aggregates and the terminal conditional state. They exclude raw
rectangles, coordinates, display/window/process identifiers, PIDs, names, paths, AX values, text and
content hashes. Online/sleep evidence remains attribution-only, and the raw one-time Architecture
geometry cannot migrate into durable runtime output.

The original safe awake-display path remains unchanged: one exact owner window, one active awake
containing display, exact converted titlebar geometry, two samples plus the adjacent check, one
precreated down/up pair and the full lifecycle/CG/AX/text postconditions. No largest-window,
main/auxiliary, maximal-area, online-display or alternate-surface heuristic is authorized. Tests must
cover error/zero/nonzero enumeration, capacity races, active-to-sleep and sleep-to-active transitions,
mapping/ID changes at every boundary, exact V11 attribution, redaction/order/caps, multiwindow
rejection and the unchanged happy path.

Scope remains wholly nonshipping: only the standalone AppKit integration driver, directly affected
source/deterministic tests and this plan may change. Pure fixtures must stay outside every Pebble
product. No shipping source, package membership, product pin, release verifier, receipt or installed-
signoff format change is authorized. The current release executable remains exactly
`648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`; focused and full gates
must rehash it before and after verifier work.

The immutable sleeping-display run is diagnostic evidence only and cannot populate denial recovery,
Finder, Dock, human-click or VoiceOver fields. After focused tests, typecheck, source scans and hash
proof, the unchanged full XCTest, security scan, warning-free release build, `pebsmoke` and pipeline
remain mandatory. A new awake exact-package set is required before the complete manual installed and
Accessibility matrix, Designer Sign-off and Deploy. Any failure or code/artifact change invalidates
later evidence; there is no wake fallback, evidence substitution, regold, replacement, resumed run,
action retry or second product pin.

**Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** Typed display states preserve error attribution, active-and-awake
authority gates every input boundary, sleeping or ambiguous states fail early and sticky with zero
input, and the verifier cannot wake, substitute, retry or leak display/content identity. A fresh
independently awake set and every unchanged security, runtime, installed and deployment gate remain
required on the exact immutable product.

## Architecture — V12 Exact-pair Grant / Transitional-surface Separation (2026-07-12)

This amendment diagnoses the immutable awake V12 run against plan input SHA-256
`0f61e940d8e24c1b0afa54666014662055f6377a8bdef4cb2b04bbd8a5584256`. All static,
completion, process, file, hash, permission and awake-display authorities were exact. The prearmed
workspace ledger received exactly the completion-bound Pebble activation and exact predecessor
deactivation at offsets 507 and 508 milliseconds, completely drained ingress, and selected
`expectedPairSurface`. The conditional loop posted zero input, but terminated as
`conditional_launch_mixed_evidence` because the active/frontmost properties had matured before one
simultaneously eligible typed AX grant surface.

The product remains immutable at SHA-256
`648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`. V12 is not a product
activation failure and not a reason to click. It reveals an ownership error between two verifier state
machines: the conditional action answers whether a synthetic denial click is still permitted, while
`LaunchPresentationSettlement` answers whether the activated app has published a complete stable
surface that can authorize navigation. The exact pair resolves the first question as system grant;
only the second state machine should absorb bounded AX/CG publication latency.

### Two distinct authorities

1. **Conditional-input authority.** This state decides only
   `skippedSystemGranted` versus `performedDeniedClick`. An exact, fully admitted and fully drained
   launch handoff pair proves that the system granted the activation transition and permanently makes
   a synthetic denial click unnecessary and unsafe. It does not prove a usable AX/CG surface.
2. **Presentation/navigation authority.** This remains the existing
   `LaunchPresentationSettlement`. It starts a fresh candidate sequence under the same workspace
   ledger generation and unchanged absolute deadline. Only its consecutive fast samples, complete
   AX/display/CG snapshots, final rehash and atomic `commitLaunchPresentation` may enter
   `boundNavigation`.

Closing the conditional action is therefore not a presentation commit, baseline retirement or
property-only success. It produces zero input and transfers unresolved publication work to the owner
that already has explicit AX/CG reset categories. The diagnostic token `expectedPairSurface` is an
authority-path name inherited from the launch ledger; before final presentation commit it means
“exact pair is the positive lifecycle path,” not “the standard surface is already published.”

This amendment narrowly supersedes the V10 Design condition that `skippedSystemGranted` itself always
requires the complete typed AX surface. That condition remains correct for the zero-event path, whose
only positive evidence is the exact surface. It is redundant and harmful after the platform has
already emitted the complete exact pair.

### Typed launch-settlement lease

Do not reconstruct pair completeness from `app.isActive`, event counts or diagnostic strings in the
conditional loop. Have `WorkspaceActivationHandoffLedger.beginLaunchSettlementSample()` return a
typed lease containing the frozen sequence and one of exactly two authority cases:

- `zeroEventSurface`, available only when ingress, retained events and pair-member flags are all zero;
- `expectedPairSurface`, available only when both exact pair members are present once each, in either
  order, `launchPairCompletionSequence` equals the retained event count, ingress equals that frozen
  sequence, identities are exact and no foreign/duplicate/third event exists.

`finishLaunchSettlementSample` validates the same lease, generation, authority case, sequence, event
count and ingress after the sample. It returns workspace stability separately from target AX
invalidation stability. A pair that completes between begin and finish invalidates the zero-event
lease; the next sample may acquire a drained expected-pair lease. A partial pair returns no lease and
can only await its exact complement within the original deadline. It cannot grant, deny, click or
fall back to zero events.

`commonModeLaunchSample` must expose at least `workspaceUnchanged` and `axUnchanged` independently.
Do not continue assigning their conjunction to a field named `lifecycleUnchanged`: AX publication is
not workspace lifecycle. Denial stability and a zero-event full-surface grant require both values;
an exact-pair conditional grant requires the exact workspace lease and may ignore AX churn because it
posts no input and immediately hands publication to the AX-aware settlement owner.

### Conditional branch order

Every sample first retains exact immutable completion/process/file/hash authority and the V11
active/awake display baseline. Then evaluate in this order:

1. **Drained exact-pair grant.** With an `expectedPairSurface` lease whose finish is workspace-exact,
   require the original app to be nonterminated, finished launching, active and exact workspace-
   frontmost by value/PID/bundle/executable. Close `ConditionalLaunchDenialAction` exactly once as
   `skippedSystemGranted`, clear any accumulated denial candidate, emit zero CG events and transfer to
   `LaunchPresentationSettlement`. Do not require AX window count, focused-window equality, role,
   subrole, typed fullscreen mode, finite AX frame, matching CG owner or AX-invalidation stability at
   this boundary.
2. **Zero-event surface grant.** With a `zeroEventSurface` lease, require unchanged workspace and AX
   sequences plus the complete existing `LaunchFastObservation.eligible` tuple. Only then close as
   `skippedSystemGranted`. Active/frontmost zero-event evidence without the complete exact surface
   remains mixed and terminal; pair semantics cannot be inferred from properties.
3. **Zero-event proven denial.** Only a stable `zeroEventSurface` lease may accumulate the existing
   inactive/predecessor/window/display/titlebar/private-state denial candidate and reach the adjacent
   one-shot click. The adjacent sample must evaluate an exact drained pair before attempting candidate
   equality or event allocation, so a late legitimate grant always produces zero input.
4. **Partial/invalid lifecycle.** One pair member waits only for its complement under the unchanged
   deadline and performs no action. Foreign, duplicate, wrong-identity, third/extra, sequence/capacity
   or ingress evidence remains immediately sticky-terminal. No AX/CG surface can repair it.

If the exact pair is drained before `isFinishedLaunching` becomes true, retain the exact-pair lease
under the same deadline until finished-launching corroboration arrives; never click. Once the pair is
complete, loss of exact target active/frontmost authority or immutable identity is terminal rather
than denial. The verifier cannot reinterpret a completed grant as a later click opportunity.

### Presentation settlement after exact pair

Create `LaunchPresentationSettlement` with a zeroed candidate history after closing the conditional
pair grant. Keep the same absolute `presentationDeadline`; pair completion neither restarts nor extends
it. Keep the same ledger phase and generation. Do not enter `boundNavigation` at the transfer.

Before binding, settlement may reset consecutive presentation samples only for publication churn:

- AX window unpublished/transitioning, focused-window lag, role/subrole or typed-mode lag;
- finite AX geometry publication and target AX invalidation;
- exact PID-owned CG surface publication/mapping and stable display containment.

Every reset discards the entire AX/mode/rectangle/display/CG candidate, as today. Settlement still
requires four consecutive exact samples spanning 0.25 seconds, the complete snapshot, final rehash,
immediate revalidation and atomic presentation commit.

It may not absorb lifecycle or immutable-authority loss. After the exact pair transfer, any new
workspace event, event/ingress drift, target deactivation, loss of target active/frontmost exactness,
termination, process/bundle/executable/file/hash change, active/awake display-authority change or
observer-generation failure is terminal immediately. Those conditions must not be categorized as
`propertyLag`, `lifecycleTransition` reset-and-continue or eventual timeout. Only AX and the exact
CG-window publication that represents that same still-active product may churn.

If the typed surface never matures, the final result remains
`launch_presentation_timeout` with `launch_activation_path=systemGranted`, zero synthetic input and
the existing bounded predicate aggregate. This is a truthful failed presentation proof, not a denial
click failure. Pair authority never authorizes navigation by itself.

The synthetic-denial-click branch remains unchanged after its one post: its exact resulting pair is
consumed by the same ledger, and the presentation settlement still requires the full stable typed
surface and all post-click identity/text invariants. This amendment grants no retry, second click or
alternate activation authority.

### File and dependency order

1. `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift` — add the typed launch-settlement lease/path, separate
   workspace and AX stability, reorder the conditional decision, and make postpair nonpublication
   authority losses terminal inside settlement.
2. `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift` — enforce the typed path/lease and branch order,
   pair-versus-surface fixture matrix, zero input on every pair path and unchanged zero-event denial.
3. Pure ledger/reducer fixtures may be added only to an existing nonshipping support/test target. No
   product target or package product may consume this authority.
4. `TEXT_ENTRY_REGRESSION_PLAN.md` — retain this evidence, downstream reviews and exact results.

No shipping source, activation coordinator, package graph, product pin, release verifier, receipt or
installed-signoff format may change. The product hash must remain exact before and after all work.

### Risk-to-test map

1. **V12 exact pair with AX lag.** Fixture target activate/predecessor deactivate at 507/508
   milliseconds, both orders, drained `expectedPairSurface`, active/frontmost exact and incomplete
   role/subrole/mode/frame. Require immediate `skippedSystemGranted`, zero mouse events, fresh
   presentation settlement and eventual PASS only after the complete stable surface.
2. **AX churn at the pair boundary.** Complete/invalidate AX in the same sample and between ledger
   finish and conditional close. Pair grant still emits zero input; settlement starts with no carried
   candidate and either binds fresh evidence or times out honestly.
3. **Pair without eventual surface.** Hold AX/CG incomplete through the original deadline. Require
   `launch_presentation_timeout`, system-granted provenance, zero click and no navigation commit.
4. **Partial pair.** Cover each sole member, pair split across begin/finish and complement after the
   deadline. No sample may grant or deny while partial; no click; exact complement within the deadline
   restarts with an expected-pair lease, while missing complement is terminal.
5. **Invalid pair.** Duplicate, foreign, wrong predecessor, wrong target, third event, capacity or
   ingress mismatch remains sticky-terminal before conditional close and cannot be repaired by an
   exact later surface.
6. **Zero-event grant.** Require the full finished/active/frontmost/typed standard AX/finite/display/CG
   surface with workspace and AX sequences unchanged. Active-only, frontmost-only or incomplete AX
   zero-event observations remain `conditional_launch_mixed_evidence` and emit no input.
7. **Zero-event denial.** Preserve two stable samples, 0.25 seconds, adjacent exact recheck, one safe
   down/up and all existing postconditions. A pair arriving before event allocation changes to grant
   and proves posting count zero.
8. **Postpair authority loss.** After transfer, independently remove active, frontmost, finished-
   launching monotonicity, target identity/hash, ledger sequence, observer generation and awake
   display authority. Each is immediate terminal, not a reset or timeout.
9. **Publication-only resets.** Independently churn AX window count/focus/role/subrole/mode/frame and
   exact CG publication before bind. Each clears the candidate and may recover only under the same
   lifecycle/identity/display authority and deadline.
10. **No premature navigation.** Assert that pair close leaves the ledger in `launchSettlement`, that
    `requireBoundNavigation()` still fails, and that only the complete final snapshot plus
    `commitLaunchPresentation` changes the phase.
11. **Deadline and one-shot invariants.** Pair close does not extend the deadline, allocate an event,
    retire a generic action or allow reuse. Later callback/F11/reactivation cannot reopen the
    conditional action.
12. **Immutable scope.** Source scans reject product activation/input changes and rehash the exact
    `648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8` product before and after
    focused/full verification.

### Conditions for Builder

Builder may edit only the nonshipping driver and directly affected source/deterministic tests after a
distinct Design Review and renewed Security(plan), because this amendment revises the V10 conditional
grant boundary. Use a typed ledger-issued lease; do not infer pair completion from properties or
duplicate ledger state outside its lock. Keep workspace and AX stability separate. Evaluate exact
drained pair before zero-event mixed/denial logic and emit zero input on every pair path. Transfer to a
fresh presentation candidate under the same ledger/deadline; do not commit navigation or carry AX/CG
stability across the transfer. Make postpair lifecycle/identity/display loss terminal while permitting
only bounded AX/CG publication resets. Add the full fixture matrix in the same pass. Security(code),
full Test, awake exact-package proof and installed human/VoiceOver evidence remain downstream.

**V12 Exact-pair Grant / Transitional-surface Separation Architecture verdict: PASS.** The exact
507/508-millisecond pair is conclusive conditional-input evidence that the system granted activation,
so synthetic input must close skipped even while AX/CG publication is transitional. It is not
presentation or navigation authority. The existing settlement binder remains the sole owner of typed
surface stabilization under the same frozen lifecycle ledger and deadline; partial/invalid pairs and
all postpair lifecycle, identity or display losses remain terminal.

## Design Review — V12 Pair-vs-Surface Separation (2026-07-12)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`a79f1d3a4481930edc099f84ce0c0409a21ad12ec5ee3533c28f97ff6f5180ce`. Separating click
authority from usable-surface authority preserves both safety and truthful launch UX. Approval is
binding on these conditions:

1. Pair status comes only from a typed ledger lease with exact identities, both members once, complete
   ingress drainage and a frozen sequence. Conditional evaluation checks the drained pair first,
   permanently closes as `skippedSystemGranted`, clears denial history and emits zero CG input. AX
   churn at that boundary cannot re-open or delay the no-click decision.
2. Pair closure is not presentation success. It transfers to a fresh, zero-history
   `LaunchPresentationSettlement` under the same generation and absolute deadline; navigation remains
   forbidden until the complete stable typed standard AX/CG surface, full snapshot, AX revalidation,
   final rehash and atomic presentation commit all pass.
3. The zero-event branch still requires unchanged workspace and AX sequences plus the complete
   finished/active/frontmost standard-surface tuple before grant. Active/frontmost with incomplete AX
   is truthful mixed evidence and emits no input. Only stable zero-event denial can reach the existing
   exact titlebar eligibility and adjacent one-shot click.
4. A partial pair may wait only for its complement within the original deadline and cannot grant,
   deny, click or fall back to zero events. Foreign, duplicate, wrong-identity, third/extra,
   capacity/sequence/ingress evidence is sticky-terminal; a later exact property or surface cannot
   repair it. A pair arriving at the final denial boundary always produces zero input.
5. After pair transfer, only target AX/CG publication churn may reset the entire presentation
   candidate. Any new workspace event, deactivation, active/frontmost or finished-launching loss,
   process/bundle/executable/file/hash change, display active/awake/configuration loss or observer-
   generation failure is immediately terminal—not a reset, timeout or denial opportunity.
6. Diagnostics keep workspace and AX stability separately named and describe pair grant as lifecycle/
   no-click authority only. Fixed bounded aggregates remain private; no identity, text/caret, raw AX/
   CG, input or clipboard data may escape. Product bytes and shipping behavior remain frozen.
7. Automated settlement remains corroboration. The exact same bytes still require the real Finder/
   Dock, human denial-click and granted/denied-then-clicked VoiceOver matrix; no pair or synthetic
   evidence may populate those human fields.

**V12 Pair-vs-Surface Separation Design Review verdict: PASS.** An exact drained pair safely closes
the destructive-input question while the fresh binder remains the sole usability and Accessibility
surface authority. No masking, premature navigation, retry or human-evidence substitution is
permitted; renewed Security (plan) and every downstream gate remain mandatory.

## Security (plan) — V12 Pair-vs-Surface Separation (2026-07-12)

Independently reviewed the awake V12 evidence, pair/surface Architecture, binding Design PASS,
current workspace ledger and presentation binder shape, and immutable release artifact through plan
SHA-256 `d5f8dbdb1f66dadf591ae48eebfe4ccc6c4c9fe819410000109fc0d5bfc72f3f`.
This gate approves only the nonshipping verifier plan; implementation and runtime evidence remain
downstream.

### Findings

None.

Pair authority is derived only from a typed ledger-issued lease, never from active/frontmost
properties, counts copied outside the ledger lock or diagnostic path strings. `zeroEventSurface`
requires zero retained events, zero pair flags and fully drained zero ingress. `expectedPairSurface`
requires both exact identities once each in either order, completion sequence equal to retained event
count, ingress drained exactly to that frozen sequence and no foreign, duplicate, third, wrong-
identity, overflow or capacity evidence. Begin/finish validation binds the same generation, authority
case, sequence and event count; a pair arriving between them invalidates the zero-event lease rather
than allowing stale denial authority.

The branch order prevents destructive-input races. A fully drained exact-pair lease with exact
finished/active/frontmost immutable target authority closes `skippedSystemGranted` once, clears all
denial history and emits zero CG events even when AX/CG publication is transitional. The adjacent
denial check must evaluate that pair before candidate equality or event allocation, so a late complete
grant cannot click. Pair closure is lifecycle/no-input authority only: it cannot bind navigation,
retire a baseline, imply a standard surface or substitute properties for presentation proof.

Zero-event behavior remains strictly narrower. A zero-event grant requires unchanged workspace and AX
sequences plus the complete finished, active, frontmost, typed standard AX, finite display/geometry and
exact CG tuple. Active/frontmost with incomplete surface is mixed terminal evidence and emits no input.
Only two-sample stable zero-event denial may reach the existing adjacent, precreated one-shot titlebar
click. A partial pair can wait only for its exact complement under the original deadline and cannot
grant, deny, click or fall back to zero events. Missing complement expires terminal; foreign,
duplicate, extra, wrong-identity, sequence/ingress/capacity or observer evidence is immediately sticky-
terminal and cannot be repaired by a later pair, property or surface.

After an exact-pair close, `LaunchPresentationSettlement` starts with zero candidate history under the
same ledger phase/generation and unchanged absolute deadline. It must reacquire and revalidate fresh
ledger leases while building four consecutive exact samples over the existing interval, then obtain
the complete snapshot, immediate AX revalidation, fresh executable rehash and atomic presentation
commit. `boundNavigation` remains impossible before that commit; pair completion does not restart the
clock, carry AX/CG stability forward, allocate input or authorize retry.

Reset and terminal categories are separated by trust boundary. Before bind, only target presentation
publication churn—AX window/focus/role/subrole/typed-mode/frame invalidation and exact still-active
product CG publication/mapping/containment—may clear the entire presentation candidate and try again
within the same deadline. Any new workspace event or ingress drift, deactivation, active/frontmost or
finished-launching loss, termination, process/bundle/executable/file/hash change, observer-generation
failure or active/awake display/configuration loss is immediately terminal. Those conditions cannot be
renamed property lag, absorbed as a reset, deferred to timeout or reopened as denial/click authority.

If publication never matures, the truthful result is `launch_presentation_timeout` with system-grant
provenance and zero synthetic input. The post-click path remains independently strict: its exact
resulting pair uses the same ledger and still requires the full stable typed surface plus retained
identity/text invariants. There is no second click, alternate activation, property-only fallback,
deadline extension, generic action retirement or evidence substitution.

Diagnostics keep workspace stability and AX stability separately named and describe the pair only as
lifecycle/no-click authority. Output remains fixed, ordered and bounded, excluding identities, PIDs,
paths, raw AX/CG, text, caret/selection, input and clipboard data. Automated pair/surface evidence
cannot populate Finder, Dock, human denial-click or VoiceOver fields.

Scope remains wholly nonshipping: only the standalone AppKit integration driver, directly affected
source/deterministic tests and this plan may change; any pure fixture must remain outside every Pebble
product. No shipping source, activation coordinator, package graph, pin, release verifier, receipt or
installed-signoff format change is authorized. The current release executable remains exactly
`648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8` and must be rehashed
before and after focused/full verification. Security (code), full Test, a fresh awake exact-package
set, installed Finder/Dock/human-click/VoiceOver proof, Designer Sign-off and Deploy all remain
mandatory on those unchanged bytes.

**Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** The typed frozen lease makes a drained exact pair conclusive only
for zero-input conditional closure, while a fresh same-ledger/same-deadline binder remains the sole
surface and navigation authority. Zero-event, partial and invalid cases are fail-closed; resettable
publication churn is narrowly separated from terminal lifecycle/identity/display loss; privacy and
nonshipping product scope remain intact.

## Architecture — V13 Denial-sampling Cadence Remediation (2026-07-12)

This amendment diagnoses immutable awake V13 through plan SHA-256
`3b7d73d3147379a6421c8b789ee58160b86998479d5323f086e96b042b581ab3`. V13 retained zero
workspace events, exact awake-display and predecessor authority, and all final denial predicates, but
timed out with one candidate sample. The release product remains exactly
`648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`.

### Exact-package fingerprint result

A separate exact-package observer posted no input and recorded every current candidate-comparison
field. The sole CG window retained ID `25096`, rectangle `(173, 95, 1440, 838)`, display `1`, titlebar
point `(893, 111)` and titlebar band `(269, 95, 1248, 32)`. The private text tuple count stayed zero,
the inactive focused-window AX object stayed equal, and workspace and AX invalidation sequences both
stayed zero.

No eligible candidate component changed. The only change was the expected pre-candidate reveal
transition from alpha zero during `fullscreen_retry_hidden` to alpha one during
`windowed_fallback_revealed`. After alpha one, consecutive fingerprints reported
`candidate_diff=none` for window ID, rectangle, alpha, display, titlebar point/band and private tuple
identity.

The failure is cadence, not overbinding. One AX hierarchy pass returned 522 descendants and took
547–793 milliseconds. Those synchronous AX requests also delayed the target main queue: fallback was
still hidden through a sample ending at 7.275 seconds and became visible near 7.3 seconds instead of
the nominal 6.1-second retry schedule. The first eligible sample ended at 7.851 seconds; the next
equal sample ended at 8.400 seconds, beyond the fixed 8.1-second deadline. The production driver is
worse than the observer because `launchDenialObservation` independently traverses the hierarchy for
focused children and again for `PrivateLaunchStateSnapshot.capture`.

`maximum_consecutive_samples=0` is also a diagnostics defect: `LaunchDenialDiagnostics.record` runs
before the loop assigns the first candidate's `denialConsecutive = 1`. It does not prove a changing
candidate.

### Remediation

Keep the exact candidate equality and fixed absolute deadline. Do not add identity tolerance, omit a
field, increase the deadline, or extend it from reveal based on this evidence.

Split denial observation into two ordered phases:

1. **Cheap pre-reveal observation.** Read immutable/lease/predecessor authority, target active state,
   input permission, the unique PID-owned layer-zero CG tuple and display authority first. While the
   exact window remains alpha zero, classify `fullscreen_retry_hidden`, clear candidate history, yield
   the run loop and perform no descendant traversal, private snapshot, titlebar event allocation or
   click work. This prevents the verifier from delaying the shipping fullscreen/fallback scheduler it
   is trying to observe.
2. **Visible denial observation.** Only after the same window is finite, on-screen and alpha exactly
   one may the verifier derive the titlebar and capture AX denial state. Capture descendants once per
   semantic sample into one immutable `DeniedAXSnapshot`; derive both focused-child absence and the
   private text tuples from that one collection. Never call `descendants(axApp)` again within the same
   sample.

Make descendant bounding exact. The current helper can exceed its nominal cap by appending an entire
child batch (the observed “512” pass returned 522). Append children individually up to a reviewed
fixed bound, return an explicit `truncated` flag, and make truncation ineligible. Builder must first
fixture the exact title hierarchy and choose a documented bound that contains it; it may not treat a
capped prefix as proof of zero focused/text children.

Retain two equal visible samples spanning 0.25 seconds plus the adjacent final recheck. Every sample
must finish before the existing deadline, and the adjacent recheck plus event allocation must also
remain before it; a sample begun before but completed after the deadline cannot authorize a click.
If optimized bounded sampling still cannot complete the contract, fail
`conditional_launch_sampling_budget_exhausted` with zero input and return measured sample durations to
Architecture. Any future deadline change requires an upfront measured bound and renewed review; it is
not authorized here.

Add component-level private diagnostics: fixed equality booleans for CG ID, rectangle, alpha,
display, titlebar point/band and private tuple identity; shipping phase; sample start/end/duration;
first-eligible offset; and consecutive count recorded after the reducer update. Ordinary output keeps
the existing privacy boundary and must not emit the raw IDs/rectangles above.

### Tests and Conditions for Builder

- Prove hidden alpha-zero retry samples perform zero descendant/private captures and cannot become
  candidates.
- Prove one visible semantic sample performs exactly one bounded traversal and supplies focused-child
  and private-tuple results from the same snapshot.
- Fixture the V13 fingerprint: alpha `0 -> 1`, then every comparison field equal. It must mature, reach
  the adjacent check and post one pair within the unchanged deadline; alpha transition itself is not
  reported as candidate instability.
- Independently mutate every comparison component and require its exact reset reason. Workspace/AX
  sequence change remains governed by the existing terminal/reset contracts.
- Cover cap-minus-one, exact-cap, cap-plus-one and wide child-batch cases; overflow is explicit and
  ineligible, never silently truncated.
- Verify first candidate records maximum consecutive count one, later samples update after reduction,
  and sample duration/deadline exhaustion is truthful.
- Prove there is no dynamic deadline extension, retry, relaxed equality, online-display fallback,
  second click or shipping-source change, and rehash the exact product before and after all gates.

Builder scope is limited to `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, directly affected
`TextEntrySourceTests`/pure nonshipping fixtures, and this plan. A distinct Design Review and renewed
Security(plan) must approve the sampling/diagnostic contract before Build. Security(code), full Test,
fresh awake exact-package proof and all installed human/VoiceOver/Deploy gates remain mandatory.

**V13 Denial-sampling Cadence Architecture verdict: PASS.** No eligible candidate identity changed;
the verifier's duplicated, over-cap AX traversal delayed product reveal and exhausted the fixed
stability budget. Cheap pre-reveal CG observation plus one exact bounded AX snapshot per visible
semantic sample removes the observer effect while preserving exact equality, deadline and zero-input
fail-closure.

## Design Review — V13 Denial-sampling Cadence (2026-07-12)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`049a74a4fe40481230519dfab52d77565e4ad3f7138b0b967d0fa5c987745e8a`. Cheap alpha-zero
observation and one bounded immutable AX snapshot per visible semantic sample preserve exact denial
identity while removing the verifier's launch-delay effect. Strict cap overflow, unchanged equality,
ordered post-reducer diagnostics and zero-input budget exhaustion are also correct.

One deadline/action ambiguity remains blocking:

- **[BLOCKING]** The Architecture requires the adjacent recheck and event allocation before the
  absolute deadline, but does not require the complete irreversible down/up action to finish before
  it. Allocation just before expiry could therefore authorize input after the evidence window. Keep
  the existing deadline unchanged, precreate both events before the final eligibility recheck, and
  require that recheck to prove enough remaining time for one reviewed fixed allocation/post budget.
  If that budget is unavailable, nonfinite, exceeded or leaves insufficient time, fail
  `conditional_launch_sampling_budget_exhausted` with zero input. After the recheck, post the single
  down/up adjacently and require the synchronous action end timestamp to remain at or before the same
  deadline. Record only fixed start/end/duration/budget predicates; never extend the deadline or retry
  if the action misses it.

The revised contract must also retain these already approved boundaries: exact drained-pair/no-click
evaluation precedes even the cheap hidden-phase classification; alpha-zero samples perform no AX/
private traversal or event allocation; each visible/final sample uses one bounded snapshot for both
focused-child and private tuples; truncation is explicit and ineligible; any candidate component or
workspace/AX authority change preserves its exact reset/terminal disposition; and raw IDs, geometry,
AX/text or content hashes never enter ordinary diagnostics.

**V13 Denial-sampling Cadence Design Review verdict: FAIL.** The sampling optimization and diagnostic
repair are sound, but Build remains blocked until the unchanged absolute deadline encloses the whole
adjacent one-shot action—not only sampling and allocation. This closes the last late-input masking
window without relaxing identity, extending time or adding retry.

## Architecture Revision — V13 Whole-action Deadline Closure (2026-07-12)

This revision responds to the binding Designer FAIL at plan SHA-256
`78e6ba036aca7c5eceea4f595f08173ebda2b02f0af4288549f4f077ace602b1`. The sampling diagnosis,
candidate equality, immutable product, absolute presentation deadline and every prior reset/terminal
boundary remain unchanged. The prior V13 Architecture PASS is superseded only at its incomplete
allocation/action deadline boundary.

### Revised action boundary

Use one fixed reviewed `denialPostBudget` of **100 milliseconds**. This is a conservative authority
reserve, not a claim that CG event posting normally takes that long. It is fixed before launch, finite,
positive, test-visible and may not be derived from remaining time, reveal time, sample duration or a
prior attempt.

Branch order remains mandatory. After each typed workspace lease is frozen and finished, evaluate an
exact drained pair/no-click branch before hidden/fullscreen-retry classification, AX traversal,
candidate work or event allocation. A drained pair closes `skippedSystemGranted` with zero events.
Partial/invalid pair and postpair authority rules remain unchanged. Only the zero-event visible denial
branch can proceed toward input.

After two equal visible denial samples span 0.25 seconds:

1. Read the monotonic clock. If it is nonfinite or fewer than 100 milliseconds remain before the one
   unchanged `presentationDeadline`, terminate as `conditional_launch_sampling_budget_exhausted` with
   zero allocation and zero input.
2. Precreate both click-count-one, zero-drag HID events at the already derived exact point. If either
   allocation fails, discard both and terminate with zero input. Event allocation grants no posting
   authority.
3. Run one complete adjacent sample after allocation. It must re-evaluate pair/no-click first, then
   every approved lease, immutable identity, active/inactive, predecessor, awake-display, CG window,
   titlebar, single bounded AX snapshot and private tuple predicate. Pair arrival discards the prepared
   events and closes skipped. Any mismatch follows its existing exact reset/terminal disposition and
   posts nothing.
4. At the end of the exact adjacent recheck, read the monotonic clock again. Require it to be finite,
   not later than the deadline, and to leave at least the full fixed 100-millisecond post budget.
   Otherwise discard both prepared events and terminate
   `conditional_launch_sampling_budget_exhausted` with zero input.
5. Arm the exact lifecycle/action boundary and close the conditional action before posting. Post the
   precreated left down followed immediately by the precreated left up at the same coordinate. There
   is no intervening sample, sleep, asynchronous dispatch, callback opportunity, retry or second
   clock-dependent decision between the pair.
6. `CGEvent.post` returns synchronously to the driver. Read the monotonic action-end time immediately
   after the up call and require it to be finite and at or before the same unchanged deadline. This
   proves only that the driver completed both posting calls inside the evidence window; it does not
   claim WindowServer delivery or target processing time. An unexpected overrun after posting is
   sticky `conditional_launch_action_deadline_miss`; input has occurred, so it cannot be reported as a
   zero-input budget failure, repaired, retried or reused.

No deadline is increased, restarted from reveal, extended for allocation, or renewed after a miss.
The 100-millisecond reserve may change only through a new measured Architecture/Design/Security
amendment; Builder cannot tune it to make a run pass.

### Retained sampling, privacy and diagnostics

The cheap alpha-zero pre-reveal path still performs no descendant/private traversal and no event
allocation. Each visible and adjacent sample still uses one exact bounded `DeniedAXSnapshot` for both
focused-child and private tuple evidence, with explicit truncation ineligible. Candidate equality,
component reset reasons, lifecycle/AX sequence separation, active/awake display authority and all
terminal identity rules remain exact.

Ordinary diagnostics expose only fixed, bounded relative timing fields and predicates:
`allocation_started_before_deadline`, `adjacent_check_completed`, `post_budget_ms=100`,
`post_budget_remaining`, `post_started_before_deadline`, `post_completed_by_deadline`, capped sample/
adjacent/post durations and the fixed terminal category. They may not emit wall-clock timestamps,
raw monotonic values, CG/AX/window/display identifiers or geometry, text/private tuples, paths, PIDs,
names or content hashes. Prepared-event contents and coordinates remain private.

### Tests and revised Conditions for Builder

- A fake monotonic-clock matrix covers nonfinite time; 99, 100 and 101 milliseconds remaining before
  allocation and after adjacent completion; exact-deadline completion; and one-tick post overrun.
- Source/order tests prove both events are allocated before the adjacent check, pair/no-click is
  evaluated first within that check, the final budget guard precedes arm/close/post, and the action-end
  timestamp follows the up call immediately.
- Pair arrival, candidate mismatch, AX truncation, display/lifecycle/identity change or allocation
  failure after preparation discards both events and records zero posts.
- The valid case proves one down and one up at the same point, synchronously completed by deadline,
  then the unchanged exact lifecycle/CG/AX/text postconditions.
- An injected synchronous post overrun is sticky terminal with exactly the calls already made and no
  retry; it cannot masquerade as `sampling_budget_exhausted` or successful denial recovery.
- Hidden-phase, V13 stable-fingerprint, component mutation, strict-cap and corrected post-reducer
  diagnostic tests from the prior amendment remain mandatory.
- Source scans prove no deadline mutation/extension, timer restart, asynchronous posting, sleep,
  retry, second click, shipping change or new diagnostic disclosure. Product rehash remains exact.

Builder remains limited to the nonshipping driver, directly affected source/deterministic tests and
this plan. Build is still blocked until Designer re-reviews this revised boundary and Security(plan)
passes it. Security(code), full Test, fresh awake exact-package proof and every installed human/
VoiceOver/Deploy gate remain downstream.

**Revised V13 Whole-action Deadline Architecture verdict: PASS.** The unchanged deadline now encloses
event preparation, the complete adjacent authority check and both synchronous posting calls. An
insufficient final 100-millisecond reserve fails with zero input; an unexpected post overrun is
truthfully terminal and never retried. Exact pair/no-click precedence, optimized sampling, privacy,
candidate equality and every approved reset/terminal rule are retained. **Design status remains FAIL
pending renewed Designer review; Build remains blocked.**

## Design re-review — V13 Whole-action Deadline Closure (2026-07-12)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`7099292ec92d2754e7dddca4cb2630e728f179890842af2f21343a9ba4ddc677`. The revision closes the
late-input blocker exactly:

1. The absolute presentation deadline remains unchanged. The fixed 100-millisecond reserve is an
   authorization margin, not added waiting time or a claim about ordinary post latency; it is checked
   before allocation and again after the complete adjacent sample. Insufficient or nonfinite time
   discards both events and fails `conditional_launch_sampling_budget_exhausted` with zero input.
2. Both click-count-one events exist before the adjacent check. That check re-evaluates the exact
   drained-pair/no-click branch first, then every lease, immutable identity, predecessor, display,
   window/titlebar, single bounded AX snapshot and private-state predicate. Pair arrival or any
   mismatch/truncation discards the events and posts nothing under its existing reset/terminal rule.
3. After the final full-budget guard, the action boundary closes and the single down/up posts
   synchronously and adjacently with no run-loop turn, sleep, dispatch, resample or retry. The action-
   end clock is read immediately after up and must be finite and at or before the same deadline.
4. A rare synchronous overrun is reported truthfully as input-already-occurred
   `conditional_launch_action_deadline_miss`, never zero-input exhaustion or success, and cannot be
   retried/reused. Changing the 100-millisecond reserve requires new measured Architecture, Design and
   Security review; Builder cannot tune it.
5. Optimized cadence remains bounded and nonmasking: alpha-zero performs no AX/private/allocation
   work; visible/final samples use one strict-cap snapshot; cap overflow is ineligible; candidate
   equality and authority dispositions are unchanged; diagnostics record post-reducer counts and
   fixed capped relative timing only, with no raw time, geometry, identity, AX/text or content data.
6. This remains nonshipping automation evidence. Product bytes stay frozen and the full installed
   Finder/Dock, human denial-click and VoiceOver matrix remains mandatory.

**V13 Whole-action Deadline Closure Design re-review verdict: PASS.** Event preparation, final exact
authority and both posting calls now live inside one unchanged evidence deadline with truthful
zero-input and post-overrun outcomes. The prior Design blocker is closed; renewed Security (plan) and
all downstream human/Accessibility gates remain mandatory.

## Security (plan) — Revised V13 Sampling and Whole-action Deadline (2026-07-12)

Independently reviewed the immutable V13 evidence, revised cadence/deadline Architecture, binding
Design PASS, current AX traversal/action helpers and immutable release artifact through plan SHA-256
`cf656c6f6ed514841e36c9e4437a723caac5f8ea5a19150e212aeea678f08686`. This gate approves
only the nonshipping verifier plan; Builder implementation, Security (code) and runtime evidence remain
downstream.

### Findings

None.

Sampling work is ordered by authority and visibility. Every iteration freezes and finishes the typed
workspace lease and evaluates an exact drained pair/no-click branch before hidden-state classification,
AX traversal, private capture, candidate work or event allocation. While the exact owner window is
alpha zero, the cheap phase reads only immutable/lease/predecessor, target activity, permission,
unique CG owner and active/awake display authority, clears denial history, yields and performs no AX
descendant/private traversal. It therefore cannot delay the fallback scheduler with work that has no
visible denial value.

Each visible and final semantic sample performs exactly one bounded traversal into one immutable
`DeniedAXSnapshot`; focused-child absence and every private text tuple derive from that same snapshot.
Children are admitted individually only up to the documented fixed cap. Query failure, a wide child
batch beyond the cap or any additional descendant sets explicit truncation and makes the sample
ineligible; a capped prefix can never prove absence. Cap-minus-one, exact-cap and cap-plus-one fixtures,
plus the exact observed title hierarchy, are required before the bound is trusted.

Candidate identity and reset/terminal dispositions remain exact. Two equal visible samples must span
the unchanged 0.25-second interval, with all reducer counters recorded only after the reducer update.
No field is omitted or tolerated, and workspace/AX stability stays separately typed. A sample that
starts before but ends after the absolute deadline cannot authorize input. If bounded sampling cannot
complete, the verifier fails `conditional_launch_sampling_budget_exhausted` with zero allocation and
zero input rather than increasing or restarting the deadline.

The irreversible path is fully enclosed by the existing absolute deadline. The fixed 100-millisecond
reserve is established before launch and cannot depend on reveal timing, observed sample cost,
remaining time or a prior attempt. Nonfinite time or less than the full reserve before allocation
fails with zero input. Both click-count-one, zero-drag events are then precreated at the already exact
point; allocation failure releases both and posts neither. Event creation alone carries no authority.

After preparation, one complete adjacent sample re-evaluates the exact pair/no-click branch first and
then every lease, identity, target/predecessor, active/awake display, CG window/titlebar, single bounded
AX snapshot and private tuple predicate. A pair arrival discards the prepared events and closes
system-granted with zero input. Truncation or any mismatch discards both unposted events and follows
its existing reset or terminal category; prepared events and coordinates may never be reused. Any
resettable pre-action resampling starts from fresh authority within the original deadline and is not an
action/post retry.

At adjacent completion, a second finite monotonic check must be at or before the same deadline and
still leave the entire fixed 100-millisecond reserve. Only then may the driver arm the exact lifecycle
boundary, close the one-shot action and synchronously call the precreated down then up at the same
coordinate, with no run-loop turn, dispatch, sample, sleep or clock-dependent branch between them. The
end clock is read immediately after the up call and must remain finite and at or before the unchanged
deadline. This proves only timely return of both posting calls, not WindowServer delivery or target
processing.

An unexpected synchronous overrun is truthfully sticky
`conditional_launch_action_deadline_miss`: input has already occurred, so it cannot be relabeled as
zero-input exhaustion, success or recoverable lag, and the action cannot be reused or retried. There is
no deadline increase, reveal-relative restart, allocation extension, asynchronous post, second click,
sleep, repair or alternate evidence selection. Changing the 100-millisecond reserve requires new
measured Architecture, Design and Security review rather than Builder tuning.

Diagnostics expose only fixed ordered/capped relative durations, budget predicates, post-reducer
counts and terminal categories. They exclude wall-clock and raw monotonic timestamps, prepared-event
contents/coordinates, CG/AX/window/display identities or geometry, PIDs, names, paths, text/private
tuples and content hashes. Pair/no-click, zero-input exhaustion and input-already-occurred overrun
remain distinct provenance and cannot populate Finder, Dock, human-click or VoiceOver receipt fields.

Scope remains wholly nonshipping: only the standalone AppKit integration driver, directly affected
source/deterministic tests and this plan may change. Shipping source retains no CG event-generation
authority; package graph, product pin, verifier, receipt and installed-signoff format stay frozen. The
current release executable remains exactly
`648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8` and must be rehashed
before and after focused/full verification. Security (code), full Test, a fresh awake exact-package
set, the installed Finder/Dock/human-click/VoiceOver matrix, Designer Sign-off and Deploy remain
mandatory on those identical bytes.

**Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** Pair-first cheap sampling, one shared strict-cap AX snapshot,
explicit truncation failure, a fixed prechecked 100-millisecond reserve, disposable precreated events,
full adjacent authority and synchronous down/up/end proof close the observer and late-input windows
without deadline extension, action retry, privacy regression or shipping/product authority expansion.

## Architecture — V14 Launch-presentation Cadence Contract Repair (2026-07-12)

Architecture reviewed the immutable V14 cold run 1, current nonshipping driver and source-contract
tests against input plan SHA-256
`0ec1400e64e93a864ccc92ca2f20bde76420ec50aab6ea642ec8747264a66d81`. This is diagnosis and a
minimal remediation contract only. No driver, test, product, build, runtime, deployment, commit or
push was changed or performed by Architecture.

### Immutable evidence and diagnosis

`/tmp/pebble-text-entry-v14-cold-1-20260712T0842.log` is read-only and is the only V14 cold-run
artifact. The gate used exact release executable SHA-256
`648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`; its independently signed
temporary package reported executable SHA-256
`bee1551f3a117fd5d13cd1503e0eb143410074a907177ca9cf060c1ba39f026f` and CDHash
`39259d833d231189fb8a2bbc857de663fd134b89`. A post-failure read-only rehash still returns the exact
release hash. Cleanup left zero `pebble-appkit-gate.*` directories and zero matching temporary Pebble
or driver processes. V14 was stopped at run 1 and was not retried, replaced or selected around.

The one run obtained the exact expected lifecycle pair—target activation at bounded offset 401 ms and
the exact predecessor deactivation at 402 ms—with exact identity, `event_count=2`, `ingress_count=2`
and `pair_complete=true`. `LaunchPresentationSettlement` then timed out after 10 samples with
`maximum_candidate_consecutive=2` and `maximum_candidate_duration_ms=822`. All immutable target,
finished-launching, active and frontmost predicates were always exact. The final reported
`reset_reason=axUnpublished` records publication churn after the qualifying two-snapshot interval; it
does not erase the retained maximum candidate evidence.

The verifier rejected that evidence only because the call at
`Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift:3219` still passes
`requiredConsecutiveSamples: 4`. The reducer already requires candidate equality over AX object,
typed mode, rectangle, containing display and CG owner; it records the first candidate time and also
requires the independent `minimumStableDuration: 0.25` before proceeding to the mandatory final full
snapshot, immediate revalidation, rehash and atomic commit. V14 therefore supplied the semantic
stability approved in immutable v7 and retained by v8: at least two complete snapshots on distinct
common-mode turns spanning the minimum duration. Waiting for two more snapshots added cadence
dependence but no new distinct authority, and allowed normal AX publication churn to erase an already
qualified pair before the final snapshot could be attempted.

The later V12 prose saying “four consecutive” and the inherited four-sample test model did not provide
a new threat, UX or measurement rationale and conflict with the binding v7/v8 two-distinct-turn plus
minimum-duration contract. This V14 amendment supersedes only those accidental V12 count references.
It does not weaken candidate equality, distinct-turn separation, the 0.25-second minimum, final full
snapshot/rehash/drain/commit, any reset or terminal category, or any post-bind invariant.

**Immutable V14 run-1 evidence verdict: FAIL.** The exact product remains unchanged and no product
defect is established. **Root-cause diagnosis verdict: PASS.** The launch verifier enforces an
unreviewed inherited count of four after the approved semantic pair has already been established.

### Minimal remediation and dependency order

1. In `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, change only the initial
   `launchSettlement.settle` call from `requiredConsecutiveSamples: 4` to `2`. Retain
   `minimumStableDuration: 0.25`, the existing reducer, common-mode yield between observations and all
   complete/final snapshot authority. Do not change the generic settlement guard or use one sample.
2. In `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift`, replace the inherited four-candidate launch
   model with a timestamped, distinct-turn two-candidate model followed by a separately represented
   mandatory final snapshot/rehash/commit. Scope source assertions to the initial launch call so they
   prove it uses exactly two while `FinderSurfaceSettlement` and `ReadyEpoch` each continue to require
   four. The unrelated denial candidate remains its separately approved two-over-0.25-second rule.
3. Update only directly affected V12/V14 test descriptions if necessary to use the exact domain term
   “two distinct complete launch-presentation turns spanning 0.25 seconds.” No deadline, polling
   interval, activation, presentation predicate, event ledger, package, receipt, pipeline, shipping
   source, Storage/RPG or LAN behavior may change.
4. After Builder evidence and fresh Security (code) PASS, create a distinctly named next immutable
   cold set on the new nonshipping verifier bytes. Do not rerun or replace V14. Stop the new set at its
   first failure; success still requires all five runs on one exact product/verifier pair.

### Risk-to-test map and Conditions for Builder

- **Too-weak temporal proof:** deterministic fixtures must show one complete sample fails; two equal
