  samples on the same run-loop turn fail; two equal samples on distinct turns spanning 249 ms fail;
  and two equal distinct-turn samples spanning exactly 250 ms and 822 ms advance only to the final
  full-snapshot stage.
- **Identity churn hidden by the lower count:** mutate each candidate component independently—AX
  object, typed mode, rectangle, display, CG owner, workspace lease and target AX sequence. Resettable
  publication churn must require a fresh two-snapshot interval; immutable/lifecycle authority loss
  remains sticky terminal. No sample from before an exact pair close may carry forward.
- **Final authority accidentally bypassed:** fixtures and ordered source assertions must prove the
  qualifying pair alone cannot bind or navigate. The full snapshot, exact candidate revalidation,
  fresh executable rehash, final workspace/AX drainage and atomic `commitLaunchPresentation` all
  remain after the threshold and before `boundNavigation` or any navigation/input.
- **Threshold leakage into later phases:** source-slice tests must prove initial
  `LaunchPresentationSettlement` uses `2`, while `FinderSurfaceSettlement` and `ReadyEpoch` remain
  `4` with their existing 0.25-second minimum. Replace the current unscoped
  `driver.contains("requiredConsecutiveSamples: 4")` assertion with phase-specific assertions.
- **Evidence selection or product drift:** source scans must prove there is no retry, sleep, deadline
  extension/restart, added activation/input, relaxed predicate or shipping-source edit. Rehash the
  release executable before and after focused/full gates and retain the immutable V14 run-1 artifact.
- **Regression gates:** compile the standalone driver, run the focused `TextEntrySourceTests`, all
  directly affected deterministic tests, `scripts/security-scan.sh`, warning-free release build,
  full XCTest, 457/457 `pebsmoke`, release-surface/binary verification and fresh Security (code).
  Runtime proof remains downstream and must use exact same-byte product/verifier evidence with normal
  cleanup and no replacement runs.

Builder is limited to `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, the directly affected
`Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift` fixtures/assertions and this plan. Product source,
package graph, launch deadline, presentation semantics, hooks, installed receipt, deployment and LAN
remain frozen. Design Review and Security (plan) must both PASS this amendment before Build.

**V14 Launch-presentation Cadence Remediation Architecture verdict: PASS.** Two equal complete
launch-presentation snapshots on distinct common-mode turns and spanning at least 0.25 seconds are the
reviewed semantic threshold; the mandatory final snapshot, revalidation, rehash, drainage and atomic
commit remain independent fail-closed authority. Build is blocked pending Design Review and Security
(plan).

## Design Review — V14 Launch-presentation Cadence Contract Repair (2026-07-12)

Reviewed through plan SHA-256
`e80b6fa6ac172fd56df7b2001246c358b931b72236a37a9c14a7a2f72556f321`. Restoring the initial
launch-presentation threshold from four observations to the previously approved two-semantic-turn
contract removes accidental polling-cadence dependence without weakening visible readiness, focus or
Accessibility authority. Approval is binding on these conditions:

1. The value `2` applies only to the initial `LaunchPresentationSettlement`. Both samples must be
   complete and equal on every approved candidate component, occur on distinct common-mode run-loop
   turns and span at least 0.25 seconds. One sample, two samples on one turn or a 249-millisecond span
   is ineligible. `FinderSurfaceSettlement` and `ReadyEpoch` remain at four complete samples over
   their unchanged minimum interval, and the separate denial rule remains unchanged.
2. The qualifying pair is not usable-surface success and grants no navigation or input. After it, the
   verifier must acquire the existing mandatory fresh full snapshot, revalidate exact AX candidate
   identity and all workspace/lifecycle/display authority, freshly rehash the executable, drain the
   callback ledgers and atomically commit presentation before `boundNavigation`. The second candidate
   cannot be reused as that final snapshot or populate a human/VoiceOver result.
3. Publication or identity churn at any point after the pair but before commit remains observable and
   follows its existing reset or sticky-terminal disposition. A reset clears the whole candidate
   interval; a terminal failure cannot be relabeled as cadence exhaustion. No sample from before the
   exact lifecycle pair or from a prior reset may carry forward.
4. Diagnostics must distinguish candidate count/duration, final-snapshot failure, reset and terminal
   failure using only the existing fixed ordered/capped safe aggregates. They must not expose raw
   timestamps, geometry, identities, paths, AX objects, text, clipboard data or hashes, and must not
   describe a qualifying pair as an accessible or navigable surface.
5. V14 cold run 1 remains the immutable failed artifact. Builder may neither rerun nor replace it,
   extend or restart a deadline, add sleeps/activation/input, retry around AX churn, or select a later
   passing result. The next distinctly named cold set must stop at its first failure and retain one
   exact product/verifier pair across all five successful runs.
6. This remains nonshipping corroboration. The exact installed bytes still require the real Finder/
   Dock, manual denial-click and granted/denied VoiceOver matrix, including field names/roles, focus
   announcements, caret movement, field switching, reactivation context and presentation retirement.
   Product UI, manual Paste/privacy behavior and RPG Accessibility behavior remain frozen.

**V14 Launch-presentation Cadence Contract Repair Design Review verdict: PASS.** Two complete equal
launch-presentation observations on distinct turns spanning at least 250 milliseconds are the approved
semantic readiness threshold; the independent final snapshot and fail-closed authority chain prevent
the lower count from masking churn or premature navigation. Later four-sample settlements, safe
diagnostics, immutable failure/no retry and installed human/Accessibility proof remain mandatory.

## Security (plan) — V14 Launch-presentation Cadence Contract Repair (2026-07-12)

Independently reviewed the immutable V14 run-1 evidence, V14 Architecture and separate Design PASS
through plan SHA-256
`52670ed2f0ea08b496101172f177360f66978366e6246e9132f36b8dbc72db43`. Review covered premature
navigation/input, polling-cadence substitution, same-turn or sub-duration evidence, candidate/identity
churn, final-snapshot bypass, threshold leakage, deadline/retry masking, evidence selection, diagnostic
privacy and shipping/product-scope expansion.

### Findings

None.

The amendment changes one authority threshold only: the initial
`LaunchPresentationSettlement.settle` call moves from four candidate observations to two. Both remain
complete snapshots of the exact same AX object, typed presentation mode, finite rectangle, containing
display and exact CG owner under unchanged workspace and target-AX sequences. They must occur on
distinct common-mode run-loop turns and span at least 0.25 seconds. One observation, two observations
on one turn, or a 249-millisecond span cannot advance. Any publication reset clears the full candidate
and its first timestamp; lifecycle, immutable identity, display or observer authority loss remains
sticky-terminal. No pre-pair or pre-reset evidence may carry forward.

Crossing the two-observation threshold grants neither input nor navigation. The existing subsequent
full snapshot remains an independent authority sample and must freshly revalidate the candidate,
process/file/display/CG predicates and executable hash, then finish and drain the exact workspace and
AX leases before atomic `commitLaunchPresentation` can enter `boundNavigation`. The qualifying second
candidate cannot substitute for that final snapshot. Any mismatch in the final sequence resets or
terminates according to the existing typed disposition; it cannot be reclassified as cadence delay or
recovered through a later selected result.

The threshold change is phase-scoped. `FinderSurfaceSettlement` and `ReadyEpoch` remain four complete
samples across their unchanged 0.25-second minimum, and the separate conditional-denial proof remains
two samples plus its adjacent authority check. Builder tests must slice each call site independently,
exercise one/same-turn/249/250/822-millisecond boundaries, mutate every candidate and lease component,
and prove ordered final snapshot, rehash, drainage and commit before navigation or input. An unscoped
source-presence assertion is insufficient because it could conceal threshold leakage between phases.

The absolute presentation deadline, common-mode polling/yield behavior, reset and terminal classes,
activation/input ledgers and one-shot action rules remain frozen. No deadline extension or restart,
sleep, retry, alternate-result selection, additional activation/input, predicate relaxation or V14
rerun/replacement is authorized. The next distinctly named cold-five set must use one exact product and
verifier pair, retain the immutable V14 failure and stop at its first failure.

Scope remains nonshipping and limited to the standalone AppKit driver, directly affected deterministic
source tests and this plan. Package graph, products, shipping source, release verifier/pin, receipt,
pipeline, Storage/RPG and LAN behavior remain unchanged. Diagnostics retain fixed ordered/capped
aggregate categories only and may not expose raw clocks, geometry, object or process identity, paths,
text/private tuples, clipboard content or hashes. The release executable must remain SHA-256
`648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8` before and after all focused
and full gates. Fresh Security (code), full Test, same-byte runtime proof and the independent installed
Finder/Dock/manual-click/VoiceOver/Designer matrix remain mandatory downstream.

**V14 Launch-presentation Cadence Contract Repair Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** Initial-launch-only
two-turn stability removes accidental polling-count dependence while the unchanged 250-millisecond
minimum, exact candidate equality, fresh final snapshot/rehash/drain/atomic commit and fail-closed
churn handling preserve the security boundary. No later threshold, deadline, retry, privacy, input or
shipping/product authority is weakened. Build is authorized only within the Architecture and Design
scope above.

## Security (code) — Current Actual Diff Through V14 (2026-07-12)

Acting only as the independent adversarial Security reviewer, inspected the current uncommitted
text-entry, launch-reveal, standalone AppKit verifier, release-gate/Keychain, observer/Designer,
packaging, pipeline, hook, product-graph, Accessibility and directly affected test bytes against the
approved plan through input plan SHA-256
`abeba65a7cf83ad4909a52c73ffc8135e9d246c37f2c0fb492cbbef43abc6916`. The immutable V14 run-1
failure remains runtime evidence only; it is not treated as code approval.

### Findings

- **[HIGH] `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift:2953-2965`;
  `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift:697-712` ->** The current implementation does not
  execute the exact pair/no-click branch first. `commonModeLaunchSample` begins a lease and then runs
  the full `launchCheapObservation`, including CG owner enumeration, display capture/mapping,
  titlebar derivation and input preflight, before it tests `lease.authority == .expectedPairSurface`.
  The source test named `UsesCheapPairFirst` explicitly asserts that incorrect
  `begin -> cheapCapture -> pairFirst` order, so it machine-enforces the opposite of the binding V13
  Security(plan) contract. Split the lease-only pair/no-click disposition from denial observation,
  finish and close an exact expected pair before any CG/display/titlebar/preflight/phase work, and
  make the source/executable test require `begin -> exact pair branch -> cheap denial observation ->
  optional bounded AX snapshot`. Prove the pair branch performs zero denial observation and zero
  event allocation/posting.

- **[HIGH] `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift:3175-3205`;
  `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift:778-786,839-867` ->** The final 100-millisecond
  guard does not enclose the irreversible action as approved. After the adjacent reserve check, the
  driver mutates the lifecycle/action ledgers, reads `postStart`, and posts both HID events without
  first requiring `postStart` to be finite, at or before the deadline, and to satisfy the retained
  authorization boundary. It discovers a late/nonfinite start only after input has already occurred,
  through the `postEnd` guard. The passing test uses standalone arithmetic and token ordering rather
  than an injected clock/action seam, so it cannot expose the late-start window. Add an immediate
  finite/deadline authorization guard after arm/close and before `postZeroDragPair`; insufficient
  authority must discard the prepared events and post zero input, while only a synchronous overrun
  that begins under valid authority may become sticky `conditional_launch_action_deadline_miss`.
  Exercise nonfinite, 99/100/101-ms, exact-deadline, pre-post stall and one-tick post-overrun cases
  against the executable reducer/action seam with exact post counts and no retry.

- **[HIGH] `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/ReleaseGateTests.swift:568-640`;
  `scripts/security-scan.sh:118-137` ->** The mandatory Revision-II adversarial matrix is still a
  false green. The test called `testActualHooksFinalizeResumeAndInteractiveWrappers...` replaces the
  authoritative receipt command and every security/build/verifier/AppKit command with scripts that
  unconditionally exit zero; it therefore tests wrapper spelling, not actual prepare, observe,
  Designer, finalize, commit recovery or push authority. `security-scan.sh` additionally checks only
  that selected method names exist before running this weakened suite. There is still no real CLI
  fake-log/public-prepare forgery, observer/checklist/helper/evidence substitution, per-transition
  artifact replacement, real temporary-repository reflog recovery, or per-Keychain/evidence/cache
  fault matrix required at plan lines 4627-4645. Invoke the real release-gate binary through injectable
  closed dependencies in isolated repositories/stores, assert every authoritative state and sealed
  artifact, execute every forgery/fault/recovery category, and make the scan validate actual executed
  category results rather than method-name presence. Stubs may supplement failure injection but must
  not stand in for the authority under test.

- **[MEDIUM] `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift:3217-3221`;
  `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift:663-867` ->** The newly approved V14 repair is not
  present in the actual code: the initial `LaunchPresentationSettlement` still passes
  `requiredConsecutiveSamples: 4`, and no phase-sliced one/same-turn/249/250/822-ms plus independent
  final-snapshot fixture exists. Change only this initial threshold to `2`; keep Finder settlement,
  ReadyEpoch and their four-sample contracts unchanged; and add the exact scoped temporal, component-
  mutation, reset, final rehash/drain/commit-before-navigation tests required by V14. This is a
  blocking missing implementation, not a runtime-only condition.

- **[MEDIUM] `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift:197-216,247-254`;
  `scripts/security-scan.sh:118-137`;
  `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/ReleaseGateTests.swift:403-418` ->** A clean checkout cannot execute
  the self-authoritative order as written. `source-security` is gate one, but the security scan runs
  all ReleaseGate tests, and `testRealPackageCodesignRequirementMatchesFreshParserAndSecurityFramework`
  unconditionally captures `.build/out/Products/Release/Pebble`; the clean release build is gate two.
  Thus the source-security gate depends on a pre-existing release artifact and can fail before the
  authority has built one, or validate a stale prior artifact. Make the package/codesign test create
  and bind its own isolated executable fixture, or otherwise remove its dependency on the current
  release product while preserving the fixed seven-gate order. Add a clean-`.build` integration case
  proving `run-prepare-gates` reaches and produces its own warning-free release build without stale
  artifact assistance.

### Read-only evidence and boundary

`swift test --filter
'TextEntrySourceTests|PebbleTextInputTests|PebbleAppSupportTests|ReleaseGateTests|KeychainReceiptStateStoreIntegrationTests'`
reported TextInput 21/21, ReleaseGate/Keychain 25/25, TextEntrySource 24/24 and AppSupport 6/6 passing.
`bash scripts/security-scan.sh` and `git diff --check` passed. Those green results demonstrate the
false-green coverage defects above; they do not close them. `swift package describe --type json`
reports exactly two products, `Pebble` and `pebsmoke`, with `PebbleReleaseGate` remaining a nonproduct
target. The current release, smoke, Storage, Core and TextInput hashes exactly match their verifier
pins (`Pebble` `648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`), all three hooks and
release-gate wrappers are mode `0755`, the router contains no `NSEvent.eventNumber` access, exhausted
`flagsChanged` is nondispatching, equal-key RPG refresh mutates scalar attributes without parent/child
publication, and no ReleaseGate/TextInput/AppSupport/driver LAN surface was found. These reviewed
boundaries pass.

No implementation, runtime action, receipt transition, deployment, commit or push was performed by
Security. The failed V14 runtime set remains immutable and incomplete. Any fix must return through
the earliest affected Architecture/Design/Security(plan) gate; in particular, findings one through
three are outside the narrow V14 count-only Builder authorization and require explicit remediation
review before code changes.

**Security (code) verdict: FAIL.** Exact pair-first ordering and the whole-action deadline are not
implemented, mandatory adversarial coverage remains materially substituted by stubs/token checks,
the approved V14 threshold/tests are absent, and the authoritative clean-start path depends on a
pre-existing release product. Installed Design Sign-off, full Test, fresh cold-five runtime proof,
pipeline/receipt finalization, deployment, commit and push remain blocked.

## V15 Final Security-code Remediation Architecture — executable authority, action deadline and clean start (2026-07-12)

This Architecture reviews the actual failed bytes and the five formal Security findings above through
input plan SHA-256
`3d1ce7a2b41822eafc9eb556a7175d7c4a991025c89e33c9f37a2f668d82dfcc`. It supersedes the
contrary V13/V14 source assertions and the stubbed Revision-II integration test, while retaining every
previously approved product, privacy, evidence, deadline and installed-signoff invariant.

### 1. Close the exact pair before all denial observation

`Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift` must replace the current always-populated
`CommonModeLaunchSample` path with a typed pair-or-denial disposition. Immediately after
`beginLaunchSettlementSample()`, inspect only the immutable lease authority. If it is
`.expectedPairSurface`, call `finishLaunchSettlementSample(lease:)` and close that exact pair before
calling any routine that enumerates CG windows, captures/maps displays, derives a titlebar point,
calls `CGPreflightPostEventAccess`, allocates an event, starts an AX sample, captures a private AX
snapshot or derives a shipping phase. The returned pair disposition may then carry only bounded
minimum authority needed by the no-click branch: the finished lease result plus fresh runtime,
finished-launching, active and exact-frontmost process predicates. It must not manufacture an empty
`LaunchCheapObservation`. A changed pair lease is terminal; an exact unfinished pair may wait; an
exact finished/active/frontmost pair closes the conditional action as `.skippedSystemGranted` and
hands off to the independent launch-presentation settlement. It grants neither navigation nor input.

Only `.zeroEventSurface` may proceed to a separately named denial-observation routine. That routine
performs the current bounded CG/display/titlebar/preflight work, then—only for visible-alpha-one
denial sampling—takes the one capped AX snapshot. Invalid lease authority remains terminal before
either branch. `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift` must reverse the currently contrary
`begin -> cheapCapture -> pairFirst` assertion: slice the complete function and prove
`begin -> pair switch -> finish exact pair -> denial cheap observation -> optional AX snapshot`.
Pair-branch source and executable fixtures must prove zero calls to CG-window/display/titlebar/input-
preflight/AX capture and zero event construction/posting. Denial fixtures must prove exactly one of
each bounded observation at most, and pair-to-denial or denial-to-pair evidence never carries across a
lease boundary.

### 2. Make the last clock read authoritative before HID down

In the same driver, extract the conditional click into one private action executor used by production
and an executable self-test mode. Its closed inputs are: the already prepared two-event action, the
absolute presentation deadline, the current closed conditional-action state, a monotonic `now`
closure and a post/discard actuator. Production constructs it only with
`ProcessInfo.processInfo.systemUptime` and the prepared CGEvent pair; the self-test supplies a scripted
clock and a counting fake actuator. There is no production environment or caller-selected clock,
deadline, action or result.

Event creation remains before the adjacent authority sample so allocation cannot consume the final
posting reserve. Every non-posting exit—including exact-pair arrival, adjacent mismatch, authority
loss, reserve failure, arm/close failure and the new final guard—must explicitly discard the prepared
pair and record zero HID posts. After adjacent revalidation, arm and one-shot close, read `postStart`
once and immediately require it to be finite, no later than the unchanged absolute deadline, and
consistent with the retained armed/closed authorization. This guard is immediately adjacent to and
before the HID down call: no ledger mutation, diagnostic work, second clock read or other fallible
operation may intervene. A nonfinite or late start is pre-action exhaustion: discard, post zero and
fail as sampling/authorization exhaustion, never as an action overrun. Only a finite post begun under
valid authority may post exactly down then up. Its immediately captured `postEnd` must be finite and
at or before the deadline; a synchronous completion one clock tick beyond becomes sticky
`conditional_launch_action_deadline_miss`, with no retry and no second pair.

The driver's closed `--self-test-conditional-denial-action-v1` mode must run before interactive/AppKit
preflight and execute the same executor. `TextEntrySourceTests` must compile the standalone driver and
run this mode, not restate its arithmetic. Required cases are: allocation reserve 99/100/101 ms;
nonfinite allocation/start/end; a prepared pair discarded before posting; adjacent exact-pair and
adjacent denial outcomes; a pre-post stall from an otherwise valid 100-ms reservation to one tick
after the deadline; `postStart` exactly at the deadline; completion exactly at the deadline; and one-
tick post overrun. Every row asserts disposition, prepare/discard count, down/up counts, first/last
action order and one-shot/no-retry behavior. The production command-line path must reject any other
self-test argument and the AppKit gate must continue to invoke the ordinary six-argument mode only.

### 3. Replace the Revision-II false green with the actual authority matrix

Move the receipt command orchestration currently hidden behind globals in
`scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift` into package-internal workflow types in
`Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/ReleaseGate.swift` (or one new file in that same nonproduct target).
`ReleaseGateCommandDispatcher` must own the existing closed command enum and state transitions;
dependencies are narrow protocols for receipt store/coordinator, closed-command execution, durable
evidence writes, repository/Git snapshots, artifact package/install identity, live-process capture,
interactive observer/Designer operations, clock/challenge generation and process identity. The
production CLI constructs exactly one production dependency graph and accepts only the existing
closed commands/arguments. No environment variable, path, manifest, log, digest, status, count, PASS
boolean, alternate executable or dependency selector may choose a production dependency.

Add a compile-time test-only executable at
`Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/Fixtures/ReleaseGateWorkflowProbe/main.swift`, excluded from SwiftPM test
sources as `Fixtures` already is. It calls that exact dispatcher with isolated ephemeral/Keychain-test
stores, private temporary evidence roots, temporary Git repositories, real
`FoundationClosedCommandRunner` helper processes and deterministic injected faults. It is a dependency
adapter, not an authority stub: the dispatcher, coordinator, parser, stable-file validation, durable
writer, state machine, command argument validation and evidence sealing under test are the production
implementations. Test helper programs may create deterministic release/package/process fixtures or
induce one named failure, but may never return a preconstructed transition, receipt, evidence digest
or PASS decision. The production stable tool must be compiled without the test adapter, and
`scripts/run-release-gate-tool.sh` plus a source test must prove no testing define/fixture/dependency
selector enters production.

Replace `testActualHooksFinalizeResumeAndInteractiveWrappersInTemporaryGitRepository` rather than
supplementing its unconditional-zero receipt/security/build/verifier/AppKit scripts. The replacement
must invoke the test-built real dispatcher for `run-prepare-gates`, `observe-interactive`,
`designer-attest`, `finalize`, `precommit`, `postcommit`/`resume-commit` and `prepush`, while copying
and executing the real observer/Designer/finalizer/resume wrappers and all three hooks in an isolated
repository. Closed helper commands provide genuine stdout/stderr/status/version/argv to the real
runner and parser; the test asserts every Keychain state/sequence, private log and
`AutomatedGateEvidence` entry, artifact identity, challenge consumption, evidence digest and Git
transition. A helper that merely exits zero without the exact real parser output must fail.

The mandatory matrix is divided into individually executable categories with cleanup in `defer`:

1. seven fixed prepare gates in exact order, actual helper process/version/argv/status/count/log/hash
   capture and successful prepared publication;
2. nonzero, missing executable, wrong version, wrong argv, wrong count, forged PASS text and timeout;
3. public `prepare`, fake log/digest/status/count/PASS and extra-argument forgery rejection;
4. observer, checklist, wrapper/helper, Designer, one-shot challenge and sealed-evidence substitution;
5. unrelated path, symlink, hard link, rename and byte replacement at initial capture, packaging,
   install, prepare publication and every later transition;
6. live stale process, PID/start tuple reuse, executable/signature/hash/CDHash mismatch and process exit;
7. real temporary-Git precommit/postcommit interruption, exact-parent resume, divergent/amended commit,
   reflog recovery, prepush tuple/replay/different-ref and successful hook ordering;
8. concurrent stale transitions and every Keychain load/add/update/delete malformed, duplicate,
   unavailable/locked, authorization, persistence, before-update and after-update boundary;
9. every `DurableFilePoint` and `ReleaseGatePersistencePoint`, evidence/log/cache swap and deletion,
   before/after fsync and rename recovery, with authoritative state winning and zero residue;
10. production-Keychain isolated identity cross-process visibility, required accessibility class,
    stale coordinator race and identity-only cleanup after every success/failure.

`Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/ReleaseGateTests.swift` owns categories 1–9;
`KeychainReceiptStateStoreIntegrationTests.swift` owns category 10 and the injected real adapter
failure rows. A new `scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh` must first enumerate the exact expected
test names, then run every category separately, capture its XCTest log, require exit zero and a
nonzero executed-test count, and emit one fixed final category count only after all subprocess and
cleanup assertions pass. `scripts/security-scan.sh` must execute this runner. Delete the current
`rg "func $category"` presence loop: method-name presence, skipped/zero-test filters, unconditional-
success helpers and token-order assertions are not closing evidence. Any missing category, skipped
test, zero executed tests, leaked Keychain item/process/temp repo/evidence file, or failed cleanup is
an overall scan failure.

### 4. Apply the approved launch threshold only at the initial call

Change only the `LaunchPresentationSettlement.settle` call beginning at the unique
`let launchSettlement = LaunchPresentationSettlement()` site from
`requiredConsecutiveSamples: 4` to `2`. Preserve `minimumStableDuration: 0.25`, common-mode distinct
turns, exact complete candidate equality and the mandatory later full snapshot, rehash, ledger drains
and atomic `commitLaunchPresentation`. `FinderSurfaceSettlement` and `ReadyEpoch` remain exactly four
samples over their existing 0.25-second intervals; the denial candidate remains its separately
approved two-sample rule.

Replace unscoped `driver.contains("requiredConsecutiveSamples: 4")` checks with function/call-site
slices. Deterministic tests must prove one sample fails; two on the same turn fail; equal distinct-turn
samples spanning 249 ms fail; 250 ms and 822 ms advance only to final-snapshot validation. Independently
mutate AX object, typed mode, rectangle, display, CG owner, workspace lease and target-AX sequence;
resettable churn clears count/time, immutable loss is terminal, and no pre-reset sample carries.
Executable/source order must retain final full snapshot, exact candidate comparison, fresh executable
rehash, workspace/AX drainage and atomic commit before `boundNavigation` or input. The existing
four-sample source tests must slice and assert the later Finder and Ready calls separately.

### 5. Make source-security independent of the release artifact built at gate two

`testRealPackageCodesignRequirementMatchesFreshParserAndSecurityFramework` must stop opening
`.build/out/Products/Release/Pebble`. It must compile a minimal isolated executable fixture into its
private temporary directory, capture that regular single-link executable with `StableFileHasher`, run
the real `scripts/package-app.sh` against it, and compare the emitted designated requirement with both
`CodesignRawCapture` and Security.framework. The fixture exercises package/codesign parsing without
asserting product correctness; production product continuity remains exclusively downstream of the
authoritative clean release-build gate and package/install capture. No source-security test may read,
hash or require `.build/out/Products/Release/Pebble` or `.build/release/Pebble`.

Add a clean-start dispatcher integration row to the category-1 workflow probe. Its isolated fixture
begins with no release product. Gate one runs the real source-security command boundary and asserts
that absence without opening the artifact. Gate two invokes a real warning-free Swift release build
of a minimal temporary package named `Pebble`, and only after its successful parsed result may the
workflow capture the newly created release identity and let gates three through seven observe it.
Assert gate-one evidence has no artifact hash, gate-two and every product-dependent later entry bind
the one newly created digest, and a stale preseeded artifact is removed/rejected rather than accepted.
This proves `run-prepare-gates` reaches and produces its release artifact without recursively invoking
the repository's own full pipeline or depending on a prior build. The production fixed seven-gate
order remains source-security → clean warning-free release build → release surface → binary scan →
AppKit → XCTest → 457/457 pebsmoke.

### Dependency order, risk-to-test map and Conditions for Builder

Builder order is binding: (A) introduce the pair/disposition split and action executor/self-test;
(B) repair pair/deadline tests; (C) apply and test the initial-only 4→2 settlement change; (D) extract
the package-internal dispatcher/dependency seams without changing production command surface;
(E) build the actual workflow probe and adversarial categories; (F) remove the package test's release
dependency and add the clean-start row; (G) replace security-scan presence checks with executed
category enforcement; then (H) run focused compilation/tests and fresh independent Security (code).
No runtime cold set, installed receipt transition, deployment, commit or push occurs before Security
(code) PASS.

- **Premature observation/input:** exact-pair executable counters prove zero denial observation and
  events; every denial branch proves bounded work only after zero-event authority.
- **Late irreversible input:** scripted action seams cover all finite/nonfinite and 99/100/101-ms
  boundaries, pre-post stalls, exact deadline and post overrun with exact down/up/discard counts.
- **False-green authority:** real dispatcher/helper processes, state/evidence assertions, temporary Git,
  substitution, artifact/process mutation, fault/recovery and cleanup categories replace stubs.
- **Polling-count regression:** phase-sliced 1/same-turn/249/250/822-ms tests distinguish initial two
  from later four and preserve independent final authority.
- **Stale artifact bootstrap:** isolated package fixture plus clean-start real Swift build proves gate
  one needs no release and gate two creates the sole accepted digest.
- **Test escape hatch:** production tool/source scans prove no fixture import, compiler testing flag,
  environment selector or caller dependency injection; Fixtures remain excluded from products/tests.
- **Privacy and product scope:** diagnostics stay fixed/capped/redacted; unattended paths retain zero
  clipboard read/write/Paste; only the nonshipping verifier/release-gate test surface changes. Pebble,
  PebbleCore, PebbleStorage, PebbleTextInput, `pebsmoke`, LAN and Sign protocol behavior remain frozen.

Conditions for Builder: preserve the unchanged absolute presentation deadline and 100-ms reservation;
post no event unless the immediate final guard passes; never relabel a pre-post rejection as a
post-overrun; never retry or select a later runtime result; keep initial settlement at exactly two and
later settlements at four; remove every unconditional-success authority stub and source-security
artifact dependency; make every required matrix category actually execute and fail closed on zero
tests or cleanup failure; retain exact `/Applications/Pebble.app/Contents/MacOS/Pebble` production
identity, monotonic Keychain authority, disposable cache, sealed private evidence, distinct Designer
transition and executable hooks. Authorized edits are limited to
`Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`, `Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift`,
`Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/**`, `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/**`,
`scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift`, `scripts/run-release-gate-tool.sh`,
`scripts/security-scan.sh`, the new adversarial runner, directly necessary receipt/observer/Designer/
finalize/resume wrappers and hooks, `Package.swift` only if fixture exclusion requires an exact update,
and this plan. Any shipping source, product graph, release deadline/predicate, Storage/Core/TextInput/
pebsmoke bytes, LAN surface or clipboard behavior change returns to Architecture and Security(plan).

Required closing evidence is: standalone driver compile and executable self-test matrix; focused
`TextEntrySourceTests`, `ReleaseGateTests` and real Keychain integration with reported counts; the new
adversarial runner showing all ten categories executed; `scripts/security-scan.sh`; warning-free clean
release build; full XCTest; 457/457 `pebsmoke`; release-surface and binary verification; unchanged
release executable hash; `git diff --check`; and a fresh independent Security (code) review. Only that
PASS reopens the next distinctly named same-byte cold-five proof and installed human/VoiceOver gates.

**V15 Final Security-code Remediation Architecture verdict: PASS.** Each formal finding now maps to a
concrete implementation boundary and executable closing evidence. Build remains blocked until a
separate Design Review and Security (plan) both PASS this exact Architecture; all downstream evidence
is stale until the authorized implementation is stable.

## Design Review — V15 Final Security-code Remediation (2026-07-12)

Reviewed the exact Architecture above through plan SHA-256
`4854605d9674e89d71b2fe92d2743d6b9e31cad5724f435076a820963d428567`. The remediation changes
nonshipping verification timing, evidence authority and developer workflow; it does not authorize a
Pebble screen, control, focus, text-entry or RPG semantic change. Approval is binding on these
user/developer-visible conditions:

1. The exact-pair branch is visually inert and zero-input. It closes the conditional action before any
   denial observation, event allocation or posting, then waits only for the independently approved
   presentation settlement. It must not flash, refocus, navigate, announce a denial, or imply that an
   exact pair alone is a usable surface. The zero-event branch may perform only the bounded private
   observations already approved for verifier provenance.
2. Deadline failures remain visibly and semantically distinct. A nonfinite or late final pre-post
   guard exits nonzero with the fixed safe reason `conditional_launch_sampling_budget_exhausted` and
   states, in bounded operator copy, that no synthetic click was sent and the verification set stopped.
   A down/up action that returns after the deadline exits nonzero with
   `conditional_launch_action_deadline_miss` and states that input may have occurred, the result is
   invalid, and the set stopped. Neither message may claim product failure, silently retry, recommend
   extending the deadline, or expose raw timestamps, geometry, process/window identity, paths, AX/text,
   clipboard content or hashes. Recovery is an explicitly new authorized same-byte run after the
   prerequisite is corrected; it is never an in-run retry or replacement of immutable failed evidence.
3. The real release-gate dispatcher, wrappers and hooks retain the existing closed commands and normal
   operator sequence. A failed stage reports one stable stage/category and a concise corrective action,
   exits nonzero, preserves the authoritative private evidence, and never prints a false PASS. Test-only
   fixture switches, dependency selectors and temporary-repository paths must not appear in production
   help, prompts or accepted arguments. Interrupted commit/resume messaging must distinguish exact-parent
   recovery from amended/divergent history and must not invite bypassing a hook.
4. A clean checkout is a supported bootstrap state: source-security runs without a release artifact,
   the build stage clearly owns creating the first accepted artifact, and a missing pre-build product is
   not described as corruption or deployment failure. A stale artifact is rejected with a bounded
   rebuild instruction. The fixed seven-stage order and per-stage progress remain legible, while raw
   private log/evidence locations and Keychain material remain undisclosed.
5. The `4` to `2` change applies only to initial launch-presentation sampling and changes verifier
   latency, not visible product readiness. Two equal samples on distinct turns spanning at least 250 ms
   still grant neither navigation nor input; the final full snapshot and later four-sample Finder/Ready
   settlements remain mandatory. No new spinner, delay, activation, focus request or UI state is added.
6. Evidence provenance stays understandable and private: automated, observer and later Designer
   transitions remain separately labeled; no automated or same-session claim may populate a human,
   Finder, Dock, real-click, Paste or VoiceOver field. Diagnostics remain fixed/capped/redacted;
   unattended paths perform zero clipboard reads, writes or Paste actions; cleanup failures are explicit
   failures rather than hidden residue.
7. The installed acceptance contract remains unchanged on the final identical bytes: all twelve text
   fields plus Recipe, local/host Sign, Chat/slash and guest read-only behavior; Standard and High
   Contrast at compact, medium and large sizes; manual user-controlled fixed-sentinel Paste; RPG glyphs
   and semantic accessibility; Finder/Dock launches; real denial recovery click; and granted plus
   denied-then-clicked VoiceOver names, roles, announcements, caret movement, field switching,
   reactivation context and presentation retirement. Executable probes and signed evidence cannot
   substitute for these human checks.

**V15 Final Security-code Remediation Design Review verdict: PASS.** Pair-first closure is safely
zero-input, deadline outcomes have truthful and actionable operator semantics, the real gate workflow
remains closed and usable from a clean checkout, and the initial-only two-sample repair does not weaken
the independent presentation or installed accessibility contract. Any implementation that conflates
the two deadline messages, exposes private evidence, introduces a fixture-facing production control,
or alters installed/manual/VoiceOver behavior returns to Design Review.

## Security (plan) — V15 Final Security-code Remediation (2026-07-12)

Independently reviewed the five formal Security (code) findings, the exact V15 Architecture at
SHA-256 `4854605d9674e89d71b2fe92d2743d6b9e31cad5724f435076a820963d428567`, the current affected
implementation boundaries and the separate binding Design PASS through input plan SHA-256
`2dc830ab6d2fd77e8672625c4b62ecb8069d5e0538960d315ddb5002b73fa341`. This gate approves only
the written remediation. Builder implementation, fresh Security (code), runtime proof, installed
Design Sign-off, Test, Deploy, commit and push remain downstream.

### Findings

None.

The exact-pair disposition is now first by construction. Immediately after the typed workspace lease
begins, only its immutable authority may select the pair branch. An expected pair is finished and
closed before CG-window enumeration, display capture/mapping, titlebar derivation, input preflight,
AX/private capture, event construction or posting. That branch carries no manufactured denial sample
and can only suppress synthetic denial recovery; it grants neither navigation nor usable-surface
success. Only a zero-event lease reaches the separately named bounded denial observation. Pair and
denial evidence cannot cross lease boundaries, and executable counters plus ordered source slices
must prove zero denial work and zero input on every pair disposition.

The irreversible action boundary is implementably closed. Both events are prepared before the final
adjacent authority sample and explicitly discarded on every non-posting outcome. After adjacent
revalidation, arm and one-shot close, one finite monotonic `postStart` read is immediately checked
against the unchanged absolute deadline and retained authorization. No diagnostic, ledger mutation,
second clock read or other operation may intervene between that guard and HID down. A nonfinite or
late start discards the pair and fails with zero posts; only an action begun under valid authority may
post exactly down then up. Its immediate end read determines timely completion versus the sticky
input-may-have-occurred overrun, with no retry or result substitution. The closed executable self-test
uses the same executor as production and covers nonfinite time, 99/100/101-millisecond reserve edges,
pre-post stall, exact-deadline start/end and one-tick overrun with exact prepare/discard/down/up counts.
No production argument or environment can select its clock, deadline, actuator or result.

The false-green release-gate coverage is replaced rather than supplemented. The production
`ReleaseGateCommandDispatcher` owns the closed command parser, coordinator transitions, evidence
sealing and state decisions; the production CLI constructs exactly one production dependency graph.
The excluded workflow probe supplies only narrow stores, real helper processes and deterministic
failure seams to that same dispatcher. It cannot supply a transition, evidence digest, result count or
PASS decision. The replacement integration suite executes the dispatcher, real wrappers and all
three hooks in isolated repositories and asserts authoritative Keychain state, sequence, command
identity/version/argv/status/count/log hashes, artifact and process identity, challenges, sealed
evidence and Git transitions. Unconditional-zero helpers, token-presence checks, public prepare and
caller-provided logs, digests, counts, statuses or PASS values are forbidden.

All ten adversarial categories are executable requirements: fixed seven-gate order; command/parser
failure and forgery; public-surface rejection; observer/Designer/helper/checklist/evidence
substitution; path and artifact replacement at every boundary; stale/reused/mismatched live process;
real temporary-Git interruption, reflog recovery and pre-push replay; stale concurrency and every
Keychain operation failure; every durable-file/persistence/fsync/rename/cache recovery point; and
production-Keychain cross-process/access-class/cleanup behavior. The new runner invokes each exact
category separately, requires a successful XCTest process and nonzero executed-test count, verifies
cleanup, and emits its final fixed count only after all categories pass. `scripts/security-scan.sh`
must execute that runner. Method-name presence, skipped filters or a helper that exits zero without
the exact parser output cannot close any category.

The launch-cadence repair is scoped to the unique initial `LaunchPresentationSettlement` call. It
changes only four candidate samples to two while retaining distinct common-mode turns, the 250-ms
minimum, exact full candidate equality and the independent final snapshot, rehash, workspace/AX
drains and atomic commit before navigation. One sample, same-turn pairs and 249-ms pairs remain
ineligible; 250-ms and 822-ms pairs advance only to final validation. Component churn and reset/
terminal behavior remain fail closed. Function-sliced tests separately retain four samples for
Finder and Ready, so an unscoped literal assertion cannot hide threshold leakage.

Clean bootstrap no longer trusts stale release state. The package/codesign test builds its own private
single-link executable fixture and runs the real package parser/signing surface without reading either
repository release-product path. In the category-one dispatcher fixture, gate one executes while the
release product is absent and cannot publish an artifact hash; gate two performs the first real clean,
warning-free Swift release build and alone creates the accepted digest used by later product-dependent
gates. A preseeded stale artifact is removed or rejected. The production seven-gate order is unchanged,
and the test may not recurse into the repository pipeline or substitute an old product.

TOCTOU, privacy and scope remain bounded. Every initial and later artifact transition exercises stable
no-follow regular/single-link identity, exact canonical release/package/install paths, exact
`/Applications/Pebble.app/Contents/MacOS/Pebble`, signature/requirement/hash/CDHash continuity and
live PID/start/executable identity. Keychain state remains monotonic authority; cache is disposable;
private logs and evidence remain sealed; observer and later Designer are distinct one-shot
transitions. Diagnostics stay fixed, capped and redacted, unattended paths have zero clipboard/Paste
access, and the binding Design deadline messages distinguish zero-input exhaustion from possible
post-overrun input without exposing paths, hashes, identities, geometry, AX/text or clipboard data.

Builder scope is limited exactly as V15 states. No shipping source, product graph, release deadline or
predicate, Storage/Core/TextInput/`pebsmoke` byte, LAN/Sign protocol or installed/manual behavior may
change. Production fixture selectors, testing compiler flags, dependency injection controls or test
paths are prohibited. Existing tests are replaced only where they machine-enforce a contrary order or
false green; every semantic contract receives stronger executable coverage. Any scope/hash drift,
weakened count/cleanup enforcement, retry, alternate evidence selection or production escape hatch
returns to Architecture and Security (plan).

**Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** Pair-first ordering, the immediate finite/deadline pre-HID guard,
the real dispatcher and ten-category adversarial execution, initial-only two-sample settlement and
release-independent clean bootstrap close all five Security (code) findings without privacy, TOCTOU,
test-strength, product-scope, hash or LAN regression. Build is authorized only in the stated order and
scope; fresh independent Security (code) PASS remains mandatory before any runtime, installed,
deployment, commit or push action.

## Build — V15 Final Security-code Remediation evidence (2026-07-12)

Builder implemented the binding V15 A-through-G order without changing shipping Pebble, PebbleCore,
PebbleStorage, PebbleTextInput, AppSupport, `pebsmoke`, LAN, Sign or clipboard source. The exact-pair
lease now closes before denial CG/display/titlebar/preflight/AX work; the real conditional-action
executor owns the finite immediate pre-HID deadline guard and distinguishes zero-input exhaustion
from possible-input post overrun. Initial launch presentation alone uses two distinct-turn samples;
Finder and Ready remain four. The stable release-gate CLI now delegates closed parsing and Git/state
transitions to `ReleaseGateCommandDispatcher`; the excluded workflow probe executes that dispatcher
through real temporary repositories, wrappers and hooks. Source-security package/codesign proof uses
an isolated executable, and the security scan executes ten separate adversarial categories with
nonzero XCTest counts instead of method-name presence.

Empirical closing evidence, all exit zero:

- Standalone optimized-driver compile succeeded; its production executor self-test reported
  `Conditional denial action self-test: PASS cases=10`. Focused `TextEntrySourceTests` executed
  25 tests with zero failures.
- `scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh` executed categories 1 through 10 separately, one XCTest
  in every category, and reported
  `Release-gate adversarial matrix: PASS categories=10 cleanup=verified`.
- `bash scripts/security-scan.sh` passed its source scans, executable adapter tests, all ten release-
  gate categories and the SQLite boundary scan of 127 production Swift files.
- Two independent clean `swift package clean && swift build -c release` runs were warning-free
  (`120.77` and `120.20` seconds). The second build was byte-identical to the first for both the
  release executable and AppSupport object.
- Final full `swift test` executed PebbleTextInput 21, PebbleReleaseGate 27, PebbleCore 1003 and
  PebbleAppSupport 6 tests: 1057 total, zero failures and zero unexpected failures.
- Final post-rebuild `swift run -c release pebsmoke` reported `457 passed, 0 failed`.
- Final post-rebuild `scripts/verify-pebble-storage-release-surface.sh` reported
  `Pebble storage release surface verified.`; `scripts/security-check-binary.sh
  .build/out/Products/Release/Pebble` reported `binary: passed`.
- Exact final release hashes are Pebble
  `648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`, `pebsmoke`
  `d989c030656377ea1dca57b563408e2f6ae739e391904bde8be25f4f1d6dde2f`, PebbleStorage.o
  `ed37590e383037968b25905cb7ecd1d29e8faa43ba1f62a4919baebf9aabc6ba`, PebbleCore.o
  `39e37366bcba82ba65131a1112ad0b262f562279e363cf621542a804bd3ae028`, PebbleTextInput.o
  `547f0c44a950be0b7287ab73b918b6a3f486502e7ba57d7897e2d751b4d040ac` and PebbleAppSupport.o
  `12d4b146ea24da2457066590f0b2c54e331fa098f8f82f54849856e838b07383`.
  The older `f5eb6657`/`57d91481` pair is superseded historical evidence; the binding Activation
  Reveal Final Pin baseline is `648af840`/`12d4b146`, which this build retains exactly.
- `swift package describe --type json` exposes only `Pebble` and `pebsmoke` products. The forbidden
  Sign-specific LAN symbol scan returned zero matches. `git diff --check` passed.

No runtime cold set, installed receipt transition, deployment, commit or push occurred. Those remain
blocked pending a fresh independent Security (code) PASS on these exact bytes.

**V15 Builder verdict: PASS.** The approved remediation is implemented and every required Builder
gate is green. Independent Security (code), runtime cold-five, installed human/VoiceOver sign-off,
deployment and source-control publication remain downstream.

## Security (code) — V15 Final Remediation Actual Diff (2026-07-12)

Acting only as the fresh independent adversarial Security reviewer, inspected the actual V15 driver,
source tests, release-gate dispatcher, production CLI adapter, excluded workflow probe, Keychain and
release-gate tests, adversarial runner, security scan, package graph and current release artifacts on
disk against the V15 Architecture, Design PASS and Security(plan) PASS through input plan SHA-256
`0630debd2c44250bd2e4e62478ea0fd07344a4acbe04fbff8eb14b02f066e573`.

### Findings

- **[HIGH] `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/ReleaseGate.swift:1036-1046,1072-1077`;
  `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/Fixtures/ReleaseGateWorkflowProbe/main.swift:36-57,103-113` ->** The
  production dispatcher does not own all state transitions as the binding V15 contract requires, and
  the test adapter remains able to manufacture authoritative success. `observeInteractive` and
  `designerAttest` are dependency methods with no typed validated result; the dispatcher delegates to
  them and performs no transition itself. The probe implementations directly transition
  `prepared -> observedPendingDesigner -> observed`, while its special bootstrap path creates a
  caller-authored payload and directly advances it through `finalized`. Its prepare dependency always
  throws, so the supposedly real workflow probe cannot execute `run-prepare-gates` at all. Move the
  observer and Designer state decisions back into `ReleaseGateCommandDispatcher`: dependencies may
  perform the real bounded observation/attestation and return closed, revalidated evidence, but only
  the dispatcher may consume challenges and perform coordinator transitions. Remove direct
  `gate.create`/`gate.transition` and `fixture-bootstrap-finalized` from the probe, make its prepare
  adapter run the real dispatcher-owned prepare publication, and prove that no dependency can return
  a preconstructed transition, payload, digest or PASS decision.

- **[HIGH] `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/ReleaseGateTests.swift:231-317,551-600,652-703,705-815`;
  `scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh:7-17,26-50` ->** The ten-category matrix still reports a
  false green because its named tests do not execute the mandatory category semantics. Category 1
  manually loops a `FoundationClosedCommandRunner` over a minimal package and helper-generated PASS
  text; it never invokes `ReleaseGateCommandDispatcher`, creates/publishes a prepared receipt, runs the
  production seven-gate specification or tests production `run-prepare-gates`. Category 4 directly
  calls coordinator transitions and fabricates `AutomatedGateEvidence`; category 5 checks one generic
  file capture rather than mutation at packaging/install/prepare/every later transition; category 9
  exercises isolated write points but not evidence/log/cache swap and deletion throughout the
  workflow. Most decisively, category 7 first bootstraps `finalized`, then replaces the receipt path,
  security scan, release verifier, binary scan, AppKit verifier and Swift build/test/smoke commands
  with unconditional-success/fake scripts before testing only the later commit/push path. It does not
  execute prepare, observer, Designer or finalize through the real dispatcher and wrappers, nor the
  required divergent/amended/reflog recovery matrix. The runner proves only that one XCTest method per
  category exited zero; it cannot detect that required rows are absent, and category 10 omits the two
  separately named production-adapter/stale-coordinator tests. Replace the category bodies with the
  exact production-dispatcher workflow required by V15, using helpers only for closed process output
  and one named fault; exercise and assert every listed substitution, transition, Git recovery,
  Keychain, persistence and cleanup row. Make each category emit a machine-checked row inventory (or
  split it into exact separately invoked tests), and make the runner require every row, including the
  production adapter and stale-coordinator category-10 rows, before printing
  `categories=10 cleanup=verified`.

- **[MEDIUM] `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/ReleaseGateTests.swift:231-317`;
  `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/Fixtures/ReleaseGateWorkflowProbe/main.swift:36-38` ->** The isolated
  codesign fixture correctly removes the old direct dependency on the repository release product, but
  the required clean-start integration evidence is still absent. The clean-start test builds a
  separate minimal package through a hand-written loop, while the only workflow probe's
  `runAuthoritativePrepare` implementation always fails. Therefore no test proves that the actual
  `run-prepare-gates` command begins without a release artifact, executes source-security first,
  permits only release-build to create the accepted artifact digest, and publishes prepared authority
  without stale assistance. Add a clean temporary-repository dispatcher test that starts with no
  release product (and separately with a stale decoy), invokes the real `run-prepare-gates` command,
  asserts the fixed seven-stage order and source-security's nil artifact identity, verifies gate two
  creates the sole accepted release digest, and validates the resulting prepared receipt/evidence.

### Closed prior findings and reviewed boundary

The other three V14/V15 blockers are closed in the actual driver. `commonModeLaunchDisposition`
begins the workspace lease and completes the expected-pair branch before
`launchCheapObservation`, CG/display/titlebar/input-preflight work, AX capture or event preparation.
The shared production/self-test `ConditionalDenialPreparedAction.execute` reads one finite
`postStart`, checks retained authorization and the unchanged absolute deadline, then calls HID down
immediately; pre-action rejection discards with zero posts, while only a validly begun synchronous
overrun becomes `conditional_launch_action_deadline_miss`. The initial launch settlement alone passes
`requiredConsecutiveSamples: 2`; Finder and Ready remain four, with independent final snapshot,
rehash, drain and commit ordering retained. No retry, alternate result, clipboard/Paste, raw private
diagnostic or production clock/deadline selector was found in those paths.

Read-only focused evidence passed but does not cure the coverage findings: `swift test --filter
'TextEntrySourceTests|ReleaseGateTests|KeychainReceiptStateStoreIntegrationTests'` executed
TextEntrySource 25/25 and ReleaseGate/Keychain 27/27 with zero failures; the standalone driver test
reported its ten-case conditional-action PASS. `scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh` reported ten
one-test category subprocesses and final PASS, which is precisely the semantically incomplete result
described above. `git diff --check` passed. `swift package describe --type json` exposes only `Pebble`
and `pebsmoke`. Current hashes match the binding pins: Pebble
`648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`, pebsmoke
`d989c030656377ea1dca57b563408e2f6ae739e391904bde8be25f4f1d6dde2f`, PebbleStorage.o
`ed37590e383037968b25905cb7ecd1d29e8faa43ba1f62a4919baebf9aabc6ba`, PebbleCore.o
`39e37366bcba82ba65131a1112ad0b262f562279e363cf621542a804bd3ae028`, PebbleTextInput.o
`547f0c44a950be0b7287ab73b918b6a3f486502e7ba57d7897e2d751b4d040ac` and PebbleAppSupport.o
`12d4b146ea24da2457066590f0b2c54e331fa098f8f82f54849856e838b07383`. No ReleaseGate, AppSupport
or standalone-driver LAN surface was found; generic bounded Sign text support in PebbleTextInput is
not a LAN protocol addition.

Reviewed: the five prior formal findings; pair/denial ordering; one-shot action timing and outcomes;
initial/Finder/Ready thresholds; dispatcher/parser and production dependency construction; probe and
all ten named adversarial categories; Keychain adapter tests; stable-file/durable-writer boundaries;
production fixture escape hatches; privacy diagnostics; package products; current pinned hashes; LAN
symbol scope; and diff whitespace. Not reviewed or executed: AppKit runtime input, installed human or
VoiceOver state, receipt mutation on the real production Keychain, deployment, commit or push. Full
XCTest, smoke, release-surface and binary results were read from Builder evidence rather than rerun by
Security; the independent focused commands above are this gate's direct execution evidence.

**Security (code) verdict: FAIL.** Pair-first input suppression, the immediate pre-HID guard and the
initial-only two-sample change are implemented, and the stale repository-release dependency was
removed from the package/codesign unit test. However, the real dispatcher still delegates observer/
Designer transition authority to its dependencies, the probe directly manufactures finalized
authority and cannot run prepare, and the advertised ten-category/clean-start workflow remains
materially substituted by manual coordinator calls and unconditional-success helpers. Runtime
cold-five, installed Design Sign-off, Test approval, pipeline/receipt finalization, deployment, commit
and push remain blocked until the exact fixes above pass a fresh independent Security (code) rerun.

## V16 Release-gate Authority and Executable-matrix Remediation Architecture (2026-07-12)

This Architecture responds only to the three formal findings in the immediately preceding Security
(code) FAIL, read at input plan SHA-256
`65d284a41621a1448a01ebff44e7b5941f9c35182d37bad7090a7cc7dbc38e28`. It does not reopen the
closed pair-first, pre-HID deadline or initial-settlement work. The shipping product, storage,
text-input and LAN surfaces are frozen at these current release hashes: Pebble
`648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`, pebsmoke
`d989c030656377ea1dca57b563408e2f6ae739e391904bde8be25f4f1d6dde2f`, PebbleStorage.o
`ed37590e383037968b25905cb7ecd1d29e8faa43ba1f62a4919baebf9aabc6ba`, PebbleCore.o
`39e37366bcba82ba65131a1112ad0b262f562279e363cf621542a804bd3ae028`, PebbleTextInput.o
`547f0c44a950be0b7287ab73b918b6a3f486502e7ba57d7897e2d751b4d040ac` and PebbleAppSupport.o
`12d4b146ea24da2457066590f0b2c54e331fa098f8f82f54849856e838b07383`. The SwiftPM product graph
remains only `Pebble` and `pebsmoke`.

### 1. Put every receipt transition behind the dispatcher

In `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/ReleaseGate.swift`, replace the authority-bearing methods on
`ReleaseGateCommandDependencies`. Remove `runAuthoritativePrepare(_:)`, `observeInteractive()` and
`designerAttest()`. No dependency receives a `ReleaseGateCoordinator`, `ReceiptStateStore`, current
payload or transition closure. The replacement seams expose only operations, never conclusions:

- a fixed `ReleaseGateWorkflowContext` containing canonical repository, private evidence, release,
  staged bundle and installed paths, bundle id and expiry duration;
- a `ClosedWorkflowOperating` boundary that executes a dispatcher-supplied `ClosedCommandSpec`,
  launches the fixed installed app and returns raw status/stdout/stderr, executable identity,
  package-manifest bytes, code-sign channels or kernel process facts;
- a clock and 32-byte challenge source returning time or random bytes, not expiry/challenge strings;
- interactive functions that display a dispatcher-supplied prompt and return the raw line, capture
  the fourteen installed records, or present sealed records to the Designer and return only the
  reviewer PID/start/timestamp. They cannot return a verdict, digest, payload, state or mutation;
- the existing read-only index/worktree/head/parent facts used by commit and push transitions.

`ReleaseGateCommandDispatcher` constructs challenges and the complete `.preparing` payload, calls
`restart(... initialState: .preparing)`, and owns the only transition to `.prepared`. A concrete
package-internal `ReleaseGatePreparationWorkflow` holds the immutable seven-command specification
and is the only constructor of a package-private `ValidatedPreparedEvidence`. It runs raw operations,
parses every channel with the production parsers, performs stable-file and package/install/signature/
live-process revalidation, durably writes and reopens the evidence, and returns sealed facts only to
the dispatcher. The raw dependency cannot instantiate that type. The dispatcher rereads the exact
`.preparing` sequence, revalidates every captured identity, then applies its fields in the single
`.preparing -> .prepared` mutation. Any error or changed sequence invokes dispatcher-owned
invalidation; a dependency never calls `create`, `restart`, `transition` or `invalidate`.

For `.observeInteractive`, the dispatcher first reads and validates the exact `.prepared` payload.
It asks for the raw `OBSERVE-<challenge>` line and compares it itself before capture begins. The
capture operation may write only beneath the dispatcher-selected private receipt directory and
returns process facts, not authority. A package-internal `InstalledObservationValidator` opens the
fixed fourteen-file inventory fd-relatively/no-follow, validates schemas/counts/image and AX records,
rehashes it, revalidates the installed process and current sequence, and privately constructs
`ValidatedObservationEvidence`. Only then does the dispatcher transition
`.prepared -> .observedPendingDesigner`, set the validated evidence digest and process identity, and
clear the observation challenge. Wrong, missing or replayed input produces no capture transition.

For `.designerAttest`, the dispatcher validates `.observedPendingDesigner`, exact sealed observation
evidence and a different reviewer PID/start. The dependency displays the evidence and returns the raw
line. The dispatcher alone interprets `FAIL` as invalidation or exact `DESIGN-<challenge>` as consent;
all other input fails without transition. The dispatcher itself creates the fixed
`PebbleDesignerAttestationV2` record with `DurablePrivateFileWriter`, reopens and validates it,
recomputes the evidence digest, consumes the one-shot challenge and transitions
`.observedPendingDesigner -> .observed`. `finalize`, `precommit`, `postcommit`/`resume-commit` and
`prepush` retain their current dispatcher-owned transitions. A source test must establish exactly
seven dispatcher transition call sites and zero coordinator mutation references in the production
dependency adapter, observation/Designer helpers and workflow probe.

`scripts/observe-installed-signoff.swift` and
`scripts/designer-attest-installed-signoff.swift` become operation adapters only: capture/presentation
and raw-input functions move no state. `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift` constructs one fixed
production dependency graph and supplies no environment-, argument-, manifest- or file-selected
implementation. `scripts/run-release-gate-tool.sh` continues compiling only that production graph,
with no testing define, fixture import or alternate executable selector.

### 2. Make the excluded workflow probe execute real `run-prepare-gates`

Replace `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/Fixtures/ReleaseGateWorkflowProbe/main.swift` completely as an
adapter to the same dispatcher. Delete `fixturePayload`, `fixture-bootstrap-finalized`, all direct
coordinator access and all state construction. Its outer syntax is a closed test-only scenario id,
an absolute private temporary-repository path and one production `ReleaseGateCommand`; scenario ids
are an exhaustive enum compiled into the excluded executable. They select one named raw-operation or
persistence fault only. They cannot supply paths inside the workflow, statuses, counts, output,
digests, payload fields, transitions or PASS values.

The probe's success fixture is a real temporary Git repository and minimal Swift package with
executable products `Pebble` and `pebsmoke` plus one XCTest. The package starts with no canonical
release executable. Its copied closed helper scripts are conditional validators: each verifies exact
argv/version, prior-stage ledger, canonical artifact identity and expected digest before emitting the
real parser grammar; a wrong call exits nonzero. Gate two remains the actual fixed
`swift package clean && swift build -c release`, not a fabricated build. The source-security helper
requires the canonical release path to be absent. Gates three through seven require the one identity
captured only after gate two. Packaging/install helpers create real regular single-link executable
fixtures and a parser-valid manifest, while the production stable-file, manifest, codesign-channel
and live-process validators decide acceptance. Helpers never return `ReleaseGatePayload`,
`AutomatedGateEvidence`, a transition, digest or Boolean success decision.

`ReleaseGateTests` must run the probe once from a clean start and once with a stale executable at the
canonical release path. Both invoke `ReleaseGateCommandDispatcher.dispatch(["run-prepare-gates"])`.
The stale row proves gate-one rejects or dispatcher-controlled cleaning removes the decoy before
source-security; no helper may simply ignore it. Assert an exact seven-entry order; nil artifact on
source-security; the newly built digest on release-build and every later entry; exact executable/
version/argv/status/count/log/hash facts; a sealed `AutomatedGateEvidenceV1`; installed/package/live
identity; and an authoritative `.prepared` receipt whose sequence, content, challenges and evidence
are dispatcher-produced. Wrong helper output or a missing stage must leave no `.prepared` authority.

### 3. Replace category labels with an exact executable row inventory

Add `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/Fixtures/ReleaseGateAdversarialRowsV16.swift` and the matching
checked-in text manifest
`Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/Fixtures/release-gate-adversarial-rows-v16.txt`. Both expand to the same
sorted 148 unique row ids below. `ReleaseGateTests` owns categories 1-9 and
`KeychainReceiptStateStoreIntegrationTests` owns category 10. A test-only `AdversarialRowLedger`
records a row only after its assertion helper observes the named real effect or expected rejection;
duplicate, unknown and unexecuted rows fail. Each category emits `ROW_PASS <id>` exactly once and a
final `CATEGORY_PASS <n> rows=<count> cleanup=verified` only after process, temporary repository,
private evidence and isolated Keychain cleanup is checked.

The exact inventory is:

1. `c01.clean-start`, `c01.stale-decoy`, `c01.fixed-seven-order`,
   `c01.source-security-nil-artifact`, `c01.release-build-sole-digest`,
   `c01.product-gates-bind-digest`, `c01.raw-process-facts`, `c01.prepared-publication` (8).
2. `c02.nonzero`, `c02.missing-executable`, `c02.wrong-version`, `c02.wrong-argv`,
   `c02.wrong-count`, `c02.forged-pass`, `c02.timeout` (7).
3. `c03.public-prepare`, `c03.fake-log`, `c03.fake-digest`, `c03.fake-status`,
   `c03.fake-count`, `c03.fake-pass`, `c03.extra-argument` (7).
4. `c04.observer-wrapper`, `c04.observer-helper`, `c04.checklist`,
   `c04.designer-wrapper`, `c04.designer-helper`, `c04.observation-challenge-replay`,
   `c04.designer-challenge-replay`, `c04.sealed-evidence-substitution`,
   `c04.dependency-authority-escape` (9).
5. The exact Cartesian product `c05.<boundary>.<mutation>` (50), where boundary is
   `initial-capture`, `packaging`, `install`, `prepare-publication`, `observe-transition`,
   `designer-transition`, `finalize-transition`, `precommit-transition`,
   `postcommit-resume-transition` or `prepush-transition`, and mutation is `unrelated-path`,
   `symlink`, `hardlink`, `rename` or `byte-replacement`.
6. `c06.stale-live-process`, `c06.pid-start-reuse`, `c06.executable-mismatch`,
   `c06.signature-requirement-mismatch`, `c06.hash-mismatch`, `c06.cdhash-mismatch`,
   `c06.process-exit` (7).
7. `c07.full-wrapper-order`, `c07.precommit-exact-index`,
   `c07.postcommit-interruption`, `c07.exact-parent-resume`, `c07.divergent-commit`,
   `c07.amended-commit`, `c07.reflog-recovery`, `c07.prepush-exact-tuple`,
   `c07.prepush-replay`, `c07.prepush-different-ref`, `c07.hook-order` (11).
8. `c08.concurrent-stale-transition`; `c08.load.<fault>` for `malformed`, `unavailable`,
   `locked`, `authorization`, `persistence`; `c08.add.<fault>` for `duplicate`, `unavailable`,
   `locked`, `authorization`, `persistence`; `c08.update.<fault>` for `malformed-current`,
   `stale-sequence`, `unavailable`, `locked`, `authorization`, `persistence`, `before-update`,
   `after-update`; and `c08.delete.<fault>` for `unavailable`, `locked`, `authorization`,
   `persistence` (23).
9. `c09.durable.<point>` for every current `DurableFilePoint`: `beforeWrite`, `afterWrite`,
   `beforeFsync`, `afterFsync`, `beforeRename`, `afterRename`; `c09.cache.<point>` for every current
   `ReleaseGatePersistencePoint`: `beforeCacheWrite`, `afterCacheWrite`, `beforeCacheFsync`,
   `afterCacheFsync`, `beforeCacheRename`, `afterCacheRename`; `c09.<object>.<mutation>` for object
   `evidence`, `log`, `cache` and mutation `swap`, `delete`; plus
   `c09.authoritative-state-wins` and `c09.zero-residue` (20).
10. `c10.cross-process-visibility`, `c10.accessibility-class`,
    `c10.production-adapter-failures`, `c10.stale-coordinator-race`,
    `c10.identity-cleanup-success`, `c10.identity-cleanup-failure` (6).

Update `scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh` to derive neither names nor counts from Swift
source. It reads the fixed manifest, validates its mode/hash, rejects duplicates and requires 148
rows and category totals `8,7,7,9,50,7,11,23,20,6`. It executes each of the ten exact XCTest category
methods separately, requires nonzero XCTest execution with zero skip/failure, parses only the capped
`ROW_PASS`/`CATEGORY_PASS` grammar, and compares the observed set byte-for-byte with the manifest.
Missing, extra, duplicate, out-of-category or reordered rows, a missing category-10 production-
adapter/stale-coordinator row, cleanup failure or residue blocks the final fixed line
`Release-gate adversarial matrix: PASS categories=10 rows=148 cleanup=verified`.
`scripts/security-scan.sh` executes the runner and independently checks that the dispatcher is the
sole coordinator-transition owner, the probe contains no bootstrap/direct transition, the wrappers
and hooks remain executable, and the production tool contains no fixture/scenario selector.

### 4. Real temporary Git, wrappers, hooks, faults and recovery

Category 7 creates private temporary repositories with a real baseline commit, local Git identity,
`core.hooksPath=.githooks` and real reflogs. Copy the repository's observer, Designer, finalizer and
resume wrappers plus all three hooks byte-for-byte, then assert source/destination hashes before each
use. A fixed fixture leaf named `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.sh` may only `exec` the already
compiled workflow probe with the current temporary root and unchanged command arguments; its source
is pinned and it owns no receipt, evidence or success logic. Interactive wrappers run under a private
pseudo-terminal so their real TTY gates and exact one-shot challenge input execute. Closed fixture
commands must verify the expected state/artifact/argv/order before returning parser-valid output;
unconditional `exit 0`, fabricated prefinalized receipts and replacement coordinator calls are
forbidden.

Drive the real sequence: dispatcher `run-prepare-gates`; real observer wrapper; real Designer wrapper;
real finalizer; real `git commit` through pre-commit/post-commit; real resume wrapper after a named
post-commit interruption; and real pre-push hook with exact stdin tuple. Record the actual commit ids,
parents, index/worktree equality, `git reflog --format=%H`, hook ledger and authoritative sequences.
Use independent fresh temporary repositories to prove exact-parent resume, divergent sibling and
amended-commit rejection, reflog-based non-destructive recovery, exact tuple/idempotent replay and
different-ref rejection. The pre-push fixture stages are conditional adapters, not success stubs:
security validates the sealed receipt, release build invokes real SwiftPM, and surface/binary/AppKit/
XCTest/smoke adapters require exact argv, artifact identity and production parser output before they
record their position. The second receipt check must occur after those stages. Hook order is compared
to the fixed production order.

Fault injection is limited to the excluded probe's closed scenario enum and package test initializers:
one raw process/channel fault, file mutation, store status, durability point, process fact, Git
operation or interruption per row. Production has no fault selector. Every fixture holds directory
descriptors, rejects links/nonprivate modes/out-of-root canonical paths, caps captured channels and
row logs, uses deterministic nonsecret values, and never accesses the general pasteboard or records
field text, user names, home paths, challenges or raw Keychain bytes. Mutation is injected between
capture and the immediately following revalidation to exercise the TOCTOU boundary. Cleanup runs in
`defer`, terminates/reaps child processes, deletes only the exact isolated Keychain identity, removes
temporary repositories/evidence and verifies absence; it never deletes production authority.

### Dependency order, risk-to-test map and Conditions for Builder

Builder order is binding: (A) define the raw-operation seams and package-private validated result
types; (B) move preparation creation/publication and invalidation into the dispatcher; (C) split
observer/Designer operations from challenge interpretation, evidence sealing and transitions; (D)
remove all mutation/bootstrap capability from the probe and implement real clean/stale prepare; (E)
replace categories 1-10 with the exact row inventory and real Git/PTY/wrapper/hook workflow; (F)
enforce the manifest in the runner and security scan; (G) run focused/full closing gates. A material
change returns to Architecture and Security(plan).

- **Forged state authority:** source and executable rows prove dependencies/probe expose no payload,
  digest, verdict, coordinator or transition and that all seven legal transitions occur in dispatcher.
- **False clean-start green:** two real dispatcher runs prove gate one sees no accepted artifact, gate
  two alone builds it and `.prepared` publication binds the sole new digest.
- **Missing adversarial semantics:** the 148-row manifest, exact per-category totals and runner set
