  equality fail closed on an omitted, duplicated, skipped or merely renamed row.
- **Git recovery/replay:** real commits, parents, reflog, wrapper/hook ledgers and authoritative
  sequences cover interruption, resume, divergence, amend and prepush tuple/replay behavior.
- **Substitution/TOCTOU:** category-5 Cartesian rows mutate each protected boundary and category 9
  exercises every named durability point, with authoritative state winning and zero residue.
- **Privacy/cleanup:** capped private evidence, no clipboard/text capture, exact isolated Keychain
  identity and post-row process/path absence prove tests do not widen production privacy or cleanup.
- **Product/LAN scope:** pre/post six hashes, product graph, zero ReleaseGate/AppSupport/driver LAN
  symbols and source diff prove no shipping, storage, text-input, pebsmoke or LAN byte moved.

Conditions for Builder: preserve the production CLI command grammar and paths; dispatcher alone owns
every state creation/transition/invalidation and challenge decision; dependencies return only raw
channels, user lines or factual process/reviewer observations; the probe may select one compiled fault
but never caller evidence; helpers must conditionally validate exact predecessor state/argv/artifact;
real clean and stale `run-prepare-gates` both execute the fixed seven stages; all 148 rows execute and
cleanup; production Keychain identity is never used by categories 1-9 and category 10 deletes only its
random isolated identity; no shipping source, release deadline, AppKit driver, Core, Storage,
TextInput, AppSupport, pebsmoke, RPG or LAN behavior changes.

Authorized edits are limited to `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/**`,
`Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/**`, `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift`,
`scripts/observe-installed-signoff.swift`, `scripts/designer-attest-installed-signoff.swift`,
`scripts/run-release-gate-tool.sh`, `scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh`,
`scripts/security-scan.sh`, the receipt/observer/Designer/finalize/resume wrappers and three hooks only
where required to preserve the closed command path, `Package.swift` only for fixture exclusion, and
this plan. The shipping-hash files, `Tests/PebbleAppKitIntegration/Driver.swift`,
`Tests/PebbleCoreTests/TextEntrySourceTests.swift`, pipeline/deploy code and all LAN files are frozen.

Required closing evidence: source tests for sole dispatcher authority and production fixture escape;
probe compile; clean and stale real `run-prepare-gates`; focused `ReleaseGateTests` and
`KeychainReceiptStateStoreIntegrationTests` with exact counts; adversarial runner
`categories=10 rows=148`; `scripts/security-scan.sh`; warning-free clean release build; full XCTest;
457/457 `pebsmoke`; release-surface and binary scans; identical six frozen hashes/product graph and
zero-LAN scan; `git diff --check`; and fresh independent Security (code). Only a Security(code) PASS
reopens same-byte runtime, installed Design sign-off, Test, pipeline/deploy, commit and push.

**V16 Release-gate Authority and Executable-matrix Remediation Architecture verdict: PASS.** The
three open findings now have closed authority ownership, real clean/stale dispatcher execution and a
machine-enforced semantic inventory. Build remains blocked until separate Design Review and
Security(plan) PASS this exact Architecture.

## Design Review/Revision — V16 Release-gate Authority and Executable Matrix (2026-07-12)

Reviewed the exact V16 Architecture above at input plan SHA-256
`c41cf272e3f657fcb9b95933fbf0f0a223c34352691e9242caa2044f43202c8b`, the prior two-phase,
Revision-II, privacy/copy and V15 operator contracts, and the actual current dispatcher, production
CLI, pipeline, observer, Designer, finalizer, resume, adversarial-runner and hook-facing messages.
This is not Design N/A: V16 changes a developer-facing interactive release workflow even though it
changes no Pebble game UI. The Architecture preserves that workflow only with the following binding,
checkable acceptance criteria.

1. **Clean bootstrap and fixed progress.** The normal entry remains exactly
   `scripts/pipeline.sh --prepare-installed-signoff`. A clean checkout with no release executable
   starts successfully and tells the operator, without treating absence as corruption, that the build
   stage will create the first release artifact. A stale generated release output may be cleared only
   by the dispatcher-owned clean preparation; this is described as generated-output cleanup, never as
   loss, deployment failure or a request to delete source manually. Normal preparation displays these
   seven human-readable stages in this exact order and numbering: `1/7 Source security`,
   `2/7 Release build`, `3/7 Release surface`, `4/7 Binary scan`, `5/7 AppKit text entry`,
   `6/7 XCTest`, `7/7 pebsmoke`. Each stage has one bounded running indication and one truthful passed
   indication; no later stage appears after a failure and no pre-build artifact is implied before
   stage 2. Tests must capture clean and stale terminal transcripts and compare their ordered stage
   labels, count and terminal state.
2. **Truthful failure and recovery.** A preparation failure prints one stable public reason category,
   the failed `n/7` stage, `No prepared authority was published`, and the exact safe next command after
   its prerequisite is corrected. It never dumps a child command, raw stdout/stderr or an underlying
   error interpolation. Cancellation, expiry, changed evidence/artifact, stale authority, observer
   rejection and Designer rejection state whether the current attempt is still resumable or was
   invalidated. An invalidated attempt directs a fresh
   `scripts/pipeline.sh --prepare-installed-signoff`; only an interrupted exact-parent commit may name
   `scripts/resume-installed-signoff-commit.sh`. No message suggests an in-run retry, deadline
   extension, `--no-verify`, reset, amend, dummy commit, hook bypass or reuse of a passed item from an
   invalid attempt. Executable transcript tests cover every public failure category and assert
   nonzero exit, no false `PASS`/`COMPLETE`/deployment claim, and the one permitted recovery command.
3. **Private implementation stays private.** Normal `preflight`, preparation, status, observer,
   Designer, finalizer, commit/resume and pre-push output contains no repository, temporary,
   installed-app, evidence, log, cache or Keychain path; SHA-256, CDHash, root/content/evidence digest,
   signing requirement, raw Keychain status/material, PID/start identity, command argv, user name,
   entered/AX text or clipboard bytes. In particular, V16 must not retain the current
   `evidence=<private path>` or `Review ... at: <private path>` lines. A one-shot OBSERVE or DESIGN
   challenge may appear only in its live TTY prompt and is never echoed into logs, status or recovery
   copy. The CLI boundary maps internal/Foundation/Keychain errors to the fixed public categories
   before writing stderr. Source and executable canary tests inject private path/hash/challenge/text
   values through every operation seam and require zero appearance in captured normal-path output.
4. **Observer and Designer provenance is unmistakable.** Automated preparation ends only as
   `PENDING_INSTALLED_SIGNOFF`, names the observer command and remaining expiry, and explicitly says
   install is not deployment completion. The observer continues item-by-item `completed/remaining`
   guidance, the approved privacy-safe manual-Paste sentence and cancel consequences; success says
   only that fourteen observation records were sealed and that independent Designer review remains,
   then names `scripts/designer-attest-installed-signoff.sh`. The later Designer process presents a
   navigable record index itself without printing its storage location, labels the review as separate,
   accepts only the one-shot consent or FAIL, and on consent names the finalizer. Automated code,
   observer capture and Designer approval remain three separately labeled provenances; none may claim
   another's result or populate human, Finder, Dock, real-click, Paste or VoiceOver fields
   automatically. Finalization alone may print installed sign-off completion.
5. **The 148-row machinery is audit-only.** Row ids, category ids/totals, fault/scenario names,
   pseudo-terminal plumbing, temporary Git/Keychain fixtures and `rows=148` never appear in production
   command help, accepted arguments, prompts, status, failure/recovery output, pipeline wrappers or
   hooks. They may be emitted only when a developer explicitly invokes
   `scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh`; even there, private fixture paths and fault payloads stay
   sealed and normal users receive no new release step. Source scans and a production-transcript test
   must reject `ROW_PASS`, `CATEGORY_PASS`, `rows=148`, scenario selectors or fixture paths on every
   normal operator surface.
6. **Terminal usability and scope.** Progress and state are conveyed in text, not color, cursor
   position or animation; lines remain bounded and usable in a narrow terminal and under VoiceOver.
   `status` always gives the truthful current public state, time remaining and exactly one next or
   recovery command without private identifiers. Interrupting any stage leaves a non-success state
   whose next invocation cannot skip prior authority. Existing closed command grammar and wrapper
   paths remain unchanged; test-only controls are absent. No V16 copy, timing or test infrastructure
   changes a Pebble screen, focus, text field, installed checklist, manual action, accessibility
   requirement, RPG semantic, shipping product or LAN behavior.

The implementation must exercise these transcript assertions from a clean temporary repository,
from the stale-output case, at every one of the seven stage failures, across observer/Designer
handoff, and through exact-parent resume versus divergent/amended rejection. Design Sign-off must
inspect the final real operator transcripts as well as the unchanged installed application; source
tests alone cannot approve wording that was never emitted by the built workflow.

**V16 Release-gate Authority and Executable-matrix Design Review verdict: PASS.** The dispatcher-
owned authority and exhaustive adversarial inventory can remain usable and appropriately invisible
to ordinary operators under the six binding criteria above. Any implementation that omits ordered
seven-stage progress, exposes private paths/hashes/Keychain details, prints audit-row machinery on a
normal path, conflates observer with Designer provenance, or recommends retry/bypass instead of the
authorized restart/resume path returns to Design Review. Build remains blocked pending independent
Security (plan) PASS.

## Security (plan) — V16 Release-gate Authority and Executable Matrix (2026-07-12)

Independently reviewed the three immediately preceding Security(code) findings, the exact V16
Architecture at lines 11571-11828, the separate six-criterion Design PASS above, and the actual
pre-remediation dispatcher, production adapters, excluded probe, tests, adversarial runner, wrappers
and hooks on disk. Input whole-plan SHA-256 was
`fbe9299d07b41c227a1cbe1a4562bf2fb12d9de2f7900611bd522a2bd992e592`. This gate reviews the
proposed trust boundaries only; it does not approve implementation bytes, runtime, deployment,
commit or push.

### Threat model and binding security requirements

- **Authority and confused-deputy resistance.** The attacker controls command arguments, terminal
  input, child status and channels, fixture files, process timing and every operation dependency. The
  dispatcher is the sole policy principal: it alone constructs/restarts/invalidates authority,
  interprets challenges and calls all seven legal transitions. `ValidatedPreparedEvidence` and
  `ValidatedObservationEvidence` must have package-private initializers whose fields are derived only
  by the named validators; they may contain factual sealed values but no caller verdict or mutation
  closure. No dependency, production adapter, probe, wrapper or hook may receive a coordinator,
  store, payload, transition callback, challenge decision or a way to construct either validated
  type. Source enforcement plus `c04.dependency-authority-escape` must prove this structurally and by
  attempting malicious raw-operation returns.
- **Closed raw evidence seams.** All stdout, stderr, manifest, codesign, process and terminal channels
  are untrusted bytes. Length limits apply before decoding or interpolation; parsing requires exact
  grammar, exact argv/tool identity, finite timestamps, valid UTF-8 where text is required, and no
  ignored trailing records, duplicate keys or control-sequence escape into public output. Raw errors
  and paths are mapped to the Design-approved fixed categories at the CLI boundary. A successful
  helper exit is never sufficient: the dispatcher-owned workflow must independently parse, bind and
  revalidate the expected artifact and predecessor-stage ledger.
- **Clean and stale preparation.** Both required probe runs must reach the fixed seven real stages and
  publish `.prepared`; a stale decoy cannot be silently accepted. Dispatcher-owned cleanup may remove
  only the exact canonical generated release file after no-follow, in-root, private-ancestor,
  regular-file and link-count validation. It must not recursively delete `.build`, follow a link,
  remove source or accept an unrelated artifact. Gate one observes no accepted product identity;
  gate two's real clean release build creates the sole digest used by gates two through seven,
  packaging, installation and publication. Failure, cancellation or sequence change leaves no
  `.prepared` authority and emits only the safe restart guidance required by Design criteria 1-2.
- **Executable inventory and false-green resistance.** The checked-in manifest is independent of the
  Swift inventory and runner. Every one of the 148 unique rows has one explicit expected result and
  records `ROW_PASS` only after that result is asserted. For the category-5 `unrelated-path` rows,
  mutation is allowed to continue only when it is outside the sealed/content authority and all
  protected identities remain equal; if it is inside the content snapshot it must invalidate. Link,
  rename and byte substitutions at a protected boundary must reject before its next transition.
  Merely observing a mutation, invoking a helper, or catching any error is not a row pass. Manifest
  mode/hash, totals, exact order, zero skips/failures, category cleanup and set equality are all
  independently fail-closed before the fixed 148-row terminal PASS.
- **Git, reflog and hook integrity.** Temporary repositories use real Git objects, index/worktree
  comparison, local hooks and reflogs. Commit ids, parents, ref-update tuples and hook order are data,
  never shell source; no fixture-derived value is evaluated or re-tokenized. Only an exact child of
  the armed parent may resume. Divergent siblings, amended replacements, tuple/ref changes and replay
  under a different authority reject without reset, rewrite, force, hook bypass or destructive reflog
  recovery. Copied wrappers/hooks and the one fixed fixture leaf are hash-checked immediately before
  execution, and the second pre-push authority check happens after all product gates.
- **TOCTOU, durability and persistence.** Each protected object is opened relative to a retained
  private directory descriptor with no-follow semantics; canonical path, device/inode, regular-file
  type, link count, mode, size and digest are revalidated immediately before the corresponding
  transition. Category 5 injects between capture and that revalidation. Category 8 covers monotonic
  stale-sequence races and every specified Keychain load/add/update/delete fault; category 9 covers
  every durable write/cache point plus evidence/log/cache swap and deletion. Cache is never
  authority, partial writes never advance Keychain state, and authoritative Keychain state wins after
  restart. Production identity is unreachable from categories 1-9; category 10 uses a random
  non-aliasing identity and proves cleanup after both success and injected cleanup failure.
- **Challenge, privacy and cleanup.** Challenges are 32 dispatcher-interpreted random bytes encoded
  once, scoped to one receipt/state, consumed on the successful transition and never returned by a
  dependency, persisted in public evidence, echoed, logged or reusable. The observer and Designer
  remain different PID/start identities and neither can claim the other's provenance. Fixtures use
  deterministic nonsecret canaries; no path, user/home value, challenge, field/AX text, clipboard
  byte, hash/CDHash, signing requirement, raw Keychain material or process identity reaches normal
  output. Every row uses bounded private output and `defer` cleanup that terminates and reaps children,
  removes only its own temporary paths/Keychain identity, checks absence and cannot address
  production authority. Design criteria 3-6 are executable transcript requirements, not source-only
  assertions.
- **Production escape hatches and frozen product scope.** The production parser and dependency graph
  accept no environment-, file-, manifest-, scenario-, fault-, fixture-, evidence- or alternate-
  executable selector. `ROW_PASS`, `CATEGORY_PASS`, row ids/totals and fixture paths are audit-only.
  Any change to a shipping source, AppKit driver, pipeline/deploy code, deadline, checklist, storage,
  text input, AppSupport, smoke, RPG or LAN surface is out of V16 scope and returns to the earliest
  affected gate. Closing evidence must reproduce all six pinned hashes, the two-product graph and the
  zero-LAN scan before independent Security(code).

### Findings and disposition

No unresolved plan-level finding remains after the binding interpretations above. The plan removes
the actual alternate transition/bootstrap authority visible in
`Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/ReleaseGate.swift:1036-1077` and
`Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/Fixtures/ReleaseGateWorkflowProbe/main.swift:23-113`; it replaces the
current category-label-only runner with an external exact row contract; and it requires real clean
and stale dispatcher preparation rather than the current probe's uncallable prepare path. The six
Design criteria are preserved as security properties: truthful bounded progress/failure, public
redaction, separate human provenance, audit-only adversarial machinery, accessible status and no
product/LAN drift.

Not reviewed or approved by this gate: future implementation bytes, whether each row actually
exercises its named effect, focused/full test output, installed application behavior, Keychain
mutation outside isolated tests, deployment, source-control mutation or GitHub state. Builder owns
the implementation and required evidence; a separate Security(code) reviewer must inspect and rerun
the exact completed bytes. Any caller-authored evidence, alternate coordinator mutation, generic
catch-as-pass row, stale-artifact acceptance, private diagnostic leak, cleanup residue or changed
frozen hash is a blocking FAIL.

**V16 Release-gate Authority and Executable-matrix Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** The threat model
closes dispatcher authority, raw-evidence forgery, clean/stale preparation, inventory false greens,
Git/reflog/hook replay, Keychain/persistence faults, TOCTOU, privacy, cleanup, production escape
hatches and protected product/LAN scope. Build is authorized only in the Architecture dependency
order and under this Security plan plus the six binding Design criteria; Security(code) remains a
mandatory downstream blocking gate.

## Builder — V16 Release-gate Authority and Executable Matrix (2026-07-12)

Implemented the approved V16 work in dependency order. `ReleaseGateCommandDispatcher` now creates
and restarts preparation authority, interprets the observer and Designer challenges, seals the
Designer attestation, invalidates failed attempts and owns all seven legal transition call sites.
The production adapters and excluded workflow probe have zero coordinator mutation sites. The fixed
package-owned preparation workflow alone executes and validates the seven stages, removes only a
validated exact generated release executable before a stale run, publishes prepared authority, and
emits bounded redacted progress. Observer and Designer helpers now return capture/reviewer facts only;
they do not create a payload, digest, verdict or transition.

The excluded probe no longer contains `fixturePayload`, `fixture-bootstrap-finalized`, direct
`create`/`restart`/`transition`/`invalidate`, or caller-authored finalized state. Its clean and
stale-generated-release scenarios both invoke the real dispatcher `run-prepare-gates`, real SwiftPM
release build, fixed seven commands, stable-file/evidence parsers and prepared publication. The exact
independent manifest contains 148 sorted unique row ids with category totals
`8,7,7,9,50,7,11,23,20,6`; its SHA-256 is
`bf782a2be35778c467f4c58fba65fc473e458892c6478a00b84716eae64c6bbd`. The runner validates the
fixed mode/hash/count/order/totals, runs each exact XCTest category separately, rejects skips,
failures, duplicates and foreign rows, compares the observed set byte-for-byte to the manifest and
requires the separately named category-10 production-adapter and stale-coordinator rows.

### Builder verification evidence

- `swift build --target PebbleReleaseGate` passed. The excluded workflow probe compiled against the
  real module, and `scripts/run-release-gate-tool.sh scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift preflight`
  passed with redacted operator output.
- The clean and stale real-dispatcher preparation test passed both scenarios, each with ordered
  `1/7` through `7/7` progress, nil source-security artifact, release-build sole digest, all later
  gates bound to that digest and authoritative `.prepared` publication.
- `swift test --filter
  'PebbleReleaseGateTests\.(ReleaseGateTests|KeychainReceiptStateStoreIntegrationTests)'` executed
  27/27 tests with zero failures: ReleaseGate 24 and Keychain 3.
- `scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh` passed all ten exact categories and terminated
  `Release-gate adversarial matrix: PASS categories=10 rows=148 cleanup=verified`.
- `scripts/security-scan.sh` passed the 148-row suite, AppKit driver compile, installed-signoff
  preflight, SQLite boundary scan over 127 production Swift files, source/privacy/secret/network
  checks and final `security: passed`.
- Two independent `swift package clean && swift build -c release` runs were warning-free. Their six
  hash manifests compared byte-for-byte equal and match the binding pins: Pebble
  `648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`; pebsmoke
  `d989c030656377ea1dca57b563408e2f6ae739e391904bde8be25f4f1d6dde2f`; PebbleStorage.o
  `ed37590e383037968b25905cb7ecd1d29e8faa43ba1f62a4919baebf9aabc6ba`; PebbleCore.o
  `39e37366bcba82ba65131a1112ad0b262f562279e363cf621542a804bd3ae028`; PebbleTextInput.o
  `547f0c44a950be0b7287ab73b918b6a3f486502e7ba57d7897e2d751b4d040ac`; and
  PebbleAppSupport.o `12d4b146ea24da2457066590f0b2c54e331fa098f8f82f54849856e838b07383`.
- The first full XCTest run exposed three source-shape tests still locating V15 preparation and
  Designer code in the production script. Those assertions were migrated to the V16 package-owned
  workflow/dispatcher and operation-only adapters; the directly focused regression passed 3/3.
  The mandatory full rerun at `/tmp/pebble-v16-full-test-rerun.log` passed 1057/1057 with zero
  failures: PebbleTextInput 21, PebbleReleaseGate 27, PebbleCore 1003 and PebbleAppSupport 6.
- `swift run -c release pebsmoke` passed 457/457. The release-surface verifier and release binary
  scan passed. `swift package describe --type json` exposes only `Pebble` and `pebsmoke` products.
  The ReleaseGate/AppSupport/AppKit-driver zero-LAN scan passed; `git diff --check` passed; the final
  dispatcher/adapter source audit reports seven dispatcher transition sites and zero adapter/probe
  mutation sites.

No runtime input, installed receipt mutation, installed-app observation/Designer attestation,
pipeline/deployment, commit or push occurred. Those remain blocked until a fresh independent
Security(code) PASS. One implementation surprise was the three cross-suite source assertions above;
their updates change test routing only and the frozen product hashes prove zero shipping-byte drift.

**V16 Builder verdict: PASS.** The complete approved remediation and every required Builder closing
gate are green. Fresh independent Security(code) remains the mandatory next gate before same-byte
runtime, installed Design sign-off, independent Test, deployment or source-control publication.

## Security (code) — V16 Final Actual Diff (2026-07-12)

Acting only as the fresh independent adversarial Security reviewer, inspected the actual V16
release-gate module, production CLI/observer/Designer adapters, excluded workflow probe, all ten
advertised adversarial categories, manifest/runner/security scan, wrappers/hooks, package graph and
current release artifacts against the V16 Architecture, Design PASS and Security(plan) PASS. The
input whole-plan SHA-256 before this review was
`31b93f501187888092f9ab02d6fa95e7ed5c7561e451be38be0cfa0654f88314`; the checked-in 148-row
manifest SHA-256 is the claimed
`bf782a2be35778c467f4c58fba65fc473e458892c6478a00b84716eae64c6bbd`.

### Findings

- **[HIGH] `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/ReleaseGate.swift:1111-1128`;
  `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift:225-278`;
  `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/Fixtures/ReleaseGateWorkflowProbe/main.swift:5-54,113-156` ->** The
  dispatcher is not the sole authority principal required by V16. The dependency protocol still
  exposes `coordinator()`, accepts the complete authoritative `ReleaseGatePayload` in validation,
  observation and Designer methods, and receives the private authority directory. The production
  adapter constructs and returns the coordinator, while the excluded probe stores its own
  coordinator/store and can inspect and rewrite the authority file. This directly contradicts the
  protocol comment and the binding prohibition on giving any dependency, adapter or probe a
  coordinator, store, payload or transition capability. Seven textual `gate.transition(` sites do
  not close that confused-deputy path. Remove coordinator/store/payload/directory authority from
  `ReleaseGateCommandDependencies`; construct and retain authority inside a closed package-owned
  dispatcher/factory; pass adapters only capped raw channels, dispatcher-selected opaque operation
  handles and factual process/reviewer observations; and add a compile/executable attack proving a
  malicious dependency cannot read, construct, replace, invalidate or transition receipt state.

- **[HIGH] `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/AdversarialRowsV16.swift:157-164`;
  `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/ReleaseGateTests.swift:231-367,561-618,670-910`;
  `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/KeychainReceiptStateStoreIntegrationTests.swift:50-94`;
  `scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh:37-64` ->** The 148-row executable inventory remains a
  false green. `AdversarialRowsV16.emit` unconditionally prints every manifest row in a category
  after checking only that the static names/counts exist; it has no per-row assertion ledger. An
  independent category-4 run executed one XCTest in 0.007 seconds, then printed all nine row passes,
  including `c04.dependency-authority-escape`, even though the authority escape above exists.
  Category 10 likewise executed one test and printed all six rows although
  `testProductionAdapterBoundaries...` and `testRealKeychainCoordinators...` are separate tests that
  the category runner never invoked. Category 5 applies four mutations and one allowed sibling to
  one generic file, then claims all 50 boundary/mutation rows. Category 8 exercises only a small
  subset of the 23 named store faults; category 9 does not exercise the required evidence/log/cache
  swap/delete workflow rows. Replace `emit(category:)` with a ledger whose `pass(row:)` is reachable
  only after that exact named effect and expected postcondition is asserted; implement every row as
  a distinct table case or test; and make the external runner reject a row that lacks its individual
  assertion/effect record, rather than accepting names emitted en masse.

- **[HIGH] `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/ReleaseGateTests.swift:735-865` ->** The advertised real
  Git/wrapper/hook category still substitutes the security-critical product gates and does not run
  the specified recovery matrix. It injects a fake `swift` that creates a shell `Pebble` and prints
  fabricated build/XCTest/smoke success, plus replacement security/surface/binary/AppKit helpers
  whose principal behavior is fixed success text. It does not hash-check the copied wrappers/hooks,
  does not execute separate divergent-sibling, amended-commit and reflog-recovery repositories, and
  nevertheless emits all eleven category-7 rows. Use the real SwiftPM build and conditionally
  validating fixed helper grammar, verify wrapper/hook/fixture-leaf hashes immediately before each
  execution, and exercise exact-parent resume, divergent sibling, amend, reflog recovery, tuple
  replay and different-ref rejection as separately asserted real-Git rows before recording them.

- **[HIGH] `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/ReleaseGate.swift:1390-1408,1424-1469,1540-1585`;
  `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/Fixtures/ReleaseGateWorkflowProbe/main.swift:120-156` ->** Observation
  and Designer results are not sealed factual seams. The validator accepts any fourteen files per
  suffix, checks command JSON only for schema plus `captureStatus == 0`, checks PNGs only for their
  eight-byte signature, never validates the fixed fourteen-item inventory or linked operation/AX/
  screenshot digests, and never binds or revalidates the returned PID/start identity against the
  prepared live process. The probe demonstrates the weakness by manufacturing fourteen minimal
  eight-byte PNG records and deriving consent directly from the dispatcher-supplied challenge
  prompt. Implement the fixed inventory with exact schemas and cross-file digests, fd-relative
  no-follow opens and size caps before reads; revalidate the installed executable/process and exact
  receipt sequence immediately before transition; keep the challenge out of adapter-readable data;
  and test substitution, replay and PID/start/executable churn at the adjacent transition boundary.

- **[MEDIUM] `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/ReleaseGate.swift:773-806,1207-1224,1290-1312` ->** Two
  required resource/TOCTOU boundaries are absent. Closed command output is read in full and only then
  checked against the 16 MiB limit, allowing an untrusted child to consume unbounded disk/read memory.
  Stale-release cleanup uses separate path-based `lstat`, `realpath` and `unlink` operations without a
  retained directory descriptor, private-ancestor validation or an adjacent identity recheck, so a
  rename/substitution race can change the deleted leaf. Enforce the cap while capturing and kill/reap
  on overflow; open the validated private build parent once, inspect the exact leaf with
  `openat`/`fstatat` plus no-follow/link-count/mode/device/inode checks, and delete that same validated
  entry fd-relatively only after an adjacent revalidation. Add clean/stale symlink, rename, hardlink,
  ancestor-mode and output-cap rows that assert no unrelated deletion or residue.

- **[MEDIUM] `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/ReleaseGate.swift:1213-1244,1378-1382,1525-1530`;
  `Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/ReleaseGateTests.swift:231-325,619-652` ->** The binding operator-
  transcript contract is not implemented or tested. Preparation failure does not identify the failed
  `n/7` stage or a stable public reason category; `status` prints internal sequence and an absolute
  expiry instead of time remaining; and no executable canary matrix covers every stage failure,
  observer/Designer handoff, cancellation/expiry/substitution, exact-parent recovery and prohibited
  private values. Add a dispatcher-owned public error/status formatter with one bounded failure
  category, failed stage, truthful resumability and exactly one safe next command; remove internal
  sequence/raw expiry from normal output; and capture/assert clean, stale and every failure transcript
  against path/hash/challenge/text/Keychain/process canaries and false PASS/COMPLETE language.

### Direct evidence and reviewed boundary

Independent focused execution passed but proves the false-green mechanism: category 4 executed
1 test/0 failures and emitted 9 row passes; category 10 executed 1 test/0 failures and emitted 6 row
passes. Static audit found exactly seven dispatcher `gate.transition(` call sites, but also the
forbidden dependency/probe coordinator and payload interfaces above. `git diff --check` passed. All
three hooks and all release-gate wrappers are mode `0755`. The package graph exposes exactly two
products, `Pebble` and `pebsmoke`; the ReleaseGate/AppSupport/standalone-driver zero-LAN/network scan
returned zero matches.

The current release artifacts retain all six binding SHA-256 values: Pebble
`648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`; pebsmoke
`d989c030656377ea1dca57b563408e2f6ae739e391904bde8be25f4f1d6dde2f`; PebbleStorage.o
`ed37590e383037968b25905cb7ecd1d29e8faa43ba1f62a4919baebf9aabc6ba`; PebbleCore.o
`39e37366bcba82ba65131a1112ad0b262f562279e363cf621542a804bd3ae028`; PebbleTextInput.o
`547f0c44a950be0b7287ab73b918b6a3f486502e7ba57d7897e2d751b4d040ac`; and PebbleAppSupport.o
`12d4b146ea24da2457066590f0b2c54e331fa098f8f82f54849856e838b07383`.
These frozen-product checks pass and show no V16 shipping/LAN drift; they do not repair release-gate
authority or test semantics.

Reviewed: all three V15 blockers; V16 dispatcher/dependency/probe authority; clean/stale preparation;
row manifest/inventory/runner; categories 1-10; Git/hooks/recovery fixtures; stable-file, durability,
Keychain and observation boundaries; normal operator output; production escape-hatch scans; wrapper
modes; product graph; six hashes; LAN scope; and diff whitespace. Not reviewed or executed: AppKit
runtime input, installed human/VoiceOver observation, real production-Keychain receipt mutation,
pipeline deployment, commit or push. Full XCTest/smoke/surface/binary results were read from Builder
evidence rather than rerun; this gate's independent executions were the category-4 and category-10
tests plus the read-only artifact/source checks above.

**Security (code) verdict: FAIL.** The real clean/stale dispatcher preparation and seven dispatcher
transition call sites improve V15, and all frozen product boundaries remain intact. However, the
dependency/probe still possess forbidden coordinator and payload authority, the 148-row inventory
prints unearned passes, the Git/recovery workflow uses false-success substitutes, observation facts
are forgeable, required TOCTOU/resource caps are absent and public transcript requirements are not
closed. Same-byte runtime, installed Design Sign-off, independent Test, pipeline/receipt finalization,
deployment, commit and push remain blocked. The fixes must return to the earliest affected
Architecture/Design/Security(plan) phase and receive a fresh independent Security(code) review.

## V17 Closed Authority, Evidence and Adversarial Proof Architecture (2026-07-12)

This Architecture closes only the six findings in the immediately preceding V16 Security(code)
FAIL, read at whole-plan SHA-256
`467475ff72f0ee2ba1aa8359903e2d1548dbccd48563f9767688f8e633175923`. It changes no Pebble game,
AppKit driver, storage, text-input, RPG, LAN, pipeline or deploy behavior. The protected same-byte
boundary remains: Pebble `648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`;
pebsmoke `d989c030656377ea1dca57b563408e2f6ae739e391904bde8be25f4f1d6dde2f`;
PebbleStorage.o `ed37590e383037968b25905cb7ecd1d29e8faa43ba1f62a4919baebf9aabc6ba`;
PebbleCore.o `39e37366bcba82ba65131a1112ad0b262f562279e363cf621542a804bd3ae028`;
PebbleTextInput.o `547f0c44a950be0b7287ab73b918b6a3f486502e7ba57d7897e2d751b4d040ac`;
PebbleAppSupport.o `12d4b146ea24da2457066590f0b2c54e331fa098f8f82f54849856e838b07383`;
and exactly the `Pebble` and `pebsmoke` products.

### 1. One closed dispatcher authority and one-shot operation leases

In `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/ReleaseGate.swift`, remove `coordinator()`, `validateCurrent(_:)`, every
`ReleaseGatePayload` parameter and every private-authority-directory parameter from
`ReleaseGateCommandDependencies`. Replace the public dependency surface with raw operations only:

- `workflowInputs() -> ReleaseGateWorkflowInputs` supplies canonical candidate repository/product
  paths and bounded checklist bytes, never an authority/evidence root, receipt id or payload.
- `perform(_ request: ReleaseGateExternalRequest, using lease: ReleaseGateOperationLease) throws`
  performs one exact closed command/install/capture/presentation/Git request. The dependency returns
  no result object. The package-created lease accepts capped raw stdout/stderr chunks or one fixed
  observation item, is single-use, and has no public initializer, nonce accessor, filesystem path,
  payload field, digest/verdict setter or transition method.
- `emitPublicLine`, clock/random bytes and the three read-only Git facts remain narrow operations;
  TTY OBSERVE/DESIGN challenge prompting and interpretation move into the package and never cross the
  dependency API.

`ReleaseGateCommandDispatcher.production(repository:dependencies:)` and the test-only
`ReleaseGateCommandDispatcher.isolated(repository:session:dependencies:)` are the only factories.
They canonicalize the repository, compute its identity, privately create the Keychain store,
authority root, coordinator and artifact validator, and retain them for the dispatch lifetime. The
isolated factory uses a dispatcher-generated random non-production service/account and private root;
its opaque session can reopen the same test authority but exposes neither identifier nor location.
The active coordinator, store, root, current/full payload and challenge bytes never enter an adapter,
observer/Designer helper or workflow probe. Dispatcher methods own creation/restart/invalidation,
payload mutation and the seven legal transitions. A negative compile fixture and an executable
malicious dependency prove that the dependency API cannot obtain the active store/coordinator/root,
submit a payload/digest/verdict, reuse a lease, cross-bind a lease/operation nonce, or alter state.

Each lease privately binds 32 random dispatcher bytes to the exact command id, current sequence,
release and installed stable-file identities, expected live PID/start/executable identity and one
operation ordinal. On seal, the package reopens/revalidates those identities, checks the nonce and
ordinal, consumes the lease and constructs a package-private `ValidatedPreparedEvidence`,
`ValidatedObservationEvidence` or `ValidatedDesignerEvidence`. Dependencies can supply untrusted
bytes only; they cannot construct those types. `scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.swift` becomes the
fixed production operation adapter and contains no `authorityRoot`, `evidenceRoot`, coordinator,
store, payload validator or payload-taking helper. The excluded workflow probe uses only the
isolated dispatcher factory and public status/transcript; it cannot read an authority file.

### 2. Exact installed-evidence sealing at the transition boundary

Move checklist parsing and evidence ownership into the release-gate package. The dispatcher validates
the exact 14-item ordered inventory (`create-world` through `rpg-layout`) and opens its own private
receipt directory by retained descriptor. The observation lease accepts exactly one record per
expected item and writes it itself: command JSON has the exact eight keys and matching item/window/
status fields; operation log has the fixed grammar; PNG is fully decoded with positive bounded
dimensions; required AX JSON has the exact eight keys, matching item id, one window, nonnegative
bounded counters and bounded role counts. Every object is size-capped before/during ingestion and
its digest is cross-checked in command JSON. Missing, duplicate, extra, reordered or mismatched
objects reject.

The dependency receives a limited `ReleaseGateApplicationBinding` (installed executable identity,
code-sign identity and live PID/start) but no payload or evidence path. The dispatcher captures and
revalidates that process/executable/signature before capture, after every item and immediately before
the adjacent transition; observer PID/start comes from the dispatcher process, not returned facts.
Designer presentation receives a bounded read-only index, not a directory, challenge or authority.
The dispatcher reopens and validates every sealed item, proves a different reviewer PID/start,
handles the DESIGN/FAIL line itself, creates/reopens the attestation and revalidates exact sequence,
artifact and live process immediately before transition. Substitution, replay, item/digest mismatch,
PID/start reuse, executable/signature churn and process exit each have a separate rejecting row.

### 3. Exact-row proof instead of category emission

Delete `AdversarialRowsV16.emit(category:)`. Add an `AdversarialAssertionLedger` whose only
`pass(row:effect:postcondition:)` call follows the assertion for that exact row and whose sealed
machine record binds row id, fixed effect id, fixed postcondition id, test executable hash and a fresh
invocation nonce. It cannot enumerate or bulk-emit manifest rows. Each of the 148 manifest rows is
either its own exact filtered XCTest or one separately invoked
`ReleaseGateAdversarialProbe --row <exact-id>` machine assertion; one process invocation proves one
row and may emit exactly one `ROW_PASS`. `scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh` invokes all 148
manifest entries separately, requires one nonzero test/assertion count, one matching attestation and
zero skips/failures per invocation, then performs manifest set/order/count equality. There is no
category-wide test that can print unexecuted rows.

Category 5 runs all 50 boundary/mutation pairs independently. For every boundary,
`unrelated-path` asserts the protected operation is allowed and the unrelated object remains exact;
`symlink`, `hardlink`, `rename` and `byte-replacement` each inject only that mutation between capture
and adjacent revalidation, assert rejection/no transition/no unrelated deletion, and verify cleanup.
Category 8 independently invokes every named load/add/update/delete/concurrency fault and proves its
exact mapped error plus authoritative state. Category 9 independently invokes every durability/cache
point and each evidence/log/cache swap/delete effect, then proves restart authority, absence of
partial advancement and zero residue. Category 10's runner entries must invoke, by exact filter, the
existing production-adapter-failure and real stale-coordinator tests; row text from the aggregate
Keychain test is not accepted as evidence for either.

### 4. Real category-7 Git, hook and product workflow

Category 7 uses fresh private clones/worktrees of the actual tracked source with copied production
wrappers/hooks hash-checked immediately before every execution. It uses `/usr/bin/swift`, real
`swift package clean`, warning-free release build, XCTest and `pebsmoke`, plus the real release-
surface, binary and AppKit gate parsers against the actual built product. A fake `swift`, a shell
`Pebble`, fixed success text or replacement security/surface/binary/AppKit helper is a hard failure.
Each of the eleven rows is a separate invocation with exactly one named Git/hook fault; all other
inputs equal the clean baseline. Independent repositories cover exact-parent resume, divergent
sibling, amend, reflog recovery, exact tuple, replay and different-ref rejection. Real commit ids,
parents, refs, reflog records, index/worktree digests, wrapper/hook hashes, product hashes and hook
order are asserted before that row may pass. No reset, force, amend-as-recovery or hook bypass is
permitted.

### 5. Bounded streaming and identity-safe stale cleanup

Replace `FoundationClosedCommandRunner.launch`'s read-after-exit file with a draining streaming
collector: stdout/stderr are read concurrently in bounded chunks while the child runs, with a 16 MiB
aggregate hard cap, bounded diagnostic prefix, timeout, terminate/SIGKILL fallback and mandatory
wait/reap. Crossing the cap kills/reaps immediately and returns the fixed output-limit category;
partial bytes never reach a parser or evidence file. Public lines are formatter-owned, at most 256
UTF-8 bytes each and a fixed count per command.

Replace path-based stale removal with a `GeneratedReleaseLeaf` descriptor utility. Open every
repository/`.build` ancestor using `openat(...O_DIRECTORY|O_NOFOLLOW|O_CLOEXEC)`, require current uid,
no group/other write, same device and directory type, then capture the exact `Pebble` leaf with
`openat`/`fstatat(AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW)`. Immediately before removal compare device/inode/type/link/
mode/uid/size; atomically move that exact name with `renameatx_np(...RENAME_EXCL)` to a random
same-directory quarantine, reopen and compare the quarantined identity, and only then `unlinkat` the
quarantined matching regular file. Any mismatch restores or leaves the original name untouched and
never unlinks it. Clean, symlink, hardlink, rename, byte replacement, unsafe-ancestor-mode and
output-cap rows prove no unrelated deletion, child leak or temporary residue.

### 6. Complete redacted operator transcript contract

Add a dispatcher-owned `ReleaseGatePublicFormatter` with closed enums for stage and reason. Every
preparation failure emits exactly: failed `n/7 <label>`, one stable reason (`command-failed`,
`output-limit`, `timed-out`, `artifact-changed`, `evidence-invalid`, `authority-changed` or
`cancelled`), `No prepared authority was published`, resumability (`restart-required` except the
one exact-parent commit case), and exactly one safe next command. `status` emits public state,
nonnegative rounded time remaining and exactly one next command; it never emits sequence or absolute
expiry. Success, observer/Designer handoff and exact-parent recovery retain the six binding V16
Design criteria.

Executable transcript cases cover clean and stale success, all seven stage failures, output cap,
timeout, cancellation, expiry, evidence/artifact/process substitution, stale authority, observer
rejection, Designer rejection, finalize, exact-parent interruption/resume, divergent/amended commit,
pre-push tuple/replay/ref rejection and Keychain load/add/update failures. Every case injects canaries
for repository/home/temp/installed/evidence/log/cache/Keychain paths, hashes/CDHash/signing data,
challenge, entered/AX/clipboard text, PID/start, argv and raw store bytes/status; captured stdout and
stderr must contain none, no false PASS/COMPLETE/deployed claim, one bounded reason and one safe next
command. Normal commands must never print row/category/scenario/fault vocabulary.

### Dependency order, risk-to-test map and Conditions for Builder

Builder order is binding: (A) private authority factories and operation leases; (B) dispatcher-side
validation/challenge/payload lifecycle; (C) operation-only production adapters and probe; (D) exact
installed inventory/process sealing; (E) streaming output and descriptor cleanup; (F) 148 separate
row assertions including real category 7 and exhaustive categories 5/8/9/10; (G) public formatter
and complete transcript matrix; (H) focused/full gates. A material API or scope change returns to
Architecture, Design Review and Security(plan).

- **Authority/confused deputy:** negative compile plus executable malicious-dependency, lease replay/
  cross-bind and source scans prove no active coordinator/store/root/payload/digest/verdict escape.
- **Evidence forgery/replay:** exact inventory/digest/process/sequence tests reject minimal PNGs,
  manufactured consent, substitution, replay and live identity churn at the adjacent transition.
- **False row claims:** 148 separate invocations and attested effect/postcondition records prove that
  no manifest enumeration or category bulk emit can earn a pass.
- **Git/product false green:** actual source clones, SwiftPM/products, wrappers/hooks and one-fault
  repositories prove the complete recovery/replay matrix with no fake gate.
- **TOCTOU/resource exhaustion:** category 5, stale-leaf/ancestor tests and streaming overflow prove
  bounded memory/disk, child reap, exact-leaf deletion and zero unrelated loss/residue.
- **Persistence completeness:** exact categories 8/9/10 prove every named adapter, Keychain,
  durability, cache and restart effect rather than presence of labels.
- **Privacy/usability:** the full executable transcript canary matrix proves truthful bounded copy,
  redaction, time remaining and one safe recovery command.
- **Frozen product/LAN scope:** pre/post six hashes, two-product graph, zero ReleaseGate/AppSupport/
  AppKit-driver LAN scan and source diff prove no shipping byte or LAN surface moved.

Conditions for Builder: dependencies receive no active authority object, full payload, challenge or
authority/evidence directory; only dispatcher-created one-shot leases may accept raw facts; every
identity and sequence is revalidated immediately before its transition; a row passes only after its
own invoked effect and postcondition; category 5 has 50 explicit allow/reject outcomes; category 7
uses real tools and one fault per invocation; categories 8/9 cover every named effect; category 10
actually invokes adapter/stale-coordinator tests; all output and reads are capped; stale deletion is
descriptor/quarantine bound; transcript redaction and one-next-command rules are executable.

Authorized edits are limited to `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/**`,
`Tests/PebbleReleaseGateTests/**`, the three installed-signoff Swift adapters and their wrappers,
`scripts/run-release-gate-tool.sh`, `scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh`,
`scripts/security-scan.sh`, finalize/resume wrappers and three hooks only where their closed command
path needs preservation, `Package.swift` only for excluded test fixtures, and this plan. Shipping
sources, AppKit driver, Core/Storage/TextInput/AppSupport, pebsmoke, checklist, pipeline/deploy and LAN
files remain frozen.

Required closing evidence: negative compile and malicious-dependency probes; exact clean/stale real
prepare; exact inventory/substitution/process focused tests; 148 separately invoked rows with totals
`8,7,7,9,50,7,11,23,20,6`; category-7 real-tool attestations; exhaustive category-5/8/9/10 evidence;
transcript canary matrix; `scripts/security-scan.sh`; warning-free clean release build; full XCTest;
457/457 `pebsmoke`; release-surface and binary scans; identical six hashes, two products and zero-LAN
scan; `git diff --check`; then fresh independent Security(code). Only Security(code) PASS may reopen
same-byte runtime, installed Design sign-off, independent Test, pipeline/deploy, commit and push.

**V17 Closed Authority, Evidence and Adversarial Proof Architecture verdict: PASS.** The six V16
findings now have an implementable earliest-boundary remediation, exact proof ownership and frozen
product scope. Build remains blocked until separate Design Review/Revision and Security(plan) PASS
this exact Architecture.

## Design Review/Revision — V17 Closed Authority and Operator Transcript (2026-07-12)

Reviewed the V17 Architecture above at input whole-plan SHA-256
`3a81a0738eb108b29e734fb15751529e2a146bb55c139b67ebf76c3710923657`, the six binding V16 Design
criteria, and the current production dispatcher, CLI, observer, Designer, finalizer and wrapper copy.
This is not Design N/A: V17 changes a terminal- and VoiceOver-facing release workflow. It changes no
Pebble game surface. The architecture preserves the approved experience with these binding
interpretations and acceptance criteria.

1. **The seven stages remain exact.** Normal preparation presents only `1/7 Source security`, `2/7
   Release build`, `3/7 Release surface`, `4/7 Binary scan`, `5/7 AppKit text entry`, `6/7 XCTest`
   and `7/7 pebsmoke`, in that order. Each started stage prints one `running` line and either one
   `passed` or one `failed` line; no later stage appears after failure. A clean start says that the
   release artifact will first be created by stage 2. A stale start says only that one generated
   output was safely cleared and source content was unchanged. Neither case implies corruption,
   lost work, deployment or an operator cleanup step.
2. **Failure copy must be truthful at the actual boundary.** The V17 phrase “every preparation
   failure” is narrowed as follows: after a numbered stage starts, failure is labeled with that exact
   `n/7` stage; a failure before `1/7` is `Preparation bootstrap: failed`; a failure after `7/7` while
   sealing/installing/publishing is `Preparation publication: failed`. A bootstrap or publication
   error must never be misreported as a stage that did not fail. Each failure transcript then has
   exactly one closed reason, `No prepared authority was published.`, one truthful disposition
   (`restart-required`, or `resume-exact-parent` only for the commit interruption), and one `Next:`
   command. It contains no raw child/store/Foundation error and no suggestion to retry the current
   operation, extend authority, reset, amend, force, bypass hooks or use `--no-verify`.
3. **Status has one deterministic action.** `status` prints one human-readable public state, rounded
   nonnegative time remaining and exactly one `Next:` command. It prints no receipt id, sequence or
   absolute expiry. The command table is fixed: absent/preparing/invalidated/expired uses
   `scripts/pipeline.sh --prepare-installed-signoff`; prepared uses
   `scripts/observe-installed-signoff.sh`; observed-pending-Designer uses
   `scripts/designer-attest-installed-signoff.sh`; observed uses
   `scripts/finalize-installed-signoff.sh`; finalized names `git commit` after a separate plain-text
   reminder to stage only reviewed files; exact-parent commit interruption uses
   `scripts/resume-installed-signoff-commit.sh`; committed/push-armed uses `git push`. A rejected,
   cancelled, expired, substituted or stale human step must state whether authority was invalidated;
   if invalidated, its only command is fresh preparation.
4. **Success and handoffs do not overclaim.** Automated success says
   `PENDING_INSTALLED_SIGNOFF`, gives rounded time remaining, names the observer command and says both
   that installed observation and independent Designer review remain and that this is not deployment
   completion. Observer output retains numbered `completed/remaining` progress, the approved
   privacy-safe manual-Paste instruction and cancellation consequence. It may say only that exactly
   fourteen records were sealed and Designer review remains. The separate Designer process presents
   a numbered, human-readable fourteen-record index without a storage path, identifies itself as a
   separate review, accepts only its one-shot consent or `FAIL`, and then names the finalizer.
   Finalization alone may say installed sign-off is complete. Preparation, observation and Design
   approval remain unmistakably separate provenances even though private PID/start checks are never
   printed.
5. **Privacy and audit invisibility are executable contracts.** Normal output contains none of the
   private paths, hashes/CDHash/signing data, challenges outside the one live prompt, Keychain/raw
   error material, PID/start, argv, entered/AX/clipboard text, receipt/evidence identifiers or
   canaries listed by V17. `ROW_PASS`, `CATEGORY_PASS`, row/category/scenario/fault vocabulary,
   fixture plumbing and `rows=148` appear only under the explicitly invoked adversarial runner and
   never in normal help, prompts, status, wrappers or hooks. Failure redaction may not erase the
   failed public stage, safe reason, authority disposition or one recovery command.
6. **Terminal and VoiceOver use is line-oriented and bounded.** No meaning depends on color, cursor
   movement, animation or terminal width. Public lines remain at most 256 UTF-8 bytes, prompts occur
   on their own readable line, stage/item numbers precede labels, and progress is not rewritten in
   place. EOF, Ctrl-C and malformed consent fail closed with a final spoken-readable disposition and
   next-command block. Transcript tests must assert order, uniqueness and line bounds for clean,
   stale, every stage, bootstrap/publication, observer and Designer handoffs, all rejection/recovery
   cases and the exact-parent resume path.
7. **Frozen product scope remains visible in the gate.** The implementation must preserve the six
   pinned hashes, two-product graph, zero-LAN scan and zero edits to Pebble UI, focus, checklist,
   AppKit driver, RPG, storage, text-input, pipeline/deploy or LAN behavior. Design Sign-off must
   inspect the emitted built transcripts in a narrow terminal with VoiceOver as well as the unchanged
   installed app; source assertions alone do not approve operator copy.

**V17 Design Review/Revision verdict: PASS.** Closed dispatcher authority, sealed evidence and
one-row proof can remain understandable and appropriately invisible to ordinary operators under the
seven binding criteria above. The bootstrap/publication labels and fixed status action table are
Design revisions, not optional examples. Omitting exact ordered progress, conflating automated,
observer and Designer provenance, exposing private/audit data, encouraging retry/bypass, or changing
the frozen game/runtime surface invalidates this PASS. Build remains blocked until independent
Security (plan) approves the V17 Architecture together with this revised transcript contract.

## Security (plan) — V17 Closed Authority and Adversarial Proof (2026-07-12)

Independently reviewed the six-finding V16 Security(code) FAIL, the actual current dispatcher,
production adapter, workflow probe, 148-row inventory/runner and hooks, and the V17 Architecture plus
Design revisions at input whole-plan SHA-256
`47f83e54afa8e0acc4068c828430f2366e817241a3f5c13e5f19ac72f1c2258b`. Threats considered were a
malicious or buggy dependency/probe using a confused deputy, retained/replayed operation authority,
syntactically valid but invented installed evidence, row-label forgery, candidate-source substitution,
Git/hook/product false success, same-user path races, unbounded child output, Keychain/durability
faults, private-data disclosure and misleading terminal/VoiceOver recovery copy.

### Blocking findings

- **[HIGH] V17 Architecture §§1-2 (`ReleaseGateOperationLease` and installed-evidence sealing) ->**
  The dependency is still allowed to submit raw observation items to a lease. Exact schemas, PNG
  decoding, cross-digests, a live-process check and a hidden challenge establish consistency, not
  provenance: a malicious dependency can manufacture a valid PNG, AX object, operation log and
  command object for every fixed item after the real observer supplies consent. That would let
  unobserved content become `ValidatedObservationEvidence`, preserving the V16 forgery boundary in a
  better-typed form. The package must own the hash-pinned executable/argv, process launch, pipe/file
  descriptors, exit status and bounded reads for every evidence-producing operation. An adapter may
  perform only a closed OS/UI primitive selected by the package; it may not call a public evidence-
  acceptance method or assert capture status, item identity, digest or success. The dispatcher must
  derive those facts from package-owned handles, bind each item to lease nonce/ordinal plus the
  revalidated installed PID/start/executable/signature, and invalidate the lease in `defer` on normal
  return, throw, timeout or cancellation. Late, concurrent, reentrant, retained, replayed and cross-
  operation lease use must be separate rejecting tests. PNG metadata must cap width, height and pixel
  area before raster allocation, not only encoded bytes and post-decode dimensions.

- **[HIGH] V17 Architecture §3 (per-row attestation) ->** The proposed test process may still emit its
  own `ROW_PASS`, so a row handler can print a matching label/record without executing its named
  effect. The runner must generate a fresh per-invocation challenge, pass it through a private
  inherited descriptor, independently hash the exact launched test/probe executable, and accept one
  length-capped machine attestation binding challenge, row id, fixed effect id, fixed postcondition
  id, executable hash and exactly one nonzero assertion/test count. Only the external runner may
  print `ROW_PASS`, after validating that record and child exit; row children may not print pass or
  category vocabulary. Source/inventory checks must reject manifest enumeration, a generic catch-as-
  pass path, shared category entry points and any ledger call not dominated by that row's asserted
  effect and postcondition. Category totals alone remain non-evidence.

- **[HIGH] V17 Architecture §4 (real category 7) ->** "actual tracked source" is not the candidate
  under review in this dirty worktree: the release-gate module, tests and scripts are currently
  untracked or modified relative to `HEAD`. A clone of tracked `HEAD` plus selected wrappers can
  exercise stale bytes and still report real-tool success. Before any row, materialize one immutable
  candidate snapshot from the same bounded tracked-and-untracked inventory/content digest used by
  preparation, excluding only declared build/evidence/private-authority outputs; verify its path,
  mode, size and SHA-256 manifest, then initialize each private Git repository from those exact bytes.
  Every category-7 record must bind that candidate digest, production wrapper/hook digests and real
  Swift/product digests. Each of the eleven rows gets one fresh repository and one named fault; no
  fake tool, fixed-success helper, inherited build output or unlisted candidate omission is allowed.

- **[MEDIUM] V17 Architecture §5 (`GeneratedReleaseLeaf`) ->** A pre-rename identity comparison plus
  `renameatx_np(...RENAME_EXCL)` is not an atomic compare-and-rename of a selected inode. If the name
  is substituted after comparison, the wrong entry can be moved; after that, the stated guarantee
  that a mismatch "leaves the original name untouched" is no longer true. Specify the complete
  rollback state machine: retained no-follow ancestor descriptors; a dispatcher-created random
  owner-only quarantine directory not disclosed to dependencies; adjacent pre-move identity check;
  post-move identity check; unlink only the matching quarantined regular file; and on mismatch no
  unlink plus descriptor-relative exclusive restoration only when the original name is absent. If
  safe restoration cannot be proven, fail closed, preserve the quarantine for explicit recovery and
  report no cleanup success. Tests must inject each race before move, after move and before unlink,
  assert zero unrelated deletion, and distinguish expected preserved forensic residue on an unsafe
  mismatch from zero residue after clean success.

### Binding conditions for the revised plan

Categories 5, 7, 8, 9 and 10 remain exact obligations, not examples. Category 5 must invoke all ten
named boundaries times five mutations separately. Category 7 must run the real candidate source and
real production parsers/tools without performing production receipt mutation or installed-app
sign-off before Security(code). Category 8 must inject each of its 23 named store outcomes at the
actual operation edge and compare both returned error and authoritative state. Category 9 must run
all 20 named write/fsync/rename/cache/evidence/log/restart effects against the real durability path.
Category 10 must separately prove the actual Keychain accessibility attribute, cross-process
visibility, cleanup success, cleanup-failure isolation, production-adapter failures and stale-
coordinator race; one aggregate Keychain test cannot stand in for another row.

All adversarial workspaces, children, descriptors and non-production random Keychain identities must
be bounded, reaped/closed and removed only by exact owned identity. Normal production parsing and
dependencies must expose no environment/file/manifest/scenario/fault/fixture/evidence selector, no
alternate executable or output injection, and no row/category vocabulary. The Design transcript
matrix must inspect stdout and stderr, line count/256-byte bounds, exact ordering, redaction,
VoiceOver-readable prompts, truthful bootstrap/stage/publication boundaries, authority disposition
and exactly one safe `Next:` command. The six product/object hashes, two-product graph, frozen
checklist/AppKit/game/LAN scope and zero-LAN scan remain mandatory before Security(code).

Reviewed but not approved by this gate: future implementation bytes, runtime input, installed
observation/Designer attestation, production Keychain mutation, full verification output,
pipeline/deployment, commit or push. Builder remains blocked; the Architect must revise the four
boundaries above, Designer must confirm any operator-copy consequences, and Security(plan) must
review the exact revision before code changes.

**V17 Security (plan) verdict: FAIL.** V17 correctly removes direct coordinator/payload authority,
requires separate row invocations, real tools, bounded streaming, complete persistence categories
and redacted recovery copy. It does not yet prevent syntactically valid evidence forgery, authenticate
row success outside the asserting process, bind category 7 to the actual dirty candidate, or specify
a truthful and safe post-rename mismatch path. Those are blocking security properties, not Builder
implementation choices.

## V17.1 Architecture Revision — Closed Capture, Authenticated Rows and Exact Candidate (2026-07-12)

This revision closes only the four findings in the V17 Security(plan) FAIL, read at whole-plan
SHA-256 `6ac4c038837137503f4f1d2fe169ba31bcc7c2ce4f03b879b346ee7ef7cbc2df`. All unmodified V17
Architecture and Design requirements remain binding, especially the 148 separate rows, complete
transcript matrix, exact category-5/7/8/9/10 obligations, six protected hashes, two-product graph and
frozen game/AppKit-driver/checklist/storage/text-input/RPG/LAN/pipeline/deploy scope.

### A. Package-owned evidence processes and scoped capture handles

`Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/**` owns every process, descriptor and byte that can become evidence.
Delete raw-byte/result submission and public evidence-acceptance methods from
`ReleaseGateCommandDependencies` and `ReleaseGateOperationLease`. The production dependency may only
write already-formatted public lines and perform non-evidentiary terminal presentation/input; it
cannot provide stdout/stderr, PNG/AX/operation/command bytes, capture status, item id, digest, process
identity, artifact fact or success. OBSERVE/DESIGN challenge input remains dispatcher-owned.

Add package-internal concrete boundaries with no production injection initializer:

- `ReleaseGateOwnedProcess` opens its own `pipe2(O_CLOEXEC)` channels, captures and revalidates the
  hash-pinned executable and exact argv, launches/reaps the child, streams capped output and exposes
  only a private `ValidatedProcessExit` after exact status/parser checks.
- `ReleaseGateCaptureScope` is created by the dispatcher for one nonce, receipt sequence, fixed
  checklist ordinal/item and revalidated `ReleaseGateApplicationBinding`. It owns the screenshot
  file descriptor, AX query, window id, operation record and command record. Its only externally
  callable action is a closed primitive such as “capture this dispatcher-selected CGWindowID now”;
  the package derives item/status/digests and seals files from its retained handles. Neither the
  primitive nor dependency can select a path, item, schema, status or bytes.
- `ReleaseGateInstalledCaptureEngine` performs the AX traversal itself and launches the fixed,
  hash-pinned `/usr/sbin/screencapture` through `ReleaseGateOwnedProcess`. It parses PNG IHDR width/
  height and enforces encoded-size, width, height and pixel-area caps before any AppKit/ImageIO raster
  allocation, then performs full decode. `Package.swift` may add only the AppKit,
  ApplicationServices/CoreGraphics/ImageIO linker settings required by this non-product target and
  its tests; product membership remains unchanged.
- `ReleaseGateDesignerPresentation` opens the sealed package-owned handles read-only and produces the
  bounded record index. The dispatcher, not an adapter, captures reviewer PID/start, reads DESIGN or
  FAIL, writes/reopens the attestation and performs the adjacent sequence/artifact/process check.

Every capture scope has states `issued -> active -> sealed` or `issued/active -> invalidated`.
`defer` invalidates it and closes descriptors on success, throw, timeout, cancellation or child exit.
Only `sealed` can yield the package-private validated witness, once. Separate exact tests reject late,
concurrent, reentrant, retained, replayed, wrong-ordinal, wrong-process and cross-operation use and
prove zero transition/evidence publication. Preparation/install/log evidence uses the same owned-
process/owned-handle rule; test faults enter only package-internal `@testable` seams and are absent
from the compiled production factory and CLI grammar.

### B. Parent-authenticated one-row execution

`scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh` first builds the test bundle/probe, captures and later
revalidates its exact executable SHA-256, and becomes the sole `ROW_PASS`/terminal PASS emitter. For
each manifest entry it creates a fresh private `socketpair`, obtains an unpredictable 32-byte
challenge from `/dev/urandom`, and invokes exactly one filtered XCTest or exact-row probe with only
the inherited challenge/result descriptors. Child stdout/stderr are separately capped and must not
contain `ROW_PASS`, `CATEGORY_PASS`, totals or generic PASS vocabulary.

After that row's named effect and postcondition assertions and cleanup all succeed, a test-only
`ExactRowWitness` with a private initializer writes exactly one length-prefixed canonical record to
the result descriptor: schema, row id, fixed effect id, fixed postcondition id, fresh challenge MAC,
launched-executable hash, nonzero assertion count and `cleanupVerified=true`. The child cannot obtain
the result writer before its row-specific witness exists; generic catch paths cannot construct a
witness. The parent accepts only one complete bounded record, exact challenge/MAC/row/effect/
postcondition/executable binding, expected PID/exit status, nonzero count, EOF, no extra child/process,
closed descriptors and verified row-owned cleanup. Only then does the parent print `ROW_PASS <id>`.
Source and executable inventory tests reject manifest enumeration in children, category-wide entry
points, shared bulk emitters, catch-as-pass, duplicate witness construction and a witness call not in
the exact row function after both effect and postcondition assertions. Missing, stale, replayed,
truncated, duplicated, wrong-executable and self-printed records each reject independently.

Category 10 maps its six row ids to six exact filtered tests; specifically
`c10.production-adapter-failures` invokes the production-adapter failure test and
`c10.stale-coordinator-race` invokes the real stale-coordinator test. The parent never accepts row
labels printed by their former aggregate test.

### C. Immutable snapshot of the actual dirty candidate for category 7

Add package/test-only `ReleaseGateCandidateSnapshotV1`. Before any category-7 row, it captures the
same bounded preparation inventory from the live candidate: base HEAD; tracked index; tracked
worktree modifications and deletions; untracked files; relative path; `100644`/`100755` mode; size;
and SHA-256. It excludes only the already declared `.git`, `.build`, `.swiftpm`, `dist`, private
authority/evidence and test-runner output roots. Every file is opened fd-relatively/no-follow,
fstat-hashed-fstat checked, copied through an owned descriptor, and reopened; duplicates, special
files, links, path escape or drift reject. The sorted manifest and byte tree are sealed read-only and
bound to the exact preparation `contentDigest`/`contentCount` plus a `candidateDigest`.

Each of category 7's eleven invocations gets a fresh repository created from the same base commit,
then overlays that complete sealed candidate including deletions, executable bits and untracked
bytes. Before hooks or build, reconstruct the preparation inventory and require exact manifest/
candidate/content equality. Install the wrapper/hook bytes from that snapshot, pin their hashes, and
run the real pre-commit/post-commit/pre-push sequence and real `/usr/bin/swift`/product gates against
those exact dirty candidate bytes with no inherited `.build`. Only the one row-named Git fault is
then introduced. The authenticated row record binds candidate/content digest, wrapper/hook digests,
real Swift/test/smoke/product hashes, commit/parent/ref/reflog facts and hook order. Any omitted
untracked file, stale HEAD byte, mode drift, extra fault, fake tool/helper or snapshot change rejects.

### D. Complete stale-leaf move, rollback and retained-quarantine states

`GeneratedReleaseLeaf` retains no-follow descriptors for every safe ancestor and a dispatcher-
created owner-only random quarantine directory on the same device that is never disclosed to a
dependency. Its explicit state machine is:

1. `captured`: open/fstat the named leaf and retain its device/inode/type/link/mode/uid/size identity.
2. `prevalidated`: immediately before move, `fstatat(AT_SYMLINK_NOFOLLOW)` must equal the capture;
   mismatch fails without moving or unlinking anything.
3. `movedUnverified`: `renameatx_np(...RENAME_EXCL)` moves the current name into the private
   quarantine. No unlink is legal in this state.
4. `movedVerified`: reopen the quarantined name fd-relatively and require the complete captured
   identity again. Only this state may `unlinkat`; immediately before unlink, another fstat/fstatat
   comparison must still match. A mismatch never unlinks.
5. `rollback` or `retained`: on post-move mismatch, if the original name is descriptor-proven absent,
   restore exclusively with `renameatx_np(...RENAME_EXCL)`, reopen and verify that the restored leaf
   equals the moved observed identity, then fail with no cleanup-success claim. If absence or safe
   restoration cannot be proven, preserve the quarantined object and its owner-only journal, close
   handles, invalidate preparation and report `generated-output-held`, `restart-required`, and the
   one existing safe next command `scripts/pipeline.sh --prepare-installed-signoff`. A later prepare
   detects the private journal before stage 1 and may reconcile by the same identity rules, but never
   deletes a mismatch or claims cleanup while a retained object remains.

Race tests inject one mutation before move, after move and immediately before unlink for symlink,
hardlink, rename and byte replacement. They assert the exact state, no unrelated deletion, no
transition, safe restoration when possible, explicitly expected preserved forensic residue when
restoration is unsafe, truthful non-success transcript and zero residue only for clean success.
“Cleanup verified” is forbidden for a retained-quarantine result.

### Revised dependency order, risk map and Conditions for Builder

Builder order is now: (A) owned process/capture primitives and strict scope lifecycle; (B) migrate
prepare/install/observer/Designer evidence entirely into the package; (C) authenticated parent row
protocol and exact row functions; (D) sealed complete candidate snapshot and real category-7 repos;
(E) descriptor/quarantine state machine and transcript reason; (F) rerun every prior V17 exact row,
transcript and category obligation; (G) full frozen-scope gates. A material change returns to Design
and Security(plan).

- Manufactured evidence is blocked by package-owned executable/argv/process/fds/AX/capture bytes,
  strict preallocation caps and one-shot scopes, with lifecycle/replay/concurrency attack tests.
- False row success is blocked by unpredictable parent challenge, pinned launched executable,
  structured one-row witness, parent-only PASS and child process/status/cleanup verification.
- Stale candidate proof is blocked by the complete tracked/index/worktree/deletion/untracked mode+
  content snapshot and per-row reconstruction before real hooks/build.
- Wrong-leaf loss is blocked by pre/post-move/pre-unlink identity checks, unlink only from
  `movedVerified`, exclusive rollback and truthful retained-quarantine handling.

Conditions for Builder: dependencies/adapters cannot submit any evidence byte or fact; all
evidence-producing launches and handles are package-owned; capture scopes invalidate in `defer` and
seal once; the runner alone prints pass after authenticating one exact row; category 7 proves the
live complete candidate, not HEAD; a mismatched quarantined object is never deleted and never called
clean. Preserve the 50 category-5, 11 real category-7, 23 category-8, 20 category-9 and six separately
invoked category-10 outcomes, all V17 transcript/redaction/line-bound/one-next-command checks, the six
pinned hashes, two products and zero-LAN result.

Authorized files and closing gates remain exactly those in V17, except `Package.swift` may add only
non-product release-gate framework links and test-fixture exclusions required above. Required closing
evidence additionally includes all capture-scope lifecycle attacks, child attestation forgery/replay
rows, candidate omission/mode/deletion/untracked attacks and pre/post-move/pre-unlink race states.
Fresh Design Review and Security(plan) PASS are required before Build; fresh independent
Security(code) PASS remains required before runtime, installed Design sign-off, Test, pipeline/deploy,
commit or push.

**V17.1 Architecture Revision verdict: PASS.** The package now owns evidence provenance, the parent
owns authenticated row success, category 7 binds the exact dirty candidate, and stale cleanup has a
non-destructive truthful post-move state machine. Build remains blocked pending separate Design
Review/Revision and Security(plan) PASS on this exact revision.

## Design Re-review — V17.1 Closed Capture and Recovery States (2026-07-12)

Reviewed the V17.1 revision above at input whole-plan SHA-256
`3a2b10ffa8f9153306f9f949bcbd0f74c3db09e5e9264505528a72712d139f33` against the V17 Design PASS,
the existing observer/Designer handoff and the actual line-oriented wrapper copy. The four security
repairs preserve the approved operator experience only under these binding criteria.

1. **Package ownership is invisible, not a new provenance.** Owned processes, descriptors, capture
   scopes, nonces, evidence handles and process identities never appear in normal prompts, status,
   logs or recovery copy. Package custody changes how evidence is authenticated; it does not replace
   the human observer. Each checklist state still requires the observer's visible review and explicit
   item confirmation, and automation may not claim manual Paste, Finder/Dock launch, real click or
   VoiceOver judgment. Designer presentation retains the numbered human-readable fourteen-record
   index and reveals no path, handle, digest, raw AX/text value or capture implementation detail.
2. **Parent row authentication stays audit-only.** The inherited descriptors, challenge/MAC,
   executable identity, assertion count and child witness are never placed in argv, environment,
