   stdout/stderr, prompts or normal command grammar. Only explicit invocation of
   `scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh` may show line-oriented `ROW_PASS` progress and the fixed
   terminal matrix result. Even there, no challenge, candidate digest/path, child channel, private
   fixture location or raw failure is printed. Descriptor/challenge failure produces bounded
   non-success copy and cannot invite a manual replay, alternate row selector or bypass.
3. **Dirty-candidate proof is truthful without exposing it.** Category 7 may say only that the
   current candidate snapshot includes tracked, modified, deleted and untracked content with file
   modes. It must not call that candidate a clean checkout, committed `HEAD` or deployed product, and
   it must not print its path, manifest, filenames, counts or digests. No normal release command gains
   candidate/row terminology. The final audit PASS means the exact current candidate was exercised;
   it does not claim installed observation, Design approval, deployment, commit or push.
4. **Generated-output recovery has three distinct public outcomes.** A pre-move mismatch reports
   `artifact-changed`; a post-move mismatch that is safely restored reports `artifact-changed` and
   states only that generated output was restored without deletion; an unsafe restoration reports
   `generated-output-held` and states, `A generated build output was preserved for safe
   reconciliation. Source content was not changed.` None says `cleanup verified`, names quarantine,
   a journal/path/inode or asks the operator to locate, inspect, move or delete anything. All three
   are non-success, publish no prepared authority, use `restart-required`, and give exactly one safe
   command: `scripts/pipeline.sh --prepare-installed-signoff`.
5. **Retained state remains actionable across invocations.** `status` and the next preparation detect
   a retained private journal before stage 1 and repeat the public `generated-output-held` state plus
   the same one command; they never silently loop, claim a numbered stage failed or display an
   implementation error. If descriptor-safe reconciliation later succeeds, preparation may say,
   `Previously held generated output was reconciled safely. Source content was not changed.`, then
   begin the ordinary clean seven-stage transcript. If it remains unsafe, the object stays preserved
   and the run ends before `1/7` with the same truthful non-success block.
6. **Recovery remains terminal- and VoiceOver-safe.** Each state is emitted on stable lines of at
   most 256 UTF-8 bytes, with the public reason before disposition and `Next:` command; no color,
   cursor rewrite, animation or terminal width carries meaning. EOF, Ctrl-C, process failure and
   retained-state reconciliation failure end with one spoken-readable disposition. Transcript tests
   must cover pre-move mismatch, successful restoration, retained output, repeated retained status,
   later successful reconciliation and later still-retained reconciliation, asserting order,
   redaction, no false PASS/COMPLETE/cleanup/deployment language and no retry/bypass suggestion.
7. **Scope remains frozen.** V17.1 introduces no Pebble UI, checklist, text-entry, AppKit-driver,
   RPG, storage, LAN, pipeline/deploy or product behavior. The six pinned hashes, two-product graph
   and zero-LAN result remain Design-sign-off prerequisites.

**V17.1 Design Re-review verdict: PASS.** Package-owned evidence and parent-authenticated audit rows
remain invisible to normal operators, the exact dirty candidate can be described without overclaim,
and the quarantine state machine has safe, private and accessible recovery copy under the seven
binding criteria above. Any manual-delete instruction, hidden retry, private quarantine/challenge
detail, false cleanup claim, automated human-proof claim or game/runtime drift invalidates this PASS.
Build remains blocked until independent Security(plan) approves V17.1 together with this re-review.

## Security (plan) — V17.1 Closed Capture and Exact Candidate (2026-07-12)

Independently reran Security(plan) against the four V17 findings, V17.1 Architecture Revision and
Design re-review at input whole-plan SHA-256
`1b3ad38f0bccab4e5114a2ef41627c89e1e7741ee506efe7717505df99414309`. The review retained the
malicious-dependency, forged-row, dirty-candidate, same-user path-race, resource-exhaustion,
persistence, privacy and operator-recovery threats plus every exact category-5/7/8/9/10 obligation.

### Resolution review

- **Package-owned evidence: closed subject to implementation proof.** No dependency can submit a
  byte, status, identity, digest, item id or verdict that becomes evidence. The package owns the
  executable/argv, child, pipes, AX traversal, screenshot handle, parser, record construction,
  application binding and adjacent transition checks. This also requires package-owned, ordinal-
  bound TTY confirmation for every human checklist decision that affects acceptance; a presentation
  adapter may neither assert confirmation nor emit arbitrary public text. Scope invalidation in
  `defer`, preallocation image caps and the named late/concurrent/reentrant/replay/cross-bind tests
  close the former raw-evidence confused deputy.
- **Parent-authenticated rows: closed subject to implementation proof.** The parent is the only PASS
  emitter and accepts one capped challenge/executable/PID/row/effect/postcondition/count/cleanup-
  bound record from one exact child. The MAC is invocation/replay binding, not a substitute for the
  row assertion: private witness construction must remain reachable only after that exact row's
  effect and postcondition succeed, and independent source/executable inventory checks must enforce
  that dominance. All 148 rows remain separate invocations; no label, total or aggregate category
  output is evidence.
- **Actual candidate binding: closed subject to implementation proof.** The snapshot includes base,
  index, tracked worktree bytes, deletions, untracked bytes and modes and binds the preparation
  digest. It must perform equal complete live inventories before and after capture to reject cross-
  file drift, and reconstruct the Git index and worktree as distinct states so staged-only,
  unstaged and deletion cases are not flattened into one overlay. Each category-7 child must verify
  that exact reconstruction before its one named fault and real production gate.
- **Stale-leaf deletion: not closed.** V17.1 rechecks the quarantine pathname immediately before
  `unlinkat`, but pathname recheck plus unlink is not an atomic compare-and-delete. A same-user
  mutator can replace the quarantine entry after the final `fstatat` and before `unlinkat`; the call
  can then delete the replacement. Random naming, mode `0700`, a retained directory descriptor and
  an undisclosed path do not isolate an in-process dependency or another same-UID process, which can
  enumerate and mutate owner-accessible directories. The pre-move, post-move and pre-unlink test
  seams do not eliminate the untestable interval inside the last check/use pair. Darwin exposes
  pathname-based `unlinkat`, not an unlink-by-retained-file-descriptor primitive, so the stated
  "never delete a mismatch" property cannot follow from this algorithm.

### Required revision and retained conditions

Under the declared same-user race threat, automatic unlink must be removed from this path unless an
OS-enforced capability boundary makes the quarantine namespace unmodifiable by dependencies from
move through deletion. The safe portable disposition is: descriptor-relative exclusive move,
post-move identity proof, then retain the object and journal without unlinking; a mismatch is
restored exclusively when provably safe or retained, and neither case claims cleanup. If zero residue
is still required, Architecture must name a real atomic inode-bound deletion facility or an isolated
helper/sandbox boundary and prove it on the supported macOS target; path secrecy and another
check-before-`unlinkat` are insufficient. Any change to the public clean/stale/retained outcome must
return to Design review.

All other V17/V17.1 conditions remain binding: 50 category-5 outcomes; eleven real category-7
candidate repositories; 23 category-8 store outcomes; 20 category-9 durability/cache/evidence/log
outcomes; six separately invoked real category-10 Keychain/adapter/race outcomes; bounded/reaped
children, descriptors and private identities; no production fault/evidence/alternate-executable
escape hatch; complete stdout/stderr redaction and VoiceOver transcript matrix; exactly one safe
next command; and identical six product/object hashes, two products, frozen AppKit/game/checklist/
pipeline/LAN scope and zero-LAN scan.

Reviewed but not approved here: implementation bytes, runtime or installed behavior, production
Keychain mutation, test output, deployment, commit or push. Architecture and any affected Design
copy must be revised, then this Security(plan) gate must rerun before Build.

**V17.1 Security (plan) verdict: FAIL.** Package-owned capture closes evidence forgery,
parent-authenticated exact-row execution closes child PASS forgery, and the complete candidate
snapshot closes stale-HEAD proof under the binding conditions above. The final check followed by
pathname `unlinkat` does not close the same-user TOCTOU deletion threat and can still delete a
substituted entry. That single unresolved destructive boundary blocks Build.

## V17.2 Architecture Revision — Non-destructive Generated-output Holding (2026-07-12)

This revision closes only the sole V17.1 Security(plan) finding, read at whole-plan SHA-256
`7496de548720e13f06508de10f7b21908029816123f0177ca1c936879d453c2b`. All V17/V17.1 authority,
package-owned capture, parent-authenticated exact-row, complete dirty-candidate, transcript,
category-5/7/8/9/10, six-hash, two-product and frozen AppKit/game/checklist/pipeline/LAN conditions
remain binding.

### Replace deletion with a durable hold/restore-only state machine

`GeneratedReleaseLeaf` and its production call graph must contain no `unlink`, `unlinkat`,
`removeItem`, recursive removal or helper command that deletes a quarantined entry. Remove the
V17.1 `movedVerified -> unlink` transition and all cleanup-success wording for a moved leaf. The
package retains no-follow descriptors for the original parent and dispatcher-created owner-only
same-device quarantine directory, and uses these closed types:

- `GeneratedLeafIdentity` records device, inode, uid, type, link count, mode, size and content digest
  from retained descriptors; it has no pathname-only equality.
- `HeldGeneratedOutputJournalV1` durably records the attempt id, original parent-directory identity,
  original leaf name, quarantine-directory identity, random held leaf name, captured identity,
  post-move identity and disposition. It is written/fsynced/reopened under dispatcher authority
  before descriptors close and is never exposed to dependencies or normal output.
- `GeneratedOutputDisposition` is closed to `notMoved`, `heldVerified`, `heldMismatch`, `restored`
  and `restoreUnsafe`. There is no deleted/cleaned disposition.

The exact sequence is: capture and adjacent descriptor-relative prevalidation; exclusive
descriptor-relative no-replace move into quarantine; reopen the moved entry and compare its complete
identity; durably journal either `heldVerified` or `heldMismatch`; invalidate the preparation; close
descriptors only after the journal reopens exact; then emit the public non-success state
`generated-output-held`, `restart-required`, `No prepared authority was published.`, and exactly
`Next: scripts/pipeline.sh --prepare-installed-signoff`. Even an exact post-move match remains held;
it is never automatically deleted and never earns `cleanup verified` or zero-residue evidence.

The existing preparation command is the only reconciliation entry point. Before stage 1 it opens the
journal and both directories by retained no-follow descriptors and revalidates directory plus held-
leaf identities. It may perform exactly one mutation: restore the exact retained entry with an
exclusive no-replace descriptor-relative rename only when the original leaf is proven absent and the
held entry still exactly matches its journaled identity. It then reopens the original name, proves
that same identity, durably marks `restored`, and still reports non-success/restart-required without
a cleanup claim. If the original exists, any identity differs, a descriptor cannot be revalidated or
the no-replace restore loses a race, it performs no mutation, retains the entry and journal, records
`restoreUnsafe`, and repeats the same truthful held state. No output recommends, implies or exposes a
path for manual inspection, move or deletion; no automatic path can discard the held bytes.

### Verification and Builder conditions

Add source/binary scans proving the generated-output path imports/calls no deletion API and launches
no deletion helper. Exact race rows inject substitution before move, after move, before journaling,
before reconciliation and inside the original-absence/no-replace window. Each asserts: no deletion;
the moved entry remains reachable by the retained quarantine descriptor and matches either its
captured identity or the explicitly journaled mismatch identity; journal durability/restart loading;
no prepared transition; truthful `generated-output-held` copy; and no cleanup/complete/PASS claim.
Separate rows prove exact safe restore, occupied original, changed held entry, changed directory,
rename race, restart with held journal and repeated unsafe reconciliation. Safe restore asserts only
identity-preserving relocation and `restored`, never cleanup.

The category-5 mutation rows retain their 50 independent allow/reject outcomes, but any row that
moves a generated leaf expects durable held evidence rather than zero residue. Category-9 durability
rows cover every journal write/fsync/rename point and prove authoritative held state wins after
restart. Test workspaces may be destroyed only by the parent harness after the challenged child is
reaped and its held-state assertions are authenticated; that isolated fixture teardown is not a
production reconciliation or cleanup claim.

Builder order is: (A) delete the deletion transition/API reachability; (B) implement the durable
identity journal and closed dispositions; (C) implement restore-only reconciliation; (D) update the
held-state transcript matrix and exact race/durability rows; (E) rerun every unchanged V17/V17.1
focused, 148-row, full and frozen-scope gate. Conditions for Builder: no quarantined entry is ever
automatically deleted under the declared threat model; restore is identity-exact, descriptor-
relative and no-replace or no mutation occurs; every move is journaled and publicly held; neither
match nor restore is called cleanup; no manual-delete advice or private location is emitted.

Authorized files and closing evidence remain those of V17/V17.1. Fresh Design Review and
Security(plan) PASS are required before Build, and fresh Security(code) PASS remains required before
runtime, installed Design sign-off, Test, pipeline/deploy, commit or push.

**V17.2 Non-destructive Generated-output Architecture verdict: PASS.** The destructive TOCTOU is
removed rather than narrowed: quarantined bytes are durably held, and reconciliation can only restore
the exact retained identity without replacement. Build remains blocked pending separate Design
Review/Revision and Security(plan) PASS on this exact revision.

## Design Re-review — V17.2 Non-destructive Generated-output Holding (2026-07-12)

Reviewed V17.2 at input whole-plan SHA-256
`dda697cfe61fbaa637bb3675e1912084c02bd3588ffa511c2a4154e78aecc644` against the V17 and V17.1
Design contracts. The no-delete rule preserves private data and does not change Pebble game behavior,
but its current operator progression is not complete.

### Blocking finding

- **[HIGH] V17.2 restore-only sequence ->** The sole advertised command is not yet proven to make
  progress. The first preparation moves the stale generated output to the retained area and exits
  `generated-output-held`. The next preparation may restore that same output to its original name and
  must still exit non-success. V17.2 does not define how the following invocation avoids treating the
  restored canonical output as stale and holding it again before stage 1. Under the inherited V16
  clean/stale contract, this can cycle `held -> restored -> held` indefinitely. Repeating
  `Next: scripts/pipeline.sh --prepare-installed-signoff` in that state is retry guidance rather than
  an actionable reconciliation path.

### Binding closure criteria

1. Architecture must define one finite, identity-bound progression from `heldVerified`,
   `heldMismatch`, `restoreUnsafe` and `restored` to either ordinary preparation or a stable human
   blocker. A successful reconciliation must not re-enter the same hold on its next invocation.
   Preserving the held bytes while a fresh build proceeds is acceptable only if the exact security
   rules authorize it and no prepared/deployed claim calls the retained object cleaned or deleted.
2. Until that progression exists, normal and `status` output must persist exactly:
   `generated-output-held`, `A generated build output was preserved for safe reconciliation. Source
   content was not changed.`, `No prepared authority was published.`, `restart-required`, and the
   sole `Next: scripts/pipeline.sh --prepare-installed-signoff`. It must contain no path, journal,
   identity, cleanup/delete claim, manual-delete advice, retry/bypass wording or false
   PASS/COMPLETE/deployment claim.
3. The next revision must state what the sole command will do and give executable transcript/state
   tests for first hold, restart loading, safe progress, occupied original, mismatch, repeated unsafe
   reconciliation and final ordinary preparation. Tests must prove a bounded number of invocations
   for the safe path and stable non-mutating behavior for the unsafe path.
4. All lines remain stable, ordered and at most 256 UTF-8 bytes, with reason before disposition and
   the single `Next:` command; no color, cursor rewrite or animation carries meaning. VoiceOver must
   read the held state and outcome once per invocation without a silent loop.
5. The frozen six hashes, two-product graph and zero-LAN result remain mandatory. This revision may
   not change Pebble UI, checklist, text entry, AppKit driver, RPG, storage, LAN, pipeline/deploy or
   shipped product behavior merely to escape the release-gate dead end.

**V17.2 Design Re-review verdict: FAIL.** The retained-output copy is private, bounded and
non-destructive, but the restore-only workflow does not yet provide a finite usable progression; its
only command can recreate the same held state and therefore functions as retry advice. Architecture
must close the post-restore state and return to Design Re-review and Security(plan) before Build. No
code, runtime, installed proof, deployment, commit or push is authorized by this review.

## V17.3 Architecture Revision — Finite Consumed Restoration (2026-07-12)

This revision closes only the V17.2 Design FAIL, read at whole-plan SHA-256
`3b6a5a1aa6b2b057249cb0ba4a13674151c8dc80cabe3980df88fc330bf40caf`. Every V17-V17.2
Security condition remains binding: no deletion or deletion helper, package-owned evidence,
parent-authenticated exact rows, complete current-candidate category 7, exact categories 5/8/9/10,
redacted bounded transcripts, six protected hashes, two products and frozen shipped/AppKit/checklist/
pipeline/LAN scope.

### Durable finite state machine

Replace the V17.2 disposition with monotonic `HeldGeneratedOutputJournalV2` states:

`none -> heldExact | stableBlocked`

`heldExact -> restoreIntentConsumed -> restoredConsumed -> reconciledExcluded -> preparing`

`heldMismatch | restoreUnsafe | ambiguousCrash -> stableBlocked`

There is no transition from `restoredConsumed` or `reconciledExcluded` to any held state, and no
delete/cleaned state. Every transition is an expected-sequence compare-and-swap followed by file and
parent-directory fsync, reopen/decode and descriptor-relative identity verification. The journal
binds attempt id, state sequence, original/quarantine directory identities, both leaf names, exact
captured/moved/restored identity, a one-way `restorationConsumed=true` marker and the later fresh
build attempt identity.

The sole safe restoration is attempted at most once. While the exact held entry and both directories
match and the original name is descriptor-proven absent, the dispatcher first durably commits
`restoreIntentConsumed`; only then may it issue one exclusive descriptor-relative no-replace rename.
On exact success it reopens the original leaf and durably commits `restoredConsumed`. It stops that
invocation without a prepared authority. No code path can clear the consumed marker or issue another
restore rename for that journal.

Crash recovery is finite and non-destructive. From `restoreIntentConsumed`, inspect both retained
names without mutation: held absent plus original exact means the one rename completed and may be
finalized as `restoredConsumed`; held exact plus original absent means the rename did not complete,
but the consumed marker forbids retry and enters `stableBlocked`; both names, neither name, changed
identity/directory or indeterminate state enters `stableBlocked`. A crash before the durable intent
remains `heldExact` and may make the one authorized attempt. A crash after `restoredConsumed` resumes
only reconciliation/exclusion. No recovery branch moves an object twice, deletes bytes or cycles.

### Reconciled output is permanently ineligible; stage 2 owns the replacement

On the next preparation after `restoredConsumed`, the dispatcher reopens the original leaf and
requires the exact journaled restored identity. It then durably commits `reconciledExcluded` before
stage 1. That identity becomes a permanent negative capability for this journal: it cannot be
captured, held, hashed as the release candidate, accepted by any gate or moved again. If it is absent
or differs, enter `stableBlocked` without mutation.

Physical absence is no longer required for release eligibility. Stage 1 reports a nil eligible
release artifact while independently validating source security and the exact excluded identity.
Stage 2 alone creates the candidate in a dispatcher-created, previously absent, private per-attempt
SwiftPM scratch root using the package-owned fixed `/usr/bin/xcrun swift build --package-path
<candidate> --scratch-path <owned-attempt-root> -c release` process. It captures a product only from
that retained scratch-root descriptor and proves it is not the excluded device/inode/path. All later
gates bind this fresh build identity. The restored object and journal remain untouched and are never
called removed, cleaned or evidence. Future preparations that see the same exact
`reconciledExcluded` identity go directly to a new stage-1/stage-2 attempt; they never re-hold it.

The ordinary safe path is therefore bounded to three invocations: first hold, second one-time restore,
third reconcile/exclude and enter the fixed seven stages. It may take fewer after a pre-intent crash,
but never more through repeated mutation. Unsafe identities enter one stable, non-mutating blocker;
repeated preparation cannot attempt restore, move, build or publish.

### Public transcript and stable blocker

First hold preserves the approved lines: `generated-output-held`, the safe-reconciliation/source-
unchanged sentence, `No prepared authority was published.`, `restart-required`, and exactly
`Next: scripts/pipeline.sh --prepare-installed-signoff`. Successful one-time restoration says
`generated-output-restored`, `A generated build output was restored once and excluded from release
evidence. Source content was not changed.`, the same no-authority/disposition/Next block, and no
cleanup claim. The third invocation says once, before `1/7`, `Previously restored generated output
was reconciled and excluded from release evidence. Source content was not changed.`, then enters the
ordinary seven-stage transcript; this is progression, not an invitation to retry.

`stableBlocked` is durable and read-only. Preparation and `status` emit `generated-output-blocked`,
`Automatic reconciliation is unavailable. The generated output remains preserved. Source content
was not changed.`, `No prepared authority was published.`, `operator-review-required`, and exactly
`Next: scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.sh status`. They perform no restore/move/build/publication,
give no retry, delete, bypass or private-location advice and never claim cleanup/PASS/COMPLETE/
deployment. Every line is ordered, VoiceOver-readable and at most 256 UTF-8 bytes.

### Persistence, tests and Conditions for Builder

Add fault points before/after every journal write/fsync/rename and after the one restore rename.
Exact tests cover: first hold; restart load; one successful restore; third-invocation stage entry;
subsequent preparation bypassing hold; crash before intent; crash after durable intent before rename;
crash after rename before state commit; crash after restored commit; occupied original; held/original/
directory mismatch; both/neither names; no-replace race; and repeated stable-blocked invocations.
Assertions require at most one restore syscall, no move after consumed restoration, no delete syscall,
no held/restored loop, exact identity persistence, nil stage-1 release eligibility, a distinct
stage-2 scratch product, no prepared state before ordinary gates and the exact bounded transcripts.
Category-9 includes every new journal durability point; category-5 retains all 50 exact outcomes.

Builder order is: (A) V2 monotonic journal/CAS and crash decoder; (B) consumed one-time restore;
(C) permanent reconciled-exclusion and fresh stage-2 scratch build; (D) stable blocker and transcripts;
(E) bounded invocation/crash/race tests; (F) every unchanged V17-V17.2 focused, 148-row, full and
frozen-scope gate. Conditions for Builder: safe progression reaches stage 1 by the third invocation;
unsafe progression is stable and non-mutating; restoration is issued at most once; restored identity
is never eligible or held again; only stage 2 owns a distinct fresh release candidate; no automatic
or advised deletion exists; no transcript uses retry semantics or overclaims cleanup.

Authorized files and downstream gates remain exactly those of V17-V17.2. Fresh Design Re-review and
Security(plan) PASS are required before Build; fresh independent Security(code) PASS remains required
before runtime, installed Design sign-off, Test, pipeline/deploy, commit or push.

**V17.3 Finite Consumed-restoration Architecture verdict: PASS.** Exact held output is restored no
more than once, durably excluded from future release eligibility, and replaced only by stage 2 in a
fresh owned build root; unsafe states become stable blockers. The held/restored loop and retry
semantics are eliminated. Build remains blocked pending separate Design and Security(plan) PASS.

## Design Re-review — V17.3 Finite Consumed Restoration (2026-07-12)

Reviewed V17.3 at input whole-plan SHA-256
`5264bf4b64a37f40eaf11301654d9a63003dd76513b1865ebb113b3e5e24e476` against the V17.2 Design
FAIL and all prior transcript, privacy, VoiceOver and frozen-scope criteria. The revision closes the
operator dead end under these binding acceptance criteria.

1. **The visible safe path is finite and monotonic.** Its completed-invocation sequence is exactly
   `generated-output-held -> generated-output-restored -> preparing`, with preparation entered no
   later than the third completed dispatcher invocation. Restoration is attempted at most once; no
   visible or internal state may return from restored/reconciled to held. An externally interrupted
   invocation does not promise terminal copy, but its next completed invocation must advance from the
   durable state or enter `generated-output-blocked`; it may not reset the three-step progression or
   earn another restore attempt.
2. **First hold and one-time restore keep one safe action.** Hold emits, in order,
   `generated-output-held`, `A generated build output was preserved for safe reconciliation. Source
   content was not changed.`, `No prepared authority was published.`, `restart-required`, and only
   `Next: scripts/pipeline.sh --prepare-installed-signoff`. Restore substitutes
   `generated-output-restored` and `A generated build output was restored once and excluded from
   release evidence. Source content was not changed.` while retaining the same no-authority,
   disposition and sole command lines. Neither transcript says retry, again, cleanup, delete, PASS,
   COMPLETE, deployed or asks for a bypass or manual filesystem action.
3. **The third invocation clearly distinguishes preserved bytes from the release.** Before `1/7`, it
   says once, `Previously restored generated output was reconciled and excluded from release
   evidence. Source content was not changed.` It must also make clear that the preserved output is
   not the release candidate. The ordinary seven-stage transcript then begins and stage 2 alone may
   identify a newly built candidate internally. No operator-facing path, scratch root, inode, hash or
   journal is printed.
4. **Unsafe recovery becomes a stable blocker, not a retry loop.** `generated-output-blocked` is
   durable and read-only. Preparation and `status` emit the exact preserved/source-unchanged sentence,
   `No prepared authority was published.`, `operator-review-required`, and only
   `Next: scripts/installed-signoff-receipt.sh status`. That command is a safe state inspection, not
   a reconciliation promise; repeating it performs no move, restore, build or publication and does
   not tell the operator to retry, delete, reset, amend, force or bypass anything.
5. **Status mirrors the durable state.** Held, restored and blocked states survive process restart and
   are reported without collapsing to generic invalidated state. Each reports exactly one disposition
   and one `Next:` command. Once `reconciledExcluded` enters preparation, status follows the ordinary
   preparation contract and can never expose the preserved object as eligible evidence.
6. **Every outcome is VoiceOver- and narrow-terminal-safe.** Public lines are ordered, stable and at
   most 256 UTF-8 bytes; reason precedes disposition and command; no color, cursor rewrite, animation
   or terminal width carries meaning. Each completed invocation speaks one state and one outcome.
   Tests cover the three-invocation safe path, every crash point, subsequent preparations, and
   repeated stable-blocked status with no silent cycle or duplicate announcement.
7. **Privacy and frozen scope remain absolute.** Normal output reveals no quarantine/scratch path,
   journal, identity, sequence, hash, process fact, challenge, raw error or retained bytes. Nothing
   claims deletion or cleanup. The six pinned hashes, two-product graph and zero-LAN result remain
   mandatory; V17.3 changes no Pebble UI, checklist, text entry, AppKit driver, RPG, storage, LAN,
   pipeline/deploy or shipped behavior.

**V17.3 Design Re-review verdict: PASS.** The consumed restoration marker, permanent exclusion and
fresh stage-2 candidate provide a finite usable path without deletion or retry semantics, while
unsafe states settle into a private, non-mutating blocker. The seven criteria above are binding;
another restore attempt, a fourth completed safe-path invocation before preparation, ambiguous
cleanup language, more than one recovery command or frozen product drift invalidates this PASS.
Build remains blocked until independent Security(plan) approves V17.3 and this Design contract.

## Security (plan) — V17.2/V17.3 Final Non-destructive Recovery (2026-07-12)

Independently reran Security(plan) on V17.2, the V17.2 Design FAIL, V17.3 and the final Design PASS at
input whole-plan SHA-256
`9ca5bea9c1f2c327388ca105e1536ce6e773cec509bf68ac9d04a70c2e1faaf9`. The review rechecked all
four original V17 plan findings and retained the malicious-dependency, forged-row, dirty-candidate,
same-user race, crash/durability, resource, privacy and operator-recovery threats.

### Findings and binding interpretations

No unresolved plan-level finding remains. V17.2 removes every production delete API/helper from the
generated-output path, so a substituted entry can be moved and preserved but cannot be discarded.
V17.3 then makes restoration monotonic and finite: durable intent precedes the sole no-replace
rename; ambiguous post-intent states become permanently non-mutating blockers; exact success is
marked consumed; and the restored identity becomes permanently ineligible before any gate begins.
No state transition returns to held, no recovery branch performs a second restore, and no transcript
calls a held/restored object clean or deleted.

These interpretations are binding on Build:

- `none` with no generated release leaf follows the inherited ordinary clean preparation path and
  creates no journal. A stale leaf follows at most the three completed invocations specified by
  Design. Every journal transition is sequence-checked, durably written and reopened before the
  corresponding filesystem action or public state is accepted. `stableBlocked` is terminal for
  mutation: status/preparation may read it but cannot move, restore, build, publish or reset it.
- The restored excluded identity must remain untouched by every gate. Stage 1 and any verification
  script it calls may perform source-only work or compile solely in a separate dispatcher-owned
  scratch root; they may not clean, build in, hash as candidate or otherwise mutate the default
  `.build` leaf. Stage 2 and every later Swift/XCTest/pebsmoke, surface, binary and AppKit command
  must receive the same package-owned scratch-root/product identity through fixed package-selected
  argv, never an environment/operator selector. Each later parser and artifact record binds that
  fresh identity and proves it differs from the permanently excluded device/inode/path. No inherited
  default `.build` output or cache may satisfy a gate.
- Package-owned capture remains the sole evidence provenance. It owns all evidence-producing
  processes, argv, pipes/descriptors, AX/screenshot bytes, item confirmations, parsing, identities
  and adjacent checks; dependencies cannot submit evidence or arbitrary public output. Scope
  invalidation and the required late/concurrent/reentrant/replay/cross-bind attacks remain mandatory.
- The adversarial parent remains the sole PASS emitter. It authenticates one capped challenged
  witness from one pinned child after the exact row effect, postcondition and cleanup assertions.
  The complete live candidate is captured with equal pre/post inventories and reconstructed with
  distinct index and worktree states, including modifications, modes, deletions and untracked bytes,
  before each real category-7 fault/gate.

The exact executable matrix remains: 50 independent category-5 outcomes with held evidence where a
generated leaf moved; eleven candidate-bound real category-7 repositories; 23 category-8 store
outcomes; all 20 category-9 durability/cache/evidence/log outcomes plus every added journal
write/fsync/rename/crash point; and six separately filtered real category-10 Keychain/adapter/race
outcomes. Race tests must prove zero deletion calls, at most one restore syscall, no re-hold after
consumption, safe no-replace failure, preserved identity/journal and stable repeated blocker state.

All children, descriptors, sockets, scratch roots and random non-production Keychain identities are
bounded and closed/reaped; only the parent test harness may remove its exact isolated fixture after
authenticated assertions. Production has no scenario/fault/evidence/alternate-executable/scratch-
path escape hatch. The full stdout/stderr canary and VoiceOver transcript matrix must prove finite
ordered lines, redaction, one truthful disposition and exactly one safe `Next:` command, including
every crash point and repeated blocker status. The six pinned product/object hashes, exactly two
products, frozen AppKit/game/checklist/storage/text-input/RPG/pipeline/deploy/LAN scope and zero-LAN
scan remain mandatory closing evidence.

Not reviewed or approved by this gate: future implementation bytes, test results, runtime/installed
observation, production Keychain mutation, pipeline/deployment, commit or push. Builder must follow
the V17.1 then V17.3 dependency order and produce every named focused/full/frozen-scope result; a
material API, product, transcript or threat-boundary change returns to the earliest affected gate.
Fresh independent Security(code) remains blocking before any runtime or publication step.

**V17.2/V17.3 Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** The no-delete held journal eliminates the destructive
unlink race; the consumed marker, at-most-one restore and permanent exclusion eliminate the
held/restored cycle; and unsafe recovery is stable and non-mutating. Package-owned evidence,
parent-authenticated rows and exact dirty-candidate proof remain closed under the conditions above.
Build is authorized only within this exact plan and frozen scope.

## Reviewed Exception and Bounded Closeout Architecture (2026-07-12)

The user explicitly approves this reviewed exception to end the recursive verifier-design loop. It
supersedes only the V17-V17.3 same-UID/in-process-adversary, package-owned evidence-provenance,
self-authenticating 148-row and non-destructive quarantine requirements. The binding threat model is
now a local release gate that detects accidental regressions, stale artifacts, malformed local
state and ordinary tool failure. A malicious same-UID or in-process actor, deliberate test/probe
forgery and cryptographic proof that a row tested its claim are out of scope. This does not waive the
real build, test, installed human/VoiceOver/Designer, deploy, signature, receipt, commit or push gates.
Input whole-plan SHA-256 is
`0e84991187b5fa9db088ea111561b8f250b2c10b82efd2398f02322605c0041a`.

### Exact rollback and edits

1. Restore only the interrupted V17.3 compile-surface changes to the proven V16 implementation:
   in `Sources/PebbleReleaseGate/ReleaseGate.swift`, remove only `AppKit`,
   `ApplicationServices`, `CoreGraphics` and `ImageIO` imports and restore `public` on the V16 access
   inventory: `ReleaseGateState`, `ReleaseArtifactIdentity`, `ReleaseGatePayload`,
   `ReleaseGateCodec`, `ReceiptStateStore`, `EphemeralReceiptStateStore`,
   `KeychainReceiptIdentity`, `KeychainSecurityOperating`, `SystemKeychainSecurityOperations`,
   `KeychainReceiptStateStore`, `ReleaseGatePersistencePoint` and `ReleaseGateCoordinator`. In
   `Package.swift`, restore `PebbleReleaseGate` linker settings to Security only. Do not revert any
   V16 receipt/dispatcher/test behavior or any unrelated dirty-worktree file.
2. In `scripts/security-scan.sh`, remove the blocking invocation of
   `scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh`. Replace it with bounded local-safety gates: reject
   fixture/scenario/fault/alternate-executable or caller-evidence selectors in production CLI,
   wrappers, hooks and dispatcher; require every receipt/observer/Designer/finalize/resume wrapper
   and hook to exist and be executable; compile `PebbleReleaseGate` and the production receipt tool;
   run receipt `preflight`; run the focused `ReleaseGateTests` and
   `KeychainReceiptStateStoreIntegrationTests` and require a nonzero executed count with zero
   failures/skips. Retain source-security, text-adapter, SQLite boundary, secret/network/process and
   AppKit-driver compile checks.
3. Keep `scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh` only as optional, non-authoritative diagnostics.
   Its banner and terminal line must say `NON-AUTHORITATIVE DIAGNOSTIC`; it may retain its existing
   tests and nonzero diagnostic exit, but it cannot emit receipt, installed-signoff, deploy or release
   PASS and no required gate may interpret its row/category output as authority. Add source tests
   proving it is not invoked by `scripts/security-scan.sh`, `.githooks/pre-push`,
   `scripts/pipeline.sh`, receipt preparation or deploy/package scripts.
4. `.githooks/pre-push` and `scripts/pipeline.sh` retain their real blocking work but gain no
   adversarial-runner call. Their existing security-scan edge now reaches only the bounded checks
   above. Update `SECURITY.md`, `CONTRIBUTING.md` and the relevant README verification paragraph to
   distinguish optional adversarial diagnostics from authoritative local safety, test and installed
   release gates.

### Ordered build and verification

Builder order is binding: (A) restore the V16 imports/access/linker inventory and prove the target/
tool compile; (B) remove adversarial diagnostic authority and add the bounded scan/focused tests;
(C) label the optional runner and update durable docs; (D) run focused clean/stale dispatcher
preparation tests; (E) run the complete real closeout.

The complete closeout remains: production tool compile/preflight; real clean and stale
`run-prepare-gates`; deterministic warning-free release build; full XCTest with exact executed count;
457/457 `pebsmoke`; source security and SQLite self-test; release-surface and binary scans; the six
pinned product/object hashes, exactly two products and zero-LAN scan; real installed Pebble app;
the full manual text-field/RPG/standard/high-contrast/Paste/Finder/Dock matrix; granted and denied-
then-clicked VoiceOver; separate Designer attestation; `scripts/pipeline.sh`; installed bundle/
executable hash and codesign verification; receipt finalization; logical commits; pre-push gate and
GitHub push. A code change after proof invalidates affected downstream evidence.

### Risk-to-test map and rollback

- Production fixture/fault escape is covered by fail-closed source scans and parser rejection tests.
- Missing or non-executable release entry points are covered by exact file/mode checks and tool
  compile/preflight.
- Accidental receipt/Keychain/state regression is covered by focused ReleaseGate/Keychain tests plus
  real clean/stale preparation; the optional row matrix is supplemental evidence only.
- Stale or nondeterministic product drift is covered by two warning-free release builds, six hash
  equality, surface/binary/product/LAN checks, full XCTest and 457 smoke.
- False installed/deploy claims are covered by the real installed manual/VoiceOver/Designer receipt,
  pipeline exit/status, signature/hash verification and commit/pre-push binding.
- Diagnostic authority regression is covered by no-reference source tests and the mandatory
  `NON-AUTHORITATIVE DIAGNOSTIC` grammar.

Before editing, Builder records SHA-256 for the exact exception file set. If the V16 compile surface,
bounded scan or focused tests fail, stop and reverse only these exception edits with `apply_patch`;
never reset or overwrite unrelated user changes. If a later real gate fails, fix the product or gate
at its earliest affected phase and rerun invalidated evidence; do not restore the recursive V17
threat model or promote diagnostic rows to authority without a new explicit reviewed decision.

### Conditions for Builder

Keep scope to the exact files above and directly necessary focused tests. The V16 dispatcher and
receipt behavior are the baseline; no new AppKit/capture/quarantine/candidate-snapshot production
architecture is built. The adversarial runner is never blocking and never authorizes receipt,
installed sign-off or deployment. Focused tests must prove they actually execute. All ordinary
Pebble, security/SQLite, full-suite, smoke, installed accessibility, deploy, signature and source-
control gates remain mandatory. Preserve the six hashes, product graph and zero-LAN requirement.
Report optional diagnostic failures honestly, but they do not block under this exception unless a
bounded authoritative check independently reproduces the defect.

**Reviewed Exception and Bounded Closeout Architecture verdict: PASS.** The verifier loop is ended
by an explicit narrower threat model, not by weakening real product or installed release evidence.
Build is authorized only after separate Design Review and Security(plan) confirm this exception;
runtime, deployment, commit and push remain downstream of the complete bounded closeout above.

## Design Review — Reviewed Exception and Bounded Closeout (2026-07-12)

Reviewed the exception above at input whole-plan SHA-256
`4a205e1209dc0df9485129307da317ebcd7d1a52daaac9e72389aad80860634f` against the approved
seven-stage operator transcript, installed observer/Designer handoff and manual/VoiceOver matrix.
This is a developer-facing interaction change, not Design N/A. It preserves the release experience
under these binding acceptance criteria.

1. **The optional runner self-disqualifies on every visible line.** Its first line, every row/category
   progress line, every warning/error and its final line begin with `NON-AUTHORITATIVE DIAGNOSTIC`.
   A copied excerpt can therefore never look like a release result. It emits no standalone `PASS`,
   `COMPLETE`, `READY`, `AUTHORIZED` or `DEPLOYED`, and never uses
   `PENDING_INSTALLED_SIGNOFF`, `INSTALLED_SIGNOFF_COMPLETE`, receipt-finalized or deploy-success
   grammar. Its final result says only that optional diagnostics finished or found issues and states,
   `This result does not authorize release, installed sign-off, deployment, commit, or push.`
2. **Optional means absent from the normal journey.** Pipeline, security scan, hooks, package/deploy,
   receipt preparation, status, failure recovery and installed-signoff commands never invoke,
   recommend, summarize or parse the 148-row runner. No normal failure tells an operator to run it.
   It appears only in developer documentation under an explicitly optional heading with its direct
   command, scope and non-authoritative status. Running or skipping it cannot change receipt state,
   the one safe `Next:` command or any deploy/commit/push disposition.
3. **Bounded authoritative checks remain truthful.** The required scan reports `Bounded local safety
   checks: passed`, not comprehensive security, adversarial proof or resistance to a malicious local
   actor. The ordinary seven stages retain their exact order and copy—`Source security`, release
   build, release surface, binary scan, AppKit text entry, XCTest and pebsmoke—with one running and
   one truthful pass/fail result. A stage pass means only its documented bounded checks ran. Existing
   redaction, failed-stage, no-prepared-authority, disposition and single-next-command rules remain.
4. **Real release authority stays visually separate.** Only successful required gates may reach
   `PENDING_INSTALLED_SIGNOFF`; only the observer may seal the fourteen installed records; only the
   separate Designer may attest their review; and only finalization may claim installed sign-off
   completion. Pipeline/deploy success still requires installed bundle/executable identity,
   signature and receipt evidence. Optional row/category output is never embedded in authoritative
   logs, receipts, progress counts or success summaries.
5. **Installed experience is unchanged and complete.** The real installed manual text-field, RPG,
   Standard/High Contrast, compact/medium/large, manual Paste, Finder/Dock, denial-recovery and
   granted plus denied-then-clicked VoiceOver matrix remains mandatory. Observer prompts,
   completed/remaining guidance, privacy copy, separate Designer provenance, readable fourteen-item
   index and finalizer command do not change. Diagnostic completion cannot substitute for a single
   human, accessibility or Designer item.
6. **Terminal and documentation language is accessible.** Required and optional output remains
   line-oriented, stable and at most 256 UTF-8 bytes per line; no color, cursor rewrite or animation
   carries authority. The non-authoritative prefix precedes row ids so VoiceOver announces status
   before detail. Documentation gives one clear authoritative closeout sequence and places the
   optional command outside that sequence; it does not describe the diagnostic as a gate, proof,
   certification or deployment prerequisite.
7. **Failures are honest without becoming authority.** A nonzero optional diagnostic exit says that
   optional diagnostics found issues and records the bounded result without private paths, raw child
   output or release claims. Under the reviewed exception it does not block by itself, but any defect
   independently reproduced by a required bounded check follows that check's normal blocking copy.
   Operators are never encouraged to ignore a required failure, bypass hooks, use `--no-verify` or
   treat an optional success as remediation.
8. **Product scope remains frozen.** The exception changes release tooling and documentation only.
   It changes no Pebble screen, text entry, RPG behavior, installed checklist, VoiceOver behavior,
   AppKit driver, storage, LAN, product graph, packaging or deployment behavior. The six hashes, two
   products and zero-LAN result remain required closing evidence.

**Reviewed Exception and Bounded Closeout Design Review verdict: PASS.** Demoting the 148-row runner
is understandable and safe for operators when every diagnostic line carries its non-authoritative
status and no required surface consumes or promotes it. The real seven-stage, installed manual,
VoiceOver and independent Designer gates remain the only path to release authority. A standalone
diagnostic PASS, optional-runner reference in a required path, broad security claim, abbreviated
installed matrix or product behavior change invalidates this PASS. Build remains blocked until
independent Security(plan) approves the exception and this Design contract.

## Security (plan) — Reviewed Exception and Bounded Closeout (2026-07-12)

Independently reviewed the explicit user-approved threat-model exception, rollback architecture and
Design PASS at input whole-plan SHA-256
`f9942cb6b3e5841b1e1782069b0fdc9808aa614fdbaad79e625945af196d752c`. This review accepts as out
of scope a malicious same-UID/in-process actor, deliberate probe/test forgery and cryptographic proof
that each optional row exercised its label. It retains accidental/stale/malformed-state detection,
ordinary tool failure, production escape-hatch prevention, shipping security, real installed human
and accessibility evidence, deployment integrity and source-control binding.

### Findings and binding conditions

No unresolved plan-level finding remains under that declared threat model. The rollback is narrow
and mechanically checkable: only the four framework imports, the enumerated V16 `public` access
inventory and Security-only linker setting may change in the compile surface. Builder must capture
pre-edit hashes, apply an allowlisted patch, assert the exact post-edit declarations/imports/linker
settings, and prove the diff outside those tokens plus the separately authorized scan/runner/docs/
focused-test files is empty. It must not revert dispatcher, receipt, Keychain, observer, Designer,
hook, product or unrelated dirty-worktree behavior.

The optional runner is non-authoritative by construction and presentation. Every byte it writes to
stdout or stderr—including relayed child output, errors, row/category lines and its terminal
result—must be line-capped and prefixed `NON-AUTHORITATIVE DIAGNOSTIC`; the child cannot write
directly to the operator terminal. It has no production receipt/Keychain/evidence/install/deploy/
commit/push mutation path and emits none of their authority grammar. Required source/call-graph
tests must prove no direct or transitive invocation, parsing, recommendation or status dependency
from `scripts/security-scan.sh`, all three hooks, `scripts/pipeline.sh`, package/deploy commands,
receipt preparation/status/finalization/resume, observer or Designer commands. Optional success,
failure or omission cannot change a required exit status or authority state.

The replacement authoritative checks are meaningful for the bounded accidental threat: scan the
production dispatcher, CLI, wrappers and hooks for scenario/fixture/fault/evidence/alternate-
executable selectors and test the closed parser rejects those arguments; require exact regular-file,
owner and executable-mode expectations for every entry point; compile the real target and production
receipt tool; run real preflight; and execute separately filtered ReleaseGate and Keychain suites
with independently parsed nonzero counts, zero failures and zero skips. A grep hit, missing file,
mode mismatch, zero-test filter, malformed preflight or unexpected selector is blocking. Test-only
fault seams and random non-production Keychain identities remain confined to tests and cannot be
selected by production argv, environment, manifest or file input.

The exception does not weaken shipped Pebble security or replace release evidence. Security scan
must retain its existing source/privacy/secret/network/process checks, text-adapter and AppKit-driver
compile checks, and real SQLite boundary scanner/self-test. Pre-push, pipeline and receipt preparation
remain fail-closed on that scan plus real warning-free release build, full XCTest, 457/457
`pebsmoke`, release-surface and binary scans. Closing evidence must reproduce both clean and stale
dispatcher preparation, two deterministic release builds, the six pinned product/object hashes,
exactly `Pebble` and `pebsmoke` products and zero-LAN scan. The package/product graph and frozen game,
AppKit-driver, checklist, storage, text-input, RPG, LAN, packaging and deploy behavior may not move.

Installed and publication authority remains entirely real: `/Applications/Pebble.app`, exact
bundle/executable hashes and codesign identity; the complete manual text-field/RPG/appearance/size/
Paste/Finder/Dock matrix; granted and denied-then-clicked VoiceOver; fourteen observer records;
separate Designer attestation; receipt finalization; pipeline/deploy status; logical commits; the
blocking pre-push hook; and GitHub push. The optional runner cannot satisfy, shorten or appear in any
of those records. Any code change after a proof invalidates affected downstream evidence.

Reviewed but not approved here: future implementation bytes, test output, installed observation,
deployment, commit or push. A bounded authoritative failure blocks and returns to its earliest
affected phase. An optional diagnostic issue is reported honestly but blocks only if independently
reproduced by an authoritative bounded gate, as the explicit exception requires. Any re-expansion of
the threat model or promotion of diagnostic output requires a new reviewed decision.

**Reviewed Exception Security (plan) verdict: PASS.** The exact V16 compile-surface rollback and
bounded replacement gates are sufficient for accidental/stale regression detection; the 148-row
runner is unmistakably optional and has no route into release authority; and all real security,
SQLite, build, test, surface, binary, deterministic-hash, product, LAN, installed, accessibility,
Designer, deploy and source-control gates remain mandatory. Build is authorized only within this
explicit exception, exact allowlist and Design contract.

### Builder remediation — script-wide diagnostic relay (2026-07-12)

each raw line at 220 bytes, and adds `NON-AUTHORITATIVE DIAGNOSTIC:` exactly once. The EXIT guard
emits the unchanged no-authority statement for failures that occur before `fail`; `diag` and
`diag_err` now write raw messages through the relay. The new executable missing-tool regression
passed 1/1. A direct broken-`PATH` run exited nonzero with 4/4 lines prefixed/capped and exactly one
no-authority statement; the normal optional run completed ten categories/148 rows with 13/13 lines
prefixed/capped and exactly one no-authority statement. The authoritative security scan passed its
bounded local checks and 127-file SQLite scan. Required paths remain disconnected; the six release
hashes remain exactly pinned, products remain exactly `Pebble` and `pebsmoke`, zero-LAN and
`git diff --check` pass. No runtime, receipt, installed, deploy, commit or push action occurred.

**Builder remediation verdict: PASS.** Fresh Security(code) review is required on these final bytes;
all later gates remain unapproved and sequentially downstream.

Resolved the remaining bounded defect by preserving one EXIT handler for the script lifetime,

Six release hashes remained exactly pinned; products remained exactly `Pebble` and `pebsmoke`;
single-EXIT-handler, independent-signal-trap, zero-LAN, required-path disconnect and
`git diff --check` checks passed. No runtime, receipt, installed, deploy, commit or push action ran.

**Persistent EXIT cleanup remediation verdict: PASS.** Fresh Security(code) review remains required
on these final bytes; every later gate remains unapproved and sequentially downstream.

## Security (code) — Reviewed Exception and Bounded Closeout (2026-07-12)

Independently reviewed the implemented exception against the binding accidental/stale-regression
threat model and the Architecture, Design and Security(plan) contracts above. Reviewed the actual
`PebbleReleaseGate` import/access inventory and package linker setting; production parser,
dispatcher, receipt, observer, Designer, finalize, resume, package, pipeline and three hook paths;
the bounded security scan and its focused-suite count parsing; the optional runner and its source
disconnect test; clean/stale preparation fixture; durable documentation; current product/hash/LAN
boundaries; and the complete exception diff visible on disk. A repository-wide required-path search
found no direct or transitive invocation, parsing, recommendation, receipt mutation, state
transition, installed-signoff, deployment, commit or push authority from the optional runner.

### Finding

- **[MEDIUM] `scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh:4` ->** The optional runner does not satisfy
  the binding every-visible-line self-disqualification contract during an ordinary bootstrap/tool
  failure. Prefixing begins only at lines 22-24, after `dirname`, `cd` and other shell/tool work has
  already executed, and later unguarded tools can also write directly to inherited stderr. A
  controlled read-only invocation with `PATH=/nonexistent /bin/bash
  scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh` exited nonzero with four lines, one unprefixed:
  `scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh: line 4: dirname: command not found`. The three
  runner-generated lines were correctly prefixed and included the final no-authority statement, so
  this cannot confer release authority under the approved narrow threat model; nevertheless it
  directly violates the approved Design and Security(plan) condition that every stdout/stderr byte,
  including ordinary tool failures, be line-capped and prefixed. Establish one script-wide stdout
  and stderr line-prefix/cap relay before any fallible bootstrap/tool command (using Bash built-ins
  for the relay), make `diag`/`diag_err` write unprefixed payloads through that single relay, and add
  an executable test that induces an early missing-tool/bootstrap failure and asserts every emitted
  line begins `NON-AUTHORITATIVE DIAGNOSTIC:` and the final line denies release, installed sign-off,
  deployment, commit and push. Do not add this runner to any required gate.

### Empirical evidence

- `bash scripts/security-scan.sh` exited zero: `Bounded local safety checks: passed`,
  `sqlite-boundary-scan: passed (127 production Swift files)`, and `==> security: passed`. Its real
  target/tool compile, redacted preflight, separately filtered nonzero ReleaseGate and Keychain
  suites, retained text/AppKit/source/privacy/secret/network/process checks and SQLite self-test all
  ran. The Builder's exact focused counts remain 25 ReleaseGate plus 3 Keychain, zero failures and
  zero skips.
- Independently reran
  `swift test --filter PebbleReleaseGateTests.ReleaseGateTests/testAdversarialCategory01FixedPrepareWorkflowCleanStartAndRealBuild`:
  1/1 test passed in 17.693 seconds and exercised both `clean` and
  `stale-generated-release` preparation, emitting the eight expected category-1 diagnostics.
- The V16 rollback is exact at the reviewed surface: no `AppKit`, `ApplicationServices`,
  `CoreGraphics` or `ImageIO` import remains in `ReleaseGate.swift`; all eleven enumerated access
  declarations are `public`; and `PebbleReleaseGate` links only Security. Current source/manifest
  SHA-256 values are
  `d426b659cbc8f61fcedc8a14f13d83009983a6e7ab15b3c11c206e2876d02c59` and
  `be0d1b483cf0c18ef60e061b234874cac86aaf9ff20793fc28b06cff7e38bb98`.
- The six release hashes exactly match Builder evidence: Pebble
  `648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`, pebsmoke
  `d989c030656377ea1dca57b563408e2f6ae739e391904bde8be25f4f1d6dde2f`, PebbleStorage.o
  `ed37590e383037968b25905cb7ecd1d29e8faa43ba1f62a4919baebf9aabc6ba`, PebbleCore.o
  `39e37366bcba82ba65131a1112ad0b262f562279e363cf621542a804bd3ae028`, PebbleTextInput.o
  `547f0c44a950be0b7287ab73b918b6a3f486502e7ba57d7897e2d751b4d040ac`, and
  PebbleAppSupport.o
  `12d4b146ea24da2457066590f0b2c54e331fa098f8f82f54849856e838b07383`.
  `swift package describe --type json` reported exactly `pebsmoke` and `Pebble`; the exact-word
  LAN/Network scan across PebbleReleaseGate, PebbleAppSupport and the AppKit driver returned zero;
  the stale malformed `Pebble/Pebble` artifact was absent; and `git diff --check` exited zero.

Not reviewed or performed here: installed UI/VoiceOver observation, Designer sign-off,
`scripts/pipeline.sh`, deployment, receipt finalization, commit, pre-push or GitHub push. The
Builder's full 1,058-XCTest, 457-smoke, warning-free deterministic build, release-surface and binary
results were read but not independently rerun in full because this gate's new blocking evidence is
confined to the optional diagnostic's presentation boundary.

**Reviewed Exception Security (code) verdict: FAIL.** Required release authority is correctly
disconnected from the optional runner and every authoritative bounded/product gate reviewed is
green, but the runner still leaks an unprefixed shell/tool failure before its self-disqualifying
relay exists. Fix only that bounded presentation defect and its executable regression test, rerun
the optional normal/failure prefix matrix plus the authoritative bounded scan, and return for a
fresh Security(code) review. Runtime, Design Sign-off, pipeline, deploy, receipt, commit and push
remain blocked; do not reopen V17's same-UID/in-process proof architecture.

## Security (code) Rerun — Diagnostic Relay Remediation (2026-07-12)

Reviewed only the final-byte remediation of the sole prior finding, at optional-runner SHA-256
`81ade78156722f18616168ef0d52f56f23f991a1edd63fa336c79cdb6b4c3030` and
`ReleaseGateTests.swift` SHA-256
`37e39a9914c5f30dabc40b597dd38efe1a26bc8e4316492b78dcab2a3d6c9561`.
The relay now precedes repository discovery; the focused early-missing-tool test executed 1/1 and
passed; its broken-PATH output is prefixed, capped and contains exactly one final no-authority
statement. Required paths remain disconnected and the normal successful diagnostic evidence remains
13/13 prefixed lines without a double prefix.

### Finding

- **[MEDIUM] `scripts/release-gate-adversarial-test.sh:82` ->** The remediation replaces the
  no-authority EXIT handler installed at line 34 with `trap cleanup EXIT INT TERM`. Consequently an
  unexpected failure after the work directory is created exits through `cleanup` without the exact
  final no-authority statement. This is executable, not hypothetical: a read-only fake `swift` that
  exited zero with only `Executed 1 test, with 0 failures` advanced through work-directory creation
  and then caused the unguarded row-extraction pipeline to fail. The runner exited 1 with one line;
  it was correctly prefixed and 103 bytes, but the no-authority statement count was zero. Preserve
  one EXIT handler for the entire invocation and compose cleanup inside it: initialize an optional
  work path before installing the handler, have `on_exit` perform bounded cleanup and then emit the
  statement exactly once on every nonzero exit, and make INT/TERM convert to nonzero exit without
  replacing EXIT. Extend the executable regression to induce this post-workspace unexpected failure
  and assert every line is prefixed/capped and the exact final statement occurs once. No required
  gate or release authority path may change.

### Rerun evidence

- `bash scripts/security-scan.sh` independently exited zero after the remediation:
  `Bounded local safety checks: passed`, `sqlite-boundary-scan: passed (127 production Swift files)`
  and `==> security: passed`. This includes the now 26-test ReleaseGate suite, three-test Keychain
  suite, production compile/preflight and all retained security checks.
- Repository-wide source search again found no optional-runner name or diagnostic vocabulary outside
  the optional docs/tests/runner surfaces, hence no direct or transitive receipt/state/install/
  deploy/commit/push authority path.
- The six release hashes remain exactly
  `648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`,
  `d989c030656377ea1dca57b563408e2f6ae739e391904bde8be25f4f1d6dde2f`,
  `ed37590e383037968b25905cb7ecd1d29e8faa43ba1f62a4919baebf9aabc6ba`,
  `39e37366bcba82ba65131a1112ad0b262f562279e363cf621542a804bd3ae028`,
  `547f0c44a950be0b7287ab73b918b6a3f486502e7ba57d7897e2d751b4d040ac` and
  `12d4b146ea24da2457066590f0b2c54e331fa098f8f82f54849856e838b07383`.
  Products remain exactly `Pebble` and `pebsmoke`; the frozen release-gate/AppSupport/AppKit-driver
  LAN/Network scan returned zero; and `git diff --check` exited zero.

Not performed: runtime, installed UI/VoiceOver/Designer review, pipeline, deployment, receipt,
commit, pre-push or push.

**Diagnostic Relay Security(code) rerun verdict: FAIL.** The original pre-relay leak is fixed and
all required authority remains correctly disconnected, but the cleanup trap unintentionally removes
the final-statement guarantee for later unexpected failures. Fix only the composed EXIT/cleanup
handler and its post-workspace failure test, then return for one bounded final-byte review. Do not
reopen the verifier architecture or rerun downstream runtime/publication gates before PASS.

## Final Security (code) Rerun — Persistent EXIT and Cleanup (2026-07-12)

Independently reviewed the exact final remediation at optional-runner SHA-256
`916eff8671ff15d7e6a2922426dbd11c63bee385c91beff27bebf9bbb9993b92` and

`837b274a81924b4384f49c03bfbc5ad4fc33b33d16cde61fc45b6dd56b4f4255`.
No unresolved finding remains under the approved accidental/stale-regression threat model.

The script installs one EXIT handler at line 44 and never replaces it. The optional workspace is
initialized before that handler, `on_exit` composes cleanup before its exactly-once final statement,
and INT/TERM call `on_signal`, which exits nonzero and therefore reaches the persistent EXIT handler.
Neither signal trap claims EXIT. The relay still precedes the first fallible repository/tool command
and caps the raw payload of every stdout/stderr line at 220 bytes before adding the single
`NON-AUTHORITATIVE DIAGNOSTIC:` prefix.

- The six release hashes remain exactly
  `648af840890e9feee9a5e3f5ce2743ee043f4336fb7b122a1b4d8b263fee2ac8`,
  `d989c030656377ea1dca57b563408e2f6ae739e391904bde8be25f4f1d6dde2f`,
  `ed37590e383037968b25905cb7ecd1d29e8faa43ba1f62a4919baebf9aabc6ba`,
  `39e37366bcba82ba65131a1112ad0b262f562279e363cf621542a804bd3ae028`,
  `547f0c44a950be0b7287ab73b918b6a3f486502e7ba57d7897e2d751b4d040ac` and
  `12d4b146ea24da2457066590f0b2c54e331fa098f8f82f54849856e838b07383`.

**Final Reviewed Exception Security (code) verdict: PASS.** Both prior diagnostic-presentation

The approved bounded exception may proceed to installed Design Sign-off, independent Test,
